Small-Scale LNG 101

Everything you need to understand about liquefied natural gas at small scale — what it is, how it works, why it matters, and where the industry is heading.

Cooling Temperature
-162°C
-260°F · Colder than Antarctica's record
Volume Reduction
1/600th
Gas shrinks 600× when liquefied
Energy Density
~50 MJ/kg
High energy in compact liquid form
SS-LNG Threshold
<0.5 MTPA
Per IGU: below 500K tonnes/year capacity
🧊 What Is LNG?

Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG) is natural gas cooled to -162°C (-260°F) until it becomes a clear, colorless, non-toxic liquid — ~99.9% pure liquid methane. In this liquid state, it takes up just 1/600th of its original gas volume, making it practical to store and transport where pipelines don't exist. LNG trains cool pipeline gas in stages: heavier hydrocarbons (ethane, propane, butane) and impurities (CO₂) condense at warmer temperatures and are removed as liquids — methane condenses last. Each LNG train is comparable in scale and complexity to an oil refinery, costs billions of dollars, and takes years to build.

💡 Think of it like...
Imagine you have a beach ball full of gas. LNG is like deflating that beach ball and compressing all the energy into something the size of a ping-pong ball. That's the magic of cryogenic liquefaction — same energy, tiny package, ready to ship anywhere.

LNG is odorless, non-corrosive, and non-toxic. If spilled, it evaporates harmlessly. It cannot explode in an unconfined space. These properties make it one of the safest fuels to handle and transport.

📐 The 600× Volume Shrinkage
🔗 The Small-Scale LNG Value Chain
🏗️ Small-Scale vs Large-Scale LNG

🧩 Small-Scale LNG

  • Capacity: <0.5 MTPA per unit
  • Build time: 12–30 months
  • Capex: $20–60M per facility
  • Modular, containerized, relocatable
  • Serves niche & off-grid markets
  • Short-term contracts acceptable
  • Virtual pipeline delivery (trucks)
  • Can monetize flare gas, biogas
vs

🏭 Large-Scale LNG

  • Capacity: 5–25+ MTPA per train
  • Build time: 4–10 years
  • Capex: $5–30 billion per project
  • Permanent, massive infrastructure
  • Serves global export markets
  • 20-year take-or-pay SPAs required
  • Dedicated LNG carrier fleet
  • Requires giant gas field reserves
💡 Analogy
Large-scale LNG is like a transcontinental airline — massive investment, fixed routes, long-term planning. Small-scale LNG is like Uber — flexible, on-demand, goes exactly where you need it, and you can start small.
🚛 The "Virtual Pipeline" Concept
🎯 Where Is Small-Scale LNG Used?
🌍 Emissions: LNG vs Other Fuels

LNG burns cleaner than diesel, HFO, and coal, producing fewer harmful pollutants and greenhouse gases per unit of energy. Here's how it compares:

-20%
CO₂ vs diesel
-90%
NOx vs diesel
-99%
SOx vs HFO
-99%
Particulates
⚠️ The Methane Slip Caveat
LNG engines can release small amounts of unburned methane — called "methane slip". Since methane is a potent greenhouse gas (80× stronger than CO₂ over 20 years), this partly offsets LNG's CO₂ advantage. The industry is tackling this: Wärtsilä's NextDF technology has reduced slip to as low as 1.1% in marine engines. On a well-to-wake basis, LNG's total GHG benefit is debated (2–20% improvement vs diesel), but bio-LNG eliminates this concern entirely with up to 92% lifecycle reduction.

Key regulations driving cleaner LNG use: IMO 2020 (sulfur cap), FuelEU Maritime (2% GHG cut from 2025), EU ETS (100% allowance surrender from 2026), and EU CO₂ standards for trucks (15% cut by 2025, 45% by 2030).

🔑 Why Does Small-Scale LNG Matter?
💡 The Big Picture
Small-scale LNG sits at the intersection of three megatrends: the global energy transition (cleaner fuels), energy access (reaching the unconnected), and energy security (reducing pipeline dependence, as Europe learned after Russia's invasion of Ukraine). It's not a mega-project technology — it's a distributed, modular, deploy-anywhere toolbox that brings gas to customers who otherwise couldn't get it. And when the gas turns green (bio-LNG), the same trucks, tanks, and terminals deliver a near-zero-carbon fuel with no infrastructure rebuild.

CONVERSIONS
Gas & LNG: 1 MMBtu = 1.055 GJ = 293.1 kWh · 1 mt LNG = 46.7 MMBtu · 1 m³ NG = 0.0366 MMBtu · 1 m³ LNG = 0.45 mt = 21.1 MMBtu
Oil Products: Diesel 1 gal = 0.14 MMBtu · 1 mt = 42.7 MMBtu · VLSFO 1 mt = 40.2 MMBtu · HFO 1 mt = 39.5 MMBtu · Crude 1 bbl = 5.8 MMBtu
LPG: 1 mt = 46.3 MMBtu · 1 gal propane = 0.0916 MMBtu · Coal: 6,000 kcal/kg = 24.0 MMBtu/mt · 5,500 kcal/kg = 22.0 MMBtu/mt
Alt Fuels: Methanol 1 mt = 16.8 MMBtu · Ammonia 1 mt = 15.4 MMBtu · H₂ 1 kg = 0.114 MMBtu · Electricity: 1 kWh = 0.003412 MMBtu
Biomass: Wood Pellets 1 mt = 17.0 MMBtu · Green Chips 1 mt = 8.5 MMBtu · Bagasse 1 mt = 7.5 MMBtu · Ethanol 1 L = 0.0214 MMBtu

HH–JKM Spread
~$15/MMBtu
~$3.05 (HH) vs ~$20 (JKM). "Trade of 2026." US LNG FOB ~$6.55 → Asia DES ~$20. Shipping + regas = $3–5. Margin = $7–10/MMBtu for portfolio players.
Source: EIA · Platts
TTF–HH Spread
~$13/MMBtu
~$3.05 (HH) vs ~$15.80 (TTF). US LNG to Europe: FOB ~$6.55, DES ~$15.80. Shipping $1.50. Strong arbitrage driving record US LNG exports to Europe (68% of total).
Source: EIA STEO · ICE
LNG Bunker vs VLSFO
−$150–250/mt
LNG $650/mt vs VLSFO $800/mt. Plus: FuelEU compliance + EU ETS avoidance = additional ~$5–15/mt equivalent. Bio-LNG premium: 1.8–5× fossil LNG but pooling offsets.
Source: Mordor Intelligence · SEA-LNG
China LNG vs Diesel
Threshold: 0.8×
When 1 kg LNG < 0.8 liters diesel in price, LNG trucks dominate. NW China: ¥4,125/mt LNG vs ¥7,602/mt gasoil → 0.54× = highly economic. Coastal: closer to parity.
Source: S&P Global / CPPEI

Why US Gas Decouples from Global LNG — Even in a Crisis
STRUCTURAL
⛽ US LNG Pricing
Henry Hub + liquefaction fees + fuel gas + shipping. Cost-plus model. Connected to global via LNG trains but NOT equalized.
🌍 Global LNG Pricing
Oil-linked contracts (Qatar), regional hubs (JKM, TTF), or spot cargo scarcity. Demand-driven. These are NOT the same market as US gas.
🛢️ Associated Gas (Permian)
Oil wells produce gas as byproduct. Cannot shut gas without shutting oil. Flaring constrained. Producers pay to dispose of gas — WAHA hub goes negative regularly.
📉 WAHA Negative Prices
Permian gas pricing hub. Regularly goes negative — gas supply overwhelms takeaway capacity. Higher oil prices → more Permian production → more associated gas → MORE downward pressure on US gas.

The structural paradox: During a prolonged Persian Gulf disruption (like the 2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis), the global energy system experiences three different price pressures simultaneously: crude oil higher (possibly much higher), global LNG higher (also much higher), but US domestic pipeline gas neutral or even lower. Why? Because higher oil prices drive Permian production, creating more associated gas that is price-insensitive — it must be moved into pipelines even at negative value. LNG exports are the bridge between US and global markets, but they operate on contractual volumes and physical train-level constraints. They shape the forward curve, not day-to-day price dislocations. At times, 15–20% of total US dry gas production flows into LNG export facilities, setting a floor under US gas demand — but not eliminating the structural gap between Henry Hub and global benchmarks.

Sources: Ronh Energy · AEGIS Hedging / ICE (WAHA) · EIA STEO
SS-LNG Liquefaction CAPEX
$200–1,600/tpa
Wide range by technology. SMR ~$860/tpa, N₂ expander ~$1,070/tpa. Cryobox at upper end. US brownfield: ~$460/tpa. Stabilis Galveston: $350–400M.
Source: OIES NG137
FSRU Cost
$250M+
New-build. Deploy 6–12 months (vs 3–5 yr land terminal). Conversion: $50–100M. Charter: >$100K/day. 45+ FSRUs globally. Regas tariff: $1.68–3.93/MMBtu.
Source: Market Reports World
LNG Carrier (160K m³)
$200–250M
913 carriers operational (Oct 2025). Spot charter: ~$97K/day. Shipping cost: $0.85–1.50/MMBtu by distance. Panama Canal transit: ~$380K per vessel.
Source: Seala AI · ShipFinex
LNG Bunkering Vessel
$50–80M
7,500 m³ vessel: $60–75M build + ~$8M/yr ops. Shore tank: >$120M + 24-month permitting. LNG fueling station: $2–5M. NG truck premium: +$50K (+33% vs diesel).
Source: Mordor Intelligence

US Tolling Model
115–121% HH
+ $4–4.5 constant. 20-year take-or-pay. FOB destination-flexible. Fixed tolling $2–3/MMBtu. 56% of 2025 SPAs indexed to HH. Buyer bears commodity risk.
Source: Timera Energy · S&P Global
Brent Oil-Linked
~12% Slope
At $101 Brent: ~$12.12/MMBtu. 64% of deals ≤10 yr are Brent-linked. Dominant in Middle East + Asian shorter-term. Qatar/Australia sellers committed to oil indexation.
Source: Columbia CGEP · S&P Global
Bio-LNG Premium
1.8–5× Fossil
$21–60/GJ vs $12/GJ fossil LNG. GHG-savings-based. FuelEU pooling creates demand. ZEMBA tender: ~250K mt bio-LNG. Premium declining as scale grows.
Source: S&P Global (Bio-LNG)
SS-LNG Take-or-Pay
$200M
Stabilis data center contract (multi-year). Also: 22M gal/yr marine bunkering. NFE-Norsk Hydro 15-yr. Centrica-Petrobras 0.8 Mtpa × 15 yr. Market shifting from spot to term.
Source: Stabilis IR · EIA
LNG Pricing Models at a Glance
MODELS
🇺🇸 US Tolling (FOB)
115-121% HH
+ $4-4.5 constant. 20-yr take-or-pay. Destination-flexible. Buyer bears commodity risk. 56% of 2025 SPAs.
🛢️ Brent Oil-Linked
~12% Slope
At $101 Brent: ~$12.12/MMBtu. 10-20 yr DES. 64% of deals ≤10 yr. Qatar/Australia committed. Seller bears vol risk.
📊 JKM Spot Index
~$20.5
Mar 2026 spot. Platts JKM. Growing share. 2/3 of Asian spot JKM-indexed. Volatile: $9.50 (2024) → $20.50.
🌿 Bio-LNG Premium
1.8–5×
$21-60/GJ vs $12/GJ fossil. GHG-savings-based. FuelEU pooling creates demand. ZEMBA ~250K mt tender.
🔒 SS-LNG Take-or-Pay
$200M
Stabilis DC contract. NFE-Hydro 15-yr. Centrica-Petrobras 0.8 Mtpa × 15 yr. BOOM model: $0 upfront.
📋 FSRU Charter
>$100K/d
5-10+ yr charters. Purchase ($300M) yields lower regas tariff. Regas: $1.68/MMBtu at 1.0 mtpa.

CONTRACTS
Contract TypePricing MechanismTypical TermsKey Data Points
US Tolling (FOB)115–121% of Henry Hub + $4–4.5 constant20-year take-or-pay; FOB destination-flexibleFixed tolling fee $2–3/MMBtu. Cargo cancellation rights. 56% of 2025 SPAs indexed to HH. Cameron LNG: $7.5B debt on 20-yr tolling.
Brent Oil-Linked SPA~12% slope × Brent price10–20 years; DES (delivered)64% of deals ≤10 yrs are Brent-linked. At $80 Brent: ~$9.60/MMBtu. Dominant in Middle East + Asian shorter-term deals.
JKM Spot / Short-TermPlatts JKM indexSpot to 2 yearsJKM averaged $9.50/MMBtu (Jan–Apr 2024). Now ~$20.50 (Mar 2026 crisis). Growing share of total trade.
Bio-LNG (Bunker)GHG-savings-based premium1–3 year offtake$21–60/GJ depending on GHG savings (vs $12/GJ fossil LNG). 1.8–5× fossil LNG price. ZEMBA tender: ~250K mt bio-LNG.
SS-LNG Supply (Stabilis)Take-or-pay; volume-basedMulti-year$200M data center contract. 22M gal/yr marine bunkering contract. FY2024 adj. EBITDA $11.8M on $68.2M revenue (17% margin).
FSRU CharterFixed daily rate5–10+ years>$100K/day. Purchase ($300M) yields lower regas tariff vs charter. Regas: $1.68/MMBtu at 1.0 mtpa volume.
Virtual Pipeline (Aggreko)BOOM (Build-Own-Operate-Maintain)3–7 year mine-life50% fuel savings vs diesel. 6–10 MW loads. Trucks over 500–1,000 km. Installed in 3 months. $0 upfront for customer.
Sources: Timera Energy · S&P Global · EIA · S&P (Bio-LNG) · Stabilis IR
SS-LNG Gross Margin (US)
~17%
Stabilis FY2024: adj. EBITDA $11.8M on $68.2M revenue. Margin expanding as data center + aerospace contracts scale. Galveston FID ($350-400M) = step-change.
Source: Stabilis IR
LNG vs Diesel Savings
30–50%
Mining/remote power: 50% fuel savings (Aggreko). US HD trucking: 30-40% fuel cost savings vs diesel. China NW: 20-30% cheaper. EU: compressed by higher TTF pricing.
Source: Aggreko · Cummins
NG Truck Payback
18–24 mo
$200K NG truck vs $150K diesel (+$50K premium). High-mileage fleets recoup in 18-24 months. BEV: TCO 10-26% below diesel in China already (Goldman). Payback narrowing.
Source: Cummins · Tracey Road
Virtual Pipeline BOOM
$0 Upfront
Build-Own-Operate-Maintain. Aggreko/NFE model: 3-7 yr mine-life contracts. 6-10 MW loads. Trucks over 500-1,000 km. Installed in 3 months. Customer pays per MMBtu consumed.
Source: Aggreko · NFE IR
Where SS-LNG Wins: Economic Sweet Spots by Segment
SWEET SPOTS
🖥️
US Data Centers
30–50%
savings vs grid. Behind-meter gas turbines. Grid wait 3-7 yr. LNG in months. $200M contracts.
🇨🇳
China NW Trucking
50%+
savings vs diesel. Near gas fields. ¥4,125 vs ¥7,602/mt. Widest global spread. But BEV closing fast.
⛏️
Mining / Remote
30–50%
savings vs diesel gensets. BOOM: $0 upfront. Deployed in 3 months. 6-10 MW loads.
🚢
EU Bunkering
15–30%
incl. FuelEU + EU ETS. $650 vs $800/mt VLSFO. Bio-LNG pooling = extra revenue. Regulation-boosted.
🇧🇷
Brazil Virtual Pipeline
20–35%
vs LPG / no access. 90% off-grid. LNG→regas→local pipe. Decades of TAM.
🏝️
Island Power
25–40%
vs diesel ($30-40/MMBtu remote). Indonesia 17K+ islands. ISO containers. Niche but high-margin.
SS-LNG Unit Economics by End-Use Segment
ECONOMICS
SegmentSS-LNG CostAlternativeSavingsPaybackKey Economics
🇺🇸 US Data Centers$8–12/MMBtuGrid power ($50–80/MWh)30–50%ImmediateBehind-the-meter gas turbines. $200M take-or-pay contracts. Grid connection wait: 3-7 yrs. LNG deployed in months. Stabilis, EDGE, Galileo supply.
🇺🇸 US Marine Bunkering$13–15/MMBtuVLSFO ($800/mt = $17.1)10–25%Vessel lifeSavings on fuel + compliance (no scrubber needed). Carnival, TOTE Maritime, Crowley = anchor customers. Jones Act fleet growing.
🇪🇺 EU Marine Bunkering$14–17/MMBtuVLSFO + EU ETS15–30%Vessel lifeFuelEU + EU ETS 100% = additional $5-15/mt equivalent savings. Bio-LNG pooling adds premium revenue. Rotterdam $650/mt vs VLSFO $800/mt.
🇨🇳 China HD Trucking (NW)$12–13/MMBtuDiesel ($25.7/MMBtu)50%+6–12 moNW China near gas fields: ¥4,125/mt. Diesel spread = widest globally. Economics drove 307% sales surge (2023). But BEV closing gap fast.
🇨🇳 China HD Trucking (Coastal)$17–18/MMBtuDiesel ($25.7/MMBtu)25–30%12–18 moImport terminal pricing. Narrower spread. More BEV-vulnerable. Price-sensitive: China spot imports vanish >$10/MMBtu at terminal.
🌍 Mining/Remote Power$15–20/MMBtuDiesel genset ($25–35)30–50%ImmediateBOOM model: $0 upfront. Aggreko, NFE, EDL Energy. Amazon (Barcarena), Pilbara (EDL), African mines. 6-10 MW loads. Deploy in 3 months.
🇧🇷 Brazil Virtual Pipeline$15–18/MMBtuLPG ($20–25) / No access20–35%3–5 yrLNG trucked to city → satellite regas → local pipe. Competitive vs LPG for industry. 90% of municipalities off-grid = massive TAM. GNLink, Compass, Eneva.
🏝️ Island Power$18–25/MMBtuDiesel ($30–40)25–40%2–3 yrPacific Islands, Indonesia (17K+), Greek islands, Canaries. FSRU or ISO container delivery. Diesel = $20+/MMBtu equivalent in remote locations.

SS-LNG economics in one sentence: Small-scale LNG is economically competitive wherever it displaces higher-cost fuels (diesel, LPG, VLSFO) or solves an access problem (off-grid municipalities, islands, data centers waiting for grid connection). The widest margins are in: (1) China NW trucking (50%+ savings vs diesel, but BEV eroding), (2) mining/remote power (30-50% savings, BOOM model = $0 upfront), and (3) US data centers (grid bypass, 30-50% savings). The narrowest margins are in EU bunkering (TTF-linked, compressed spread) and China coastal trucking (import terminal pricing, thin margins). The structural insight: SS-LNG economics are strongest at the extremes — remote locations where diesel is very expensive, and US where feedstock (Henry Hub) is very cheap. The middle ground (EU industrial, China coastal) is where SS-LNG faces the most competition from grid gas, renewables, and BEV alternatives.

Sources: Stabilis IR · Aggreko · Cummins · Timera Energy

Off-Grid PowerIsland EnergyCoal-to-Gas

LNG Truck Fueling Stations 1,200+CNG + LNG combined across NA 500+Quadrupled since 2018; 2,000+ by 2030 est. 5,000+China alone: 116K LNG trucks registered in 2020 0 LNG / 1,900+ CNGZero LNG truck stations. 1,900+ GNV (CNG) for taxis/light vehicles. No LNG trucking ecosystem
LNG Bunkering Fleet 10 vessels86,400 m³ total capacity 25 vessels190,757 m³ · Global leader in capacity 17 vessels179,700 m³ · China, S. Korea, Singapore 0 vesselsNo LNG bunkering at any Brazilian port. Not on major deep-sea bunkering routes
SS-LNG Liquefaction 170+ facilities PHMSA: 170+ LNG facilities total. ~80 peak-shaving plants (50% in Northeast, 25% Midwest). ~1.3B gal/yr peak-shaving capacity. Also: SS-LNG production (Stabilis 130K gpd in TX/LA, Eagle LNG in FL, EDGE 290 tpd mobile fleet), vehicle fueling, and industrial supply plants. 15 new plants under construction (2024). PHMSA updating rules first time since 2004. ~10 plants + bio-LNG Norway is the only EU country with sizable LNG liquefaction: Hammerfest LNG (4.65 MTPA, Equinor), Risavika, Kollsnes (~5 MTPA combined). A few micro-liquefaction: Wärtsilä modular plant (Norway, Feb 2024), Barents Naturgass Arctic network. Focus is on terminal SS-loading (Elengy ~70 slots/yr). Growing bio-LNG liquefaction: Gasum building 5 bioLNG plants in Sweden (Götene, Borlänge, Kalmar, Sjöbo, Hörbö — 120 GWh/yr each) + Wärtsilä 25 tpd units. Gasum has 50+ bio-LNG filling stations in Nordics. Nordsol liquefaction in NL. 300+ plants (China) China dominates with hundreds of SS/mid-scale liquefaction plants, especially in NW provinces (Xinjiang, Inner Mongolia, Sichuan) fed by pipeline gas, CBM, and wellhead gas. Linde StarLNG references include Jimunai (1,200 tpd), Beiniuchuan (813 tpd), plus Huineng, Tongkai, Huagang, Xingjie plants. Chinese manufacturers (Chuankong, CIMC ENRIC) supply 50K–2M Nm³/day modular units globally. GAIL commissioned India's first SS-LNG unit in 2024. SE Asia emerging (Singapore bunkering hub, Indonesian FSRU regas). 5 plants + 9 FSRUs Domestic liquefaction: Eneva Azulão (490 tpd, AM), Eneva Parnaíba (MA), GNLink ×3 (PR + BA + RN, 264K m³/d combined, R$450M), Alpha LNG (AL). 9 FSRUs operational (144.4 MMm³/d) — most with truck-loading potential. TRSP Santos (Compass) building SS-LNG hub. Import terminal truck-loading = fastest-growing SS-LNG supply source. GNLink + GNL Brasil (Eneva/Virtu) + Edge = new entrants driving market formation in 2025.

Supply Model Production + Virtual PipelineDomestic liquefaction → truck delivery → end-user. Strong BOO model (Linde, Aggreko). Import Terminal LoadingLarge terminals offer SS-LNG reload slots (Elengy ~70/yr). Truck + bunkering vessel distribution. Distributed LiquefactionHundreds of small plants near demand. Eneva: Reservoir-to-Wire. Compass: FSRU → SS-LNG hub → gas interiorization.

Thermal dispatch tied to drought cycles. ANP/Subida da Serra regulatory disputes. World's most expensive gas historically. Petrobras dominance + slow liberalization. 54% of NG reinjected

Strategic Group Map: SS-LNG Industry Players
PORTER

Mapping industry players by Value Chain Integration (x-axis: pure equipment/distribution → fully integrated reservoir-to-wire) and Geographic Scope (y-axis: single market/niche → global multi-region). Bubble size reflects relative market significance in SS-LNG. Based on Porter's Strategic Group framework: axes represent key mobility barriers (integration requires capital + technical capability; geographic scope requires regulatory access + logistics). Axes are independent — global OEMs have low integration while regional champions can be highly integrated in one market.

Sources: Company filings · Mordor Intelligence · IEEFA · EIA · Industry analysis. Framework: M. Porter, Competitive Strategy (1980), Ch. 7 — Strategic Group Analysis
Strategic Group Analysis: Five Analytical Steps Applied to SS-LNG
FRAMEWORK
StepApplication to SS-LNGStrategic Insight
1. Identify Mobility BarriersGlobal Majors: Massive capital base + existing LNG infrastructure + offtake contracts + shipping fleet = near-impossible to replicate. State NOCs: Regulatory monopoly + pipeline ownership. OEMs: Cryogenic IP + decades of engineering know-how (Linde CWHE, Chart IPSMR). Regional Champions: Long-term PPAs + site-specific permits + local relationships (Eneva's Azulão-Jaguatirica, Stabilis DOE export auth).The highest barriers protect Global Majors and State NOCs. Lowest barriers are in Pure Distribution — GásLocal-type businesses can be replicated (and are being disrupted by GNLink, Compass, Edge).
2. Identify Marginal GroupsEagle LNG: FERC extension to Sep 2029 but Jacksonville expansion stalled — exit candidate or acquisition target. GásLocal (White Martins): Pioneer distributor but losing share to GNLink, Compass, NFE. Pivotal LNG (Southern Co.): Small + non-core to parent → divestiture risk. Some China independents: COG-to-LNG plants face margin squeeze if gas prices rise.Marginal players will be acquired (Eagle LNG → larger player?) or exit (GásLocal losing relevance). Expect M&A consolidation in the distribution segment.
3. Chart Strategic MovementConverging → Global Majors (Shell, Total, bp) are moving DOWN toward regional SS-LNG distribution (Shell bio-LNG stations, bp Porto do Açu truck-loading). Regional Champions (Compass, NFE, Eneva) are moving RIGHT toward more integration. OEMs (Chart) are moving RIGHT via acquisitions (Howden, L.A. Turbine) toward integrated solutions. GNLink is moving RIGHT (from distributor → integrated liquefier-distributor).Groups are converging — collision risk in the middle of the map. The "integrated regional SS-LNG producer-distributor" space will be the most contested by 2027–28. Brazil, India, SE Asia = battleground geographies.
4. Analyze TrendsBEV trucks (China → EU): Closes off the LNG trucking group long-term. FuelEU Maritime: Elevates bio-LNG / e-LNG, raises barriers for non-compliant players. Data center power demand: Opens a new segment for behind-the-meter gas suppliers (Stabilis, NFE). Gas market liberalization (Brazil): Reduces barriers for new entrants (GNLink, Compass), threatens Petrobras incumbency.By 2030: LNG trucking group shrinks (BEV displacement). Marine bunkering group grows (IMO Net-Zero Framework). Data center power = new strategic group forming. Brazil SS-LNG distribution = fastest-growing competitive arena.
5. Predict ReactionsIf LNG prices spike: All trucking players react symmetrically (sales drop — China H2 2024 proved this). If EU tightens FuelEU: Bunkering players react by pivoting to bio-LNG/e-LNG. If Brazil accelerates liberalization: Petrobras accelerates divestiture, Compass/GNLink/NFE accelerate investment. If green H₂ reaches $2/kg: Grey ammonia/methanol players exit, LNG loses long-term industrial heat market.The map becomes a tool for forecasting competitive responses: firms in the same group tend to react symmetrically to shocks. Watch the China LNG-diesel price ratio and EU FuelEU compliance as leading indicators.
Framework: M. Porter, Competitive Strategy (1980), Ch. 7: Structural Analysis Within Industries — Strategic Groups
⚙️
Liquefaction Technology & Equipment
Design, manufacture, and supply of core hardware — cryogenic systems, dual-fuel engines, modular liquefaction units, fuel gas supply systems, and LNG process technology.
CI
Chart IndustriesNYSE: GTLS · Ball Ground, GA, USA · Founded 1992
Equipment Leader
FY2025 Sales
$4.26B
FY2025 Orders
$5.68B
Backlog
$5.89B
EBITDA Margin
~24%

Global leader in cryogenic equipment for LNG, hydrogen, and carbon capture. Provides complete solutions across liquefaction, distribution, storage, and end-use of LNG including fueling stations and bunkering systems.

  • FY2025 orders up 13.4% to $5.68B; backlog up 21.5% to $5.89B; ~44% converts to revenue within 12 months
  • Being acquired by Baker Hughes in $13.6B deal, expected to close mid-2026
  • Partnered with Gasum to build the EU's largest biogas fueling station at Kallhall, Sweden
  • Strategic investor in Stabilis Solutions (up to 9% equity, $7M invested in 2019)
  • Products: DynaFlow LNG dispensers, vacuum-insulated cryogenic trailers, modular liquefaction, FSRU systems
  • New Alabama facility (2024) making the world's largest shop-built cryogenic tanks
⚙️ Liquefaction Equipment 🔧 Storage & Cryogenics ⛽ Fueling Stations 🔁 Regasification
Sources: gasworld · Gas Compression Mag. · Chart Industries
Wä
WärtsiläNasdaq Helsinki: WRT1V · Helsinki, Finland
Engines & Gas Systems
Key Product
DF Engines
NextDF Methane
<1.1%
Engine Range
20–50 bore
LNGPac Systems
Global

Global leader in marine dual-fuel engines, cargo handling systems, and LNG fuel gas supply systems (LNGPac). Critical enabler of the LNG-fueled vessel fleet powering thousands of ships worldwide.

  • NextDF technology: methane slip below 2% (as low as 1.1%) — industry-leading; retrofit-capable
  • Supplied engines for Ibaizabal's 18,600 m³ LNG bunkering vessel (3× Wärtsilä 25DF, delivery 2026)
  • Won Vitol's 12,500 cbm bunkering vessel cargo handling & fuel gas supply contract (Dec 2024)
  • LNGPac™ fuel gas supply system: complete storage, bunkering & process equipment for any vessel type
  • Commissioned modular LNG liquefaction plant in Norway (Feb 2024) using nitrogen refrigeration
⚙️ Dual-Fuel Engines 🔧 Cargo Handling ⛽ Fuel Gas Supply 🏭 Modular Liquefaction

HW
Honeywell InternationalNASDAQ: HON · Charlotte, NC, USA
LNG Process Tech
LNG Acquisition
$1.81B
Target
Air Products
Closed
Sep 2024
Scope
End-to-end

Entered SS-LNG leadership via $1.81B acquisition of Air Products' LNG process technology and equipment business. Now offers end-to-end solutions from gas treatment to liquefaction to storage.

  • $1.81B acquisition of Air Products' LNG process technology & equipment completed Sep 2024
  • Integrates with existing UOP process technology, automation, and controls portfolio
  • Positions Honeywell for both large-scale and small/mid-scale LNG project opportunities
  • Complements existing hydrogen, carbon capture, and sustainable fuels portfolio
⚙️ Liquefaction Process Tech 🔧 Gas Treatment 🏭 Automation & Controls
Sources: MarketsandMarkets · Persistence Market Research
GT
Galileo TechnologiesPrivate · Buenos Aires, Argentina
Modular Micro-LNG
Key Product
Cryobox™
Unit Capacity
10–20 tpd
US Subsidiary
EDGE LNG
EDGE Units (US)
15 trailers

Pioneer in modular, containerized micro-LNG liquefaction. Cryobox™ units fit in a 40-foot container and can be paralleled. Also produces CNG compressors, virtual pipeline, and hydrogen systems.

  • Cryobox™: 40-ft container-sized unit producing 10–20 tonnes/day (~0.005–0.01 MTPA each)
  • EDGE LNG (Texas): 15 Cryobox Trailer units, 290 tpd total LNG production capacity
  • Technology selected for Indonesia's first modular micro-LNG plant (Pasuruan, Java, 2025–26)
  • Selected by Matrix Service for US peak shaving facility (June 2025)
  • Focus: flare gas monetization, virtual pipelines, island energy, wellhead liquefaction
⚙️ Modular Liquefaction 🚛 Virtual Pipeline Tech 🔥 Flare Gas Capture ♻️ H₂ & RNG Systems
Sources: Galileo Technologies · N. America Outlook · LNG Prime

SS-LNG Production & Last-Mile Distribution
Operators of small-scale liquefaction plants and virtual pipeline networks. These companies produce LNG and deliver it via trucks, ISO containers, and rail to end-users not connected to the gas grid.
SL
Stabilis SolutionsNASDAQ: SLNG · Houston, TX, USA · Founded 2013
SS-LNG Pure Play
FY2025 Revenue
$68.2M
Adj. EBITDA
$8.0M
Current Capacity
130K gpd

Leading North American SS-LNG production and distribution company. Provides clean fueling, production, storage, and last-mile delivery to marine, aerospace, power generation, and industrial end-markets.

  • Operates George West, TX (100K gpd) & Port Allen, LA (30K gpd) liquefaction plants
  • Galveston LNG project: 350K gpd new facility, $350–400M capex, FID targeted Q1 2026
  • $200M take-or-pay LNG contract for data center power generation starting 2027
  • 10-year bunkering deals with Carnival Corp at Port of Galveston; 56% capacity committed
  • Marine/aerospace/power rose from 56% to ~70% of revenue mix in 2025

SS-LNG developer focused on SE US and Caribbean. Controls full value chain including last-mile delivery. Backed by Energy & Minerals Group (~$13B AUM).

World's largest industrial gas company. Provides small on-site gas solutions and cryogenic process tech. Build-Own-Operate model minimizes customer capex.

Finnish state-owned Nordic gas expert. Pioneer in bio-LNG maritime bunkering and FuelEU Maritime compliance pooling. Operates across Finland, Norway, Sweden, and Germany.

  • Fleet (Coralius + Kairos + 2 carriers) runs entirely on bio-LNG for FuelEU compliance generation
  • Coralius: 1,000th bunkering op (Aug 2025); 270,000+ mt delivered since 2017

  • Acquired Linde's Nordic LNG + Nauticor bunkering; 12 new fueling stations in 2024

World's largest LNG trader, integrated across the entire SS-LNG value chain. Operates bunkering networks, truck fueling stations, bio-LNG production, and small-scale LNG distribution across Europe.

  • LNG truck fueling station network across Germany & Europe; targeting 40 stations by 2026
  • 2 inland bunkering vessels in ZARA region (incl. Energy Stockholm — Europe's largest at 8,000 m³)
  • Bio-LNG: Nordsol plant (NL) operational; Germany's largest bio-LNG plant near Cologne
  • Multi-year offtake agreements for cruise and container shipping price stability

Norwegian SS-LNG pioneer focused on Arctic shipping corridors. Partners with municipalities to convert ferry networks and supply off-grid Arctic communities.

  • Shortening logistics chains to Arctic shipping corridors
  • Municipal ferry conversions guarantee baseline demand for terminals

Regasification & Terminal Infrastructure
Operators of FSRUs, onshore regasification terminals, and satellite stations that convert LNG back to gas for injection into local grids or direct industrial use.

Global FSRU and regasification leader. Pioneered the FSRU concept and deploys rapidly to meet emergency or growing energy needs. Critical role in Europe's post-Russia LNG infrastructure buildout.

  • Excelsior FSRU at Wilhelmshaven 2 (online Aug 2025); 1.9 bcm → 4.6 bcm by 2026
  • Fleet of 10 FSRUs operating or available globally across multiple continents
  • Provides floating regasification and downstream distribution solutions
  • Also active in LNG trading and supply chain optimization

Strategic Timeline — Key Milestones
2024–2030
DEC 2024
Plaquemines LNG ships first cargo — 8th US export terminal. (EIA)
JAN 2025
FuelEU Maritime enters force (2% GHG cut). US LNG permitting pause lifted. EU ETS: 70% surrender. (EU Commission / IEA)
FEB 2025
Gasum & Sirius Shipping JV for bio-LNG bunker vessel "Celsius" (delivery 2027). (MarketsandMarkets)
SEP 2025
Honeywell completes $1.81B acquisition of Air Products LNG business. (MarketsandMarkets)
Q1 2026
Stabilis Galveston LNG — targeted FID. $350–400M capex. (Stabilis IR)
JAN 2026
EU ETS for shipping: 100% emission allowance surrender begins. (ACT Group)
JAN 2027
EU ban on Russian LNG imports takes effect (long-term contracts). (EU Commission)
Q4 2027
Stabilis Galveston deliveries begin. Carnival & marine anchor customers. (Stabilis IR)
2028
IMO mid-term GHG measures take effect. US LNG export capacity ~600 Mm³/d. (IMO / EIA)
SEP 2029
Eagle LNG Jacksonville extended FERC deadline expires. (Jax Daily Record)
2030
FuelEU: 6% GHG reduction. ~345 bcm/yr new global LNG capacity online (2025–2030). EU gas demand forecast -15% from 2025 peak. (IEA / IEEFA)
Sources linked per milestone above. Compiled from IEA · IEEFA · Stabilis Solutions · EU Commission

US SS-LNG Key Player Landscape
PLAYERS
⚡
Stabilis Solutions
NASDAQ: SLNG
George West TX (100K gpd) + Port Allen LA (30K gpd). Galveston LNG: 350K gpd, FID Q1 2026, $350-400M. $200M DC take-or-pay (2027-29). Carnival bunkering ended. Aerospace: SpaceX supplier. 2026 = "transition year". Top customers: Carnival, Aggreko, SpaceX.
🦅
Eagle LNG Partners
Private (EMG)
Maxville FL (50K gpd) + JAX Dames Point (120K gpd). TOTE Maritime + Carnival bunkering. JAX Export ($542M, 3.7 MMm³/d) stalled — site reportedly for sale. Antigua LNG supply. First foreign-flag bunkering in US (2020). SE US marine hub.
♻️
Clean Energy Fuels
NASDAQ: CLNE
~550 CNG+LNG stations. 47 owned LNG. Boron CA (270K gpd) = largest RNG-to-LNG hub. TotalEnergies JV (Friona TX dairy RNG). 237M GGE RNG delivered. Pilot/Flying J partnership. D3 RIN + LCFS credit stacking. Dominant US NG fueling network.
🏗️
Pivotal LNG
Southern Co. Gas
Southern Company Gas subsidiary. Multiple small plants across SE US (~90K gpd total). Trussville AL, Augusta GA. Industrial + peak shaving. Reliable utility-backed supply. Conservative, steady operator.
📦
EDGE LNG / Galileo
Private (Argentina)
15 Cryobox Trailer 600/750 units. Mobile micro-liquefaction. Cat gas-driven. Flare gas capture + wellhead liquefaction. Permian Basin focus. Virtual pipeline for off-pipeline industrial. Modular = fast deploy.
🔧
Chart Industries
NYSE: GTLS
OEM = cryogenic equipment for entire SS-LNG chain. Liquefaction, storage, transport, regasification. IPSMR® process. $7M Stabilis strategic investment. Acquired Howden + L.A. Turbine. THE equipment backbone of US SS-LNG.

US Small-Scale & Commercial LNG Liquefaction Plants
SS-LNG

Excludes large-scale export terminals (Sabine Pass, Calcasieu Pass, Freeport, Cameron, Corpus Christi, Elba Island, Plaquemines, Golden Pass). Includes commercial SS-LNG producers and representative peak-shaving facilities. EIA tracks ~50+ peak-shaving plants with on-site liquefaction via Form 176.

#FacilityLocationOperatorCapacityType & Notes
COMMERCIAL SS-LNG PRODUCERS
1George WestGeorge West, TXStabilis Solutions100K gal/d
(232 Km³/d)		Eagle Ford Shale. Stabilis flagship. Feeds SpaceX, mining, oilfield, ISO exports to MX. DOE 28-yr export authorization (1.47 bcm/yr).
2Port AllenPort Allen, LAStabilis Solutions30K gal/d
(2.5 MMcf/d)		Acquired Jun 2021. Mississippi River access. Baton Rouge industrial corridor supply.
3Galveston ExpansionGalveston, TXStabilis Solutions480K gal/d
(~40 MMcf/d)		Under construction — largest US SS-LNG plant. Commissioning H2 2026. 4.27 Mm³/d nameplate. Galveston Bay waterfront access for marine loading. Will serve data centers, marine bunkering, MX exports. Single largest SS-LNG supply event in 2026.
4JAX LNG (Dames Point)Jacksonville, FLEagle LNG Partners~120K gal/d
(~10 MMcf/d)		First US small-scale waterside LNG production. Feeds TOTE Maritime (2 LNG-powered container ships PR route), Carnival cruise bunkering at Port Canaveral. Marine bunkering hub for SE US.
5MaxvilleMaxville, FLEagle LNG Partners~50K gal/dInland trucking distribution. Feeds Florida industrial + Canaveral cruise supply chain.
6Pivotal LNG (various)Multiple, SE USPivotal LNG (Southern Co.)~90K gal/d totalSouthern Company Gas subsidiary. Multiple small plants across SE US. Industrial + peak shaving. Trussville (AL), Augusta (GA). Reliable utility-backed supply.
7Boron LNG HubBoron, CAClean Energy Fuels270K gal/dLargest RNG/LNG trucking hub. Dairy/landfill RNG → liquefied. Feeds CA I-5 + I-10 LNG trucking corridors. D3 RIN + LCFS credit stacking. TotalEnergies JV.
8FrionaFriona, TXClean Energy / TotalEnergies JV~50K gal/dDairy farm RNG → bio-LNG. Texas Panhandle. Feeds SW trucking corridors.
REPRESENTATIVE PEAK-SHAVING PLANTS (50+ facilities, EIA Form 176)
9HopkintonHopkinton, MAEversource~42 MMm³ storageNew England's largest peak-shaving plant. NE winter demand critical. Stores LNG from Everett LNG import terminal + pipeline liquefaction.
10ActonActon, MANational Grid~14 MMm³ storagePeak shaving. Boston metro area winter gas supply.
11WaterburyWaterbury, CTEversource (Yankee Gas)~11 MMm³ storageConnecticut peak demand. Winter reliability.
12Hays Pipeline (Transco)Carlstadt, NJWilliams / Transco~28 MMm³ storageNYC metro area supply. Pipeline-connected peak shaving.
13Cove PointLusby, MDDominion EnergyDual-usePrimarily export terminal (23.2 MMm³/d) but also serves as peak-shaving + truck-loading for NE/Mid-Atlantic.
14WaukeshaWaukesha, WIWEC Energy~8.5 MMm³ storageMidwest peak shaving. Wisconsin winter reliability.
15Manlove FieldChampaign, ILIllinois Power (Ameren)~14 MMm³ storageMidwest gas storage + peak shaving. Underground aquifer storage.

Total US SS-LNG landscape: ~8 commercial producers + 50+ peak-shaving plants (EIA). Commercial capacity: ~1.2M gal/d (~2.8 MMm³/d). Peak-shaving storage: ~161 MMm³ nameplate, ~55–60% utilization (seasonal). NE + Midwest = 75% of peak-shaving capacity. Galveston expansion (2026) will nearly double commercial SS-LNG production capacity. Also: truck-loading at 4–5 large export terminals (Sabine, Calcasieu, Cameron, Corpus Christi) provides growing SS-LNG supply at near-zero marginal cost.

Sources: EIA LNG Explained · RBN Energy SS-LNG · Stabilis 10-K · Clean Energy 10-K · Eagle LNG · Pivotal LNG · ADI Analytics
US LNG & CNG Fueling Stations
STATIONS
MetricCount (Q4 2025)TrendKey Details
LNG Stations81↓ from 140 peak (2016)Concentrated along CA I-5/I-10 corridors, TX, FL, Gulf Coast. Clean Energy Fuels = dominant operator (~40 stations). Pilot/Flying J partnerships. Most serve HD long-haul trucking. LNG peaked at 140 stations (2016), declined as CNG infrastructure expanded + some fleets shifted to CNG or diesel. Slight recovery since 2023 driven by Cummins X15N engine adoption + RNG credit stacking.
CNG Stations1,385↑ recovering700+ public + ~685 private. Decreased from ~1,600 peak (2016) due to closures + high operating costs. Now recovering — TTP Q4 2025 report shows expansion. RNG (renewable natural gas) = 86% of all NG used for transport fuel in 2024 (LCFS data). Clean Energy, Trillium (Love's), Blu LNG, Total, Shell.
Total NG Stations1,466ExpandingCombined LNG + CNG. Close to 1,400 CNG + 81 LNG = 1,466 total. The Transport Project: "close to 1,400 natural gas stations set to service clean transport industry in 2026." TTP has ~200 member companies (fleets, OEMs, dealers, suppliers).
Top States (LNG)LNG StationsTop States (CNG)CNG Stations
California~20California~180
Texas~12Texas~90
Florida~6Oklahoma~75
Louisiana~5New York~55
Arizona~4Ohio~45
Utah~4Utah~40
Nevada~3Georgia~35
Other (25+ states)~27Other (40+ states)~865

Key operators: Clean Energy Fuels (largest, ~550 stations total CNG+LNG, 47 owned LNG), Trillium (Love's Travel Stops subsidiary), Blu LNG (Blu-LNG brand at Pilot/Flying J), TruStar Energy, Stabilis (mobile LNG), GAIN Clean Fuel. RNG dominance: 86% of all natural gas used for US transportation in 2024 was renewable natural gas (LCFS data) — bio-CNG and bio-LNG from dairy, landfill, and wastewater. D3 RIN + LCFS credit stacking makes RNG economics work even when fossil gas-diesel spread is thin. US vs Europe: US has 81 LNG stations vs Europe's 800+ — reflecting that US trucking adopted CNG (not LNG) as primary NG fuel, while Europe adopted LNG for HD trucking. US LNG stations are mostly on long-haul corridors; EU LNG stations blanket the entire motorway network.

Sources: DOE AFDC · Clean Trucking (Dec 2025) · TTP Q4 2025 · NREL · Clean Energy 10-K
LNG Facilities (US)
170+
Regulated by PHMSA/FERC/USCG. Export, peak-shaving, satellite, vehicular fuel, marine bunkering, data centers. PHMSA 49 CFR Part 193.
Source: FERC
Export Terminals
8 + 8 + 10
8 operating + 8 under construction + 10 approved (not built) + 5 proposed. ~15 Bcf/d capacity → doubling by 2029.
Source: ICLG / FERC (Dec 2025)
PHMSA Rule Update
ANPRM
May 2025. First update since 2004. 50+ questions. Small-scale + mobile + temporary LNG now in scope. PIPES Act 2016/2020 mandate.
Source: Akin Gump (May 2025)
Trump Admin Posture
Pro-LNG
EO 14154 "Unleashing American Energy". LNG pause lifted (Jan 2025). DOE granting Final Export Authorizations. FERC expediting. NEPA/CEQ rescinded.
Source: Venable LLP (Sep 2025)
US LNG Multi-Agency Regulatory Framework
AGENCIES
🏗️
PHMSA (DOT)
49 CFR Part 193. 170+ facilities. Siting, design, construction, fire protection, security. ANPRM May 2025: first update since 2004. Now covers small-scale, mobile, temporary LNG (bunkering, trucking, data centers). 50+ questions. NFPA 59A (2023) alignment.
⚡
FERC
NGA Section 3: siting + construction of onshore LNG import/export. Section 7: certificates for interstate. NEPA lead agency. Order 871 waiver (Jun 2025): permits construction during rehearing. NEPA regs revised (Jul 2025) after CEQ rescission.
🏛️
DOE / FECM
Export authorization. FTA countries = automatic approval. Non-FTA = public interest determination. LNG pause (Jan 2024 Biden) → lifted Jan 2025 (Trump). Granting Final Export Authorizations aggressively. 5 scenarios studied (2024).
⚓
US Coast Guard
Marine safety + waterway suitability. LNG vessel regulation. Marine transfer area oversight. Waterway Suitability Assessments for ship traffic. Coordinates with FERC + PHMSA on terminal safety.
🚢
Jones Act
US-flagged, US-built, US-crewed vessels for domestic LNG transport. Creates cost barrier for coastal distribution. Stabilis building Jones Act-compliant bunkering vessel (Galveston). GLBP exploring compliance.
🏭
EPA + State
EPA: methane emissions rule (2024). Phase 3 GHG for heavy trucks. IRA methane fee ($900→$1,500/ton). States: TX Railroad Commission (intrastate). CARB (CA) ZEV mandates. NY/NE gas infra moratoriums.
Sources: PHMSA · FERC · CRS (Feb 2025) · Venable
Key 2024–2026 Regulatory Developments
TIMELINE
PHMSA Modernization (May 2025)
ANPRM for Part 193 amendments. First update in 21 years. Now explicitly covers small-scale, mobile, and temporary LNG facilities including marine bunkering, trucking fueling, data center power, off-grid generation. 50+ questions on risk-based framework, cost-benefit, interagency coordination. Aligns with NFPA 59A (2023). PIPES Act 2016/2020 mandate. Trump EOs (14154, 14192) push for deregulatory approach.
DOE Export Liberalization (Jan 2025+)
Biden LNG permit pause (Jan 2024) lifted by Trump (Jan 2025, Day 1). DOE granting Final Export Authorizations: Jefferson County TX, Cameron Parish LA. Additional time extensions granted. ~15 Bcf/d operating, 17 Bcf/d under construction, 19 Bcf/d approved. US = 22% of global LNG supply. Pro-export posture drives investment certainty.
FERC Acceleration (Jun–Jul 2025)
Order 871 waiver (Jun 2025): permits construction during rehearing — eliminates multi-year delays. NEPA implementing regulations revised (Jul 2025) after CEQ rescission. FERC approving Cameron Parish LNG (May 2025) + multiple construction authorizations. Blanket certificate authority expanded. Fastest FERC approval pace in decades.
IRA / OBBBA Clean Fuel & Emissions Framework
IRA (2022): Methane fee ($900/ton 2024 → $1,500 by 2026). 45Q CCS credit ($85/ton). 45V clean hydrogen PTC. 45Z clean fuel credit (up to $1.00/gal, extended to 2029 by OBBBA). OBBBA (Jul 2025): Dairy manure RNG = negative CI allowed. ILUC removed. These incentives shape the economics of both LNG (methane reduction) and bio-LNG/RNG (credit stacking). CARB LCFS + D3 RIN + 45Z = the credit stacking architecture that makes US RNG the world's highest-value biogas.
Sources: Akin Gump (PHMSA) · Venable (DOE/FERC) · CRS · Crux Climate (45Z)
US LNG Regulatory Outlook: What to Watch
OUTLOOK
AreaDirectionImpactKey Detail
🏗️ PHMSA Part 193ModernizingHighFirst update since 2004. Small-scale/mobile LNG now in scope. Risk-based framework. Aligning with NFPA 59A (2023). Critical for SS-LNG: clearer rules = lower permitting cost + faster deployment.
⚡ FERC ApprovalsAcceleratingHighOrder 871 waiver. NEPA streamlined. Multiple terminal approvals. Fastest pace in decades. Investment certainty restored after 2024 pause.
🏛️ DOE Export AuthPro-ExportHighPause lifted. FEAs granted. 19 Bcf/d approved not built. Political pendulum risk: next admin could re-pause. But construction can't easily be stopped once started.
🚢 Jones ActUnchangedMedNo reform expected. Still requires US-built/crewed vessels for domestic LNG. Structural barrier for coastal SS-LNG distribution. Workarounds: ATBs + foreign-flag exemptions.
🌿 IRA Methane FeeUncertainMed$900→$1,500/ton by 2026. OBBBA didn't repeal it. Increases cost of methane emissions in LNG supply chain. Favors low-methane-intensity operators.
🏭 State DivergenceWideningMedTX/LA/Gulf = pro-LNG. CA/NY/NE = anti-gas. CARB ZEV mandates. NY gas moratorium. Two Americas: Gulf Coast LNG boom vs Northeast gas phase-out. SS-LNG follows the friendly states.

US LNG regulatory story in one sentence: The Trump administration (2025+) has created the most pro-LNG regulatory environment in US history — DOE lifting export pauses, FERC expediting approvals, PHMSA modernizing 21-year-old rules to explicitly cover small-scale and mobile LNG. For SS-LNG specifically, the PHMSA ANPRM is the most consequential development: for the first time, federal regulations will address marine bunkering, trucking fueling stations, data center LNG, and off-grid power — the exact use cases driving SS-LNG growth. The risk is political pendulum (next admin could reverse) and state-level divergence (CA/NY hostile while TX/LA booming). The IRA/OBBBA clean fuel framework (45Z, methane fee, D3 RIN, LCFS) creates a parallel incentive structure for RNG/bio-LNG that coexists with fossil LNG deregulation.

Sources: PHMSA · Venable · Akin Gump · CRS · ICLG
US LNG Exports (2025)
14.6 Bcf/d
Record. +24% YoY. >100 Mt annual. World's #1 exporter (~25% of global trade). ~20 Mt ahead of Qatar. Dec 2025: 11.5 Mt (monthly record).
Source: EIA (Feb 2026)
Destination: Europe (2025)
68%
Jan–Nov 2025. Up from 53% (2024). Replacing Russian pipeline gas. Dec 2025: ~9 Mt to Europe. Turkey surged to 1.45 Mt (Dec).
Source: EIA
Capacity Trajectory
→ 2× by 2031
Peak capacity ~17 Bcf/d (2025). 18.1 Bcf/d forecast 2027. 30+ Bcf/d by next decade. 6 projects FID'd in 2025. ~9 Bcf/d began construction.
Source: NGI (Jan 2026)
10-Year Milestone
0.5→15
Bcf/d. First cargo: Feb 24, 2016 (Sabine Pass → Brazil). Sabine Pass: 3,300+ cargoes (39% of all US). A decade: zero to global #1.
Source: SPE / EIA
US LNG Export Terminal Landscape (2025–2031)
TERMINALS
TerminalOperatorStatusCapacityKey Detail
Sabine PassCheniereOperating4.2 Bcf/dLouisiana. 6 trains. 3,300+ cargoes (39% of all US). First cargo Feb 2016. World's first shale-era LNG export.
Corpus ChristiCheniereOperating + Expanding2.8 Bcf/dTexas. Trains 1-3 operating. Stage 3 (+900 MMcf/d) first cargo Mar 2025. 7 midscale trains online by end 2026. Most flexible US platform.
PlaqueminesVenture GlobalRamping3.6 Bcf/dLouisiana. First cargo Dec 2024. 16.4 Mt in first full year. 17% of US exports by Aug 2025. 27.2 mtpa across 2 phases. Modular trains = fast ramp.
Golden PassQatarEnergy / ExxonMobilUnder Construction2.4 Bcf/dTexas. 70% QatarEnergy + 30% Exxon. 18 mtpa. Train 1 → early 2026. Repurposed import terminal. Zachry bankruptcy → Chiyoda takeover.
Cameron LNGSempraOperating2.0 Bcf/dLouisiana. 3 trains. Sempra-led. Phase 2 expansion under consideration.
Freeport LNGFreeport LNGOperating2.1 Bcf/dTexas. 3 trains. Recovered from Jun 2022 explosion + 8-month outage. Full operations restored.
Elba IslandSouthern LNG / Kinder MorganOperating0.35 Bcf/dGeorgia. 10 small-scale MAPS modular units. Smallest US export terminal.
Cove PointBerkshire HathawayOperating0.75 Bcf/dMaryland. Only East Coast export terminal. Single train. Dominion → Berkshire.

Pipeline 2026+: ~2.4 Bcf/d coming online in 2026 (Plaquemines Phase 2 + Golden Pass Train 1). 6 new projects FID'd in 2025. Energy Transfer suspended Lake Charles LNG (2.2 Bcf/d, Dec 2025). 3 projects targeting FID in early 2026: Delfin FLNG, Commonwealth LNG, Texas LNG. US export capacity doubling to ~30+ Bcf/d by 2031. All Gulf Coast except Cove Point (MD) and Elba Island (GA).

Sources: EIA (Feb 2026) · EIA (Apr 2025) · OG Leads · ETF Trends
Trade Flows, Contract Model & Market Dynamics
TRADE
Destination Shifts
Pre-Ukraine (2017–21): Asia 46%. Post-Ukraine (2022): Europe surged to 69%. 2025: Europe 68% (Jan–Nov). Dec 2025: Europe ~9 Mt, Turkey 1.45 Mt (surged), Asia 1.23 Mt (dipped). Europe replacing Russian pipeline gas remains the dominant pull. Asia share compressed as EU takes priority. Brazil = 72% US-sourced LNG (2024).
US Contract Advantage
FOB, destination-flexible, HH-indexed. Unlike Qatar/Australia (oil-indexed, destination-rigid). 95% of 2025 SPAs are 20-year. Tolling: HH × 115–121% + $2–3/MMBtu fixed fee. Buyers can redirect cargoes to highest-price market in real time. This flexibility = US LNG's structural competitive advantage over all other suppliers.
Spread Compression (Q4 2025)
TTF-HH and JKM-HH spreads compressed to $4–6/MMBtu in Q4 2025 (from $8–12 in H1). HH briefly >$5 (Dec 5). Spot margins below fixed liquefaction fee ($2–3/MMBtu) in Dec. Ample supply + warmer weather. Warning signal: as US capacity doubles, sustained spread compression could delay FIDs for unsanctioned projects.
Sources: EIA · ETF Trends (Jan 2026) · Inspectioneering · OilPrice
US LNG International Trade: Outlook & Risk Matrix
OUTLOOK
FactorDirectionImpactKey Detail
📈 Export VolumeRecord GrowthHigh14.6 Bcf/d (2025). 18.1 Bcf/d (2027E). 30+ Bcf/d by 2031. Plaquemines + CC Stage 3 + Golden Pass driving near-term. US = world's swing LNG supplier.
🇪🇺 Europe DemandStrongHigh68% of US volumes (2025). Replacing Russian gas. EU storage mandates. FuelEU Maritime driving LNG demand. Europe = anchor market for US LNG for at least a decade.
🌏 Asia CompetitionUncertainMedAsia share compressed (1.23 Mt Dec 2025). Qatar expanding (North Field). Australia Scarborough. China tariff risk. Asia is the battleground where US, Qatar, Australia compete on price + flexibility.
💰 Spread CompressionWarningMedTTF-HH fell to $4–6 (Q4 2025). Spot margins below $2–3 tolling fee in Dec. If sustained, could delay unsanctioned FIDs. But 20-year contracts protect existing projects.
🏗️ New FIDsActiveHigh6 FID'd in 2025. 3 targeting early 2026 (Delfin, Commonwealth, Texas LNG). ET suspended Lake Charles. Pro-LNG admin enables rapid permitting but economics must work.
⛽ Feedgas SupplyAbundantMedHaynesville + Permian. 300K+ miles of pipelines. But: new pipeline construction needed for 30+ Bcf/d. Pipeline bottlenecks = the real supply risk, not gas availability.
🛢️ Associated Gas / WAHADecouplingMedIn the Permian, oil wells produce gas as a byproduct — producers cannot shut gas without shutting oil. WAHA hub regularly trades at negative prices. Higher oil → more associated gas → downward pressure on US pipeline prices even while global LNG spikes. 15–20% of US dry gas flows to LNG facilities, but this does NOT equalize US vs global prices.
🧊 Boston LNG ImportsExceptionNicheThe US generally only exports LNG — Boston's Everett terminal is the main exception, due to insufficient pipeline connectivity to the broader US gas network. Supplies ~20% of New England winter gas demand. A reminder that pipeline constraints create pockets of LNG import dependency even in the world's #1 exporter.

US LNG international trade story in one sentence: In just one decade (2016–2025), the US went from zero LNG exports to 14.6 Bcf/d — the world's #1 exporter, ~25% of global trade, with a FOB/destination-flexible/HH-indexed contract model that no competitor can match. Europe takes 68% of US LNG as it replaces Russian gas. Capacity is doubling to 30+ Bcf/d by 2031 (Plaquemines alone shipped 16.4 Mt in year one). The risk: spread compression in Q4 2025 ($4–6/MMBtu TTF-HH) signals that the coming supply wave could pressure economics for unsanctioned projects. But the structural advantage remains: cheap gas ($3–4 HH), flexible contracts, massive infrastructure, and a pro-LNG administration. The US is not just the world's largest LNG exporter — it is the global swing supplier that sets the floor for LNG prices worldwide.

Sources: EIA (Feb 2026) · OilPrice · ETF Trends · NGI
US Data Center Power (2024)
~25 GW
→ 80+ GW by 2030. 10+ GW added in 2025 alone (= NYC peak demand). 3-4% of US power → 9-12% by 2030. NG = primary new source.
Source: Woodway Energy / IEA
New NG Capacity Planned
17.5 GW
Highest since 2017. Gas turbine orders exceed OEM manufacturing capacity to 2031. Behind-the-meter gas = key for hyperscalers.
Source: Woodway / Utility Dive
ERCOT Load Growth (2025–27)
+10%
Texas. NG generation +105 BkWh in high-demand scenario. PJM +3%. Data centers + AI driving first sustained US load growth in 20 years.
Source: EIA (Mar 2026)
NG Production Needed for AI
+10 Bcf/d
Additional gas by 2030 for data centers alone (55 GW low-end). = 10-15% increase in US production. Comparable to entire LNG export buildout since 2017.
Source: Hamm Institute (Nov 2025)
US SS-LNG Main Demand Drivers: Ranked by Growth Potential
DEMAND
🖥️
Data Centers / AI
▲▲▲ EXPLOSIVE
~25 GW → 80+ GW by 2030. Meta Hyperion (LA): 2 GW gas plant ($3.2B). Behind-the-meter NG = hyperscaler preference. Gas turbine backlog to 2031. 10+ GW added in 2025 = NYC peak demand. THE #1 driver of US NG demand growth this decade.
🚢
Marine Bunkering
▲▲ GROWING
10 bunkering vessels (86,400 m³). Stabilis 10-yr Galveston contract (22M gal/yr). GLBP 2027. Harvey Gulf (2 vessels). LNG $150–250/mt cheaper than VLSFO. FuelEU Maritime driving newbuild demand for LNG-fuelled ships calling US ports.
🚛
Virtual Pipeline
▲▲ GROWING
Truck-delivered LNG to off-pipeline areas. Mining, oil & gas field services, remote manufacturing. Aggreko BOOM model (3–7 yr, $0 upfront). 50% fuel savings vs diesel. Eagle LNG: Caribbean islands. Stabilis: industrial + aerospace.
🚚
Heavy-Duty Trucking
▲▲ RENAISSANCE
Cummins X15N (500 HP, 2024). Clean Energy 600+ stations. UPS, J.B. Hunt, WM adopting. RNG = $1.50–2.00/DGE cheaper than diesel. X15N demo trucks driving repeat orders. Long-haul corridors = LNG's niche.
❄️
Peak Shaving
→ STABLE
~80 peak-shaving facilities (50% NE, 25% Midwest). 1.3B gal/yr capacity. Ensures winter gas supply when pipeline demand peaks. Record NG consumption Jan 2025 (polar vortex). Foundation of US SS-LNG since 1970s.
🚀
Commercial Aerospace
▲ EMERGING
Stabilis: aerospace grew from 14% → 50% of revenue (Q4 2023→Q4 2024). LNG as rocket propellant coolant + site power. Clean Energy: bulk LNG to Astrobotic, Stoke Space. SpaceX, Blue Origin = potential customers.
Sources: EIA · Hamm Institute · Woodway · Stabilis IR
Data Centers & AI: The Structural Demand Shock for US Natural Gas
AI / POWER
Scale of Demand
Current DC sector: ~15 GW. Pipeline under construction could double total capacity. 10+ GW added in 2025 alone. DC power demand could reach 325–580 TWh/yr by 2028. DC vacancy rate: record low 1.6%. 75% of assets under construction are pre-leased. Rental rates up 60% since 2021. This is not speculative — it's happening.
Why Natural Gas Wins
Combined-cycle NG plants: 80%+ capacity factor (vs solar 10-25%). 24/7 reliable baseload. Behind-the-meter NG = hyperscaler preference (avoids grid interconnection queue). Meta Hyperion: 2 GW gas plant ($3.2B, Louisiana). Gas turbine orders exceed manufacturing capacity to 2031. Utilities planning 17.5 GW new NG capacity — highest since 2017.
SS-LNG Opportunity
PHMSA ANPRM (May 2025) explicitly includes data center power as a new SS-LNG use case. Off-grid/behind-the-meter DCs in areas without pipeline = virtual pipeline LNG delivery. Stabilis $200M data center contract. LNG-to-power for remote DCs mirrors the industrial off-grid model (Aggreko BOOM). Data centers are the new industrial SS-LNG customer.

The connection to SS-LNG: Most data center power will come from grid-connected large-scale NG plants. But a growing segment of hyperscale DCs are being built in locations without adequate grid infrastructure — creating demand for behind-the-meter or off-grid NG generation. This is where SS-LNG (truck-delivered LNG for on-site power) enters the picture. PHMSA's May 2025 ANPRM explicitly recognizes "electric power continuity for data centers" as a novel SS-LNG application. Stabilis Energy's $200M DC contract is the proof point. As DC demand grows from 25 GW to 80+ GW, even a 5% off-grid share = 2.75-4 GW of SS-LNG demand — larger than the entire current US peak-shaving market.

Sources: Hamm Institute (Nov 2025) · EIA (Mar 2026) · Tech Insider · Mitsubishi Power
US SS-LNG Demand Drivers: Outlook & Growth Matrix
OUTLOOK
Demand DriverGrowthSS-LNG RelevanceKey Insight
🖥️ Data Centers / AI▲▲▲High (emerging)25→80+ GW. Behind-the-meter + off-grid = SS-LNG use case. PHMSA recognizes DC power as SS-LNG application. Stabilis $200M contract. THE growth story of the 2020s.
🚢 Marine Bunkering▲▲High10 vessels. GLBP 2027. Stabilis Galveston. FuelEU driving LNG-fuelled ship orders. Jones Act is the constraint. Growth follows vessel orders.
🚛 Virtual Pipeline▲▲CoreTruck-delivered LNG to off-pipeline areas. Mining, remote industrial, Caribbean. 50% diesel savings. Foundational SS-LNG use case. Grows with industrial activity.
🚚 HD Trucking (X15N)▲▲HighX15N 500 HP (2024). Clean Energy 600+ stations. RNG economics. UPS/J.B. Hunt repeat orders. Renaissance driven by X15N. Long-haul niche.
📤 LNG Exports▲▲▲Indirect14.6→30+ Bcf/d by 2031. Drives upstream production + pipeline buildout. Not SS-LNG directly, but creates the feedgas abundance that makes SS-LNG cheap.
❄️ Peak Shaving→ StableCore~80 facilities. 1.3B gal/yr. Winter reliability. Record polar vortex demand. Mature but essential. Foundation of US SS-LNG since 1970s.
🚀 Aerospace▲NicheStabilis: 14%→50% of revenue. Clean Energy: Astrobotic, Stoke Space. Small but high-margin. Growing with commercial space launch frequency.

US SS-LNG demand story in one sentence: Data centers are the structural demand shock that changes everything for US natural gas — and for SS-LNG specifically, the behind-the-meter/off-grid DC power segment is emerging as a major new use case alongside the established virtual pipeline, peak shaving, and marine bunkering markets. The Cummins X15N is rebooting HD trucking demand. LNG exports (14.6→30+ Bcf/d) create the feedgas abundance that keeps Henry Hub at $3-4 and makes all downstream SS-LNG applications economically attractive. The US is the only market where ALL SS-LNG demand drivers are growing simultaneously: power (DCs), marine, trucking, industrial, and aerospace. No other country has this breadth of demand.

Sources: EIA · Hamm Institute · Woodway · Pew Research

→ $33.4B by 2034 (13.2% CAGR). Trucks = largest supply mode. Transportation = largest application. Power gen = fastest-growing segment.

US SS-LNG Market: Bottom-Up Supply & Demand Model
MODEL
Methodology
Bottom-up from segment-level estimates. Units: MMm³/d and bcm/yr (NG equivalent). SS-LNG = domestic LNG excluding large-scale export terminal throughput. A = actual · P = preliminary · E = estimate. Conversion: 1 gal LNG ≈ 2.34 m³ NG. US dry gas production ≈ 3,313 MMm³/d. This model covers ~0.14–0.18% of US production.
Market Sizing Context
US SS-LNG: $9.7B (2024) → $33.4B by 2034 at 13.2% CAGR (Polaris). Global SS-LNG: $19.8B → $39.3B (7.1% CAGR). US = ~49% of global market. Key growth: trans-shipment + bunkering (fastest), power generation (DC-driven), and HD trucking (X15N reboot). IEA: 2025 = record year for US LNG FIDs (>83 bcm/yr sanctioned, >90% of global total).
Key 2025–26 Events
Plaquemines ramp (+3.6 Bcf/d). CC Stage 3 online (Mar 2025). Golden Pass Train 1 (early 2026). PHMSA ANPRM (May 2025) covering SS-LNG. Stabilis Galveston commissioning (H2 2026). QatarEnergy Ras Laffan force majeure (Mar 2026) — tightens global supply. 6 new US LNG FIDs in 2025. US capacity doubling to 30+ Bcf/d by 2031.
Sources: Polaris · Market.us · IEA (Mar 2026) · MarketsandMarkets

BALANCE

% of US Dry Gas0.13%0.15%0.16%0.18%
⚠ Data Centers (Low Confidence)
2026E = first year with material DC volume (~7% of demand). If DC disappoints, 2026E demand drops ~0.4 MMm³/d to ~5.2. Only confirmed: Stabilis $200M take-or-pay. PHMSA ANPRM creates regulatory path but not demand.
⚠ Galveston Ramp (Med Confidence)
4.27 Mm³/d nameplate commissioning H2 2026 at ~30% utilization yr 1 = ~1.3 Mm³/d incremental. Delay to 2027 halves supply growth. Single largest binary event in the model.
✓ Peak Shaving (High Confidence)
Most stable both on supply + demand side. EIA-tracked. Seasonal patterns predictable. 50+ yr history. Foundation of US SS-LNG. Not growing fast but not going away.
📊 Truck Loading = Structural Shift
Near-zero marginal cost LNG from export terminals (Sabine, Cameron, CC Stage 3, Calcasieu). From 2 bays (2023) to 4–5 (2026). This is the supply side's biggest structural driver — it makes SS-LNG abundant + cheap.
Sources: EIA · ADI Analytics · Stabilis 10-K · Clean Energy 10-K · EIA Capacity · IEA
US SS-LNG S&D Model: Key Scenarios & Outlook
OUTLOOK
Scenario2026E Demand2026E SupplyUtilizationWhat Drives It
🟢 Base Case5.59 MMm³/d6.00 MMm³/d93%Galveston on time. DC delivers ~3-5 sites. Marine bunkering grows. X15N restores trucking. Peak shaving stable. Comfortable surplus.
🔵 Bull Case6.16 MMm³/d6.30 MMm³/d98%DC accelerates (+0.3 MMm³/d). Marine faster than expected. X15N ramp strong. MX border demand surges. Galveston ramps to 40% yr 1. Tight market. Pricing power returns.
🟡 Galveston Delay5.59 MMm³/d5.37 MMm³/d104%Galveston slips to 2027. Supply unchanged except -0.63 MMm³/d. Demand unchanged. Deficit. Peak shaving plants maxed. Truck loading compensates partially.
🔴 Bear Case5.18 MMm³/d5.37 MMm³/d96%DC disappoints (-0.4). HH >$5 compresses LNG-diesel spread (-0.1 trucking). Galveston delays. Marine slower. Near-balanced. No crisis but no growth story.

US SS-LNG S&D model in one sentence: The US SS-LNG market is transitioning from a peak-shaving-dominated utility business (52% of demand in 2024) to a diversified multi-segment market where marine bunkering, data centers, HD trucking, and cruise drive incremental growth. Supply is loosening as truck loading at export terminals provides near-zero marginal cost LNG, and Stabilis Galveston (the single largest supply event) adds 4.27 Mm³/d nameplate in H2 2026. The key binary risk is Galveston timing: on time = comfortable 93% utilization; delayed = deficit at 104%. Data centers are the wildcard — potentially transformative but only Stabilis $200M contract is confirmed. The structural story is clear: the US is the only SS-LNG market in the world with simultaneous growth across all demand segments and abundant, cheap supply from the world's largest LNG export infrastructure.

Sources: Dashboard bottom-up model · Polaris · IEA (Mar 2026) · EIA

European Market

Europe has rapidly expanded LNG infrastructure post-2022 to replace Russian gas, but is now seeing a buildout slowdown as gas demand is forecast to decline 15% by 2030.

+70 bcm

Norway 50% (pipeline), US = #1 LNG. LNG = 38% of imports. Target: -25% by 2030.

PLAYERS

Molgas / Titan

Gasum
State of Finland
Nordic LNG/bio-LNG leader. Coralius (1,000+ ops). Celsius JV with Sirius (2027). 50+ Nordic stations. FuelEU pooling pioneer (Elenger/Tallink). €1.46B revenue. Daily FuelEU compliance unit pricing.
🇳🇴
Avenir LNG
Stolt-Nielsen (>95%)
Acquired by Stolt-Nielsen (>95%). 5th 20K m³ vessel under construction (delivery 2026). 6th vessel (2027). 7-yr charter with European energy trader. Growing fleet for global SS-LNG distribution.
🐚
Shell (Europe)
NYSE: SHEL
Energy Stockholm (8K m³, largest EU inland bunker vessel, Nov 2024). 37+ DE stations. Nordsol bio-LNG JV (NL). Cologne bio-LNG plant under construction. Algeciras bunkering operator. ~45% global volumes (w/TotalEnergies, ENGIE).
🇫🇷
Elengy / ENGIE
GRTgaz
3 LNG terminals (Fos Cavaou, Fos Tonkin, Montoir). ~70 SS loading slots/yr. Bio-LNG truck loading + rail containers. Montoir = Europe's STS transshipment leader. French infrastructure backbone.
🇮🇹
Snam
MTA: SRG
Italian gas infrastructure giant. Acquiring Higas (ex-Avenir, Sardinia). Oristano FSRU conversion. Ravenna FSRU (BW Singapore, operational Jun 2025). Italy's first ship-to-ship bio-LNG at Genoa (Axpo/GNV).

2025 consolidation theme: Europe's SS-LNG sector matured significantly through M&A — Molgas swallowed Titan, Stolt-Nielsen took control of Avenir, and bio-LNG went mainstream with ferry operators on multi-year contracts. UECC reached 71% bio-LNG share (vs 31% prior year). HAM Group committed to 100% bio-LNG/bio-NG at all stations by 2026. Algeciras surged to 3rd place in European bunkering (9 barges on Iberian Peninsula). The sector is no longer a collection of startups — it's an integrated, consolidated industry with real scale.

Sources: Riviera (Dec 2025) · Splash247 · SEA-LNG · IEEFA EU Tracker
