European Small-Scale Natural Gas Liquefaction Plants
GIE MAP
#CountryLocationCapacity (t/yr)Notes
1🇫🇮 FIPorvoo (Kilpilahti)20,000Gasum. Near Neste refinery. Feeds Finnish LNG trucking + bunkering.
2🇩🇪 DEGablingen6,000Bayerngas / Erdgas Schwaben. Small-scale peak-shaving.
3🇩🇪 DENievenheim (Peak Shaver)16,000Open Grid Europe (OGE). Pipeline gas liquefied for peak-shaving.
4🇩🇪 DEStuttgart (Peak Shaver)40,000terranets bw. Peak-shaving facility for SW Germany grid stability.
5🇩🇪 DEGodorf (Cologne) **100,000RheinEnergie. With LNG loading jetty. ** Carbon-neutral via biomethane certificates. Largest SS-LNG plant in Germany.
6🇮🇹 ITManerbio (BS)700Micro-scale. Northern Italy industrial satellite.
7🇮🇹 ITCampania ***50,000Southern Italy. Off-pipeline industrial supply.
8🇮🇹 ITSicily ***—Island supply. Capacity not disclosed. *** Not on GIE map.
9🇮🇹 ITVeneto ***50,000NE Italy industrial district. *** Not on GIE map.
10🇳🇱 NLMaasvlakte (Peak Shaver)90,000Gate terminal / Gasunie. Rotterdam complex. Feeds SS-LNG truck-loading + bunkering supply chain.
11🇳🇴 NOKollsnes I *40,000Gasnor / Equinor. * With LNG loading jetty. Near Bergen.
12🇳🇴 NOKollsnes II *80,000Gasnor / Equinor. * With LNG loading jetty. Expansion of Kollsnes I.
13🇳🇴 NORisavika (Sola) *300,000Largest in Europe. NSMP. * With LNG loading jetty. Near Stavanger. Liquefies pipeline gas + biogas from Kårstø. Feeds Nordic SS-LNG + bunkering.
14🇳🇴 NOSnurrevarden (Karmøy)20,000Gasnor. Western Norway coastal supply.
15🇳🇴 NOTjeldbergodden12,000Equinor. Central Norway. Methanol production complex.
16🇵🇱 PLKurpiński (coal mine)—Coal-mine methane liquefaction. Coal-bed methane recovery → LNG.
17🇵🇱 PLOdolanów—PGNIG. Helium separation plant byproduct LNG.
18🇷🇺 RUPetrodvorets—Gazprom. Near St. Petersburg. Peak-shaving / city gas.
19🇷🇺 RUKaliningrad150,000Gazprom. Exclave supply — no pipeline connection to Russia mainland.
20🇷🇺 RUKaliningrad (2nd)20,000Gazprom. Secondary facility.
21🇷🇺 RUKingisepp7,000Gazprom. Leningrad Oblast.
22🇷🇺 RUNikolskoye—Gazprom. Capacity not disclosed.
23🇷🇺 RUPskov23,000Gazprom. NW Russia gas supply.
24🇷🇺 RUVysotsk *660,000Largest in Europe (incl. Russia). Novatek Cryogaz. * With LNG loading jetty. Baltic coast. 2 trains, operational Sep 2022. Exports to Finland, Baltics.
25🇸🇮 SIJesenice2,500Adriaplin. Small-scale. Alpine region industrial supply.

* With LNG loading jetty · ** Carbon-neutral via biomethane certificates · *** Not indicated on GIE map. Total European SS-LNG liquefaction capacity (excl. Russia): ~575K t/yr. Norway = dominant (452K t/yr, 79% of EU+EEA). Including Russia: ~1.44 Mt/yr (Vysotsk alone = 660K t/yr). Bio-LNG plants are tracked separately — 104 in EU (EBA 2024).

Source: GIE Small Scale LNG Map (2024 edition) · GIE/NGVA Europe · Novatek · Gasnor · Gasum
European LNG / LNG-CNG Filling Stations by Country
STATIONS
#CountryLNG Stations (2025)Growth TrendKey Operators & Notes
1🇩🇪 Germany195+68 since 2022#1 in Europe. Shell (37+ stations), Alternoil (53 stations), Liqvis (Uniper). Bio-LNG growing: Shell Rheinland building largest German bio-LNG plant. REEFUELERY (Erdgas Südwest + avanca) bio-LNG in Burghaun.
2🇮🇹 Italy173+50 since 2022#2 in Europe. Snam, ENI, Q8. Italy = most mature SS-LNG trucking market in Southern Europe. 68 LNG stations in operation in Gmobility/GIE earlier mapping. Strong growth trajectory.
3🇪🇸 Spain105+18 since 2022Molgas Energy Group (801st EU station at Alar del Rey). HAM Group, Enagás. Iberian corridor well-served. Borriana (Castellón) CNG-LNG recent opening.
4🇫🇷 France91+26 since 2022TotalEnergies, Proviridis, Molgas. Strasbourg latest CNG/LNG. Growing along major autoroutes (A1, A6, A7). AFIR regulation accelerating deployment.
5🇸🇪 Sweden40+12 since 2022Gasum (50+ Nordic stations total). Scandinavian Biogas / St1 Biokraft: Monsteras bio-LNG facility. Chart Industries: largest bio-LNG refueling station in continental Europe (Kallhall). Strong bio-LNG focus.
6🇳🇱 Netherlands36+5 since 2022Titan LNG, PitPoint (now TotalEnergies), Nordsol (Amsterdam bio-LNG plant — Shell + Renewi JV). Gate terminal truck-loading as supply hub.
7🇵🇱 Poland27+3 since 2022PGNIG/Orlen. Świnoujście LNG terminal truck-loading. Growing along E30/E40 corridors. Emerging market.
8🇧🇪 Belgium26StableG&V Energy, DATS 24 (Colruyt). Zeebrugge/Fluxys as supply hub. Bio-LNG from organic waste emerging.
9🇫🇮 Finland23+9 since 2022Gasum. Strong growth from low base. Gasum opened 12 new Nordic stations in 2024. Hamina LNG import terminal as supply source. Bio-LNG share growing.
10🇬🇧 UK15+1 since 2022CNG Fuels (Avonmouth, Leyland). Gasrec. Shell. Small market — UK trucking favoring BEV/HVO over LNG. RenEco + Nordsol planning first UK bio-LNG plant (2024). Gibraltar SS-LNG terminal (Shell/PX Group).
11🇵🇹 Portugal14+4 since 2022Sines LNG terminal truck-loading. Dourogás, Galp. Iberian network extension from Spain.
12🇦🇹 Austria3+1 since 2022Small market. Transit corridor stations on Brenner route.
13🇭🇺 Hungary2NewEmerging. MOL Group. Central European corridor.
14🇳🇴 Norway2DecliningGasnor/Equinor. Several stations in Stavanger/Randaberg closed (2024). Norway shifting to BEV + bio-LNG marine, away from LNG road transport.
15🇪🇪 Estonia2NewBaltic corridor. Tartu station (2024). Paldiski LNG terminal under construction.
16🇨🇿 Czechia1NewCentral European transit corridor. Pilot phase.
17🇸🇮 Slovenia2StableAdriaplin. Alpine transit corridor (IT↔AT↔HU).
18🇨🇭 Switzerland2StableLidl Switzerland fleet. Alpine transit.

Total European LNG stations: ~800+ (mid-2025). Growth from ~280 (2019) → 500 (Feb 2022) → 600 (late 2022) → 700 (2023) → 800+ (Jul 2025). Germany + Italy = ~46% of total. Bio-LNG share increasing at many stations. EU AFIR (Alternative Fuels Infrastructure Regulation) mandates LNG refueling every 400 km along TEN-T core network by 2025. Despite BEV truck competition, LNG station network continues expanding — driven by existing LNG truck fleet (~10K+ vehicles) and bio-LNG compliance demand.

Sources: GIE Small Scale LNG Map · LNG Prime (Jul 2025) · Eurogas/Gmobility Stations Map · CNG Europe
Russian Gas Ban
Law
EU/261/2026 adopted 26 Jan 2026. Full permanent ban. LNG short-term: Apr 2026. LNG long-term: Jan 2027. Pipeline: Sep-Nov 2027. Dependency: 45% → 12%.
Source: EU Commission (Feb 2026)
FuelEU Maritime
In Force
Since 1 Jan 2025. -2% GHG intensity (2025), -6% (2030), -80% (2050). Well-to-wake. First reports due 31 Jan 2026. Pooling mechanism drives bio-LNG demand.
Source: EU Commission
EU ETS Maritime (2026)
100% + CH₄
Full compliance from Jan 2026. 100% of CO₂ allowances. CH₄ + N₂O included for first time. Methane slip now has financial cost for LNG ships. €38.8B revenue (2024).
Source: SAFETY4SEA (Jan 2026)
Biomethane Gap
4.9 vs 35
bcm. 4.9 bcm produced (2023) vs 35 bcm target (2030). No binding EU-wide target. RED III + ETS2 + FuelEU = indirect support. Dutch mandate = only strong signal.
Source: Rabobank
EU Regulatory Framework: The "Fit for 55" + REPowerEU Architecture
FRAMEWORK
🚢
FuelEU Maritime
In force 1 Jan 2025. GHG intensity: -2% (2025), -6% (2030), -14% (2035), -80% (2050). Well-to-wake (CO₂ + CH₄ + N₂O). Pooling mechanism: enables bio-LNG compliance. OPS at berth from 2030/2035. First FuelEU reports due 31 Jan 2026.
💰
EU ETS (Maritime)
Phase-in: 40% (2024) → 70% (2025) → 100% (2026). CH₄ + N₂O added from 2026 = methane slip now has cost. €38.8B revenue (2024). Carbon ~€60-80/t. Review Jul 2026: may extend to ships <5,000 GT. 89.8 Mt verified CO₂ (2024).
🇷🇺
Russian Gas Ban
EU/261/2026 (adopted 26 Jan 2026). Permanent ban. Short-term LNG: Apr 2026. Long-term LNG: Jan 2027. Pipeline: Nov 2027. Dependency: 45% → 12%. ~35 bcm still imported = €10B/yr → zero. Prior authorization for non-Russian imports.
⛽
AFIR
Alternative Fuels Infrastructure Regulation. LNG refueling every 400 km on TEN-T core by 2025. LNG bunkering at TEN-T core maritime ports. Drives station buildout from 800+ → 2,000+ by 2030. Electric charging points for trucks alongside.
🌱
RED III + Biomethane
RED III: transport renewable targets. REPowerEU: 35 bcm biomethane by 2030 (4.9 bcm in 2023). No binding EU-wide target. Dutch mandate = strongest signal. FuelEU drives bio-LNG maritime demand. Clean Industrial Deal overlooks biomethane.
🏭
EU Methane Regulation
First EU-wide methane rules (energy sector, 2024). Mandatory LDAR. Import standards for methane intensity of gas/LNG from 2025. Impacts both domestic production and LNG imports. Favors low-methane-intensity suppliers (US, Norway).
Sources: EU Commission (FuelEU) · SAFETY4SEA · REPowerEU · Gasum
EU LNG Regulatory Outlook: What to Watch
OUTLOOK
AreaDirectionImpact on LNGKey Detail
🚢 FuelEU MaritimeSupportiveHigh (+)-2% GHG (2025) drives LNG as lowest-cost compliance fuel. Pooling mechanism = bio-LNG demand creator. First compliance cycle underway. THE regulation that makes LNG bunkering a growth market.
💰 EU ETS + CH₄ (2026)MixedMed (±)100% CO₂ compliance + CH₄/N₂O from 2026. Methane slip default factors penalize older LNG engines. Newer engines (low-pressure) benefit. Good for LNG vs oil, but methane slip = new cost risk.
🇷🇺 Russian Gas BanStructuralHigh (+)Full permanent ban (law since Jan 2026). LNG short-term → Apr 2026, long-term → Jan 2027. Forces massive LNG import substitution. US + Qatar + Norway = winners.
⛽ AFIR StationsSupportiveHigh (+)LNG every 400 km on TEN-T core. 800+ stations already. Target 2,000+ by 2030. Mandated infrastructure buildout. But: for a truck market that may be shrinking (BEV competition).
🌱 Biomethane TargetUnderperformingMed (+)4.9 vs 35 bcm target. No binding EU mandate. RED III + ETS2 = indirect drivers. Dutch mandate = model. Bio-LNG for maritime is growing but 35 bcm by 2030 looks impossible.
📉 Gas Demand DeclineHeadwindHigh (−)IEEFA: EU gas demand -15% by 2030. LNG imports -20% by 2030. Renewables + heat pumps replacing gas heating. Structural decline in EU gas demand = the macro headwind for ALL LNG segments.

EU LNG regulatory story in one sentence: The EU has built the world's most complex regulatory architecture for LNG — FuelEU Maritime (driving bio-LNG bunkering demand), EU ETS with methane from 2026 (putting a price on methane slip for the first time), a permanent Russian gas ban (forcing import substitution), and AFIR (mandating station infrastructure). The net effect is paradoxical: these regulations simultaneously boost LNG as a maritime transition fuel (via FuelEU/AFIR) while accelerating the long-term decline of gas (via renewable mandates + gas phase-down). For SS-LNG specifically, the regulations create a strong but time-limited opportunity: marine bunkering + bio-LNG will grow through ~2035, but EU gas demand will be structurally lower. The LNG industry's strategic window in Europe is 2025–2035 — after that, hydrogen, ammonia, and full electrification take over.

Sources: EU Council (Dec 2025) · Normec Verifavia · Rabobank · IEEFA
EU LNG Imports (H1 2025)
+21% YoY
Replacing Russian pipeline gas (ended Jan 1, 2025). US = 58% of EU LNG (Q2 2025). Russia still 14%. Qatar 8%. France, NL, Spain = top importers.
Source: IEEFA EU LNG Tracker
EU Gas Suppliers (H1 2025)
NO 29% · US 27%
Norway 29% (pipeline backbone), US 27% (LNG surge), Russia 13% (declining), Algeria 12%. Pipeline = 52% of total (down from 77% in 2021).
Source: IEEFA Gas Flows
Regas Overcapacity
406 vs 133
bcm. Regas capacity → 406 bcm by 2030. LNG demand forecast: ~133 bcm. → 272 bcm UNUSED. Terminal utilization: ~58%. Massive stranded asset risk.
Source: IEEFA
EU LNG Spend (2022–H1 2025)
€258B
€117.4B from US, €37.5B from Russia. €381B on pipeline gas. EU-US Prestwick deal (Jul 2025): $750B US energy commitment to 2028.
Source: IEEFA / EU Commission
EU Gas Supply Architecture: The Post-Russia Landscape
SUPPLIERS
🇳🇴
Norway (Pipeline)
120–124 bcm/yr. ~30% of EU supply. Backbone of EU energy security. Stable, long-term, pipeline-delivered. Equinor + partners. Hammerfest LNG adds flexibility. THE anchor supplier Europe depends on.
🇺🇸
US LNG
55–60 bcm/yr. 58% of EU LNG (Q2 2025). 27% of total EU gas. Flexible FOB/HH-indexed. 68% of US LNG goes to Europe (2025). Prestwick deal: $750B commitment. EU's #1 LNG supplier — and growing.
🇶🇦
Qatar LNG
15–20 bcm/yr. 8% of EU LNG. Long-term contracts (27-yr with Eni, Shell, TotalEnergies). North Field expansion after 2027. BUT: threatened to halt EU deliveries over methane regulation "fine" (Oct 2025).
🇷🇺
Russia (Declining)
Still 13% of EU imports (H1 2025). 14% of LNG (Yamal). Pipeline via Ukraine stopped Jan 1, 2025. TurkStream still flowing. Full ban law adopted Jan 2026. LNG → Jan 2027. Pipeline → Nov 2027.
🇩🇿
Algeria + N. Africa
~12% of EU imports. Pipeline (Transmed to Italy, GME to Spain) + LNG (Skikda, Arzew). Stable but constrained by domestic demand. Egypt now net importer. Libya marginal.
🇦🇿
Azerbaijan (TAP)
~8% of EU imports via TAP/TANAP. Capacity expansion to 31 bcm planned by 2026. Southern Gas Corridor. Feeds Italy, Greece, Bulgaria. Growing strategic importance as Russian alternative.
Sources: IEEFA Gas Flows · Modern Diplomacy · EU Commission Q2 2025
EU Gas Trade: Outlook & Risk Matrix
OUTLOOK
FactorDirectionImpactKey Detail
📉 EU Gas DemandDecliningStructural-20% (2021–24). +4% in H1 2025 (cold weather, low renewables) but temporary. IEEFA: -15% more by 2030. REPowerEU: replace 100 bcm by 2030. The macro story: EU gas demand is structurally declining.
🏗️ Regas OvercapacityGrowingStranded Risk406 bcm capacity by 2030 vs ~133 bcm LNG demand. Utilization avg 58%. Spain: 5 of 7 terminals <35%. France Le Havre idle. Massive overcapacity. €41B spent on LNG infrastructure that may never be fully used.
🇺🇸 US LNG DependenceRisingGeopoliticalUS = 58% of EU LNG. Prestwick deal $750B. EU traded Russian dependence for US dependence. Trump used LNG as leverage in EU trade negotiations (Jul 2025).
🇷🇺 Russian Gas ExitOn TrackHighBan law adopted. 45% → 13% → 0% by 2027. €10B/yr savings. On track but TurkStream still flows + Yamal LNG via Zeebrugge transshipment to be stopped.
💰 TTF PriceStabilizingMed~35 €/MWh (Q2 2025). Down from 2022 highs. Asian premium thin (€0.4–2.3/MWh). Hormuz crisis spiked to $17–18/MMBtu (Mar 2026). Prices stable but vulnerable to geopolitical shocks.
🇶🇦 Qatar TensionsNew RiskMedQatar threatened to halt EU LNG over methane regulation "fine" (Oct 2025). North Field expansion = key for post-2027 supply. EU methane regulation creating friction with its 3rd largest LNG supplier.

EU gas trade story in one sentence: Europe has successfully diversified away from Russian gas (45% → 13%) but at massive cost: €258B on LNG imports since 2022, a regasification buildout heading toward 272 bcm of overcapacity by 2030, and a new dependency on US LNG (58% of EU LNG, $750B Prestwick commitment). The paradox: EU gas demand is structurally declining (-20% since 2021, -15% more by 2030 per IEEFA) while LNG infrastructure keeps expanding. Norway remains the backbone (29% of supply) and the only truly stable, pipeline-delivered, politically aligned partner. For SS-LNG, the trade picture matters because it determines the price and availability of bulk LNG that feeds the downstream bunkering, trucking, and industrial markets — and with terminal utilization at 58% and falling, surplus LNG will be abundant and cheap in Europe for years to come.

Sources: IEEFA EU LNG Tracker · IEEFA Gas Flows · EU Commission Q2 2025 · Intereconomics
EU Gas Demand (2025)
+3% YoY
~347 bcm. Strongest increase since 2021. Q1 cold weather drove +9%. Power sector = main driver (weak wind/hydro). But -20% vs 2021. Temporary rebound.
Source: IEA Gas Market Report (Jan 2026)
EU Gas Demand (2026E)
−2%
IEA forecast. Renewables → power sector gas burn −12%. Industry +3% (recovery). Heating +2% (avg weather). Kpler: 320.4 bcm. Structural decline resumes.
Source: IEA
Renewables Share of Power
~47–52%
2024: 47%. Q2 2025: 52%. Solar record highs (+20%). But wind −1%, hydro −12% (2025). Gas still sets electricity price 39.5% of hours. Declining.
Source: EU Commission Q2 2025
EU Marine Bunkering
72% Global
Europe = 72.4% of global LNG bunkering market (2025). 62 vessels + 30 on order. Rotterdam 941K m³ (+52%). FuelEU Maritime = THE growth driver.
Source: SEA-LNG / Mordor Intelligence
EU Gas & SS-LNG Demand Drivers: Growth vs Decline
DEMAND
🚢
Marine Bunkering
▲▲▲ GROWING
72.4% of global market. 62 vessels + 30 on order. Rotterdam +52%. FuelEU Maritime + EU ETS driving demand. Bio-LNG blending growing (UECC 71% bio share). Algeciras surged to #3 in EU. THE #1 EU SS-LNG growth driver.
🌱
Bio-LNG / Biomethane
▲▲ GROWING
4.9 bcm (2023). 1,678 plants. FuelEU pooling creates maritime demand. HAM Group → 100% bio-LNG by 2026. Nordsol, Gasum, Molgas producing. Bio-LNG = premium product driven by regulation.
🔒
Energy Security
→ STRUCTURAL
Post-Russia pivot. 10 FSRUs deployed. Regas capacity doubled. Russian gas ban (law 2026). But overcapacity emerging (406 vs 133 bcm by 2030). Security demand ≠ volume demand. Infrastructure built, utilization declining.
⚡
Power Generation
▼ DECLINING
Gas = ~16% of EU power. Renewables ~47-52%. IEA: power sector gas burn −12% in 2026. Gas still essential as backup for wind/solar intermittency (cold spells, Dunkelflaute). But structural share declining every year.
🏭
Industrial
▼ DECLINING
−3% in 2025 (high prices). Refining + fertilizers hit hardest. NL −10%, FR −7%, ES −5%. Some recovery 2026E (+3%). But demand destruction partially permanent. Energy-intensive industry relocating outside EU.
🏠
Heating
▼ DECLINING (slowly)
Heat pumps = only 8% of residential heating. Sales fell −23% (2024). Gas boiler bans spreading but slowly. Cold spells still drive massive spikes (daily demand +70% in Nov 2025 cold spell). Structural decline but glacial pace.
Sources: IEA (Jan 2026) · EU Commission · IEEFA · Enerdata
EU Gas Demand Outlook: Growth vs Decline Matrix
OUTLOOK
Driver20252026E2030ESS-LNG Relevance
🚢 Marine Bunkering▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲Core — THE growth market. FuelEU + EU ETS driving. Bio-LNG blending adds premium. Europe = 72% of global.
🌱 Bio-LNG▲▲▲▲▲▲Core — Premium product. FuelEU pooling. Dutch mandate. 35 bcm target (unlikely but directional). HAM 100% bio.
🚚 LNG Trucking→▼▼▼Declining — 800+ stations. AFIR mandates. But BEV trucks growing. EU −90% CO₂ HDV by 2040. Bio-LNG keeps niche alive.
⚡ Power Generation→▼▼▼Indirect — Gas backup for renewables. No direct SS-LNG role (grid-connected). But cold spells still spike demand.
🏭 Industrial▼→▼Limited — Some virtual pipeline for off-pipeline industry. But most EU industry is grid-connected. Not a SS-LNG growth driver.
🏠 Heating→→▼None — Pipeline-delivered gas. Not SS-LNG. But heating demand drives overall gas market sizing and TTF pricing.
📊 TOTAL EU GAS+3%−2%−15%Structural decline. 2025 rebound was temporary (cold weather). 2026+ resumes downward path. 335 bcm → ~285 bcm by 2030.

EU gas demand story in one sentence: Europe's total gas demand is structurally declining (−20% since 2021, −2% forecast for 2026, −15% by 2030) — but within this decline, marine bunkering and bio-LNG are the two bright spots growing rapidly, driven by FuelEU Maritime and the EU ETS. The 2025 rebound (+3%) was a weather-driven anomaly (cold Q1, weak wind/hydro), not a structural reversal. Industrial demand is permanently impaired (energy-intensive industry leaving EU), heating is slowly electrifying (heat pumps at 8% but growing), and power sector gas burn is being displaced by renewables (47-52% of generation). For SS-LNG specifically, the European opportunity is concentrated in marine bunkering + bio-LNG — everything else is declining. This makes EU SS-LNG a fundamentally different story from the US, where ALL demand segments are growing simultaneously.

Sources: IEA (Jan 2026) · IEEFA · Bruegel · Thunder Said Energy
EU SS-LNG Demand (2026E)
14.49 MMm³/d
5.29 bcm/yr. +17.6% CAGR (2023–26). Marine bunkering + bio-LNG = 64% of demand (up from 46% in 2023). Trucking declining as share.
Source: Dashboard bottom-up model
EU SS-LNG Supply (2026E)
16.03 MMm³/d
5.85 bcm/yr. +24% CAGR. Supply growing faster than demand → widening surplus. Terminal truck-loading + bio-LNG plants = main growth sources.
Source: Dashboard bottom-up model
Utilization Trend
105%→90%
2023: supply-constrained (105%). 2024: balanced (99%). 2026E: surplus (90%). Shifted from deficit to oversupply in just 3 years.
Source: Dashboard bottom-up model
EU vs US Comparison
2.6× US
EU SS-LNG demand (5.29 bcm) = 2.6× US (2.04 bcm) in 2026E. Maritime bunkering makes EU the larger SS-LNG market. US growing faster in non-maritime segments.
Source: Dashboard models
Europe SS-LNG Market: Bottom-Up Supply & Demand Model
MODEL
Methodology
Bottom-up from segment-level estimates. Scope = EU-27 + UK + Norway + Turkey. SS-LNG = all LNG consumed as LNG (not regasified into pipeline): bunkering, trucking, industrial satellite, bio-LNG, island power, coastal redistribution. A = actual · P = preliminary · E = estimate. EU gas consumption ≈ 335–347 bcm/yr. This model covers ~1.0–1.5% of total.
EU vs US: Different Growth Drivers
US SS-LNG is diversified (data centers, trucking, marine, aerospace, peak shaving — all growing). EU SS-LNG is concentrated in 2 segments: marine bunkering + bio-LNG (64% of 2026E demand). Trucking is declining. No data center or aerospace demand. EU is regulation-driven; US is market-driven. EU is 2.6× larger by volume (maritime dominance) but US is growing faster in non-maritime segments.
Key 2025–26 Events
FuelEU Maritime first compliance year (2025). EU ETS 100% + CH₄ from Jan 2026. Russian gas ban law (Jan 2026). IEA: EU gas demand −2% in 2026. Renewables → power gas burn −12%. Bio-LNG going mainstream (UECC 71%, HAM 100%). Regas overcapacity widening (406 vs 133 bcm by 2030).

Supply-Demand Balance & Scenario Analysis
BALANCE
Metric2023A2024A2025P2026E
Demand (MMm³/d)8.8810.8612.3814.49
Demand (bcm/yr)3.243.974.525.29
Supply (MMm³/d)8.4410.9613.2116.03
Supply (bcm/yr)3.084.004.825.85
Surplus (MMm³/d)-0.44+0.10+0.82+1.53
Utilization105%99%94%90%
% of EU Gas0.9%1.1%1.3%1.5%
✓ Marine Bunkering (High Confidence)
Best-supported: port-level data, vessel orderbook (3–5 yr visibility), FuelEU creates demand floor. Rotterdam +7% Q1 2025. 87 new DF orders H1 2025. Algeciras surged to #3.
⚠ Bio-LNG Supply (Highest Upside)
If FuelEU premium pulls feedstock toward liquefaction over grid injection, could exceed 1.40 bcm in 2026. Constraint = feedstock availability, not plant capacity. UECC 71% bio share proves demand exists.
⚠ HD Trucking (Highest Downside)
Only segment declining. EU HDV CO₂ = -45% by 2030. If BEV adoption accelerates (NL/DE ICE bans), 2026E trucking could drop to 0.70 bcm → total demand falls to ~5.0 bcm.
📊 EU vs US: Structural Difference
Unlike US (all segments growing), EU SS-LNG has 2 growing segments (marine + bio-LNG) and 1 declining (trucking). EU is regulation-driven (FuelEU/ETS/AFIR), US is market-driven (cheap gas/data centers/X15N).
Scenario2026E Demand2026E SupplyUtilizationWhat Drives It
🟢 Base Case14.49 MMm³/d16.03 MMm³/d90%Marine +20%. Bio-LNG +40%. Trucking -5%. Comfortable surplus. FuelEU compliance drives bio-LNG premium.
🔵 Bull Case15.89 MMm³/d16.85 MMm³/d94%Bio-LNG accelerates (+0.5). Bunkering exceeds forecast (+0.6). Trucking stabilizes. FuelEU pooling creates strong demand pull.
🔴 Bear Case12.93 MMm³/d15.07 MMm³/d86%Trucking collapses (-0.6). Bio-LNG feedstock constrained (-0.5). Bunkering slower (-0.5). EU ETS methane penalties hit LNG ships. Oversupply.

EU SS-LNG S&D model in one sentence: Europe's SS-LNG market is 2.6× larger than the US by volume (driven by maritime bunkering dominance) and growing at 17.6% CAGR — but the growth story is narrower, concentrated in just two segments: marine bunkering (FuelEU-driven, highest confidence) and bio-LNG (feedstock-constrained, highest upside). HD trucking is the only declining segment and represents the key downside risk. The market flipped from supply-constrained in 2023 (105% utilization) to surplus by 2026 (90%) as terminal truck-loading and bunkering vessels expanded. The structural distinction from the US: Europe's SS-LNG is regulation-created demand (FuelEU, EU ETS, AFIR, RED III) rather than market-driven, making it more predictable but also more vulnerable to regulatory reversal.

Sources: IEEFA · SEA-LNG Mid-Year 2025 · ACER MMR · IEA Gas Market (Jan 2026) · EBA 2024 · NGVA Europe

Asia-Pacific LNG Market

The world's largest LNG-consuming region — representing 65–67% of global LNG imports. Dominated by Japan, China, South Korea, and India, with rapidly emerging markets in SE Asia. China's domestic SS-LNG ecosystem (300+ liquefaction plants, ~1M LNG trucks) is the largest in the world.

65–67%
Japan 71 MMT + China 66 MMT + S. Korea 47 MMT (2023)

~1M
Expected 2025 · Nearly tripled since 2019 · ~30 bcm/yr consumption
Source: Global LNG Hub

4× from 110.9K mt in 2023 · Target 3 MTPA by 2028

50%+

APAC Bunkering Market
$1.52B
2025 value. China 30% share. 17 bunkering vessels, 179,700 m³ total. Growing at 6.8% CAGR to $2.41B by 2032. STS = 48% of operations.
Source: MarkNtel Advisors
Singapore LNG Bunkered
712K m³
~320K tonnes in 2025. MPA target: 3 Mtpa by 2028 (6× current). Total bunker sales: 56.77 Mt (2025). World's #1 bunkering hub adding LNG licenses in 2026.
Source: MarkNtel / MPA
Shanghai — First >50M TEU
51.5M TEU
First port globally to exceed 50M TEU (2024). LNG bunkered: 712K m³ (2025). +60% YoY. Emerging as a global mega-hub alongside Singapore + Rotterdam.
Source: MarkNtel
APAC SS-LNG Market
>8% CAGR
2025-2030 forecast (Mordor). China dominates (~95% of production). Major players: Linde, Wärtsilä, Shell, Eni, TotalEnergies. Moderately fragmented market.
Source: Mordor Intelligence
APAC SS-LNG & Bunkering: Key Player Landscape
PLAYERS
🇸🇬
Singapore Hub
World's #1 Bunker Port
FueLNG (Shell/Keppel): Bellina 7.5K m³ + Venosa 18K m³. Pavilion Energy (Temasek). TotalEnergies. 56.77 Mt total bunker sales. MPA: 3 Mtpa LNG target by 2028. New licenses + TR56 standard upgrade in 2026. Methanol bunkering from Jan 2026.
🇨🇳
China Ecosystem
30% of APAC Bunkering
300+ domestic plants. 4,500+ stations. 1M+ trucks. Shanghai 51.5M TEU — first port >50M globally. Bunkering: 712K m³ (2025). 5 vessels. CNOOC 20K m³ vessel. 95%+ of APAC SS-LNG production is China.
🇯🇵
Japan / JERA
Legacy Infrastructure
World's first LNG importer (1969). 200+ satellite stations (declining). 304 bcm regas (#1 globally). JERA = world's largest LNG buyer. Central LNG Marine Fuel Japan. MOL + NYK bunkering vessels. Shifting focus to ammonia co-firing.
🇰🇷
South Korea / KOGAS
Builds the Ships
KOGAS: 7 regas terminals (Incheon 55 Mtpa = world's largest). 4 bunkering vessels at Busan/Ulsan. HD HHI, Samsung HI, DSME = 70%+ of global LNG carriers. Korea Green Ship Initiative. ECA around major ports. AWS + SK: AI hyperscale DC powered by LNG.
🇮🇳
India / GAIL / Petronet
Very Early Stage
GAIL: 2 SS-LNG liquefaction skids (pilot). Petronet Dahej: truck-loading expanding. Shell Hazira: operational since 2021. 6 LNG import terminals. India = CNG-dominant (4,500+ stations). May skip LNG trucking → CNG → BEV. Bunkering: Kochi + Hazira stations, early stage.
🌏
SE Asia Emerging
Ground Zero
Indonesia: Pertamina floating mini-regas for island power. 17K+ islands = massive potential but near-zero infra. Philippines: first LNG terminal (Batangas, 2024). Vietnam: terminals under development. SE Asia LNG market >8% CAGR. Decades from meaningful SS-LNG.

APAC SS-LNG story in one sentence: The APAC SS-LNG market is fundamentally a China story (95%+ of production, 4,500+ stations, 1M+ trucks) surrounded by bunkering hubs (Singapore = world's #1, Shanghai = fastest-growing), legacy infrastructure (Japan's 200+ satellites, declining), shipbuilders who make the vessels (Korea = 70% of global LNG carriers), and emerging markets at ground zero (India, Indonesia, Philippines, Vietnam). Outside of China, APAC SS-LNG is almost entirely (1) bunkering and (2) import terminal truck-loading — not domestic liquefaction. The 2025-30 growth comes from: bunkering expansion (IMO compliance, ECA enforcement), Chinese plant + fleet operations (but peaking risk from BEV), and nascent SE Asian markets. The global mega-hub consolidation is underway: Singapore, Rotterdam, and Shanghai are emerging as the 3 dominant LNG bunkering nodes.

Sources: MarkNtel Advisors · Mordor Intelligence · Mordor APAC Bunkering · IEEFA
🇨🇳
China: SS-LNG Liquefaction, Trucks & Distribution
China's Domestic SS-LNG Ecosystem
CHINA
SegmentCompany / DetailKey Data
SS-LNG Liquefaction Plants300+ plants across NW & central provincesConcentrated in Xinjiang, Inner Mongolia, Sichuan, Shanxi. Fed by pipeline gas, CBM, and wellhead gas. Plants range from 50K–2M Nm³/day. Most serve domestic truck fueling and industrial users.
Linde StarLNG (China)Multiple mid-scale referencesJimunai (1,200 tpd, Xinjiang Guanghui), Beiniuchuan (813 tpd). Clients include Huineng, Tongkai, Huagang Gas, Xingjie Natural Gas.
Chinese Equipment OEMsChuankong Cryo, CIMC ENRIC, SinogasChuankong: 30+ LNG plants since 1980s, first 100% skid-mounted 50K Nm³/day plant (Inner Mongolia, 2015). CIMC ENRIC: LNG trailers, ISO containers, SS carriers. Exporting globally.
LNG Truck Fleet~1 million trucks expected by 2025Nearly tripled since 2019. Sales grew ~40% YoY in Q1–3 2024. Over 1/3 of total truck sales in 2024. Consumption: ~30 bcm/yr (≈ France's total gas demand). OEMs: Sinotruk, FAW Jiefang, Weichai engines.
LNG Fueling Stations5,000+ stations across ChinaLargest LNG truck fueling network globally. CNPC, Sinopec, ENN, and private operators. Concentrated along major highways and in coal/gas provinces.
CNOOC Terminal ExpansionTerminal capacity to 30M m³Handled 25+ MMT in 2023. 15 new regas terminals under construction (59 MTPA). China adding more regas capacity than any other country.
LNG Bunkering (China)5 operational bunkering vesselsZhoushan 30K m³ shore tanks (opened early 2025). Shanghai bunkering +58% YoY H1 2025 (173K mt). Shenzhen Yantian: PetroChina ship-to-ship ops.
Pavilion–Hangjiaxin SS-LNGCross-border SS-LNG supplyPavilion Energy (Singapore/Temasek) supplying up to 0.5 MTPA of SS-LNG to Jiaxing terminal in Zhejiang. First cargo Apr 2023.
Sources: Global LNG Hub · Linde StarLNG · Chuankong Cryo · Mordor Intelligence · Seala AI

Country / HubBunkering Vessels2024 VolumeKey Projects & Notes
Singapore3 vessels (FueLNG Bellina 7.5K m³, FueLNG Venosa 18K m³, Brassavola 12K m³)463.9K mt4× YoY growth. MPA targeting 3 MTPA by 2028 (6× 2024). 2nd bunkering terminal commissioned late 2024. 12 simultaneous bunkering ops capacity. 3 licensed suppliers (FueLNG, Pavilion/Shell, TotalEnergies).

JapanTruck-to-ship focusModerateWorld's first LNG importer (1969). 71 MMT imports (2023). 304 bcm regas capacity (global #1). Favors bunkering vessels over shore tanks to limit upfront capital. MOL + NYK ordered bunkering vessel with Qatargas (delivery 2026).

Emerging APAC Markets: India, SE Asia & Pacific Islands
India, Indonesia, Philippines, Vietnam & Pacific Island SS-LNG
EMERGING
CountryLNG StatusSS-LNG ActivityKey Data
India7 active regas terminals · 16 plannedGAIL ordered India's first 2 SS-LNG liquefaction skids (Jun 2022, commissioned 2024 pilot). Petronet LNG operates Dahej (world's 8th largest). Government planning CNG/LNG stations on 6,000 km golden quadrilateral highways.9% of Asia's regas capacity. 3 tcf planned capacity additions by 2026. Coal-to-gas switch for power + industry + city gas distribution (CGD).
IndonesiaMultiple FSRUs · SS-LNG for islandsNusantara Regas FSRU supplies power plants (Jakarta). Exploring SS-LNG tank truck distribution for 17,000+ islands. Pertamina developing small-scale terminals.LNG producer (Bontang, Tangguh) but also emerging importer for Java. LNG pricing reform needed: domestic LNG $9–12/MMBtu vs $6–9 pipeline gas.
PhilippinesStarted LNG imports 2023First LNG import via FSRU at Batangas. 31.1 Mm³/d new regas capacity added 2023. Growing demand for power sector as Malampaya gas field depletes.2023 record year: new importer. Multiple FSRU projects planned. Off-grid island power = key SS-LNG opportunity.
VietnamStarted LNG imports 2023Hai Linh (Thi Vai) terminal opened Q1 2023. ~300K tonnes imported 2024 (up from <100K in 2023). Shell identifies mainland SE Asia as major future demand source.2.8 Mm³/d initial regas capacity. Rapid growth expected as industrial parks + power plants convert from coal.
ThailandMap Ta Phut terminal (PTT)15.9 bcm regas capacity. PTT expanding LNG procurement for power sector and planned LNG bunkering at Laem Chabang port.SE Asia's most mature LNG import market after Singapore. Truck LNG distribution developing.
Pacific IslandsHigh-cost diesel dependencePapua New Guinea (LNG producer, 6.9 MTPA), but most Pacific islands still rely on diesel imports. SS-LNG ISO container model being evaluated for Fiji, Tonga, Samoa.Island power = identical use case to Caribbean SS-LNG. High diesel costs ($20+/MMBtu equivalent) make SS-LNG competitive.
Sources: Mordor Intelligence · EIA · GlobalData · APEC SS-LNG Report · APAC LNG Tank Truck Report
Asia-Pacific SS-LNG Liquefaction Plants by Country
SS-LNG PLANTS

China's domestic SS-LNG network dwarfs all other countries combined. 300+ plants produce 25 Mt/yr (IEEFA, 2024). Capacity increased 41% in 2024 alone. Outside China, SS-LNG liquefaction is nascent in APAC — mostly import terminal truck-loading or small pilot plants.

CountrySS-LNG PlantsTotal CapacityFeedstockKey Details
🇨🇳 China300+~70.76 Mm³/d
(~25 Mt/yr)		PNG (65%), COG (20%), CBM (10%), Other (5%)World's largest SS-LNG production base by far. Concentrated in NW: Xinjiang, Shaanxi, Inner Mongolia, Shanxi, Ningxia = 80%+ of output. Inner Mongolia, Shaanxi, Shanxi = 55.5% of Jan–Apr 2023 production (OilChem). 29 new plants in 2024 (OilChem), adding 29.22 Mm³/d. Capacity doubled in 5 years. Major operators: PipeChina/Kunlun Energy, ENN, China Gas, Guanghui Energy (Xinjiang Jimunai: 1,200 tpd StarLNG by Linde). COG plants: Shanxi, Hebei (coking byproduct → LNG at ¥3,200–3,800/t). CBM plants: Shanxi, Guizhou. Huineng Coal Chemical (Inner Mongolia): coal-to-LNG.
🇯🇵 Japan200+ satellite~4.1 bcm/yr (storage-based)LNG import regasWorld's most mature SS-LNG system. Peak-shaving + satellite terminals since 1970s. Tokyo Gas, Osaka Gas, JERA = major operators. ~200+ satellite receiving stations across Japan. But declining: nuclear restarts reducing LNG need. No new greenfield SS-LNG planned. Satellite model = LNG trucked from large import terminals to small regas stations for local gas supply.
🇸🇬 Singapore0 (import hub)N/ALNG importsNo domestic liquefaction — Singapore is a pure import + bunkering hub. SLNG terminal (Jurong Island): 11 Mtpa regas. FueLNG Bellina (7.5K m³) + FueLNG Venosa = STS bunkering vessels. MPA: LNG bunkered jumped to 463,900 tonnes in 2024 (4× the 110,900 tonnes in 2023). Target: 3 Mt/yr LNG bunkering by 2028. 3 methanol bunkering suppliers licensed from Jan 2026.
🇰🇷 South KoreaImport terminal TL~2.0 bcm/yrLNG importsKOGAS operates 4 large LNG import terminals (Incheon, Pyeongtaek, Tongyeong, Samcheok). Truck-loading bays at all major terminals. 4 LNG bunkering vessels operational at Busan/Ulsan. Green Ship Initiative investing in LNG refueling. HD HHI, Samsung HI, DSME = world's largest DF vessel builders. Korea = builds the ships but has modest domestic SS-LNG consumption.
🇮🇳 India2 pilot skids + TL<0.5 bcm/yrLNG imports + pilotVery early stage. GAIL: 2 SS-LNG liquefaction skids ordered Jun 2022 (pilot). Shell Hazira: truck-loading from LNG import terminal (operational since Jan 2021). Petronet Dahej: expanding truck-loading. 6 major LNG import terminals. India's SS-LNG is almost entirely truck-loading from import terminals, not domestic liquefaction. CGD expansion (300+ geographic areas) is the priority, not SS-LNG trucking.
🇦🇺 Australia3–5 small~0.4 bcm/yrDomestic gas / CSGEDL Energy: 24 remote LNG power stations (Pilbara, Kimberley mining sites). Linde StarLNG: 175 tpd plant at Kwinana (WA). QGC (Shell): coal seam gas → LNG in Queensland. Wesfarmers Kleenheat: LNG distribution in WA. Small but established market for remote mining power. BOC (Linde subsidiary) operates SS-LNG distribution.
🇮🇩 IndonesiaFloating regas<0.2 bcm/yrLNG importsPertamina: floating mini-regas for island power (Bali operational, others piloting). Donggi-Senoro LNG (onshore, Sulawesi): 2 Mtpa but export-scale. SS-LNG potential enormous (17,000+ islands) but infrastructure near-zero. Domestic gas supply declining → increasing LNG imports. Very early pilot stage.
🇵🇭 Philippines1 import terminalNewLNG importsFirst LNG import terminal: Batangas (operational 2024). First Gas Power Corp. + Tokyo Gas. Will support LNG-to-power + potential SS-LNG distribution. Market at ground zero — comparable to where Brazil was in ~2018.

APAC SS-LNG production: China = 95%+ of regional total. Japan has the infrastructure (200+ satellites) but declining volumes. Rest of APAC is mostly import terminal truck-loading, not domestic liquefaction. The APAC SS-LNG story is fundamentally a China story — every other country is a rounding error by comparison.

Sources: IEEFA (May 2025) · Mysteel/OilChem · Linde StarLNG · Mordor APAC SS-LNG · EIA · GAIL
Asia-Pacific LNG Fueling Stations & Bunkering Infrastructure
STATIONS
CountryLNG Road StationsLNG Bunkering VesselsKey Bunkering PortsDetails
🇨🇳 China~4,500+5Shanghai, Shenzhen, Ningbo, Zhoushan, GuangzhouWorld's largest LNG road fueling network. ~4,500+ LNG stations (vs 800 in EU, 81 in US). Government promoted stations as priority gas utilization. Fleet: 1M+ LNG trucks. Growth decelerating: BEV trucks at 22% of HDV sales in Q1 2025. Shanghai: bunkering +60% in first 5 months of 2025. PetroChina STS at Yantian. CNOOC Shanghai Avenir bunkering vessel (20K m³, world's largest). Hudong-Zhonghua building more bunkering vessels.
🇯🇵 Japan~303Yokohama, Kobe, NagoyaSmall road network (Japan trucking = diesel + BEV). LNG stations mostly for local fleets/buses. Bunkering: JERA + MOL + NYK operating LNG bunkering. Japan's roadmap for LNG bunkering. Focus shifting to ammonia co-firing (JERA Hekinan) rather than LNG expansion.
🇸🇬 SingaporeN/A3Singapore (world's #1 bunkering port)No road stations (city-state, no trucking market). But world's largest bunkering hub. LNG bunkered: 463,900 tonnes (2024) — nearly 4× 2023. MPA target: 3 Mt/yr by 2028 (6× current). FueLNG Bellina + FueLNG Venosa. 3 methanol bunkering suppliers licensed from Jan 2026. Also licensing ammonia bunkering (pilot).
🇰🇷 South Korea~204Busan, Ulsan, GwangyangTop-5 Autogas (LPG) market but LNG road stations small. Green Ship Initiative driving bunkering investment. KOGAS expanding LNG bunkering at Busan. 4 bunkering vessels operational. Korean shipyards (HD HHI, Samsung, DSME) = world leaders in DF vessel construction — builds the ships, other countries burn the fuel.
🇮🇳 India~5 pilot0None (planned: Mumbai, Chennai)LNG road stations: near zero. India's gas transport fuel = CNG (4,500+ CNG stations, 46% YoY sales growth H1 2024). LNG trucking pilot only (Scania). LNG bunkering: BPCL set up India's first biofuel bunker at Mumbai Port (Aug 2024). LNG marine bunkering: not yet operational at any Indian port. India may skip LNG trucking entirely → CNG → BEV.
🇦🇺 Australia~101Gladstone, MelbourneSmall road network. Viva Energy, BOC LNG stations along east coast corridors. Bunkering: limited to Gladstone (export terminal area). Australia = LNG exporter, not domestic consumer. Remote mining power is the main SS-LNG use case (EDL Energy), not road fueling.
🇮🇩 Indonesia~20NoneNear zero infrastructure. Pertamina piloting floating regas for island power. 17,000+ islands = massive theoretical potential but decades from reality. Government providing financial incentives for LNG ship retrofits and tank truck fleets.
🇻🇳 Vietnam00None (planned)First LNG import terminals under development. No SS-LNG infrastructure yet. Coal demand +12% in 2024. LNG-to-power planned but SS-LNG distribution years away.

APAC LNG bunkering fleet (end 2024): 17 vessels operational across the region with 179,700 m³ total capacity (IGU 2025) — 2nd largest after Europe (25 vessels, 190,757 m³). China (5), South Korea (4), Singapore (3) = 71% of APAC bunkering vessels.

APAC vs Europe vs US: China alone has ~4,500+ LNG road stations — 5.6× Europe's 800 and 55× the US's 81. This reflects China's unique LNG trucking ecosystem (1M+ fleet). No other APAC country has more than ~30 LNG road stations. The bunkering picture is different: Singapore leads in throughput (463K tonnes, 2024) while Shanghai is the fastest-growing hub (+60% H1 2025). Global bunkering is concentrating into 3 mega-hubs: Singapore, Rotterdam, and Shanghai.

Sources: IGU/Manifold Times (2025) · Seala AI · IEEFA Asia · OilChem · MPA Singapore · KOGAS
IMO Net-Zero Target
~2050
Revised GHG Strategy (2023): net-zero from international shipping by ~2050. Interim milestones 2030/2040. Mid-term measures from 2027. Global sulfur cap 0.5% (2020). Primary driver of APAC LNG bunkering growth.
Source: MarkNtel / IMO
Singapore MPA 2026
New Licenses
MPA issuing new LNG bunker supply licenses in 2026. Upgrading TR56 to national standard. Methanol bunkering from Jan 2026. 3 Mtpa LNG bunkering target by 2028. World's leading regulatory framework for multi-fuel bunkering.
Source: MarkNtel / MPA Singapore
South Korea ECAs
0.1% Sulfur
Emission Control Areas around Busan + Incheon. 0.1% sulfur limit + speed restrictions. Target: −60% port particulate emissions by 2025 vs 2017. Korea Green Ship Initiative driving LNG bunkering.
Source: MarkNtel
ASEAN LNG Investment
$20.4B
Planned infra investment to increase import capacity. Vietnam: first LNG from Russia (May 2025), first Shell term contract (Jan 2026). Philippines: LNG imports forecast +508% by 2029. Thailand: LNG tariff cut to 2%.
Source: Zero Carbon Analytics / GEM
APAC LNG Regulatory Framework: Country-by-Country
REGULATION
🌊
IMO Global
Sulfur cap 0.5% (2020). Revised GHG Strategy: net-zero ~2050. Mid-term measures from 2027. Arctic HFO ban (2024). 1,650+ LNG-fueled vessels + on order (2025). Primary driver of APAC bunkering. Proposals to extend ECAs to Japan + SE Asia.
🇸🇬
Singapore MPA
World's most advanced bunkering regulation. 3 licensed suppliers (FueLNG, Pavilion/Shell, TotalEnergies). New LNG licenses in 2026. TR56 → national standard. Methanol bunkering licensed from Jan 2026. Green Port Programme: 15-25% port dues discount. Target: 3 Mtpa by 2028.
🇯🇵
Japan GX Strategy
$150B GX bonds for decarbonization. Roadmap to Zero Emissions: −46% by 2030, carbon neutral by 2050. First LNG bunkering for cruise vessel (Yokohama, Jun 2025). But pivoting to ammonia co-firing (JERA Hekinan). LNG = bridge, ammonia = destination.
🇰🇷
South Korea
Carbon neutrality 2050. ECAs at Busan/Incheon (0.1% sulfur). K-Taxonomy includes gas as green transition fuel. KOGAS import monopoly being reformed. Green Ship Initiative funding bunkering vessels. AWS + SK: AI hyperscale DC in Ulsan powered by LNG.
🇮🇳
India
Gas share target: 15% of energy (currently ~6.7%). CGD expansion: 300+ geographic areas. GAIL: pilot SS-LNG skids. Golden Quadrilateral: planned CNG/LNG stations on 6,000 km highway. 1/3 HDV fleet LNG mandate (policy, limited execution). LNG bunkering: Kochi + Hazira pilot.
🌏
ASEAN Emerging
$20.4B planned LNG infra investment. Vietnam: first LNG from Russia (May 2025), Shell term contract (Jan 2026, 400K tpa). Philippines: LNG imports +508% by 2029. Thailand: LNG tariff cut 5% → 2% (May 2025), 40% LNG by 2030. Indonesia: Pertamina floating regas for 17K+ islands.
Sources: MarkNtel · Zero Carbon Analytics · APEC · Mordor Intelligence
APAC LNG Regulatory Outlook: What to Watch
OUTLOOK
AreaDirectionImpact on SS-LNGKey Detail
🌊 IMO Mid-Term MeasuresTighteningHigh (+)2027+ measures. Carbon intensity targets. LNG = lowest-cost compliance pathway for existing fleet. Drives DF vessel orders and bunkering demand across all APAC ports.
🇸🇬 Singapore Hub PolicyExpandingHigh (+)New licenses 2026. TR56 national standard. Multi-fuel (LNG + methanol + ammonia). MPA orchestrating the global energy transition at port level. 3 Mtpa target = 6× current.
🇰🇷 Korea ECAsTighteningMed (+)0.1% sulfur at Busan/Incheon. Speed restrictions. Green Ship Initiative. Korea builds the vessels, ECAs ensure they burn cleaner fuel at home ports.
🇯🇵 Japan Ammonia PivotShifting AwayMed (−)Japan investing in ammonia co-firing over LNG expansion. JERA Hekinan. GX bonds funding ammonia, not LNG. LNG demand stable but not growing. SS-LNG declining (satellite stations).
🇮🇳 India CGD / LNGSlowLow15% gas share target = ambitious but lagging. CNG dominates transport. LNG trucking = pilot only. India may skip LNG trucks entirely → CNG → BEV. SS-LNG relevant mostly for bunkering (long-term).
🌏 ASEAN LNG-to-PowerExpandingMed (+)$20.4B infra. Vietnam, Philippines, Thailand expanding. But renewables cheaper and faster. Risk: LNG lock-in at high cost. SS-LNG relevant for island power (Indonesia) and coastal distribution.

APAC regulatory story in one sentence: APAC LNG regulation is a patchwork — Singapore leads with the world's most advanced bunkering framework (new licenses, multi-fuel, TR56 national standard), South Korea is tightening ECAs at major ports, Japan is pivoting from LNG to ammonia (GX bonds funding ammonia co-firing), India has ambitious targets but CNG dominance and slow execution limit SS-LNG relevance, and ASEAN is investing $20.4B in LNG import infrastructure even as renewables become cheaper. The IMO's Revised GHG Strategy (net-zero ~2050) is the overarching driver that makes all of this matter — it forces the shipping industry toward cleaner fuels, and LNG is currently the only scalable compliance pathway. For SS-LNG specifically, APAC regulation is most supportive for marine bunkering (IMO + Singapore MPA + Korea ECAs) and least supportive for road transport (no major APAC country outside China has meaningful LNG trucking regulation, and India may skip LNG trucks entirely).

Sources: MarkNtel · Zero Carbon Analytics · ERIA / IEEJ · Eco-Business
Asian LNG Imports (2025)
240 Mt
Down from 254 Mt (2024). −6% through Oct 2025. China = 90% of the decline (−16%). Japan may reclaim #1 importer as China pulls back. Asia = 65-67% of global LNG.
Source: IEEFA / Kpler
Global LNG Trade (2024)
411 Mt
+2.4% YoY. 22 exporting → 48 importing markets. APAC = largest exporting region (139 Mt). US = 97.5 Mtpa capacity (#1). Supply surge: +23 Mt in 2025, Qatar NFE start mid-2026.
Source: IGU World LNG Report 2025
JKM Spot Price
~$12.4/MMBtu
Jul 2025. Avg 2024: $11.91 (−13.5% YoY). JKM-NWE spread narrowed to $1.15/MMBtu. China vanishes from spot >$10. 2/3 of Asian spot trades JKM-indexed. Inter-basin competition intensifying.
Source: Seala AI / Platts
Hormuz Dependency
~20% Global
80% of Hormuz LNG → Asia. Qatar = primary (263 MMm³/d). Iran war June 2025 → JKM +16% in days. If blocked 1 year: −110 bcm. Singapore + Thailand most exposed (45% + 28% from Qatar/Oman).
Source: Zero Carbon Analytics
APAC LNG Trade Architecture: Suppliers & Flows
TRADE
🇦🇺
Australia
87.6 Mtpa capacity (#2 globally). NW Shelf, Gorgon, Wheatstone, Ichthys, Prelude FLNG. Largest supplier to Japan, China (declining). APLNG lowered Sinopec price. Legacy projects facing plateau. Oil-indexed contracts.
🇶🇦
Qatar
77.1 Mtpa (#3). NFE start mid-2026. NFW doubled to 32 Mtpa (by 2030). 27-yr contracts with Sinopec, CNPC, Shell, TotalEnergies, Eni. Oil-indexed (~12% slope). Overtook Australia as China's #1 in 2025. Lowest-cost LNG globally.
🇺🇸
United States
97.5 Mtpa capacity (#1). Hub-indexed (Henry Hub). Plaquemines LNG started late 2024. US LNG fell across ALL Asian markets in 2025 (not just China). Destination-flexible = prices drive flows, not politics. Cape of Good Hope route: 37.5 days.
🇷🇺
Russia (Pipeline + LNG)
PoS-1: 38 bcm to China (full capacity). Yamal LNG + Arctic LNG 2 (sanctioned but delivering to China). 5% of Asian LNG (flat since 2015). Northern Sea Route → Asia faster + cheaper. EU banned Russian LNG transshipment (Mar 2025).
🇲🇾
Malaysia + SE Asia
PETRONAS: major regional exporter + supplier. Short shipping routes = cost advantage. Indonesia: domestic declining → net importer. PNG: Papua LNG (TotalEnergies) under development. Brunei: small but legacy exporter.
📊
APAC Importers
China: 79 Mt (2024), −16% (2025). Japan: 68 Mt (declining, nuclear restarts). S. Korea: 47 Mt (−20% plan by 2036). India: 26 Mt (+19% 2024). Taiwan: 17 Mt (nuclear phaseout = LNG growth). SE Asia: Thailand top importer, growing.

2025: the year Asia's LNG demand fell. Through November, Asian LNG imports dropped from 254 to 240 Mt — China accounted for 90% of the decline. But this was NOT demand destruction: China's gas demand actually rose. The drop was caused by structural supply pivots (domestic production + Russian pipeline) and high prices that drove spot purchases to near-zero. US LNG fell across ALL Asian markets (not just China), as European buyers bid higher. The 2026 outlook is different: IEA forecasts Asia LNG imports +10% as Qatar NFE starts, prices soften, and China returns as a marginal buyer. The fundamental dynamic: Asia is shifting from "hungry buyer at any price" to "sophisticated, price-sensitive market" — with China as the swing variable that determines whether global LNG trades tight or loose.

Sources: IEEFA (Dec 2025) · IGU 2025 · IEA Q3-2025 · Zero Carbon Analytics · Columbia CGEP
APAC LNG Trade Outlook: Key Factors
OUTLOOK
FactorDirectionImpactKey Detail
🌊 Global Supply WaveSurgingBearish Prices+23 Mt in 2025. Qatar NFE mid-2026. Global capacity +33% by 2028 (665→884 bcm/yr). Nameplate capacity may exceed IEA 2050 demand projections. Oversupply from late 2026.
🇨🇳 China Swing RoleVolatileDecisive−16% in 2025, but may return +5.5 Mt in 2026 if prices soften. China as swing buyer = THE variable. If <$10/MMBtu, China buys aggressively. If >$12, China pivots to pipeline/domestic.
🇯🇵🇰🇷 NE Asia DeclineStructuralBearishJapan LNG demand −20% since 2018 (nuclear restarts + renewables). Korea: −20% plan by 2036. Both shifting away from gas-fired power. 92 long-term contracts expiring this decade.
🌏 SE Asia EmergingGrowingUncertain$20.4B planned infra. But: gas turbine bottlenecks (Philippines canceled 1.1 GW project). Vietnam: 22 GW planned, only 1 has PPA. Investors describe it as "like entering a trap."
💰 Pricing FragmentationEvolvingComplexQatar/Australia: oil-indexed. US: Henry Hub. Spot: JKM (2/3 of Asian spot). No single methodology will dominate. JKM-NWE spread narrowing → inter-basin competition intensifying.
⚠️ Hormuz / GeopoliticalElevatedTail RiskIran war Jun 2025: JKM +16%. 20% of global LNG through Hormuz, 80% → Asia. Red Sea disruptions: Cape of Good Hope adds +10 days. Singapore + Thailand most exposed.

APAC LNG trade story in one sentence: Asia's LNG imports fell 6% in 2025 as China executed a structural pivot away from LNG (−16%) toward domestic production and Russian pipeline gas — but the drop masked diverging trajectories: Japan and Korea are in structural decline (nuclear + renewables), India is growing but price-sensitive, and SE Asia is ambitious but execution-challenged (turbine bottlenecks, delayed PPAs, contractual complexity). The 2026 outlook is a potential turning point: a massive supply wave (US + Qatar NFE) should push prices below $10/MMBtu, which is the threshold that brings China back into the spot market and makes coal-to-gas switching viable in SE Asia. For SS-LNG specifically, APAC trade dynamics matter because: (1) lower LNG prices make SS-LNG bunkering more competitive vs HFO/VLSFO, (2) lower prices improve economics for SS-LNG trucking in China (diesel-LNG spread), and (3) lower prices enable nascent SE Asian island power applications. The geopolitical wildcard: Hormuz disruption could spike prices and erase all these benefits overnight.

Sources: IEEFA · IEEFA Data Dive · IEA Q3-2025 · LNG Industry
Asia Gas Demand Growth
+35% (2015–23)
China = 90% of this increase. Gas demand also rose 30%+ in India, Korea, Taiwan. But fell in Japan (−20%), Thailand, Pakistan. 2025: weakest growth since 2022 (<1%). 2026E: +4% (IEA).
Source: IEEFA Data Dive
China Gas-Fired Power
99→144 GW
Surged in 2025. Target: 261–308 GW by 2030. Guangdong +23 GW. Power gen = APAC's largest long-term gas growth sector. But gas = only ~4% of China power. Peaking role for renewables backup.
Source: Mysteel
BEV Outselling LNG (China)
Since Jun 2025
Battery-electric HDVs outselling LNG trucks since Jun 2025. BEV = 22-28% of HDV sales. Battery swap technology accelerating. Goldman: costs −50% by 2026. China trucking = peaking.
Source: IEEFA
Japan + Korea LNG Decline
Structural
Japan: LNG −20% since 2018 (nuclear restarts + renewables). Korea: −20% plan by 2036. Both pivoting away from gas-fired power. NE Asia = declining demand, not growing.
Source: IEEFA (Dec 2025)
APAC Gas & SS-LNG Demand Drivers by Country
DEMAND
🚛
China LNG Trucking
→ PEAKING
1M+ fleet, 4,500+ stations, ~30 bcm/yr (≈ France). But BEV outselling since Jun 2025. LNG share slipping from 33% peak. Transport gas demand grew 85% (2015-23) — near-term growth continues but long-term decline. Largest APAC SS-LNG segment.
🚢
Marine Bunkering
▲▲▲ SURGING
$1.52B market (2025). Singapore: 56.77 Mt total bunker sales, 712K m³ LNG. Shanghai: first >50M TEU port, LNG +60%. Korea: 4 bunkering vessels. Japan: first cruise bunkering (Jun 2025). IMO + ECAs driving. THE APAC SS-LNG growth story.
⚡
Power Generation
▲▲ GROWING (China)
China: 99→144→300 GW. SE Asia: Vietnam plans 22 GW LNG-to-power but only 1 has PPA. Japan/Korea: gas-fired share DECLINING. India: no plans for new gas plants (existing ones stranded). Growth concentrated in China peaking role.
🏭
Industrial / Manufacturing
→ MIXED
China: 300+ SS-LNG plants. −3% in 2025 (high prices). India: fertilizers = main LNG demand (subsidized). SE Asia: manufacturing growing but coal-to-gas needs <$6/MMBtu. Industrial = largest volume of growth since 2015 but cyclical.
🏝️
Island Power / Energy Access
▲ NICHE
Indonesia: 17K+ islands, Pertamina floating mini-regas. Philippines: Malampaya depleting, Batangas terminal (2024). Pacific Islands: diesel $20+/MMBtu. SS-LNG ISO containers + FSRUs. Massive potential but decades from scale.
🖥️
Data Centers / AI
▲ EMERGING
Singapore power demand surging. Korea: AWS + SK hyperscale DC in Ulsan (all LNG-powered). Japan: GX bonds fund gas backup. Unlike US (behind-the-meter SS-LNG), APAC DCs mostly use grid-connected gas. Indirect for SS-LNG but growing fast.
Sources: IEEFA Data Dive · IEEFA (Dec 2025) · Mysteel · MarkNtel
APAC Gas Demand Outlook: Multi-Year Trajectory by Driver
OUTLOOK
Driver20252026E2030ESS-LNG Relevance
🚢 Marine Bunkering▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲Core — Singapore 3 Mtpa target, Shanghai +60%, Korea ECAs. Only APAC demand segment growing across ALL countries. IMO-driven.
🚛 China LNG Trucking→▼▼▼Core — Still 67% of APAC SS-LNG demand. 1M+ fleet runs for a decade. But new sales declining. BEV = existential long-term threat.
⚡ Power Gen (China)▲▲▲▲▲▲▲Indirect — Mostly grid-connected, not SS-LNG. But drives overall gas demand which feeds SS-LNG plant feedstock economics.
⚡ Power Gen (Japan/Korea)▼▼▼▼None — Nuclear restarts + renewables displacing gas. LNG demand in structural decline. Not an SS-LNG driver.
🏭 Industrial▼▲→Core — China 300+ plants. Cyclical but foundational. India: fertilizers (subsidized). Coal-to-gas needs <$6/MMBtu for SE Asia.
🏝️ Island Power→▲▲▲Core — Perfect SS-LNG use case. Indonesia 17K+ islands, Philippines, Pacific. But decades from scale. Renewable competition growing.
🖥️ Data Centers▲▲▲▲▲▲▲Indirect — APAC DCs mostly grid-connected (unlike US behind-the-meter). Korea Ulsan LNG-powered DC = exception. Drives gas-fired gen but not directly SS-LNG.

APAC gas demand story in one sentence: Asia's gas demand grew 35% between 2015 and 2023 — but China accounted for 90% of this increase, and the 2025 slowdown (<1% growth, weakest since 2022) reveals that the growth narrative is fragmenting: China's gas demand is still growing (428→620-700 Bcm long-term) but LNG's share is declining (domestic + pipeline pivot); Japan and Korea are in structural decline (nuclear + renewables); India is growing but mainly on subsidized fertilizer demand, not broad-based gas adoption; and SE Asia is ambitious but execution-challenged (turbine bottlenecks, PPA delays, prices too high for coal switching). For SS-LNG specifically, APAC demand is driven by three segments: (1) marine bunkering (the only segment growing across ALL APAC countries, IMO-driven, Singapore + Shanghai as mega-hubs), (2) China trucking (massive but peaking — BEV outselling LNG since Jun 2025), and (3) China industrial satellite (300+ plants, cyclical). Everything else — island power, data centers, SE Asian LNG-to-power — is either too small, too indirect, or too far from execution to materially affect the APAC SS-LNG market in the 2025-2030 timeframe.

Sources: IEEFA Data Dive · IEEFA (Dec 2025) · IEA Q3-2025 · Zero Carbon Analytics
APAC SS-LNG Demand (2026E)
155.3 MMm³/d
56.7 bcm/yr. +11.7% CAGR (2023-26). China = 80% of total. China trucking alone = 61% of APAC SS-LNG. Growth decelerating (BEV erosion).
Source: Dashboard bottom-up model
APAC SS-LNG Supply (2026E)
167.2 MMm³/d
61.1 bcm/yr. +18% CAGR. China domestic plants = 67% of supply. Swung from deficit (2023: 111%) to surplus (2026E: 93%) in just 2 years as China capacity surged +41%.
Source: Dashboard bottom-up model
Utilization Trend
111%→93%
2023: supply-constrained (111%). 2024: balanced (98%). 2026E: surplus (93%). Chinese producers responded to demand explosion at unprecedented speed. Now risk of overcapacity.
Source: Dashboard bottom-up model
APAC vs US + EU
~7.7×
APAC SS-LNG (56.7 bcm) = 7.7× US (2.04 bcm) + EU (5.29 bcm) combined. China trucking alone (34 bcm) > Austria's entire gas consumption. Scale is staggering.
Source: Dashboard models
Asia-Pacific SS-LNG Market: Bottom-Up Supply & Demand Model
MODEL
Methodology
Bottom-up from segment-level estimates. Scope = China + Japan + Korea + India + Singapore + SE Asia + Australia. A = actual · P = preliminary · E = estimate. This market is ~10× larger than US or Europe — dominated by China's domestic SS-LNG ecosystem (300+ plants, 1M+ trucks, 4,500+ stations). China alone = ~80% of APAC demand. 1 Mt LNG ≈ 1.36 bcm NG.
APAC = Three Markets in One
China (80%): Trucking-dominated but peaking. 300+ plants, self-sufficient ecosystem. BEV is THE risk. NE Asia (Japan/Korea, 10%): Mature & declining. Nuclear/renewables displacing gas. Emerging (India/SE Asia/Singapore, 10%): Bunkering hubs + island power + CGD. High growth from tiny base. Very different risk profiles for each.
Key 2025–26 Events
BEV outselling LNG trucks in China (Jun 2025). Asian LNG imports −6% (2025). China −16% (structural, not cyclical). Singapore new LNG licenses (2026). Japan first cruise bunkering (Jun 2025). Korea ECA enforcement. Philippines 1.1 GW LNG project canceled. Vietnam: only 1 of 22 GW LNG-to-power has PPA.

(40.8)		21%World's largest SS-LNG supply base. IEEFA: 25 Mt domestic production (2024), capacity +41% in 2024 alone, doubled in 5 years. 300+ plants drawing from pipeline gas, coal-bed methane, and coke oven gas (COG). PipeChina + Kunlun Energy + ENN operate most plants. Rising feedstock from domestic production (246 bcm, 2024) + Power of Siberia (22.7→38 bcm by 2025). 2026E: continued plant additions but pace slowing as trucking demand plateaus.High
2. Japan: Domestic Facilities11.18
(4.08)		11.18
(4.08)		11.18
(4.08)		10.44
(3.81)		-2.3%~200+ satellite terminals + peak shaving plants. Mature, well-documented. Slow decline as nuclear restarts reduce LNG throughput. Tokyo Gas, Osaka Gas, JERA = major operators. No new greenfield SS-LNG planned.High
3. APAC: Import Terminal Truck-Loading14.90
(5.44)		18.63
(6.80)		22.36
(8.16)		26.08
(9.52)		21%Truck-loading bays at major import terminals across China (Dapeng, Rudong, Qingdao, etc.), Japan, Korea, India (Hazira, Dahej, Ennore), Thailand (Map Ta Phut), Philippines (Batangas). China expanding truck-loading at all new terminals. India Shell Hazira = anchor. Growing as terminals add SS-LNG bays.Med
4. SG / KR / CN: Bunkering Vessels + Infra9.32
(3.40)		11.18
(4.08)		13.04
(4.76)		14.90
(5.44)		17%Singapore: FueLNG Bellina + FueLNG Venosa. Korea: KOGAS bunkering infra at Busan. China: PetroChina Yantian STS, CNOOC Shanghai barges. ~15 bunkering vessels in APAC waters. 2026E: ~20 vessels. Each: 1K–2K ops/yr. Singapore = anchor infrastructure.Med
5. India + SE Asia: Emerging SS-LNG1.86
(0.68)		2.60
(0.95)		3.29
(1.20)		3.97
(1.45)		29%India: GAIL 2 SS-LNG skids (pilot, 2022). Shell Hazira truck-loading. Petronet Dahej expansion. Philippines: Batangas LNG (2024). Indonesia: Pertamina floating regas. Vietnam: early import terminals. High growth from very low base. Mostly import-terminal truck-loading + small-scale regas.Low
TOTAL SUPPLY100.6
(36.7)		136.8
(49.9)		154.3
(56.3)		167.2
(61.1)		18%China domestic plants = 67% of APAC supply (2024). IEEFA key finding: China produces enough LNG domestically (25 Mt) to meet its trucking needs (22 Mt). APAC moved from deficit (2023) to surplus (2024+) as Chinese capacity surged +41%.
Supply-Demand Balance & Scenario Analysis
BALANCE
Metric2023A2024A2025P2026E
Demand (MMm³/d)111.4133.5146.5155.3
Demand (bcm/yr)40.748.753.556.7
Supply (MMm³/d)100.6136.8154.3167.2
Supply (bcm/yr)36.749.956.361.1
Surplus / (Deficit) MMm³/d-10.8+3.3+7.8+11.9
Utilization111%98%95%93%
China % of APAC Demand79%82%81%80%
APAC vs US + EU Combined8.6×8.6×8.4×7.7×
⚠ China Trucking (THE global risk)
61% of APAC demand. BEV = 20-28% of HDV sales (Q1 2025). Goldman: battery −50% by 2026. If BEV captures 30%+ by 2027, China SS-LNG peaks in 2026-27. The single most important variable in global SS-LNG.
✓ Singapore Bunkering (Highest confidence)
Best port-level data. MPA tracks volumes. SEA-LNG: +18% H1 2025. FueLNG vessels operational. New licenses 2026. Clear, measurable, regulation-backed growth trajectory.
✓ China Domestic Supply (Well-documented)
IEEFA: 25 Mt production (2024). +41% capacity. Trackable via provincial filings. Key feedstocks well-understood (PNG, COG, CBM). Risk: overcapacity if trucking demand peaks.
⚠ India + SE Asia (Uncertain)
Fastest growth rate (India 49% CAGR) but from tiny base. Actual volumes uncertain. Philippines canceled 1.1 GW project. Vietnam: 1 of 22 GW has PPA. Turbine bottlenecks. "Like entering a trap."
Scenario2026E Demand2026E SupplyUtilizationWhat Drives It
🟢 Base Case155.3 MMm³/d167.2 MMm³/d93%China trucking plateaus. Bunkering grows (Singapore + Shanghai). Japan declines. India/SE Asia grow from low base. Moderate surplus.
🔵 Bull Case168.5 MMm³/d172.6 MMm³/d98%LNG prices <$10 → China truck sales rebound. India CGD accelerates. SE Asia executes on LNG-to-power. Bunkering surges. Near-balance returns.
🔴 Bear (BEV Acceleration)140.4 MMm³/d167.2 MMm³/d84%BEV 35%+ of China HDV. LNG truck sales collapse. Japan nuclear accelerates. SE Asia projects stall (turbine bottlenecks). APAC SS-LNG demand peaks in 2025 and structurally declines. Massive overcapacity.

APAC SS-LNG S&D model in one sentence: The Asia-Pacific SS-LNG market is 7.7× larger than US + EU combined — but it is effectively a China trucking story (61% of demand) surrounded by mature declining markets (Japan), world-class bunkering hubs (Singapore, Shanghai), and emerging markets at very early stages (India, SE Asia). The market swung from severe supply deficit in 2023 (111% utilization) to surplus by 2024 as Chinese producers added capacity at +41%/year. The critical question for the entire global SS-LNG industry is whether China's LNG truck fleet (1M+ vehicles, 34 bcm/yr consumption) peaks in 2026-28 or continues growing — BEV trucks outselling LNG since June 2025 suggests the former. If China trucking peaks, APAC demand peaks, and the world's largest SS-LNG market enters structural decline. The offset: marine bunkering is growing across ALL APAC countries (Singapore 3 Mtpa target, Shanghai +60%, Korea ECAs), and this could eventually become the dominant APAC SS-LNG segment — but today, it's still only ~8% of the total. The diverging trajectories within APAC (China peaking, Japan declining, bunkering surging, SE Asia ambitious but struggling) make this the most complex and consequential SS-LNG market to forecast.

Sources: IEEFA (May 2025) · IEEFA China LNG (Apr 2025) · SEA-LNG Mid-Year 2025 · IEEFA (Dec 2025) · Columbia CGEP

China LNG & Natural Gas Market

The world's largest LNG importer and fourth-largest gas producer. China consumed 434 bcm of natural gas in 2024 (+7% YoY), with ~45% supplied by imports. A unique domestic SS-LNG ecosystem — 300+ liquefaction plants, ~1 million LNG trucks, and 5,000+ fueling stations — makes China the undisputed global leader in small-scale LNG deployment.

NG Consumption (2024)
434 bcm
+7% YoY · Forecast 550–600 bcm by 2030 · Peak 620–650 bcm by 2040
Source: ScienceDirect · Columbia CGEP
Domestic NG Production (2024)
246 bcm
Covers 56% of demand · CNPC + Sinopec + CNOOC = 95% of output
Source: Columbia CGEP
Total Gas Imports (2024)
176.5 bcm
+10.6% YoY · LNG: 106 bcm · Pipeline: 71 bcm · Import dependency ~45%
Source: ScienceDirect
LNG Truck Fleet (2025E)
~1 million
Tripled since 2019 · ~30 bcm/yr gas consumption · 5,000+ stations
Source: Global LNG Hub
LNG Regas Terminals
29+
6.2 Tcf (176 bcm) capacity (2023) · 15 new under construction (+59 MTPA) · 55% avg utilization
Source: EIA China Brief 2025
SS-LNG Liquefaction Plants
300+
NW & central provinces · Fed by pipeline, CBM, wellhead gas · 50K–2M Nm³/day modular units
Source: APEC
Underground Gas Storage
35+ sites
0.94 Tcf (~27 bcm) capacity · Only 4–5% of consumption vs 12–15% global avg
Source: EIA
Electricity from Gas
~4%
Coal still ~60%. Gas-fired capacity target: 261–308 GW by 2030 (CNPC). Growing rapidly from low base.
Source: Oxford Energy / CGCP
China NG Consumption vs Production (bcm)
SUPPLY-DEMAND
Sources: ScienceDirect · EIA
China LNG Import Sources (2024)
IMPORTS
Sources: EIA · ScienceDirect
China LNG Imports (2024)
~53 Mt
CNOOC 27.4 Mt (#1), Sinopec 13.4 Mt, PetroChina 12.5 Mt. World's largest/2nd largest LNG importer. 25% US tariff → imports shifted to pipeline + resales.
Source: Energy Intelligence / Kpler
Domestic SS-LNG Plants
300+
Across 15+ provinces. 25 Mt produced (2024). +41% capacity. Feeds 4,500+ LNG stations + 1M+ trucks. Self-sufficient for trucking demand (IEEFA).
Source: IEEFA / Mysteel
PipeChina Network
98K km
Trunk pipelines. 7 LNG terminals (30 Mtpa). Third-party access mandate. Sichuan-East expansion completed (12 bcm/yr, Oct 2024). Power of Siberia at full 38 bcm.
Source: Mordor Intelligence
Gas Demand Forecast
620–700 Bcm
By 2030–35 (PipeChina: 650–700, CNPC: 620–650, Sinopec: 620). Current: ~400 Bcm. NOCs raising forecasts despite short-term LNG import weakness.
Source: Energy Intelligence (May 2025)
China LNG Key Player Landscape
PLAYERS
🔴
CNOOC
HKG: 0883
China's #1 LNG importer: 27.4 Mt (2024). 5 regas terminals. Zhoushan III (5 Mtpa, Sep 2024). 2 Mt/yr from VG Plaquemines. Active LNG resales to Europe during US tariff period. Offshore production leader.
🟡
CNPC / PetroChina
SHA: 601857
Largest producer: 158.6 bcm (2024). 12.5 Mt LNG imports. 1.8 Mt/yr Cheniere CC (24-yr). 4 Mt/yr Qatar NFE. Gate terminal NL (2 bcm/yr from 2026). First EU LNG bunkering with Titan. Global trader.
🔵
Sinopec / Unipec
SHA: 600028
13.4 Mt LNG imports. 4 Mt/yr from VG Plaquemines (20-yr). +7 Mt/yr from Qatar NFE/NFS. $1.5B for UGS (6 provinces). Shale gas leader (Fuling). Entering LNG retail at existing fuel stations.
🔧
PipeChina
State-owned (2019)
Midstream monopoly. 98K km pipelines. 7 LNG terminals (30 Mtpa). Third-party access mandate (TPA). Sichuan-East expansion (12 bcm/yr). Power of Siberia China-side. Demand forecast: 650-700 Bcm by 2030-35.
🏢
Tier-2 Players
Private/Regional
ENN Energy (only private 3 Mtpa terminal). Guanghui Energy (Xinjiang, largest private). Jovo Group. Shenzhen Gas. Suntien. Beijing Gas. Rising via PipeChina TPA. SOEs still = 85% of capacity.
🚛
OEM Ecosystem
Manufacturing
Truck OEMs: Sinotruk, FAW Jiefang, Dongfeng, Shaanxi Auto. Engines: Weichai Power. Cryogenic: CIMC ENRIC (trailers, ISO, bunkering vessels), Chuankong Cryo (30+ liquefaction plants). Exporting globally.

US-China tariff impact (2025): 25% tariff on US LNG. China LNG imports fell 22% over 4 months (Jan-Apr). CNOOC shifted to reselling US cargoes to Europe rather than landing in China. PetroChina gaining domestic market share as CNOOC focused on profit-taking abroad. Chinese buyers signed 44 Mt of long-term contracts in 2021-22 (mostly US, flexible destination) — these give NOCs optionality to resell globally. Sinopec/PetroChina leaning on Qatar + Australia volumes to reduce US dependency. Tier-2 players (Zhejiang Energy, Guangzhou Gas, Pearl River Investment) signing non-US deals (BP, Mercuria, ConocoPhillips) on conference sidelines.

Sources: Energy Intelligence (Sep 2025) · IEEFA (Apr 2025) · Columbia CGEP · Mordor Intelligence
China's National Oil Companies & Key Gas Players
NOCs
CompanyRoleKey Data
CNPC / PetroChina
SHA: 601857		Largest producer + pipeline operator~158.6 bcm marketable gas (2024). +4.6% YoY. Operates Power of Siberia (China side). 3 LNG regas terminals (1.0 Tcf). Plans 7–8 new UGS (21.7 bcm). Controls most onshore conventional + Tarim Basin.
Sinopec
SHA: 600028		Shale gas leader + refining1.3 Tcf gas production (2023, +7.1%). 3 LNG regas terminals (1.2 Tcf). Fuling shale field (Chongqing) = China's largest. Ultra-deep shale breakthroughs in Sichuan Basin. Plans 55 bcm additional UGS.
CNOOC
HKG: 0883		Offshore production + LNG terminalsControls all offshore NG. 5 LNG regas terminals (1.2 Tcf). Record 2024 production (+11.4% profit). Shenhai-1 Phase II (160 MMcf/d) online 2025. Terminal capacity expanded to 30M m³.
PipeChina
(State-owned, est. 2019)		Midstream monopoly7 LNG regas terminals (1.3 Tcf, largest share). Operates interprovincial gas pipelines stripped from NOCs. Created to ensure third-party access and market liberalization. Manages national pipeline grid.

HKG: 2688		Private LDC + only private terminalOperates China's only privately-owned 3 MTPA LNG terminal. Leading city gas distributor. SS-LNG distribution to industrial + residential. Active in LNG bunkering.
Chinese Equipment OEMsSS-LNG manufacturingChuankong Cryo (30+ plants since 1980s, first 100% skid-mounted 50K Nm³/day). CIMC ENRIC (LNG trailers, ISO containers, bunkering vessels — building 20K m³ vessel for Somtralux). Exporting globally.

LNG Truck OEMsVehicle manufacturingSinotruk, FAW Jiefang, Dongfeng, Shaanxi Auto. Weichai Power (engines). 152K LNG trucks sold in 2023 (+307% YoY). H1 2024: +104% YoY. Over 1/3 of all heavy truck sales.
