China's LNG Regas Terminal Infrastructure
TERMINALS
OperatorTerminalsCapacityKey Details
PipeChina7 terminals1.3 Tcf (~37 bcm)Largest share. Transferred from NOCs in 2019 restructuring. Mandated to provide third-party access. Dalian, Tangshan, Rudong, Shenzhen, others.
CNOOC5 terminals1.2 Tcf (~34 bcm)Expanded to 30M m³ total capacity. Handled 25+ MMT in 2023. Guangdong Dapeng (China's first, 2006), Fujian Putian, Zhejiang Ningbo, others.
Sinopec3 terminals1.2 Tcf (~34 bcm)Qingdao, Tianjin, Beihai. Expanding capacity. Active in long-term SPA signing (TotalEnergies 2 MTPA × 15 yr from 2028; QatarEnergy 4 MTPA × 27 yr).
CNPC3 terminals1.0 Tcf (~28 bcm)Dalian (transferred to PipeChina), Tangshan, Jiangsu Rudong. CNPC also manages pipeline import infrastructure (Power of Siberia, Central Asia).
ENN + Others11+ terminals~43 bcmENN: only private 3 MTPA terminal. Other private/JV terminals emerging. 15 new terminals under construction adding 59 MTPA. China = #1 in global regas construction.
Sources: EIA China 2025 · Seala AI
China Domestic SS-LNG Liquefaction Plants: Provincial Breakdown
300+ PLANTS

China has the world's largest domestic SS-LNG production base: 300+ plants producing 25 Mt/yr (2024, IEEFA). Capacity +41% in 2024. 29 new plants in 2024 adding 29.22 Mm³/d (OilChem). Total nameplate: ~70.76 Mm³/d. The network doubled in 5 years. Eight major producing provinces account for ~90% of output.

Province/RegionShare of OutputPlants (est.)Primary FeedstockKey Details
NORTHWEST — Pipeline Natural Gas (PNG) Belt (~55% of national output)
Inner Mongolia~20%50+PNG + COGLargest producing province. Ordos Basin gas + coal-to-gas. Huineng Coal Chemical: LNG from coal-derived gas. Linde StarLNG Beiniuchuan (813 tpd). Inner Mongolia Xingjie Natural Gas. Government actively promoting gas exploration in Ordos Basin.
Shaanxi~16%40+PNGOrdos Basin gas production hub. PipeChina/Kunlun Energy plants along pipeline. Many new PNG plants in 2024 (OilChem). Close to West-East Pipeline system. Plant gate cost: ¥2,800–3,500/t.
Xinjiang~12%30+PNGTarim Basin gas. Guanghui Energy: Jimunai plant (Linde StarLNG 1,200 tpd = largest single SS-LNG plant in China). Westernmost production. Feeds West-East Pipeline but also local LNG. LNG truck replacement ratio >60–70% in Xinjiang (S&P Global). Cheapest LNG prices in China.
Ningxia~5%10+PNGLNG truck replacement ratio >60% (highest in China along with Xinjiang). Small province but high LNG penetration due to low gas prices from pipeline proximity.
NORTH — Coke Oven Gas (COG) Belt (~20% of national output)
Shanxi~12%35+COG + CBMChina's coal capital → world's largest COG-to-LNG conversion center. 9 new COG plants in 2024 (OilChem). Standalone coking sector restructuring: closing small → large = more reliable COG supply. CBM liquefaction from coal-bed methane extraction. COG cost: ¥3,200–3,800/t. Jincheng Huagang Gas (Linde StarLNG customer).
Hebei~5%15+COG + PNGBeijing-Tianjin-Hebei (Jing-Jin-Ji) corridor. Blue Sky mandate zone. Steel industry byproduct COG → LNG. Industrial satellite supply for off-pipeline factories. Winter peak-shaving for northern heating.
CENTRAL/SOUTHWEST — Mixed Feedstock (~15% of national output)
Sichuan~7%20+PNG (shale + conventional)Sichuan Basin = China's largest conventional + shale gas producing area (23.8 bcm/yr shale). CNPC ultra-deep wells. Sichuan Tongkai Energy (Linde StarLNG customer). Feeds central China via pipeline + LNG trucking.
Henan~4%10+PNGCentral hub. Transit province on West-East Pipeline. LNG plants draw from pipeline throughput. Serves Henan + Hubei industrial demand.
OTHER PROVINCES (~10% of national output)
Shandong, Guizhou, Heilongjiang, others~10%50+PNG + CBM + biogasShandong: industrial demand hub (ceramics, chemicals), some local production + large import terminal supply (Qingdao, Rizhao). Guizhou: CBM liquefaction. Heilongjiang: pipeline gas from Daqing field. Emerging: biogas-to-LNG pilots in agricultural provinces (Henan, Shandong).

Major operators: PipeChina/Kunlun Energy (state, largest), ENN Energy (private, ~20 plants), China Gas Holdings, Guanghui Energy (Xinjiang, largest private), Sichuan Tongkai, Inner Mongolia Xingjie. Equipment: Linde StarLNG (multiple China installations), Chart Industries IPSMR, domestic Chinese OEMs (LifenGas, Chuankong General — 30+ plants delivered). Key trend: Sinopec entering LNG retail — expanding LNG refueling at existing gas station network, shifting from pure gasoline/diesel to multi-fuel.

Sources: Mysteel/OilChem (2024) · IEEFA (May 2025) · MDPI Environmental Impact Study · Linde StarLNG · EIA China · S&P Global
China LNG Refueling Stations & Marine Bunkering Infrastructure
~4,500+ STATIONS
InfrastructureCount / ScaleKey Details
LNG ROAD REFUELING NETWORK
Total LNG Stations~4,500+World's largest LNG road refueling network. 5.6× Europe's 800, 55× US's 81. Government promoted LNG stations as priority gas utilization sector. Growth driven by 1M+ LNG truck fleet (nearly tripled since 2019). Sinopec entering LNG retail — adding LNG dispensers at existing gas stations.
Top Provinces (LNG Truck Sales)Hebei 20.4% · Shanxi 17.4% · Shandong 8.9% · Henan 8.8%Q1 2024 LNG truck sales by province (OilChem/Mysteel). Hebei + Shanxi = 38% of sales — driven by logistics corridors (Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei freight hub) and low LNG prices near production areas. Traditional leaders Shaanxi + Inner Mongolia losing share to eastern demand centers.
Highest LNG PenetrationNingxia >60% · Xinjiang >60–70%LNG-fueled truck replacement ratio vs diesel (S&P Global/CPPEI). In NW provinces near gas fields, LNG is cheaper than diesel by 20–30%. When 1 kg LNG < 0.8 liters of diesel in price, LNG trucks are economically dominant. NW China LNG: ~¥4,125/t vs gasoil ~¥7,602/t.
OperatorsPetroChina · Sinopec · CNOOC · ENN · Kunlun · PrivatePetroChina/Kunlun Energy = largest network. Sinopec shifting to LNG — constructing LNG refueling at existing fuel stations. CNOOC: coastal stations near import terminals. ENN Energy: private network. Thousands of small private operators in NW China. Station economics: ¥0.3–0.5/m³ margin.
Growth OutlookDeceleratingStation additions slowing as BEV trucks capture 22% of HDV sales (Q1 2025, up from 8.1%). LNG share slipped from 33% → 31%. Goldman: battery costs -50% by 2026. Station network may plateau at ~5,000 by 2027 and begin declining if BEV HDVs reach 30%+ share. Bio-LNG not yet meaningful in China (unlike EU).
LNG MARINE BUNKERING
Bunkering Vessels5 operationalCNOOC/Avenir: 20,000 m³ vessel at Shanghai (world's largest LNG bunkering vessel, acquired from Avenir LNG Jan 2022). PetroChina: STS bunkering at Shenzhen Yantian. Hudong-Zhonghua (CSSC): building additional bunkering vessels for Anhui LNG. Total APAC fleet: 17 vessels (China = 29%).
Key Bunkering PortsShanghai · Shenzhen · Ningbo · Zhoushan · GuangzhouShanghai: bunkering volumes +60% in first 5 months of 2025 vs 2024 (SEA-LNG). Shanghai + Singapore + Rotterdam = emerging as 3 global mega-hubs. Ningbo-Zhoushan: world's largest port by cargo tonnage — LNG bunkering expanding. Yangtze River emission control area driving inland bunkering demand.
LNG Import Terminals (Truck-Loading)29 terminals (2023)EIA: 29 LNG regas terminals, total capacity 6.2 Tcf. Operated by: PipeChina (7), CNOOC (5), Sinopec, Beijing Gas, ENN, Guanghui. Most major terminals (Dapeng, Rudong, Dalian, Qingdao, Tianjin, Fujian, Zhuhai) have truck-loading bays: 50–200 truck loads/day each. Truck-loading supplements domestic plants for coastal demand (Guangdong, Zhejiang, Fujian). New terminals: Huizhou (Guangdong Energy Group, Sep 2024), Beijing Gas/Caofeidian.
SATELLITE RECEIVING STATIONS (INDUSTRIAL)
Industrial Satellite Stations500+SS-LNG delivered by truck → satellite regas → piped to factory. Blue Sky mandate zones: Foshan (ceramics), Tangshan (steel auxiliaries), Jingdezhen (ceramics), Hebei industrial parks. Each station: 30–100K m³/d gas supply. Off-pipeline factories in Guangdong, Zhejiang, Shandong, Hebei = primary customers. LNG replaces coal for industrial heat (kilns, furnaces, boilers) where regulation mandates clean fuel.

China vs Rest of World: China alone has more LNG road stations (~4,500) than Europe (800), US (81), Japan (30), Korea (20), India (5), and Australia (10) combined (~4,950 total vs China's ~4,500). China's SS-LNG ecosystem is self-contained: domestic production (25 Mt) feeds domestic stations (4,500+) serving a domestic fleet (1M+ trucks). Unlike Europe/US where SS-LNG is tied to imported LNG, China's system draws primarily from domestic pipeline gas and COG — reducing import dependence. This is the structural insight IEEFA emphasizes: China's LNG trucking boom does not pull LNG cargoes off the global market.

Sources: IEEFA (May 2025) · S&P Global · Mysteel/OilChem · IGU 2025 · EIA China · SEA-LNG
Gas Production (2024)
246.4 Bcm
Exceeded 14th FYP target of 230 Bcm. +7% from 2023. Could reach 259 Bcm in 2025. Ultra-deep shale (Sichuan), Tarim Basin, offshore = growth drivers.
Source: S&P Global / NBS (Mar 2025)
New Infra Rules
Jan 2026
NDRC finalized oil/gas infrastructure regulations. Storage revised: NOCs 5% (from 10%). Private investment in LNG terminals explicitly encouraged. Replaces 2014 rules.
Source: S&P Global (Nov 2025)
Storage Gap
26.7 vs 55
Bcm. Built 26.7 Bcm (2024) vs 55-60 Bcm target. = 6.3% of consumption (vs 15-20% in EU/US). PetroChina: 11 new UGS by end of 15th FYP. Critical vulnerability.
Source: S&P Global / PetroChina
Dual Carbon Goals
2030 / 2060
Carbon peak 2030, neutrality 2060. Gas = "bridge fuel" replacing coal. Gas share: ~9% → 12-15% by 2030. Non-fossil target: 32% by 2025, 35% goal.
Source: Oxford Energy / State Council
China Gas Regulatory Framework: State-Driven Market Reform
FRAMEWORK
🔧
PipeChina Reform (2019)
Most ambitious gas reform in 20 years. Stripped NOCs of pipelines + 7 LNG terminals. 98K km + 30 Mtpa. Third-party access (TPA) mandate. Goal: more competition, lower prices. But leadership = 7/8 ex-NOC executives. Enforcement of TPA remains uneven.
📋
New Infra Rules (Jan 2026)
NDRC finalized rules replacing 2014 regs. NOC storage: 5% (down from 10%). PipeChina: 5% of national supply. Local govts: 5 days emergency reserve. Private investment in LNG terminals explicitly encouraged. Adapts to post-PipeChina structure.
💰
NDRC Pricing Reform
Moving from government-set to market-based. Province-mandated max prices at LNG terminals. Coastal provinces (with multiple terminals) = market pricing. Industrial cross-subsidy of residential gas narrowing. Citygate tariffs being deregulated.
🏔️
Blue Sky / Coal-to-Gas
Coal-to-gas mandates for heating + industry (2017-2025). Direct driver of 300+ SS-LNG plant buildout. Expanded to NW provinces. Foshan ceramics, Tangshan steel = Blue Sky mandate zones. Provincial LNG truck subsidies + highway toll discounts.
⛏️
Shale Gas + Self-Reliance
Resource tax: 6% → 4.2% (extended through Dec 2027). Sichuan Basin shale: 23.8 bcm/yr. CNPC ultra-deep wells (Tarim). "Seven-Year Action Plan" for E&P. Technology self-reliance for LNG equipment (4 breakthrough technologies, 2024 report).
🌍
Dual Carbon + Methane
Carbon peak 2030, neutrality 2060. Gas: 9% → 12-15% of energy by 2030. China Methane Alliance (7 members: NOCs + PipeChina + ENN + Beijing Gas + CR Gas). Target: methane intensity <0.25% by 2025. Did NOT sign Global Methane Pledge.
Sources: S&P Global (Nov 2025) · Columbia CGEP · Oxford Energy · Columbia CGEP (2024)
China Gas Regulatory Outlook: What to Watch
OUTLOOK
AreaDirectionImpact on SS-LNGKey Detail
🔧 PipeChina TPALiberalizingHigh (+)TPA enables Tier-2 players (ENN, Guanghui, Jovo) to access terminals. More participants = more competitive downstream pricing. KEY enabler for SS-LNG market development.
📋 New Infra Rules (2026)SupportiveHigh (+)Private investment in LNG terminals explicitly encouraged. Storage obligations revised. Provincial pipeline integration. Opens space for non-SOE SS-LNG infrastructure.
💰 Price LiberalizationGradualMed (+)Market pricing in coastal provinces. Province-mandated max at terminals. Industrial prices declining (coal-to-gas competitiveness). Lower prices = more gas demand = more SS-LNG.
🚛 LNG Truck SubsidiesShifting to BEVMed (−)Mar 2025: LNG trucks qualified for subsidies. But NEV subsidies for replacing old diesel → BEV. BEV = 22% of HDV. LNG and BEV both subsidized, but BEV momentum stronger.
⛏️ Domestic ProductionSurpassing TargetsHigh (+)246.4 Bcm (2024) exceeded 230 Bcm target. 259 Bcm forecast (2025). Power of Siberia at 38 Bcm full capacity. More domestic supply = cheaper feedstock for SS-LNG plants.
🗄️ Storage GapCritical RiskMed26.7 vs 55 Bcm. 6.3% vs global avg 15-20%. Winter supply risk. Storage gap creates SS-LNG demand for peak-shaving — but also highlights systemic vulnerability.

China's gas regulatory story in one sentence: China's gas sector is undergoing the most ambitious restructuring since the NOC creation in 1998 — PipeChina's midstream unbundling (2019), new infrastructure rules explicitly encouraging private LNG terminal investment (Jan 2026), and gradual price liberalization are creating a more competitive market. But the state still dominates (SOEs = 85% of capacity), and the regulatory posture is fundamentally different from the US (market-driven deregulation) and EU (climate-driven regulation). China's approach is supply-security-driven: the priorities are domestic production (exceeded targets), pipeline imports (Power of Siberia full capacity), storage (massive gap), and technology self-reliance. For SS-LNG, this regulatory environment is broadly supportive — PipeChina TPA opens downstream, Blue Sky mandates drive coal-to-gas switching, and 300+ domestic liquefaction plants demonstrate that China has already built the world's largest SS-LNG ecosystem without any Western-style regulatory framework. The emerging risk: BEV truck subsidies may erode the LNG trucking market faster than coal-to-gas mandates can sustain it.

Sources: S&P Global (Mar 2025) · S&P Global (Nov 2025) · Columbia CGEP · IEEFA
China LNG Imports (2024)
79 Mt
107 bcm. World's #1 importer. Australia 34%, Qatar 24%, Malaysia 10%, Russia 9%, US 5%. But H1 2025: −20% YoY — steepest drop since 2022.
Source: CSIS / Kpler
Pipeline Imports (2024)
71 bcm
40% of gas imports. Russia PoS-1: 38 bcm (full capacity 2025). Turkmenistan ~35 bcm. PoS-2 deal signed (Sep 2025): +50 bcm/yr via Mongolia. Far East Pipeline: 10 bcm by 2027.
Source: Carnegie / CSIS
US LNG to China
HALTED
125% tariff (Apr 2025). Zero cargoes since Nov 2024. Last US LNG carrier left for China Nov 2024. 27 Mtpa in long-term contracts → being resold globally.
Source: CSIS (Jun 2025)
H1 2025 LNG Drop
−20%
−9 Mt YoY. Spot imports −40%. NOT demand-driven (gas demand +8 bcm). Structural pivot: domestic production surging, PoS-1 at full capacity, storage 90% full in Feb. China = the swing variable in global LNG.
Source: Global LNG Hub (Jul 2025)
China Gas Supply Architecture: The Strategic Rebalancing
SUPPLIERS
🇦🇺
Australia LNG
23.5 Mt (12mo to Aug 2025). Historically #1 but slipping. NW Shelf, Gorgon, Wheatstone, Ichthys. APLNG lowered Sinopec contract price. Some projects declining. Anti-China alliance tensions → China diversifying away.
🇶🇦
Qatar LNG
Overtook Australia in 2025 (20.4 Mt full year). Sinopec 4 Mtpa × 27 yr. CNPC 4 Mtpa from NFE (mid-2026). Sinopec +7 Mtpa from NFE/NFS. Oil-indexed, long-term. Rising to #1 supplier.
🇷🇺
Russia (Pipeline + LNG)
PoS-1: 38 bcm (full capacity). PoS-2 deal signed: +50 bcm via Mongolia. Far East Pipeline: +10 bcm by 2027. LNG: 7.5 Mt (incl. Arctic LNG 2 sanctioned cargoes). If PoS-2 materializes: Russia = 27-36% of China imports.
🇹🇲
Turkmenistan (Pipeline)
~35 bcm/yr. Central Asia pipeline (55 bcm capacity). Historically #1 pipeline supplier. Line D expansion (+30 bcm) planned but delayed for years. Beijing holds negotiating upper hand on price.
🇲🇾
Malaysia + SE Asia
Malaysia: 6.5 Mt (12mo). Indonesia: 4.1 Mt. PETRONAS long-term. Short shipping routes = cost advantage. Indonesia declining. Papua LNG (TotalEnergies) = future addition.
🇺🇸
US LNG (Halted)
125% tariff. Zero cargoes since Nov 2024. Was only 5% in 2024. But 27 Mtpa in long-term contracts (VG, Cheniere, etc.) starting 2026-28. Being resold to Europe/Asia. May never land in China if tariffs persist.

2025: structural rebalancing, not demand collapse. China's H1 2025 LNG import drop (−20%) was NOT because gas demand fell — demand actually rose ~8 bcm. The drop was caused by: (1) domestic production surging (+6%), (2) PoS-1 at full 38 bcm, (3) storage 90% full in Feb, (4) spot LNG prices >$10/MMBtu (Chinese buyers disappear above $10). Chinese NOCs resold contracted US LNG volumes to Europe/Asia, demonstrating growing trading sophistication. 44 Mt of long-term contracts signed in 2021-22 (mostly US, destination-flexible) gives China optionality — they can be resold globally if domestic economics favor pipeline + domestic gas. This is the structural insight: China is becoming a swing buyer that enters the LNG market only when prices are right, and pivots to pipelines/domestic when they're not.

Sources: CSIS (Sep 2025) · Global LNG Hub · LNG Industry (Dec 2025) · IEEFA · Seala AI
China Gas Trade: Outlook & Risk Matrix
OUTLOOK
FactorDirectionImpact on LNGKey Detail
🏭 Domestic ProductionSurgingBearish LNG246→259 Bcm. Exceeded 14th FYP target. Ultra-deep shale. More domestic gas = less need for LNG imports. Structural headwind.
🇷🇺 Russian PipelineExpandingBearish LNGPoS-1 full (38 bcm). PoS-2 deal (+50 bcm). Far East (+10 bcm). If all materialize: Russia = 27-36% of imports. Pipeline gas is cheaper than LNG. Every bcm of pipeline = 1 bcm less LNG.
🇺🇸 US-China Tariff WarEscalatingMixed125% tariff. Zero US cargoes. 27 Mtpa contracted → resold. US LNG may never return to China if tariffs persist. But destination-flexible contracts give Chinese NOCs trading optionality.
🇶🇦 Qatar NFE ExpansionGrowingBullish QatarNFE start mid-2026. Sinopec + CNPC = 11+ Mtpa. Qatar overtaking Australia. Qatar = China's most strategically aligned LNG partner.
💰 Price SensitivityHighVolatileSpot imports vanish >$10/MMBtu. H1 2025: spot −40%. If LNG prices soften (2026 supply wave), China returns. China is a price-driven swing buyer, not a baseload LNG customer.
🔋 BEV + RenewablesSurgingBearish GasBEV trucks 22% of HDV. Wind/solar suppressing gas-fired power. Long-term: renewables + BEV erode gas demand in power + transport — the two sectors that could have driven LNG growth.

China gas trade story in one sentence: China is the world's largest LNG importer but is actively reducing its LNG dependency — domestic production exceeded targets (246 Bcm), Power of Siberia reached full capacity (38 bcm), a PoS-2 deal would add 50 bcm more, and US LNG is halted by a 125% tariff. The result: H1 2025 LNG imports fell 20% even as gas demand rose 8 bcm, and Chinese buyers slashed spot purchases by 40% while reselling contracted US volumes to Europe. China is evolving from a hungry LNG importer to a strategic swing buyer that enters the market only when prices are attractive (<$10/MMBtu) and pivots to pipeline/domestic when they're not. For global LNG markets, China is now the single most important variable: if China buys aggressively, markets tighten; if it pulls back (as in 2025), the global supply wave creates oversupply. Qatar is rising as China's preferred LNG partner (long-term, oil-indexed, geopolitically aligned), while US LNG may be permanently displaced by the trade war.

Sources: CSIS · Carnegie · OilPrice · IEEFA
China Gas Consumption (2024)
428 Bcm
+8.4% YoY. 2025 forecast: +6.5% (~456 Bcm). Import dependency: 40.9%. Domestic production = 60% of supply for 5th straight year. Peak forecast: ~610-700 Bcm by 2035-40.
Source: Anadolu / NBS
Gas-Fired Power Capacity
99→144 GW
Surged in 2025. Guangdong alone +23 GW. Target: 261-308 GW by 2030 (CNPC). Power gen = key growth driver. Peaking/backup for 1,200+ GW renewables.
Source: Mysteel / GL Consulting
LNG Trucking (2025)
Peaking
1M+ fleet. 24.6% of total heavy truck mileage. But: BEV = 22-28% of HDV sales. BEV outselling LNG since Jun 2025. LNG truck share declining from 30% peak. Bridge fuel → shorter than expected.
Source: IEEFA (2025)
Industrial Gas Demand
−3% (2025)
Largest sectoral consumer but under pressure. High prices + weak real estate hit ceramics/glass. Methanol/ammonia margins poor. 2026E: +5.5% recovery on policy support.
Source: Mysteel
China Gas & SS-LNG Demand Drivers: Growing vs Peaking vs Declining
DEMAND
⚡
Power Generation
▲▲▲ SURGING
99→144 GW installed (2025). Target: 261-308 GW by 2030. Guangdong +23 GW. Peaking/backup for 1,200+ GW renewables. But: Guangdong slashed power tariffs 16%. Gas only ~4% of generation. Largest long-term growth sector.
🏠
City Gas / Residential
▲▲ GROWING
Tripled since 2014 (3.6→9.3 Bcf/d). Urbanization + coal-to-gas heating in northern provinces. +8% in 2023. But: Apr 2025 heat pump action plan may slow growth. Storage deficit (6.3%) causes winter volatility.
🚛
LNG Trucking
→ PEAKING
1M+ fleet. 4,500+ stations. 24.6% of truck mileage (+21%). But: BEV outselling LNG since Jun 2025 (22-28% of HDV sales). CATL "75#" battery swap. Goldman: battery costs −50% by 2026. Bridge was shorter than expected.
🏭
Industrial
▼ UNDER PRESSURE
Largest sectoral consumer. −3% in 2025 (high gas prices). Ceramics, glass hit by real estate slump. Methanol/ammonia margins poor. 300+ SS-LNG plants serve inland factories. 2026E: +5.5% recovery expected.
🚢
Marine Bunkering
▲▲ GROWING
Shanghai bunkering +60% (H1 2025). 5 vessels operational. Zhoushan 30K m³ shore tanks. Shenzhen STS. CIMC building vessels. Yangtze River emission control area. Small but fastest-growing SS-LNG segment.
🔋
BEV Displacement
▼ HEADWIND
BEV 22-28% of HDV sales (2025). BYD, SANY, XCMG. €58-85K. TCO 10-26% below diesel. CATL battery swap: 1/3 of BEV HDV sales. Wind/solar suppressing gas-fired power in some provinces. Long-term structural threat to gas.
Sources: Mysteel (Feb 2026) · IEEFA · IEEFA (Apr 2025) · Anadolu / NBS
China Gas Demand Outlook: Growth vs Displacement Matrix
OUTLOOK
Driver20252026E2030ESS-LNG Relevance
⚡ Power Generation▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲Indirect — 99→300 GW target. Largest long-term growth sector. But mostly grid-connected. SS-LNG for off-grid/island peaking = niche.
🏠 City Gas / Heating▲▲▲→Limited — Pipeline-delivered. Coal-to-gas slowing as targets achieved. Heat pump plan (Apr 2025) = new headwind. Not SS-LNG.
🚛 LNG Trucking→▼▼▼Core — 1M+ fleet. 4,500+ stations. 22 Mt/yr consumption. Peaking now. BEV outselling since Jun 2025. Will decline but existing fleet runs for a decade.
🏭 Industrial▼▲→Core — 300+ SS-LNG plants serve off-pipeline industry. Coal-to-gas in Blue Sky zones. But cyclical (real estate, margins). Foundation of China's SS-LNG ecosystem.
🚢 Marine Bunkering▲▲▲▲▲▲▲Core — Shanghai +60%. Zhoushan expanding. Yangtze emission control. Small today but fastest-growing. China will be a top-3 global bunkering hub by 2030.
🔋 BEV + Renewables▼ Gas▼▼ Gas▼▼▼ GasHeadwind — BEV trucks + wind/solar eroding gas demand in transport + power. Battery costs −50% by 2026. THE long-term structural threat.
📊 TOTAL CHINA GAS+6.5%+3-5%+2-3%Still growing toward 620-700 Bcm peak (2035-40). But growth rate decelerating. LNG's share of supply declining as pipeline + domestic surge.

China gas demand story in one sentence: China's total gas demand is still growing (428 Bcm → 620-700 Bcm peak by 2035-40) but the composition is shifting fundamentally: power generation (99→300 GW) is becoming the dominant growth driver, while LNG trucking (the segment that built the SS-LNG ecosystem) is peaking as BEV trucks capture 22-28% of heavy-duty sales. Industrial demand — the foundation of China's 300+ SS-LNG plant network — is cyclical and under pressure from the real estate slump. Marine bunkering is surging (Shanghai +60%) but still small. The structural insight: China's gas demand growth will be served primarily by domestic production (+6%/yr) and Russian pipelines (PoS-1 + PoS-2 + Far East), leaving LNG imports as the marginal, price-sensitive residual — not the primary growth vehicle. For SS-LNG, the trucking fleet (1M+ vehicles, 22 Mt consumption) will generate demand for a decade even as new sales decline, but the growth story is pivoting from road transport to marine bunkering and industrial satellite supply.

Sources: Mysteel (Feb 2026) · IEEFA · Energy Intelligence · Anadolu
China SS-LNG Demand (2026E)
139.2 MMm³/d
50.8 bcm/yr. +13.2% CAGR (2023–26). HD trucking = 67% (declining share). Marine bunkering = fastest-growing (+59%). ~10× US + EU combined.
Source: Dashboard bottom-up model
China SS-LNG Supply (2026E)
138.4 MMm³/d
50.5 bcm/yr. +21% CAGR. Domestic plants (300+) = 80% of supply. Import terminal truck-loading = 20%. Capacity +41% in 2024 alone. Converging toward balance.
Source: Dashboard bottom-up model
Utilization Trend
123%→101%
2023: severe deficit (123%). 2024: deficit narrowing (108%). 2026E: near-balance (101%). Supply response (+41% capacity) closing gap just as BEV slows demand.
Source: Dashboard bottom-up model
China = World's Largest
~10× US+EU
China SS-LNG (50.8 bcm) = ~10× US (2.04 bcm) + EU (5.29 bcm) combined. HD trucking alone (34 bcm) exceeds France's total gas consumption. Self-contained ecosystem.
Source: Dashboard models
China SS-LNG Market: Bottom-Up Supply & Demand Model
MODEL
Methodology
Bottom-up from segment-level demand and feedstock-level supply. Scope = all domestic SS-LNG: trucking, industrial satellite, city gas virtual pipeline, peak shaving, bunkering, mining. A = actual · P = preliminary · E = estimate. China = world's largest SS-LNG market by far (~80% of APAC, ~10× US + EU combined). Total gas consumption: 428 bcm (2024). Domestic LNG production: 25 Mt.
China vs US vs EU
China SS-LNG (50.8 bcm) = 10× US (2.04 bcm) + EU (5.29 bcm). US is diversified (DC, marine, trucking, aerospace). EU is maritime-focused (bunkering + bio-LNG). China is trucking-dominated (67% of demand). This concentration is both its strength (massive scale) and vulnerability (BEV displacement risk). Unlike US/EU, China's SS-LNG is self-contained: domestic plants feed domestic demand.
Key 2025–26 Events
BEV outselling LNG trucks since Jun 2025 (22-28% of HDV sales). CATL "75#" battery swap standard. Gas-fired power 99→144 GW. Shanghai bunkering +60%. PoS-1 at full 38 bcm. 125% US tariff → LNG imports −20% H1 2025. Domestic gas production 246→259 Bcm. New NDRC infra rules (Jan 2026). Storage gap: 26.7 vs 55 Bcm target.

⚠ HD Trucking Peak Risk (THE risk)
If BEV HDV reaches 30% by 2027 (Q1 2025: 20%), LNG fleet peaks at ~1.1M vehicles. Caps SS-LNG demand at 52-55 bcm then erodes. Wood Mackenzie + IEEFA flag as likely. If China trucking peaks, the entire global SS-LNG growth narrative changes.
⚠ COG Feedstock (Steel Dependency)
Cheapest feedstock (waste product). But depends on steelmaking trajectory. Steel demand plateau → coke plateau → COG supply ceiling. If China steel peaks (mid-2020s expected), COG-LNG faces feedstock constraint.
✓ PNG Plants (Most Reliable)
Pipeline gas = most predictable. Domestic production on 5-6% growth. PoS-1 at 38 bcm adds cheap feedstock. NW China economics well-understood (¥2,800-3,500/t). ~200+ plants, 65% of domestic production.
📊 Import Terminal Truck-Loading
29 terminals, 20% of SS-LNG supply. Well-documented (EIA). Critical for coastal provinces far from NW production centers. Each terminal: 50-200 truck loads/day. Growing with new terminals.
Scenario2026E Demand2026E SupplyUtilizationWhat Drives It
🟢 Base Case139.2 MMm³/d138.4 MMm³/d101%HD trucking plateaus. BEV captures ~25% of new HDV. Marine + industrial grow. 20 new plants commissioned. Near-balance.
🔵 Bull Case150.7 MMm³/d145.2 MMm³/d104%Diesel-LNG spread widens again. Truck sales rebound. Industrial recovery stronger. Marine bunkering surges. Supply can't keep up → deficit returns.
🔴 Bear (BEV Acceleration)126.0 MMm³/d138.4 MMm³/d91%BEV reaches 35% of HDV. LNG truck sales collapse −30%. CATL battery swap goes mainstream. Industrial weak. China SS-LNG demand peaks in 2025 and structurally declines.

China SS-LNG S&D model in one sentence: China built the world's largest SS-LNG ecosystem in just 5 years — 300+ domestic liquefaction plants, 4,500+ stations, 1M+ LNG trucks, 25 Mt domestic production — then its own BEV truck industry began to overtake it. The market was severely supply-constrained in 2023 (123% utilization) and producers responded at unprecedented speed (+41% capacity in 2024). By 2026, supply and demand converge at ~50 bcm/yr (near-balance at 101%) — just as BEV trucks structurally slow demand growth. HD trucking (67% of demand) is THE variable that determines whether China SS-LNG peaks in 2026-28 or continues growing. If BEV acceleration is faster than expected (bear case: 35% of HDV by 2027), China SS-LNG demand could peak in 2025 and decline, creating overcapacity in the 300+ plant network. The offsetting growth segments — marine bunkering (Shanghai +60%), industrial coal-to-gas, and mining — are growing fast but cannot replace the scale of trucking if it declines. This is not just a China story: if the world's largest SS-LNG market peaks, the entire global growth narrative must be recalibrated.

Sources: IEEFA (May 2025) · IEEFA China (Apr 2025) · Columbia CGEP · Mysteel/OilChem · EIA China

Brazil LNG Market

Latin America's largest LNG importer and one of the world's fastest-growing regas markets. Brazil more than doubled its LNG import capacity since 2020, driven by an integrated LNG-to-power strategy to back up a hydro-dependent grid. The Novo Mercado de Gás reform is transforming a historically Petrobras-dominated sector into an open, competitive market.

Regas Capacity (Aug 2025)
144.4 Mm³/d
More than doubled from 70.8 Mm³/d (2020) · 9 terminals active

$24.4B
Forecast: $51.8B by 2034 · CAGR 8.74%

152.9 Mm³/d
85% offshore (pre-salt) · 54% reinjected for pressure
Source: EIA

72%

Regas Capacity (Aug 2025)
144.4 MMm³/d
5.1 Bcf/d. Doubled since 2020 (2.5 Bcf/d). 9 FSRUs operational. +3 terminals in 2024 alone (Barcarena, Gás Sul, Cosan). Suape 2026. 10th by early 2026.
Source: EIA (Oct 2025)
Petrobras Production
152.9 Mm³/d
2024. Pre-salt = 80%. But 54% reinjected. $98.2B capex (2025-29). Rota 3 + Boaventura online. Centrica SPA: 0.8 Mtpa × 15 yr from 2027. First CCS pilot.
Source: Petrobras / EnkiAI
Private Terminal Wave
6 of 9
Private-operated FSRUs (GNA, Eneva, NFE, Gás Sul, Compass, Excelerate). Before 2019: only 3 terminals (all Petrobras). Gas-to-Power model: each FSRU paired with GW-scale power plant.
Source: Argus Media
NE Gas Price Drop
−15%
In regions with competition, gas prices fell ~15%. Petrobras forced to adapt pricing to avoid losing customers. "Brazil Cost" of gas (10× US) is finally being challenged by private LNG entry.
Source: Click P&G
Brazil LNG Key Player Landscape
PLAYERS
🛢️
Petrobras
B3: PETR3/4
152.9 Mm³/d production. Guanabara + Pecém FSRUs. $98.2B capex (2025-29). Pre-salt = 80%. Rota 3 online. Centrica SPA: 0.8 Mtpa × 15 yr. First CCS pilot. Still the house — but losing monopoly.
⚡
GNA (Prumo/BP)
LNG-to-Power
Porto do Açu: 3 GW gas-fired complex. GNA I + GNA II (started May 2025). 21 Mm³/d FSRU. First private LNG terminal in Brazil (2021). South America's largest LNG-to-power. Connecting to national grid.
🏭
Eneva
B3: ENEV3
Sergipe FSRU + 1.5 GW power plant. Azulão SS-LNG (Amazonas → Manaus). Parnaíba gas fields + thermals. Integrated gas-power player. Unique model: own fields + own plants + SS-LNG trucking.
🚢
New Fortress Energy
NASDAQ: NFE
Barcarena FSRU (21.2 Mm³/d, 2024). 15-yr Norsk Hydro Alunorte contract. 2.2 GW power station. Gás Sul (SC). Aggressive entry: North + South. NFE's largest LatAm asset. Modular, fast-deploy model.
🏠
Compass (Cosan/Mitsui)
Distribution / LDC
Acquired Petrobras' Gaspetro → Commit. Cosan terminal (14.2 Mm³/d, SP). TRSP Santos: SS-LNG loading center. Brazil's largest private gas distributor. Pioneer in gas interiorização via SS-LNG logistics.
🔬
GNLink + SS-LNG Players
Small-Scale LNG
GNLink: 3 plants (PR + BA + RN), 264K m³/d, R$450M invested, Copa Energia strategic investor. Plans 15 projects. GásLocal (White Martins/Linde): legacy distributor. Edge: entered 2025. Alpha LNG (AL). SS-LNG market finally has competition.

Brazil's "silent LNG revolution": Before 2019, Brazil had only 3 LNG terminals (all Petrobras). Today: 9 operational + 1 under construction (Suape 2026). Private players (GNA, Eneva, NFE, Compass, Excelerate) now operate 6 of 9. Each FSRU is paired with a GW-scale gas-fired power plant ("Gas-to-Power" model). Excess gas is sold to industry — creating a new competitive supply source that's already driving NE gas prices down ~15%. The Novo Mercado de Gás reform + Eletrobras privatization law (14,182/2021) mandated 8 GW of new gas-fired capacity, which anchored the terminal investment wave. For SS-LNG specifically, GNLink (3 plants, 264K m³/d) and Eneva's Azulão (Amazonas) are the pioneering domestic liquefaction plays, while Compass TRSP Santos and NFE Barcarena provide truck-loading from import terminals. Brazil's SS-LNG market is in its infancy but growing fast.

Sources: EIA (Oct 2025) · Click P&G · Argus Media · ICLG Brazil O&G 2026
🔌
LNG Regasification Terminals
Brazil FSRU & LNG Import Terminals
TERMINALS
TerminalOperatorCapacityStatusKey Notes
Guanabara Bay FSRUPetrobras22.7 Mm³/dOperationalRio de Janeiro. Online 2009. Petrobras' flagship terminal. Largest capacity among legacy terminals.
Pecém FSRUPetrobras~10.2 Mm³/dOperationalCeará (Northeast). Brazil's first LNG terminal (2008). Seasonal/drought-response operation.
Porto do Açu FSRUGNA (Prumo/BP)21.0 Mm³/dOperationalRio de Janeiro state. Online 2021. First private-use terminal in Brazil. Feeds 3 GW gas-fired power complex (GNA I online, GNA II started May 2025).
Sergipe FSRUEneva (ex-Celse)21.0 Mm³/dOperationalSergipe (Northeast). Online 2019. Feeds 1.5 GW power plant. Pipeline to grid under construction. Acquired by Eneva.
Bahia FSRUExcelerate Energy20.1 Mm³/dOperationalBahia. Online 2014, leased by Excelerate from Petrobras after 2021. Serves Bahia industrial corridor.
Sepetiba Bay FSRU—10.2 Mm³/dOperationalRio de Janeiro. Complements Guanabara Bay. Adds supply flexibility to SE region.
Barcarena FSRUNew Fortress Energy21.2 Mm³/dNew 2024Pará (Amazon/North). NFE's flagship Brazil asset. 15-yr contract with Norsk Hydro Alunorte (world's largest alumina refinery). Feeds 2.2 GW Novo Tempo Barcarena power station (CELBA 2 early ops Oct 2024).
Terminal Gás Sul FSRU—14.2 Mm³/dNew 2024Southern Brazil. Expands gas access to an underserved region. Added capacity in 2024 wave.
Cosan TerminalCompass Gás & Energia14.2 Mm³/dNew 2024São Paulo state. Compass is Cosan + Mitsui Gas JV (acquired Petrobras' Gaspetro, rebranded Commit). Major LDC player.
Suape FSRU—19.8 Mm³/d2026Pernambuco (Northeast). Under development. Completion early 2026. Will further strengthen NE supply.
Sources: EIA (Oct 2025) · Argus Media · ITA

Key Players in Brazil LNG & Gas Market
Companies Driving Brazil's LNG Expansion
PLAYERS
CompanyRoleKey Data
Petrobras
B3: PETR3/4		Incumbent Producer152.9 Mm³/d production (2024). Operates Guanabara + Pecém FSRUs. $98.2B capex plan (2025–29). Rota 3 + Boaventura (21M m³/day) online 2024. Pre-salt = 80% of output. Centrica SPA: 0.8 MTPA, 15 yrs from 2027. First CCS pilot (100K tCO₂/yr).
Eneva
B3: ENEV3		Gas-to-PowerAcquired Sergipe FSRU + 1.5 GW power plant from Celse. Also operates onshore gas fields + thermal plants in Maranhão and Amazonas. Emerging as integrated gas-power player post-Novo Mercado.
GNA (Prumo/BP)LNG-to-PowerPorto do Açu: 3 GW gas-fired complex (GNA I + GNA II, started May 2025). 21.0 Mm³/d FSRU. First private LNG terminal in Brazil. Pipeline to national grid under planning. Açu also serves as industrial port hub.
New Fortress Energy
NASDAQ: NFE		SS-LNG / FSRUBarcarena FSRU (21.2 Mm³/d, 2024). 15-yr contract with Norsk Hydro Alunorte. Building 2.2 GW power station. NFE's largest single asset in LatAm. Also sold Miami SS-LNG facility (Jul 2024) to focus on downstream.
Compass Gás & Energia
(Cosan + Mitsui)		Distribution / LDCAcquired Petrobras' Gaspetro (Jul 2022), rebranded Commit. Cosan terminal (14.2 Mm³/d, 2024) in São Paulo. Brazil's largest private gas distributor. Expanding grid and seeking new LNG sources.
Excelerate Energy
NYSE: EE		FSRU OperatorOperates Bahia FSRU (20.1 Mm³/d) leased from Petrobras. Also operates Wilhelmshaven 2 in Germany. 10 FSRUs globally. Proven model of rapid FSRU deployment for emerging markets.
Shell / TotalEnergies / EquinorIOC PresenceAll hold pre-salt production shares. Shell Brasil, TotalEnergies, and Equinor are significant upstream producers. TotalEnergies signed 2 MTPA × 15-yr LNG SPA with Sinopec (for China, not Brazil) sourced partly from Brazilian-linked assets.
Sources: ITA · EIA · Argus Media · IMARC · ICLG
Brazil LNG Infrastructure: FSRUs, SS-LNG Plants & Truck-Loading Facilities
INFRASTRUCTURE

Brazil's LNG infrastructure has been transformed since 2020. Regas capacity doubled from 2.5 to 144.4 MMm³/d. Prior to 2020, only 3 Petrobras terminals existed. Today, 4 major private players (GNA, NFE, Compass, Eneva) have reshaped the landscape. Most are anchored by Gas-to-Power projects: a large thermoelectric plant guarantees demand, and excess gas is sold to industry.

#FacilityLocationOperatorCapacityType & SS-LNG Role
LNG IMPORT TERMINALS (FSRUs) — 9 operational, 1 under construction
1Guanabara BayRJPetrobras22.7 MMm³/dPetrobras legacy terminal (2009). FSRU. Rio de Janeiro metro supply. No dedicated SS-LNG truck-loading.
2PecémCEPetrobrasFlexiblePetrobras legacy terminal. Feeds Ceará + Fortaleza thermal plants. NE supply anchor.
3Bahia (TR-BA)BAExcelerate (Energos Sequoia)20.1 MMm³/dFSRU. Commenced Dec 2021. 173,400 m³ storage. Salvador/Bahia region. Camaçari industrial complex supply.
4Porto de SergipeSEEneva21.0 MMm³/dFSRU. Operational since 2020. Feeds 1.6 GW Porto de Sergipe I power station (largest CCGT in LatAm). Connected to NE pipeline (since Oct 2024). Now also on-grid gas trading.
5Porto do AçuRJGNA / bp21.0 MMm³/dFSRU (BW Magna, 21 Mm³/d). Feeds GNA I (1.3 GW) + GNA II (1.7 GW, operational May 2025). SS-LNG hub: bp structuring truck-loading for regional industrial distribution.
6Sepetiba BayRJNFE10.2 MMm³/dFSRU. Operational since 2022. Smaller capacity. Rio de Janeiro state supply.
7BarcarenaPANFE21.2 MMm³/dFSRU. Operational Q1 2024. Feeds 2.2 GW Novo Tempo Barcarena power station (under construction). SS-LNG anchor for Amazon/North: truck-loading for Norsk Hydro Alunorte (long-term industrial contract) + regional mining.
8Terminal Gás SulSCNFE14.2 MMm³/dFSRU. Operational Q1 2024. Southern Brazil. Industrial supply for Santa Catarina.
9TRSP Santos (Cosan)SPCompass / Cosan14.2 MMm³/dFSRU Höegh Giant (14 Mm³/d regas). Operational Apr 2024. Key SS-LNG hub: building truck-loading center at Port of Santos for SS-LNG distribution into SP interior. Pioneer of gas interiorization strategy via virtual pipeline. Feeds Comgás network + prospecting industrial B2B clients.
10SuapePEUnder development19.8 MMm³/dUnder construction. Expected early 2026. Pernambuco. Will add NE supply capacity. SS-LNG potential: truck-loading for NE LDC virtual pipeline projects (Copergás, Algás, Cegás).
DOMESTIC SS-LNG LIQUEFACTION PLANTS
11AzulãoAM (Silves)Eneva490 tpd
(~0.6 Mm³/d)		Brazil's only large SS-LNG liquefaction plant. Azulão gas field → liquefaction → 33 Galileo Cryoboxes → 1,100 km truck to Boa Vista (RR) → Jaguatirica II (126 MW). Reservoir-to-Wire model. Replaced Roraima's 100% diesel dependency. 2 liquefaction trains (2nd launched Dec 2024).
12Parnaíba SS-LNGMAEneva / Virtu GNL~600K m³/yrSmall liquefaction at Parnaíba complex (Santo Antônio dos Lopes, MA). 2nd train operational Feb 2025. Full commercial operation. Feeds NE industrial + virtual pipeline projects.
13GNLink Barra BonitaPRGNLink (Lorinvest)44K m³/d (Phase 1)
→ 88K m³/d (Jan 2026)		ANP operational authorization Nov 2024. Phase 1: 44K m³/d. Gas from Barra Bonita field (Tradener onshore). Galileo Technologies equipment. Feeds Compagas Londrina-Maringá virtual pipeline. Phase 2 doubles capacity from Jan 2026. 10-year transport contract with Contatto (carbon-zero certificates).
14GNLink ItabunaBAGNLink + Petrobahia + Bahiagás98K m³/d
(88K GNL + 10K GNC)		Operational. R$155M investment. PPP signed with Governor of Bahia (Oct 2023). Gas purchased from Bahiagás pipeline → liquefied + compressed → trucked by Petrobahia/Transbahia up to 1,000 km into Bahia interior (Vitória da Conquista, Juazeiro, Eunápolis, Porto Seguro). First NE plant liquefying gas from pipeline. Industrial + GNV markets. Bahiagás chamada pública for 10 interior cities.
15GNLink Rio Grande do NorteRNGNLink (Lorinvest)~98K m³/dANP authorization received (Feb 2026). Construction completed H2 2025. Third GNLink plant, replicating Itabuna model. Gas from NE onshore field. Potigás concession area. All 3 GNLink plants = 264K m³/d combined. R$450M total investment. Copa Energia entered GNLink's capital to finance expansion. Plans to double capacity by 2027 (new plants in SE + South).
16Alpha LNG AlagoasALOrigem Energia / Alpha LNG30K m³/dAuthorized. Small-scale. Alagoas industrial supply. Earliest stage of the domestic producers.
ESTABLISHED SS-LNG DISTRIBUTORS
17GásLocal FleetNationalWhite Martins / Linde~10–15 trailersBrazil's original SS-LNG distributor (since early 2000s). Cryogenic trailer fleet. Delivers LNG to industrial customers across SE/S Brazil. Now facing competition from Compass, GNLink, NFE, and Edge (new entrant 2025). Market position eroding as new players enter.

Total Brazilian LNG regas capacity: 144.4 MMm³/d (Aug 2025). Doubled since 2020. 9 FSRUs operational + Suape under construction = 10 by early 2026. SS-LNG supply sources: Domestic liquefaction (Eneva Azulão + Parnaíba, GNLink ×3 plants = 264K m³/d, Alpha LNG) + import terminal truck-loading (TRSP Santos, Porto do Açu, Barcarena, Suape) + GásLocal fleet. GNLink milestone: 3 plants operational/authorized (PR + BA + RN), 264K m³/d combined, R$450M invested, Copa Energia as strategic investor. Plans to double by 2027. New competitors: Edge (entered 2025), GNL Brasil (Eneva/Virtu GNL). SS-LNG distribution is gaining traction in Brazil in 2025 — the market is no longer GásLocal alone.

Sources: EIA (Oct 2025) · LNG Prime (GNLink) · LNG Prime (Eneva) · Click P&G · Eneva · GEM LatAm
Brazil LNG/CNG Fueling & Gas Distribution Infrastructure
STATIONS
InfrastructureCount / ScaleKey Details
LNG ROAD FUELING — Near Zero (no dedicated LNG truck stations)
LNG Truck Stations0Brazil has zero dedicated LNG truck refueling stations. Unlike China (4,500+), Europe (800+), or US (81), Brazil has no LNG trucking ecosystem. No Cummins X15N equivalent engine available in Brazilian market. Scania + Compagas CNG/GNV pilot in Paraná (19% CO₂ reduction, 10% fuel savings) = closest analog but uses CNG not LNG. Brazilian trucking is 100% diesel. LNG trucking may never develop — Brazil could leapfrog from diesel directly to BEV (TRATON e-Delivery produced in Brazil since 2021).
CNG (GNV) STATIONS — Established urban network
CNG (GNV) Stations~1,900+Brazil has one of the world's largest CNG vehicle fleets (~1.8M GNV vehicles, 5th globally). ~1,900 GNV stations concentrated in RJ (largest), SP, MG, CE, PE. CNG used primarily for taxis + ride-hailing (Uber) + light commercial vehicles — NOT for HD trucking. GNV competes with ethanol + gasoline, not diesel. Petrobras, Comgás, CEG (Naturgy) = major suppliers.
Top States (GNV)RJ ~650 · SP ~350 · MG ~180 · CE ~120 · PE ~100Rio de Janeiro = GNV capital of Brazil (largest fleet + station network). São Paulo growing as Comgás expands piped gas. NE states: CNG supplied from piped gas (Petrobras/Bahiagás/Copergás). GNV = urban phenomenon, not interurban.
SS-LNG TRUCK-LOADING & VIRTUAL PIPELINE DISTRIBUTION
SS-LNG Truck-Loading Points3–4 active (2025)GásLocal: fleet of cryogenic trailers, loads from Petrobras terminals/pipelines. TRSP Santos (Compass): building SS-LNG loading center at Port of Santos — trucks loaded directly from FSRU terminal. NFE Barcarena: truck-loading for Norsk Hydro + regional mining. Porto do Açu (bp): structuring truck-loading. By 2026E: 4–5 terminals with active SS-LNG truck-loading bays.
LDC Virtual Pipeline Projects5–8 in structuringCopergás/Petrolina (PE): 40K m³/d, 40 km local network (operational). Compagas/Londrina-Maringá (PR): starting 2025, GNLink supply, R$2.5B 30-year concession. GNLink: 15 projects mapped (4 Mm³/d target over 10 years). Compass TRSP → SP interior structuring. NE LDCs (Algás, Cegás, Potigás, Sergas) → Infra Gás consortium. Each project: LNG trucked to city → satellite regas → local pipe network → residential + commercial + industrial customers.
MARINE BUNKERING — Zero infrastructure
LNG Bunkering0 portsNo LNG bunkering at any Brazilian port. Santos (world's largest sugar + coffee port), Paranaguá, Rio Grande, Itajaí — none offer LNG bunkering. Nimofast Brasilia signed partnership with Norway's KanferShipping (Nov 2022) for small/medium-scale LNG shipping + bunkering solutions, but no operational infrastructure yet. Brazil is not on major deep-sea LNG bunkering routes (unlike Singapore, Rotterdam, Shanghai).
BIOMETANO (BIO-LNG / BIO-CNG) — Emerging
Biometano Plants~20+ operationalCompass/Orizon: Paulínia biomethane from landfill (180–300K m³/d from 2025). Compagas: 14 proposals received in 2nd chamada pública, target 100K m³/d (15% of supply) by 2026. GNLink/Migratio JV: biometano (Paraná). ANP Res. 971/2024 + 973/2024: regulate LNG/CNG handling + transport. Brazil = world's largest sugarcane + livestock producer → enormous biogas potential. SABESP (SP water utility): biogas from sewage → biomethane. Raízen: sugarcane vinasse → biogas at mills. Growing fast from near zero.

Brazil vs other regions: Brazil's SS-LNG infrastructure is fundamentally different from China, Europe, or the US. Brazil has zero LNG truck stations (vs China 4,500+) and zero LNG bunkering (vs Singapore 460K tonnes). Instead, Brazil's SS-LNG is built around: (1) Gas-to-Power (FSRU → power plant), (2) Virtual Pipeline (SS-LNG trucked to unserved cities → local pipe distribution), and (3) Industrial off-pipeline (LNG truck to factory). The CNG/GNV network (1,900+ stations) is large but entirely urban/light-vehicle — it doesn't serve the same market as LNG. Brazil's SS-LNG future is in gas interiorization (taking gas to 90% of municipalities that have none), not in LNG trucking or bunkering.

Sources: EIA (Oct 2025) · ABEGÁS · epbr · ANP · Compagas · Copergás · Compass
New Gas Law
14,134/2021
Novo Mercado de Gás. Open, competitive market. Unbundling, TPA, entry-exit system, transparency. Replaced concession model with authorization regime. IEA advised since 2018. Ministry: gas prices could fall 40%.
Source: IEA
LNG-to-Power Mandate
8 GW
Law 14,182/2021 (Eletrobras privatization). Required 8 GW of new regionalized gas-fired capacity. Each FSRU paired with GW-scale plant. Anchored the private terminal investment wave.
Source: EIA (Oct 2025)
Hydro Dependency
56% Hydro
80% renewables (hydro 56% + wind/solar 24%). Reservoirs fell to 29% in 2024 drought. LNG = flexible thermal backup. 2021 drought → record 6.3 MMT LNG imports. LNG demand = f(rainfall).
Source: ITA / EPE
Fuel of the Future Law
14,993/2024
CCS regulatory framework + SAF mandates. Blue hydrogen (NG + CCUS) = transition vector. Petrobras first CCS pilot (100K tCO₂/yr). ANP Res. 971 + 973/2024: regulate LNG/CNG handling + transport.
Source: ICLG Brazil O&G 2026
Brazil Gas Regulatory Framework: The Novo Mercado Revolution
REGULATION
⚖️
Novo Mercado de Gás
Law 14,134/2021. Open, competitive market. 4 pillars: unbundling, TPA, entry-exit, transparency. Authorization regime (not concession). ANP regulates. Ministry estimate: gas prices could fall 40%, industrial GDP +8.4%. Most ambitious gas reform in LatAm.
🏗️
8 GW LNG-to-Power
Law 14,182/2021 (Eletrobras privatization). Mandated 8 GW of new regionalized gas-fired capacity. Each FSRU anchored by GW-scale power plant: Açu 3 GW, Barcarena 2.2 GW, Sergipe 1.5 GW. Created the business case for 6 private FSRUs.
🔓
Petrobras Divestment
Sold NTS, TAG pipeline companies. Sold Gaspetro → Compass/Mitsui (rebranded Commit). CADE agreement to reduce market power. Still operates 2 legacy FSRUs. From monopoly to competition in 5 years.
🔧
ANP SS-LNG Regulation
Res. 971/2024 + 973/2024: regulate LNG/CNG handling, transport, and distribution. LNG terminals = essential infra, not subject to state distribution monopoly. ANP authorization (not concession). TPA regulated for non-discrimination. Market-based LNG pricing.
💧
Hydro Backup Role
80% renewables (hydro 56%). Reservoirs fell to 29% (2024 drought). LNG = flexible thermal backup. 2021 drought: 6.3 MMT LNG (record). Rota 3 + Boaventura: 21M m³/d pre-salt gas processing (online late 2024). GASFOR II (NE network) completed 2025.
🌱
Fuel of the Future + CCS
Law 14,993/2024: CCS framework + SAF mandates. Blue hydrogen (NG + CCUS) recognized. Petrobras first CCS pilot (100K tCO₂/yr). Biometano: Compass/Orizon Paulínia (landfill), Compagas 15% target. Brazil = world's largest sugarcane + livestock → enormous biogas potential.
Sources: IEA · ICLG Brazil O&G 2026 · EIA (Oct 2025) · ITA
Brazil Gas Regulatory Outlook: What to Watch
OUTLOOK
AreaDirectionImpact on SS-LNGKey Detail
⚖️ Novo Mercado ImplementationLiberalizingHigh (+)TPA + unbundling creating space for GNLink, Compass, Edge. NE gas prices already −15%. More players = more SS-LNG distribution competition. THE enabler for Brazilian SS-LNG.
🏗️ 8 GW Gas-to-PowerExecutingHigh (+)GNA II started May 2025. Barcarena CELBA 2 started Oct 2024. Suape 2026. Each FSRU creates SS-LNG truck-loading point as surplus capacity is available. Gas-to-Power = SS-LNG's anchor.
🔓 Petrobras RoleReducingMed (+)Divested midstream. Still 2 FSRUs. Centrica SPA shows shift to long-term contracts. But still dominates upstream (80% production). Less Petrobras dominance = more room for SS-LNG players.
💧 Hydro/Climate RiskIncreasingMed (+)Climate change → more droughts → more thermal dispatch → more LNG demand. 2021 drought: 6.3 MMT record. Reservoirs 29% (2024). Climate risk = structural LNG demand driver.
🇧🇴 Bolivia DeclineDecliningMed (+)Bolivia gas → net importer by 2030. GASBOL declining. Gap filled by LNG + pre-salt. Every bcm of Bolivian decline = 1 bcm of additional LNG opportunity.
🌱 Biometano / CCSEmergingLow (+)Enormous biogas potential (sugarcane, livestock). Compagas 15% bio target. Law 14,993/2024 CCS framework. Early stage but could be transformative long-term.

Brazil regulatory story in one sentence: Brazil is executing the most ambitious gas market reform in Latin America — the Novo Mercado de Gás (Law 14,134/2021) broke Petrobras' monopoly, the Eletrobras privatization law mandated 8 GW of new gas-fired capacity (anchoring 6 private FSRUs), and ANP Res. 971+973/2024 formalized SS-LNG regulation — creating a competitive market where gas prices are already falling 15% in regions with multiple suppliers. For SS-LNG specifically, Brazil's regulatory framework is uniquely supportive: LNG terminals are essential infrastructure exempt from the state distribution monopoly, pricing is market-based, and the Gas-to-Power model ensures each new FSRU has both a power plant anchor AND surplus capacity available for truck-loading and virtual pipeline distribution. The structural tailwind: Brazil's 80% renewable grid means LNG serves as a flexible backup for hydropower droughts — and climate change is making droughts more frequent and severe. Bolivia's declining production creates additional LNG demand. The combination of market reform + climate risk + Bolivia gap + Petrobras divestment makes Brazil the most attractive regulatory environment for SS-LNG development in the emerging world.

Sources: IEA · IEA White Paper · ICLG 2026 · IMARC Group
US LNG Share (2024)
72%
US = Brazil's dominant LNG supplier. 2.22 Mt in 2024 (nearly tripled from 0.8 Mt in 2023). Spot-dominated but shifting to long-term. Pecém DES: $10.15/MMBtu (Oct 2025).
Source: NGI / Kpler
Domestic Production
5.4 Bcf/d
Record 2024. 85% offshore (pre-salt Santos Basin). But 54% reinjected for pressure maintenance. Net available: ~47.7 Mm³/d. Rota 3 + Boaventura: +21M m³/d processing (late 2024).
Source: EIA (Oct 2025)
Bolivia Decline
61→30 MMcmd
Bolivian gas output collapsed from 61 MMcmd (2014) to <30 MMcmd (2025). GASBOL = 25% of Brazil supply. Gap being filled by LNG + Argentina Vaca Muerta (first Petrobras import Oct 2025).
Source: Oil & Gas Journal
LNG Import Variability
0.6→6.3 Mt
Extreme swing: 6.3 Mt (2021 drought) → 0.6 Mt (2023 full reservoirs) → ~2.2 Mt (2024). Demand = f(rainfall). Shifting from pure spot to long-term (Centrica 0.8 Mtpa × 15 yr from 2027).
Source: EIA
Brazil Gas Supply Architecture: LNG + Pipeline + Domestic
TRADE
🇺🇸
US LNG (72%)
Dominant supplier. 2.22 Mt (2024). Nearly tripled from 2023. Hub-indexed + spot. Petrobras in talks with US suppliers for additional long-term deals. Pecém DES: $10.15/MMBtu (Oct 2025) — below Europe's TTF. Brazil = growing US LNG market.
🇧🇴
Bolivia (GASBOL)
~25% of Brazil supply via GASBOL pipeline. But production collapsed: 61→<30 MMcmd (2014-2025). Bolivia may become net gas importer by 2030. Structural decline = permanent. Gap filled by LNG + Argentina.
🇦🇷
Argentina (Vaca Muerta)
Petrobras made first import via GASBOL reverse flow (Oct 2025). Gasoducto Norte reversal project (2024-25). New pipeline via Uruguay planned. "Real possibility" of deal — Tolmasquim. Could reshape South American gas flows.
🛢️
Domestic Pre-Salt
5.4 Bcf/d (2024 record). 85% offshore. Pre-salt = 80% of Petrobras output. But 54% reinjected. Net available: ~47.7 Mm³/d = 77% of supply. Rota 3 + Boaventura: +21M m³/d processing. Equatorial Margin + Pelotas Basin = exploration frontier.
📊
Spot → Long-Term Shift
Centrica-Petrobras: 0.8 Mtpa × 15 yr (from 2027). Brazil's first long-term LNG SPA. NFE-Norsk Hydro: 15-yr industrial contract (Barcarena). Petrobras: "We are moving to the long-term market." Spot still dominant but transition underway.
💧
Hydro-Driven Volatility
LNG imports swing 10×: 6.3 Mt (2021 drought) → 0.6 Mt (2023 rain). Brazil consumes 65-70 MMcmd. Demand = function of reservoir levels. Climate change → more extreme droughts → structurally higher LNG baseline.

Brazil gas trade story in one sentence: Brazil's gas supply is a three-legged stool — domestic pre-salt production (77% of supply but 54% reinjected), declining Bolivian pipeline gas (25% but collapsing from 61→<30 MMcmd), and LNG imports (variable, US 72%, spot-dominated) — and each leg is shifting simultaneously. Domestic production is growing (Rota 3 online) but reinjection limits net availability; Bolivia is in structural decline and may flip to net importer; LNG imports are tripling as 9 FSRUs create absorptive capacity; and Argentina's Vaca Muerta is emerging as a fourth leg via GASBOL reverse flow. The Centrica SPA (0.8 Mtpa × 15 yr, from 2027) marks Brazil's shift from pure spot buying to long-term contracting — a maturation signal for the market. For SS-LNG, the trade dynamics matter because: every new FSRU creates a truck-loading point for virtual pipeline distribution, and cheaper LNG (spot below $10.15/MMBtu) improves the economics of SS-LNG distribution to unserved municipalities.

Sources: NGI (Oct 2025) · EIA · Oil & Gas Journal · Brazil Energy Insight · ITA
Brazil Gas Trade Outlook: Key Factors
OUTLOOK
FactorDirectionImpact on LNGKey Detail
🇺🇸 US LNG SupplyGrowingBullish72% of Brazil imports. Tripled 2023→2024. Supply wave (Plaquemines, Golden Pass) will push more cargoes to LatAm. Short Atlantic route = cost advantage vs Asia.
🇧🇴 Bolivia DeclineCollapsingBullish LNG61→<30 MMcmd. Net importer by 2030. Every bcm of Bolivian decline = 1 bcm of LNG + Argentina opportunity. Structural, irreversible.
🇦🇷 Argentina Vaca MuertaEmergingBearish LNGPetrobras first import Oct 2025 via GASBOL reverse flow. New pipeline via Uruguay planned. If scalable, reduces LNG dependence. But: Bolivia tolling fees + pipeline capacity constraints.
🛢️ Domestic Pre-SaltGrowingBearish LNGRota 3 + Boaventura: +21M m³/d. But reinjection keeps net availability limited. Equatorial Margin = frontier. More processing capacity = less LNG needed for baseload.
💧 Hydro/Climate RiskIncreasingBullish LNG2021: 6.3 Mt record. 2024: reservoirs 29%. Climate change = more droughts. LNG baseline demand rising even in wet years as 9 FSRUs need throughput.
📊 Spot → Long-TermShiftingStabilizingCentrica 0.8 Mtpa × 15 yr. NFE-Hydro 15 yr. Petrobras seeking more US long-term deals. Market maturing from volatile spot to predictable contracts.

Brazil gas trade in one sentence: Brazil is transitioning from a spot-dependent, hydro-reactive LNG importer to a diversified gas market with long-term LNG contracts (Centrica), Argentine pipeline gas (Vaca Muerta via GASBOL reverse), growing domestic pre-salt processing (Rota 3), and a baseline of 9 FSRUs that need throughput even in wet years. The Bolivia collapse (61→<30 MMcmd) is the most underappreciated structural driver — it permanently increases Brazil's external gas dependency. The US supplies 72% of Brazil's LNG and this share will grow as the supply wave pushes more Atlantic Basin cargoes to Latin America. For SS-LNG, the key trade implication: every FSRU is a potential SS-LNG truck-loading point, and lower spot prices (below $10/MMBtu at Pecém) make virtual pipeline distribution to unserved municipalities economically viable for the first time.

Sources: NGI · EIA · Oil & Gas Journal · Brazil Energy Insight
Brazil Gas Consumption
65–70 MMcmd
~24 bcm/yr. Consistently outpaces net production (~48 Mm³/d available after reinjection). Gap filled by Bolivia pipeline + LNG imports. NG market: $24.4B → $51.8B by 2034 (8.74% CAGR).
Source: OGJ / EPE
Gas-to-Power Capacity
8 GW New
Mandated by law. Açu 3 GW (GNA II started May 2025). Barcarena 2.2 GW (CELBA 2 Oct 2024). Sergipe 1.5 GW (Eneva). Each paired with FSRU. Ensures minimum dispatch even in wet years.
Source: EIA (Oct 2025)
Gas Interiorização
~90% Off-Grid
~90% of Brazilian municipalities have zero gas access. GNLink: 15 projects mapped, 4 Mm³/d target. Compass TRSP Santos: SS-LNG to SP interior. Compagas: R$2.5B Londrina-Maringá concession. Virtual pipeline = Brazil's SS-LNG growth frontier.
Source: Click P&G / EPE
Renewables Backup Need
80% Renewable
Hydro 56% + wind/solar 24%. Wind/solar growing fast but intermittent. Gas = flexible backup for droughts + Dunkelflaute equivalents. EPE: LNG ensures grid flexibility for non-dispatchable integration.
Source: ITA / EPE
Brazil Gas & SS-LNG Demand Drivers
DEMAND
💧
Hydro Backup
PRIMARY DRIVER
80% renewables (hydro 56%). Reservoirs 29% (2024 drought). 2021 drought: 6.3 Mt LNG record. Each FSRU paired with GW-scale thermal plant. Climate change → more extreme droughts. LNG demand = f(rainfall). THE structural driver.
🏗️
8 GW Gas-to-Power
▲▲ EXECUTING
Mandated by Law 14,182/2021. Açu 3 GW (GNA II May 2025), Barcarena 2.2 GW (CELBA 2 Oct 2024), Sergipe 1.5 GW. Ensures minimum gas dispatch. Creates FSRU anchor + surplus for SS-LNG truck-loading.
🚛
Gas Interiorização (SS-LNG)
▲▲ EMERGING
~90% of municipalities off-grid. GNLink: 3 plants, 15 projects, 4 Mm³/d target. Compass TRSP Santos → SP interior. Compagas R$2.5B Londrina-Maringá. LDC virtual pipeline: LNG trucked → satellite regas → local distribution. Brazil's SS-LNG growth frontier.
🏭
Industrial Growth
▲ GROWING
NG market: $24.4B → $51.8B by 2034 (IMARC). Alunorte alumina (NFE 15-yr), Portobello ceramics, Suzano pulp (Petrobras contracts 2025). Manufacturing, chemicals, steel switching to gas. NE gas prices −15% → more competitive.
⚖️
Novo Mercado Reform
STRUCTURAL
Gas prices could fall 40% (Ministry). NE already −15%. TPA + unbundling. Free consumer criteria expanding. New entrants competing with Petrobras. Lower prices = more demand = more SS-LNG.
🔄
Reinjection Constraint
▼ LIMITING
54% of produced gas reinjected for pre-salt pressure. 5.4 Bcf/d gross → ~2.5 Bcf/d available. Limits domestic supply despite record production. Regulatory reform discussed. If reduced: less LNG needed. Supply ceiling = demand driver for imports.
Sources: EIA (Oct 2025) · Click P&G · ITA · IMARC
Brazil Gas Demand Outlook: Growth vs Constraint Matrix
OUTLOOK
Driver20252026E2030ESS-LNG Relevance
💧 Hydro Backup / Thermal▲▲▲▲▲▲▲Core — Each FSRU = thermal anchor + truck-loading point. Climate risk rising. Structural, not cyclical.
🏗️ 8 GW Gas-to-Power▲▲▲▲▲→Core — Executing now (GNA II, CELBA 2). Creates SS-LNG infrastructure as byproduct. Once built, transitions to baseload role.
🚛 Gas Interiorização▲▲▲▲▲▲Core — THE SS-LNG growth vector. GNLink 15 projects. Compass TRSP. 90% of municipalities off-grid. Decades of addressable growth.
🏭 Industrial▲▲▲▲▲Core — Alunorte model: anchor industrial customer + FSRU. Off-pipeline factories = SS-LNG target. NE prices −15% unlocking demand.
⚖️ Novo Mercado▲▲▲▲▲▲▲Enabler — Lower prices → more demand. TPA → more participants. Free consumers expanding. Market reform compounds over time.
🔄 Reinjection▼▼?Supply Cap — 54% reinjected. If regulation changes, more domestic gas → less LNG. If unchanged, structural import need persists.
📊 TOTAL BRAZIL GAS▲▲▲▲▲▲All segments growing. No structural decline in any demand sector. Brazil = rare market where every driver is positive for SS-LNG.

Brazil gas demand story in one sentence: Brazil is the rarest thing in the global SS-LNG landscape — a market where every demand driver is growing simultaneously: thermal dispatch is increasing (climate-driven droughts), 8 GW of new gas-fired capacity is being built (regulatory mandate), 90% of municipalities have zero gas access (interiorização frontier), industrial demand is rising (cheaper gas from reform + Alunorte-type anchor contracts), and the Novo Mercado reform is structurally lowering prices to unlock latent demand. There is no BEV displacement threat (Brazil has no LNG trucking), no nuclear restart threat (Brazil has minimal nuclear), and no declining baseload sector. The only constraint is supply-side: 54% reinjection limits domestic availability, and Bolivia is collapsing. This makes Brazil uniquely positioned: it needs more gas, has the reform framework to absorb it, and has 9 FSRUs as entry points — the question is how fast the SS-LNG virtual pipeline network can scale to serve the ~5,000 municipalities without gas access.

Sources: EIA · IEA · IMARC · ITA
Brazil SS-LNG Demand (2026E)
3.95 MMm³/d
1.44 bcm/yr. +28% CAGR (2023-26). LNG-to-power = 49%. LDC virtual pipeline = fastest growing (95% CAGR). Only ~1% of Brazil's total gas. Tiny but growing fast.
Source: Dashboard bottom-up model
Brazil SS-LNG Supply (2026E)
4.00 MMm³/d
1.46 bcm/yr. Balanced. Domestic liquefaction (Azulão + GNLink) = 62%. Import terminal truck-loading = 27% (TRSP Santos + Suape = game changers). Bio-LNG emerging.
Source: Dashboard bottom-up model
Addressable Market
~200 Cities
>100K pop with zero gas access. If each consumed 20K m³/d: 4.0 MMm³/d = 1.46 bcm. 2026E demand = ~1% penetration. Decades of addressable growth runway ahead.
Source: Dashboard model / EPE
Brazil vs China (2012)
Similar Stage
Brazil SS-LNG today = where China was ~2010-12. Before China's trucking boom grew the market 20× in a decade. Will Brazil follow an S-curve or stay niche? The structural enablers are in place.
Source: Dashboard analysis
Brazil SS-LNG Market: Bottom-Up Supply & Demand Model
MODEL
Methodology
Bottom-up from segment-level estimates. Scope = all SS-LNG within Brazil: LNG-to-power (isolated grids), industrial off-pipeline, LDC virtual pipeline, mining, emerging. Includes domestic liquefaction (Eneva Azulão, GNLink) + import terminal truck-loading. A = actual · P = preliminary · E = estimate. 90% of municipalities off-grid. 200+ cities >100K without gas. Regas capacity doubled since 2020.
Brazil vs US vs EU vs China
Brazil SS-LNG (1.44 bcm) = 3% of China (50.8 bcm), 27% of US (5.59 bcm), 70% of US ex-data centers. Tiny but growing 28% CAGR vs US 14%, EU 18%, China 13%. Brazil is nascent with no structural headwinds — no BEV displacement (no LNG trucking), no nuclear (minimal), no declining sector. Pure greenfield growth. The question is velocity, not direction.
Key 2025-26 Events
TRSP Santos SS-LNG hub operational (Compass). GNLink Barra Bonita plant (Paraná) commissioned. GNA II 1.7 GW started May 2025. CELBA 2 early ops Oct 2024. Suape FSRU early 2026. Petrobras first Argentina import (Oct 2025). Compagas R$2.5B Londrina-Maringá starting. NE gas prices −15%.

Supply-Demand Balance & Scenario Analysis
BALANCE
Metric2023A2024A2025P2026E
Demand (MMm³/d)1.892.383.073.95
Demand (bcm/yr)0.690.871.121.44
Supply (MMm³/d)1.892.473.124.00
Supply (bcm/yr)0.690.901.141.46
Surplus (MMm³/d)+0.00+0.08+0.05+0.05
% of Brazil Total Gas0.7%0.8%1.0%1.3%
Brazil SS-LNG vs China2.0%2.0%2.3%2.8%
Addressable Market~200 cities with >100K pop and no gas. If each consumed 20K m³/d avg: 4.0 MMm³/d = 1.46 bcm. 2026E demand (1.44 bcm) is only ~1% penetration of this addressable market. Long runway for decades of growth.
✓ GásLocal (Highest confidence)
20+ year track record. Volumes well-known. Slow growth but highly predictable. ~10-15 trailers. Established B2B relationships. Now facing competition from new entrants.
⚠ LNG-to-Power (Hydro dependent)
49% of 2026E demand. In wet years, thermal curtailed → LNG drops 20-30%. 2024 drought = high LNG call. 2026E assumes normal hydrology. Climate change = more volatility, not less.
⚠ LDC Virtual Pipeline (Highest uncertainty)
95% CAGR but from near zero. Each project = LDC + supply contract + ANP approval + pipe build + customer ramp. Copergás/Petrolina took 3+ years. If only 2-3 of 5-8 materialize, volume drops 60%.
📊 TRSP Santos (Game Changer)
Compass SS-LNG loading center at Port of Santos. Trucks loaded from FSRU. Opens SP interior + neighboring concessions. Near-zero marginal cost. Could unlock the "interiorização wave."
Scenario2026E Demand2026E SupplyWhat Drives It
🟢 Base Case3.95 MMm³/d (1.44 bcm)4.00 MMm³/dNormal hydrology. 5-8 LDC projects structuring. GNLink 2 plants. TRSP Santos operational. Barcarena ramping. Balanced market.
🔵 Bull (Drought + Reform)5.21 MMm³/d (1.90 bcm)4.66 MMm³/dSevere drought → thermal dispatch surges. Novo Mercado prices drop 30%+ → industrial demand unlocked. 10+ LDC projects materialize. GNLink accelerates. Supply tightens → more terminal truck-loading needed.
🔴 Bear (Wet Year + Delays)2.47 MMm³/d (0.90 bcm)3.29 MMm³/dAbundant rainfall → minimal thermal dispatch. LDC projects delayed (regulatory, construction). Industrial demand stays with piped gas/LPG. Mining projects stall. SS-LNG stays niche for another 3-5 years.

Brazil SS-LNG S&D model in one sentence: Brazil's SS-LNG market is tiny (1.44 bcm, ~3% of China's, ~27% of US) but growing at 28% CAGR from near zero — it's at the stage China was in ~2010-12 before the trucking boom grew that market 20× in a decade. The structural enablers are all in place: New Gas Law broke Petrobras' monopoly, 9 FSRUs provide coastal supply, TRSP Santos opens truck-loading for the SP interior, GNLink is building domestic liquefaction plants, and 90% of municipalities have zero gas access (decades of addressable growth). The question is whether Brazil follows a China-style S-curve or stays niche — and the answer depends on: (1) how fast LDC virtual pipeline projects convert from "structuring" to "operational" (Copergás took 3+ years), (2) whether Novo Mercado reforms actually deliver the promised 40% gas price reduction, and (3) drought frequency (each severe drought triggers a step-change in LNG infrastructure that doesn't reverse). Brazil has no structural headwinds — no BEV displacement, no nuclear restart, no declining sector — making it the purest SS-LNG growth story in the world. The constraint is execution speed, not market size.

Sources: EIA (Oct 2025) · ABEGÁS · Gas Week · epbr · Legal 500 · Company filings

Industrial & Power Generation

Small-scale LNG serves as the backbone for off-grid power, mining operations, factory fuel switching, peak shaving for utilities, and the fast-growing data center power market — replacing expensive diesel and heavy fuel oil in areas without pipeline access.

1,000+
Globally, as substitute for fuel oil & coal

Key Industrial SS-LNG Players & Projects
PLAYERS
CompanyRoleKey Industrial / Power DetailsSource
Aggreko
(Brookfield-owned)		 Off-Grid Power Global leader in modular off-grid LNG power. Virtual gas pipelines active in 4+ countries (Australia, Canada, Spain, Chile). 6–10 MW mine sites. LNG trucks over 500–1,000 km. Gold mine case: installed in 3 months replacing diesel. Up to 50% lower fuel costs. BOOM model (Build-Own-Operate-Maintain). Partnered with Prometheus/Stabilis for LNG supply. Aggreko
Stabilis Solutions
NASDAQ: SLNG		 Production & Supply $200M take-or-pay LNG contract for data center behind-the-meter power (2027–2029). Aerospace revenue up 83% YoY (Q2 2025). Supplies NASA, SpaceX-linked operations. Marine/aerospace/power = ~70% of revenue mix. Aggreko identified as key industrial customer (Aggreko Plc listed in SEC 10-K). SEC 10-K
Eagle LNG Partners Island Power Antigua LNG project: supplying APCL power plants. 30%+ CO₂ reduction for the island's electricity generation. Willing to invest up to $1B in Caribbean LNG import infrastructure. Targeting underserved Caribbean & Central American power markets. Eagle LNG
Galileo / EDGE LNG Flare Gas / Wellhead 15 Cryobox Trailer units in TX (290 tpd). Capture flare gas at wellhead → liquefy on-site → sell as LNG. Addresses 140B m³/yr global flaring problem. Also peak shaving units for US utilities (Matrix Service contract, Jun 2025). N. America Outlook
Linde PLC
NYSE: LIN		 On-Site Gas Plants 59 new long-term on-site agreements (2024) → 64 BOO plants at customer sites worldwide. Builds, owns, and operates small gas plants embedded in customer factories. Industrial gas leader supplying LNG, nitrogen, oxygen to heavy industry. Market.us
WM (Waste Management) Fleet + RNG ~14,000 natural gas vehicles. 225 fueling stations. Displaced 110M gallons of diesel in 2024. Plans 100% RNG by 2026. Operates its own fueling infrastructure — an industrial-scale captive LNG/RNG consumer. Cummins
Greenville LNG
(Nigeria)		 Off-Grid Industrial Operates SS-LNG plant in Nigeria delivering gas to industries and companies not linked by pipeline. Addresses Africa's off-grid industrial energy gap. Demonstrates emerging market SS-LNG model. Energy Capital & Power
TotalEnergies
EPA: TTE		 Integrated Major 2nd largest private LNG operator globally (target: 50 MTPA by 2025). SS-LNG via Elengy terminals (France) for truck loading + small carrier loading. Defines SS-LNG as two applications: mobility (marine + trucks) and industry (power, steam, heating in off-grid locations). TotalEnergies Gas Mobility
Sources linked per row above. Additional: Data Centre Magazine · International Mining · Burns & McDonnell
Data Centers & AI: The Emerging Mega-Driver for Gas-Fired Power
DATA CENTERS
Metric / DriverValueDetail
Global DC Electricity (2024)415 TWh1.5% of world electricity. IEA projects doubling to ~945 TWh by 2030. AI + cloud computing = primary growth drivers.
US DC Demand by 203075–100 GW new1,000+ data centers under construction or permitted (Hamm Institute). 10 GW added in 2025 alone (≈ NYC peak). Could reach 11–15% of US total power by 2030 (from 6–8% today).
Grid Interconnection QueueUp to 5 yearsBerkeley Lab data. US utilities face $174B capex by end-2024 for grid upgrades. Behind-the-meter gas generation bridges the gap in months, not years.
xAI Colossus (Memphis)~200 MWElon Musk installed VoltaGrid gas generators → 100K Nvidia chips powered in 122 days (Jun–Sep 2024). Doubled capacity within months. Landmark behind-the-meter project that catalyzed industry interest.
Behind-the-Meter Economics$0.09–0.14/kWhOn-site gas generation often cheaper than utility grid. Avoids 5% transmission line losses. VoltaGrid: "9 cents/kWh cheaper than large DC load in California." Texas: 200 MW permit in 21 days.
Stabilis DC Contract~$200M (2027–29)Multi-year take-or-pay for behind-the-meter power. LNG stored on-site, bridges 30–35 days/yr of grid shortfalls. "Five nines" (99.999%) uptime target. Redundancy = key cost driver.
Incremental US Gas Demand+340 MMm³/d by 2030Power sector alone could add 340 Mm³/d of gas demand (Deloitte). On top of 991–1,048 MMm³/d baseline. NG production may need to grow 10–15% in 5 years (Hamm Institute).
Key ProvidersVoltaGrid, Stabilis, SapphireVoltaGrid: gas turbines for xAI, rapid deploy. Stabilis: LNG virtual pipeline for backup. Sapphire Gas: CNG/LNG mobile delivery for DC backup. Hydria: carbon-fiber CNG cylinder storage (180–200 units per 100 MW).
Sources: NGI (Jun 2025) · Hamm/AOGR · Woodway Energy · Deloitte/NGI

SECTORS
