MiningOff-grid power for gold, copper, lithium, iron ore6–50 MW30–50% cheaper than diesel. CO₂ -25%. Deploy in 3 months via virtual pipeline. BOOM model ($0 upfront).Australia (Aggreko), Chile (copper), Peru (gold), Canada (oil sands), Africa (gold/platinum). Aggreko global leader in 100+ countries.
Metals & RefiningProcess heat for alumina, aluminum, steel, glass, ceramics20–200 MWCleaner than coal/HFO for high-temp processes. Lower SOx/NOx. Gas-fired kilns increasingly standard in ceramics.NFE Barcarena → Norsk Hydro Alunorte (world's largest alumina refinery, 15-yr contract). China: 300+ SS-LNG plants serve inland ceramics/glass. India: GAIL SS-LNG skids for off-grid factories.
Data Centers / AIBehind-the-meter primary + backup power50–500+ MWGrid queue = 5 years. Gas gen online in weeks–months. $0.09–0.14/kWh delivered. "Five nines" redundancy. 10 GW added in 2025.US: xAI Colossus (Memphis, VoltaGrid 200 MW in 122 days), Stabilis ~$200M DC contract. Texas, Virginia, Louisiana, Oklahoma, Pennsylvania leading build-out.
Peak Shaving / UtilitySupplement pipeline gas during demand spikes10–50 MW~80 peak-shaving plants in US (50% NE, 25% Midwest). 1.3B gal/yr capacity. Winter Storm Fern: Jan 2025 record consumption.US Northeast: KeySpan, National Grid, Eversource. Satellite LNG plants refilled by truck. Burns & McDonnell peak-shaving engineering leader.
Oilfield ServicesWell pad power, frac pumping, drilling, flare capture5–30 MWCaptures stranded/flare gas at source. Eliminates venting. Replaces diesel for frac fleet. Galileo Cryobox: no electrical grid needed.US Permian (EDGE LNG 290 tpd, 10+ Cryoboxes), Bakken. Colombia: Canacol + Galileo stranded gas-to-power. 140B m³/yr global gas flared.
Food & BeverageProcess heat, steam, refrigeration1–10 MWCleaner than propane/fuel oil for food-grade applications. Consistent temperature control. Lower operating costs.Brazil: SS-LNG expanding to NE food processing. China: inland F&B factories via SS-LNG trucks. Europe: bio-LNG for dairy + brewing (Gasum).
Chemicals & FertilizerFeedstock + process energy50–500+ MWNG = primary feedstock for ammonia/urea (Haber-Bosch). LNG enables feedstock access where pipeline doesn't exist.India: GAIL city gas distribution for chemical belt. Indonesia: SS-LNG for fertilizer plants on Java. Trinidad: legacy LNG-to-chemicals model.
Construction / TemporaryCamps, modular buildings, temporary power0.5–5 MWRapid deploy (days). Relocatable. Predictable pricing vs volatile diesel. Modular units <50K GGE.Global: Aggreko temporary power for events, disaster relief, pipeline construction. Gulf Coast: LNG terminal construction sites use own product.
Commercial AerospaceRocket propellant coolant + launch site power1–10 MWHigh-purity LNG (methane) required. +65% demand growth expected over 5 years. ~25M gal/yr US market.US: Stabilis supplies ~40% of high-purity LNG to space launch providers (SpaceX = top-3 customer). Cape Canaveral, Boca Chica, Vandenberg AFB.
Sources: Aggreko · Stabilis 10-K · NGI · Galileo · Grand View Research
Off-Grid Power Cost: LNG vs Alternatives ($/kWh)
COST
Sources: NGI · Aggreko · Woodway Energy
US Data Center Power Demand (2025)
61.8 GW
→ 75.8 GW (2026) → 134.4 GW (2030). +22% in 2025 alone. 45 GW shortfall vs grid capacity.
Source: S&P Global / 451 Research
PHMSA LNG Facilities (US)
170+
Peak-shaving + satellite + vehicle fuel + industrial. 15 under construction. NE + Midwest = 75%.
Source: US DOT PHMSA
Stabilis DC Contract
$200M
Multi-year take-or-pay. Behind-the-meter power gen. Q1 2027 → Q1 2029.
Source: Stabilis 10-K (Mar 2026)
Everett LNG (NE Peak Day)
35%
Of NE peak winter day gas demand met by LNG. Contracts through 2030.
Source: MA DPU / Argonne
US Data Center Power Demand vs Grid Capacity
DATA CENTERS

Hyperscalers committed $969B capex ($662B in DC leases not yet started). Lawrence Berkeley Lab: 176 TWh (2023, 4.4% of US electricity) → 325–580 TWh by 2028 (6.7–12.0%). Grid interconnection queue: 5+ years avg. Transformer lead times: 3–4 years. Power constraints extending DC construction by 24–72 months. In Q4 2025 developers added only 25 GW to pipeline — half of Q3 (Wood Mackenzie).

Sources: S&P Global / 451 Research · Berkeley Lab · Goldman Sachs · Wood Mackenzie
Behind-the-Meter Power: Why Data Centers Need LNG
BYOP
⚡
THE PROBLEM
Grid queue 5+ years. 45 GW shortfall. 2,600 GW in queue (2× US installed capacity). AEP Ohio cut DC pipeline from 30→13 GW.
🏭
BYOP SOLUTION
On-site gas generation bypasses grid queue. Deployable in weeks–months vs years. Meta: 2 GW Hyperion ($3.2B, Louisiana). 700 MW Ohio plant.
🧊
SS-LNG ROLE
In pipeline-constrained areas (NE, VA, OH), LNG truck delivery is the fastest path to behind-the-meter gas. Stabilis: $200M DC contract.
⏳
LNG WINDOW
2025–2035: LNG = only scalable solution. Post-2035: Nuclear SMRs + BESS reduce gas role. SS-LNG for DCs: 7% → 20–30% of US demand.
MODEL 1: Commissioning
Short-duration power during DC construction + testing. Days–weeks. Portable gensets + LNG trailers.
MODEL 2: Bridging ⭐
Medium-term (1–3 yrs) while waiting for utility grid connection. Stabilis $200M contract = this model. Q1 2027 → Q1 2029.
MODEL 3: Permanent Backup
Ongoing backup + reliability. Grid-connected DCs keep gas gensets for outages/peaks. Lower volume but recurring.

Hyperscalers prioritize "speed to power" over "immediacy of green power" — gas is deliberate, temporary while nuclear/renewables scale. Competing: Nuclear SMRs (NuScale, Kairos, X-energy) not before 2030. MSFT restarting Three Mile Island. Google signed Kairos PPA. Amazon bought Talen nuclear campus. Solar+BESS: 4-hr duration insufficient for 24/7 DC baseload. MSFT+Brookfield: 10.5 GW PPA ($10B+) but still needs gas backup.

Sources: Stabilis Q4 Earnings Call · S&P Global · Harvard Belfer Center · WRI · Fortune / Wood Mackenzie
Top US Data Center Markets (2025 GW)
GEOGRAPHY

Emerging: Idaho, Louisiana, Oklahoma, W. Texas — areas with ample NG + stranded power. On-site gas gen preferred.

Sources: S&P Global / 451 Research · AEP · Dominion
US Industrial SS-LNG Market Segment Mix
SEGMENTS

DC power rising from ~7% (2026E) to 20–30% by 2030. Peak shaving = legacy anchor. Oilfield cyclical.

Sources: Stabilis 10-K · EIA · PHMSA · RBN Energy
New England LNG Supply Chain: Winter Reliability Infrastructure
PEAK SHAVING
🚢
LNG Tankers
377 MMm³ imported (2023)
→
🏗️
Everett Terminal
Constellation Energy
Contracts → 2030
→
🚛
Truck Delivery
To 47 LNG tanks
across New England
→
🏠 Home Heating
⚡ Power Plants
🏭 Industry
Pipeline Constraint
NE has insufficient pipeline capacity for peak winter heating + power. No new major pipelines since Algonquin (2016). Winter gas spikes to $20–30/MMBtu (vs $4 HH). LNG imports = safety valve.
Everett Terminal
Only east coast facility accepting large LNG tankers for NE. Mystic (1,413 MW) retired May 2024. 6-year contracts with National Grid, Eversource, Unitil keep EMT open through 2030. 100% of 2023 US LNG ship imports.
47 Customer Tanks
Hopkinton MA (~42 MMm³, largest), Acton MA (~14 MMm³), Waterbury CT (~11 MMm³), Carlstadt NJ (~28 MMm³). Total ~161 MMm³ nameplate. Fill Apr–Oct, draw Nov–Mar. = 35% of NE peak day gas.
ISO-NE IEP Program
Inventoried Energy Program: compensates generators for on-site fuel storage in winter. Cost: ~$75M (winter 2023–24, $2.45–2.66/MWh). Designed as short-term bridge. Successor program likely needed.

PHMSA nationally: 170+ LNG facilities. Types: peak-shaving, satellite (regas only), vehicle fuel, industrial. NE = ~50%, Midwest = ~25%. 15 under construction (2024). Regulations updating first time since 2004 (PIPES Act 2020). National Center of Excellence for LNG Safety being established in Louisiana.

Sources: EIA · MA DPU · Argonne · FERC · PHMSA · ISO-NE · CES
Traditional Industrial SS-LNG Verticals
INDUSTRIAL
🛢️
Oilfield Services
Stabilis George West (TX): Eagle Ford. Frac fleets, drilling rigs, wellpad generators. LNG $7–10/MMBtu vs diesel $25+ = 60–70% savings. Original SS-LNG use case (since 2013).
⛏️
Mining
Surface mining: haul trucks, excavators. Caterpillar + Westport dual-fuel engines. Copper (AZ), coal (WY, WV). Smaller in US than Australia (most US mines grid-connected).
🏗️
Remote Construction
Pipeline/highway projects off-pipeline. Stabilis: turnkey LNG. Gulf Coast: 9.5M gal LNG for grid resiliency (23 MWh). 25% of Stabilis 2023 revenue from power gen.
🚀
Aerospace
SpaceX: methane (LNG) = Raptor engine propellant (Starship). Stabilis supplies Boca Chica TX. NASA Artemis. Small volume, high-profile + high-margin.
🌪️
Emergency / Resiliency
Post-disaster: hurricanes (TX, FL, LA), winter storms (Uri 2021, Elliott 2022). Portable LNG gensets in hours. FERC/NERC now mandate fuel security coordination.
🏭
Manufacturing
Off-pipeline factories: glass, ceramics, food, chemicals. Clean Energy Boron (CA): dairy RNG → bio-LNG. Pivotal LNG (Southern Co.): SE US. EDGE: 290 tpd mobile fleet.
Sources: Stabilis 10-K · RBN Energy · LNG Prime · Clean Energy · Pivotal LNG · EDGE
US Industrial SS-LNG: Segment Outlook & Risk Matrix
OUTLOOK
SegmentShareGrowthConfidenceKey Upside / Risk
⚡ Data Center BTM~7%Very High ▲High45 GW shortfall. Stabilis $200M = proof of concept. Risk: SMRs by 2030–32, political backlash.
❄️ Peak Shaving NE~40%Stable →HighStructural pipeline constraint. Everett → 2030. 47 tanks embedded. Risk: heat pumps, 2030 renewal.
❄️ Peak Shaving MW~15%Stable →MedWinter reliability persists. Grid stress from DC + EV. Risk: warmer winters, new pipelines.
🛢️ Oilfield / Mining~15%Moderate ▲MedPermian high activity. Dual-fuel frac growing. Risk: oil price decline, e-frac competition.
🌪️ Emergency~5%Lumpy ⟷LowMore extreme weather → demand. FERC mandates. Risk: unpredictable, event-driven.
🚀 Aerospace<1%Niche ▲HighSpaceX Starship scaling. High-margin. Risk: tiny volume, concentration.

The structural shift: US industrial SS-LNG is transitioning from peak-shaving + oilfield (legacy) to data center + peak-shaving + oilfield (2026+). DC behind-the-meter power is the single most important growth vector. Stabilis Galveston expansion (350K gpd, FID Q1 2026, 56% capacity committed, $350–400M capex) + two 10-year cruise bunkering contracts + DC power supply = diversified demand base.

Sources: Stabilis 10-K · Earnings Call · S&P Global · PHMSA · EIA · FERC · Argonne
EU Gas Demand Outlook
-15%
Forecast decline 2025→2030 (IEEFA). Already peaked. De-industrialization + heat pumps + renewables.
Source: IEEFA European LNG Tracker
Heat Pumps Installed (Europe)
28M
2.64M sold in 2025 (+11% YoY). Target: 60M by 2030. Each 7% gas boiler swap = -13 bcm gas imports.
Source: EHPA Market Report 2025
LNG Terminal Utilization (EU)
52%
Avg H1 2025. 6 terminals below 20%. Germany Wilhelmshaven shut for winter 2024–25. Overcapacity structural.
Source: IEEFA
Russian LNG Ban
Jan 2027
EU sanctions: long-term contracts from 1 Jan 2027. Short-term within 6 months. 13% of EU LNG imports (H1 2025).
Source: EU Commission
The Dominant Narrative: EU Gas Demand in Structural Decline
MACRO

EU gas demand dropped >20% between 2021–2024 (REPowerEU, efficiency, de-industrialization). Domestic production: ~40 bcm (2024), down 12.4% YoY — permanent import dependency. Gas consumption rose 6% in H1 2025 (cold weather) but IEEFA forecasts continued decline from 2026. LNG regas capacity: 3× projected 2030 demand = massive overcapacity. Implication for SS-LNG: the pie is shrinking. SS-LNG growth depends on taking share from pipeline gas (off-grid niches) rather than overall market expansion.

Sources: IEEFA EU LNG Tracker · Kpler · ACER · EU Commission · Modern Diplomacy
Heat Pumps vs Gas: Europe's Industrial & Residential Heat Transition
ELECTRIFICATION
🏠
RESIDENTIAL <200°C
Heat pumps COP 3–5. 28M installed, 2.64M sold in 2025 (+11%). 17 countries banning fossil boilers. IEA: -21 bcm gas by 2030. Gas losing.
🏭
INDUSTRIAL <200°C
Industrial heat pumps grew +12% in 2024. Paper, food, chemicals = 30% of heat addressable. 15 GW potential in EU (3,000 facilities). Gas under pressure.
🔥
INDUSTRIAL >500°C
Ceramics, glass, steel, cement = not electrifiable. Process heat >1,400°C = gas domain. Green H₂ is the 2035+ competitor, not electricity. Gas's fortress.
🧊
SS-LNG NICHE
Off-pipeline factories in S/E Europe. Satellite regas stations. Italy, Spain, Greece, Poland, Balkans. SS-LNG grows where pipes don't reach.

EHPA: if 7% more EU households swapped gas boilers for heat pumps (14M homes), it would avoid gas imports equivalent to all EU imports from Russia. Germany: heat pump/boiler sales ratio 2.2:1 (2023) → expected 0.3:1 by 2035. UK: +27% HP sales in 2025 (125K units). But: Germany sales dropped 54% in H1 2024 (subsidy cuts + lower gas prices). Market recovering in 2025.

Sources: EHPA 2025 · IEA Future of Heat Pumps · JRC Heat Pumps in EU · Renewable Energy Magazine
EU SS-LNG Industrial Verticals
SEGMENTS
🏺
Ceramics & Glass
Italy (Sassuolo district), Spain (Castellón), Portugal. Kilns at 1,100–1,400°C. Gas = only option. Some factories off pipeline → SS-LNG satellite supply. Sector under pressure from cheap Chinese imports.
⚗️
Chemicals & Pharma
Germany (BASF Ludwigshafen), Belgium (Antwerp), Netherlands. CHP plants. Gas as feedstock + heat. De-industrialization risk: BASF shifting investment to China. EU chemicals output -12% since 2021.
🏗️
Off-Grid Industry (S/E Europe)
Greece: Revithoussa terminal → truck-loading for islands + inland factories. Croatia, Bulgaria, Romania: limited gas pipeline coverage. Balkans: SS-LNG replacing coal + HFO for industrial heat. Growing niche.
❄️
Peak Shaving
Maasvlakte NL (90K t/yr), Stuttgart/Nievenheim DE. Norway: Risavika (300K t/yr), Kollsnes. Declining relevance — EU pipeline redundancy improving. LNG terminal overcapacity (52% avg utilization).
💻
Data Centers
Dublin (FLAD), Amsterdam, Frankfurt, London. Mostly grid-connected (unlike US congestion). Ireland: grid stress → gas backup growing. EU DCs don't need behind-the-meter LNG — grid works. Minimal SS-LNG role.
⚡
Power Generation
Gas-to-power declining — renewables + nuclear replacing baseload. Gas retained for peaking/flexibility. LNG-to-power rare (dense pipeline infra). Malta: only EU country with LNG-to-power as primary generation.
Sources: IEEFA · EU Commission · Norton Rose Fulbright · GEM Europe Gas Tracker · ACER
EU Regulatory Framework Impacting Industrial Gas Use
REGULATION
🏠
17 Countries
Fossil boiler bans
(new builds/retrofit)
+
💨
EU ETS 100%
Maritime from 2026
Industrial already
+
🇷🇺
Russian LNG Ban

Short-term: 6 months
+
🔋
REPowerEU
End Russian fossil fuels
by 2027. -55% GHG by 2030
→
📉
Gas Demand -15%
By 2030. Industrial gas
displaced by electrification

EU imports: Norway 120–124 bcm/yr (pipeline), US 55–60 bcm/yr (LNG), Qatar 15–20 bcm/yr. US = 58% of EU LNG imports (2025). EU-US framework: $750B in US LNG + energy products through 2028. Domestic production: ~40 bcm (2024, -12.4% YoY). EU has entered permanent import dependency for gas — but demand is falling faster than production.

Sources: EU Commission · IEEFA · Kpler · Modern Diplomacy · GEM
EU Industrial SS-LNG: Segment Outlook & Risk Matrix
OUTLOOK
SegmentTrendConfidenceKey Insight
🏺 Ceramics / Glass (>500°C)Stable →HighGas's most defensible segment. Not electrifiable. SS-LNG serves off-pipeline factories in Italy, Spain, Greece. Risk: Chinese import competition shrinks EU production.
⚗️ Chemicals / Industrial CHPDeclining ▼MedDe-industrialization: EU chemicals output -12% since 2021. BASF shifting to China. High gas prices eroding competitiveness. Risk: structural demand destruction — factories leaving EU.
🏗️ Off-Grid S/E EuropeGrowing ▲HighGreece, Croatia, Balkans, Poland expanding gas access. SS-LNG replaces coal/HFO. Revithoussa truck-loading. EU cohesion funds support gas infrastructure in East.
❄️ Peak ShavingDeclining ▼HighPipeline redundancy improving. LNG terminal overcapacity (52% utilization). Maasvlakte, Stuttgart still operational but importance declining. Risk: becomes stranded infrastructure.
💻 Data CentersMinimal →HighEU DCs are grid-connected — no US-style grid queue crisis. Ireland is only exception. SS-LNG has near-zero role in EU DC power. This is a US story, not an EU story.
⚡ Power GenerationDeclining ▼HighRenewables + nuclear replacing gas baseload. Gas retained for peaking only. Malta = only LNG-to-power primary. Risk: zero growth trajectory for new gas-to-power in EU.

Europe's SS-LNG industrial story is fundamentally different from the US. In the US, SS-LNG is growing (data centers, grid constraints). In Europe, SS-LNG is defending share in a shrinking market. The opportunity is narrowing to: (1) off-pipeline industrial in S/E Europe (growing), (2) high-temp ceramics/glass (stable), and (3) bio-LNG credit arbitrage (FuelEU). Everything else is declining. The strategic question for EU SS-LNG players: pivot to bio-LNG + marine bunkering, or accept a structurally shrinking industrial market.

Sources: IEEFA · EHPA · IEA · EU Commission · Kpler · GEM · ACER
China Gas Consumption (2024)
420 bcm
+6.2% YoY (2023). Domestic production: 246 bcm (+28% since 2020). 43% imported. Gas = 9% of primary energy.
Source: EIA China 2025
SS-LNG Domestic Production
25 Mt
300+ plants. Capacity +41% in 2024. 70.76 Mm³/d nameplate. Self-sufficient for trucking (25 Mt > 22 Mt consumed).
Source: IEEFA (May 2025)
Industrial Gas Use (2020)
129 bcm
From 19.5 bcm (2010). 6.6× in a decade. Coal-to-gas policy driver. Ceramics, glass, metallurgy, food, textiles.
Source: China Engineering Science
Coal-to-Gas Capacity (New)
19.5 bcm
Under construction. Will double annual capacity. NW China. Coal gas 33% cheaper than imported LNG.
Source: IER / Reuters
China's Gas Supply Ecosystem: Why SS-LNG Thrives Domestically
SUPPLY
⛽
Domestic Production
246 bcm (2024)
+28% since 2020
+
🔄
Pipeline Imports
Russia 38 bcm (PoS1)
C. Asia 55 bcm
+
🚢
LNG Imports
106 bcm (78 Mt, 2024)
AU 34%, QA 24%
+
⚫
Coal-to-Gas
19.5 bcm under constr.
33% cheaper than LNG
→
300+ SS-LNG Plants
4,500+ LNG Stations
500+ Satellite Regas

Key insight: China's SS-LNG system is domestically self-sufficient. Domestic production (246 bcm) + pipeline imports (93 bcm) + coal-to-gas (growing) = LNG imports fill only the marginal gap. 300+ SS-LNG plants turn cheap domestic/pipeline gas into LNG for trucking + industrial distribution. Unlike Europe/US where SS-LNG draws from imported LNG, China's SS-LNG is fed primarily by domestic gas, COG, and CBM — keeping costs far below international LNG prices.

Sources: EIA China · IEEFA · IER/Reuters · Columbia CGEP · Kpler
Coal-to-Gas Switching: Blue Sky Mandate vs Energy Security
POLICY
✅ PRO-GAS: Blue Sky Mandate
"2+26" cities (Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei): 4M+ households switched from coal to gas. Industrial boilers, kilns in ceramics/glass/metallurgy mandated to switch. 500+ satellite LNG stations built for off-pipeline factories. Gas use in industry grew from 19.5→129 bcm in a decade. Clean winter heating plan extended to wider northern China.
⚠️ ANTI-GAS: Energy Security
NDRC (2023): "strictly control expansion of coal-to-gas projects." Natural Gas Utilization Policy (2024): gas "restricted" for baseload power in 14 coal-producing regions. Xi Jinping: national energy self-reliance. Coal = cornerstone of energy security (14th FYP). 276 Mt coal converted to chemicals/gas (2024). Coal capacity additions continue (1,210→1,332–1,600 GW by 2030).
The Resolution: Gas Grows in Industry & Transport, Not in Power
China's policy is clear: gas for industrial heat (ceramics, glass, food) + trucking (LNG trucks) + city gas = encouraged. Gas for baseload power = restricted. Coal keeps its role in power generation + energy security. Renewables (solar 380 GW deploying in 2025 alone) take share from both coal and gas in power. SS-LNG benefits from the industrial/transport policy direction. The policy tension is resolved by segmentation: gas wins industry, coal keeps power, renewables win everything long-term.
Sources: IEEFA China LNG · Columbia CGEP Blue Sky · Climate Action Tracker · DNV Greater China
China SS-LNG Industrial Verticals
SEGMENTS
🏺
Ceramics & Glass
Foshan (Guangdong): 50K+ m³/d per factory. Jingdezhen (Jiangxi): porcelain capital. Kilns at 1,100–1,400°C. Blue Sky mandate → coal banned in industrial zones. Satellite LNG stations deliver gas to off-pipeline factories. Largest SS-LNG industrial use case.
🏙️
City Gas Virtual Pipeline
2.4 bcm demand (2026E). PipeChina/Kunlun, ENN, China Gas. LNG trucked to towns off trunk pipelines → satellite regas → local distribution. Critical for winter heating in northern cities. 2017–18 shortages left communities without heat — drove infrastructure buildout.
⛏️
Mining & Remote Power
0.9 bcm (2026E). Inner Mongolia, Xinjiang open-pit mines. Weichai dual-fuel engines for haul trucks. Remote construction power. Replaces diesel gensets at mine sites. Smaller than trucking but growing steadily.
🏭
Industrial Park CHP
Guangdong, Zhejiang, Fujian: coastal provinces with high electricity cost. LNG-fired CHP: 80%+ efficiency vs 40% grid. On-site power for industrial parks. Gas cost: ¥2–2.5/m³. Gas power cost: ¥0.5–0.6/kWh (vs coal ¥0.23–0.31). Premium price = barrier but Blue Sky mandate forces compliance.
❄️
Winter Peak Shaving
Critical for northern heating. 14th FYP: gas storage target 55–60 bcm (up from 35 bcm). 35+ underground storage facilities (0.94 Tcf, end 2023). 36 more under construction (+1.2 Tcf by 2028). LNG terminals with truck-loading bays supplement pipeline storage.
⚡
Gas-to-Power (Restricted)
Gas = only 3% of China power gen. NDRC: "restricted" in 14 coal regions. Guangdong: gas power < coal + imports + nuclear. Gas power cost 2× coal. Renewables (solar 380 GW in 2025) win long-term. SS-LNG has near-zero role in China power generation.
Sources: IEEFA · Columbia CGEP · EIA · China Eng. Science · PipeChina · DNV
Competitive Threats to China's Industrial SS-LNG
COMPETITION
🔋
BEV Trucks (22% HDV)
BEV rising from 13%→22% of HDV sales (Q1 2025). Battery swap: 5-min. Prices €58–85K. Fuel costs 70% lower than diesel. LNG trucking may peak ~2027. But industrial/city gas SS-LNG is NOT threatened by BEV.
☀️
Solar (380 GW in 2025)
China deploying 380 GW solar in 2025 alone. Coal peak already passed. Renewables displacing both coal AND gas in power gen. Gas power share will NOT grow. But gas for industrial heat is insulated — solar can't fire a kiln at 1,400°C.
🔄
Pipeline Expansion
PipeChina adding 99K km. Power of Siberia 2 (+50 bcm). Far East Pipeline (+10 bcm, 2027). As pipelines reach more cities, SS-LNG virtual pipeline demand DECLINES. Pipeline expansion is the long-term threat to city gas SS-LNG.
Sources: IEEFA · CleanTechnica · DNV Greater China · Columbia CGEP PipeChina
China Industrial SS-LNG: Segment Outlook & Risk Matrix
OUTLOOK
SegmentTrendConfidenceKey Insight
🏺 Ceramics / Glass / MetalGrowing ▲HighBlue Sky mandates expand to more cities/provinces. Not electrifiable (>1,000°C). SS-LNG satellite stations serve off-pipeline factories. Risk: factory closures from economic slowdown.
🏙️ City Gas VPGrowing ▲ then ▼MedNear-term growth as LNG fills gaps in pipeline network. But as PipeChina completes 99K km expansion, SS-LNG VP gets displaced by pipes. Transitional role — SS-LNG is the bridge, not the destination.
⛏️ Mining / RemoteStable →MedSteady demand from NW China mining. Weichai dual-fuel engines. Risk: electrification of mining equipment (BYD electric mining trucks).
🏭 Industrial CHPStable →MedPremium-cost segment. Viable where Blue Sky mandate forces it. Guangdong/Zhejiang/Fujian. Risk: power tariff cuts (Guangdong -16%) erode gas CHP economics.
❄️ Peak ShavingGrowing ▲High14th FYP: storage 55–60 bcm target. 36 facilities under construction. Winter heating demand structural. Upside: 2017–18 crisis memory drives overinvestment in storage.
⚡ Gas PowerRestricted ✖HighNDRC policy: restricted in 14 coal regions. Gas = 3% of power. Solar + wind + coal dominate. SS-LNG has no role in China's power sector. This is settled policy.

China's industrial SS-LNG story in one sentence: Gas wins in industrial heat (ceramics, glass) and city gas VP (until pipelines arrive), but is restricted in power gen, and trucking faces BEV displacement by ~2027–28. The 300+ SS-LNG plant network is a domestic asset that feeds domestic demand — it does NOT pull cargoes off the global LNG market (IEEFA). China's SS-LNG market is the world's largest but increasingly self-contained: domestic gas + COG + CBM feed domestic plants that serve domestic trucks and factories.

Sources: IEEFA · EIA · Columbia CGEP · DNV · Climate Action Tracker · China Eng. Science · IER
APAC SS-LNG Market (2021)
$4.4B
→ $12.9B by 2029. CAGR 14.3%. China + Japan dominate. SE Asia fastest growing sub-region.
Source: Data Bridge Market Research
Australia Off-Grid Mines
100+
Of ~350 total mines. EDL Energy: 713 MW portfolio (LNG + renewables). Pilbara + Kimberley + Kalgoorlie = key regions.
Source: Mine Australia / GlobalData
India Gas Share Target
15%
By 2030 (from ~6% today). GAIL: 2 SS-LNG pilot skids. 4,500+ CNG stations. LNG industrial = nascent.
Source: Mordor Intelligence
Indonesia Islands
17,000+
Pertamina floating mini-regas. Bali operational. SS-LNG replaces diesel for island power. Genting FLNG FID (1.2 Mtpa).
Source: GEM SE Asia Gas 2024
APAC (Ex-China) SS-LNG: A Mosaic of Very Different Markets
OVERVIEW
🇯🇵
JAPAN: Mature & Declining
200+ satellite receiving terminals. World's #4 LNG importer (66.5 Mt, 2024). Demand falling — nuclear restarts (12 reactors online) + renewables. SS-LNG serves remote islands + peak shaving. JERA shifting focus to ammonia co-firing.
🇦🇺
AUSTRALIA: Mining Dominated
100+ off-grid mines (of 350 total). EDL Energy: 713 MW (LNG + hybrid). Pacific Energy: Pilbara lithium + gold mines. BOC (Linde): SS-LNG trucking in WA. LNG→solar+battery hybrid transition underway.
🇮🇳
INDIA: Nascent Giant
Gas = 6% of energy (target 15%). GAIL: 2 SS-LNG pilot skids. Shell Hazira truck-loading. 4,500+ CNG stations dominate road transport. CGD network expanding fast. LNG for industry = still pilot stage. Enormous potential, limited present.
🌏
SE ASIA: LNG-to-Power Wave
Indonesia: 17,000 islands, Pertamina floating mini-regas. Philippines: Batangas terminal (2024). Thailand: 53% gas by 2037 (PDP). Vietnam: planned LNG-to-power. Fastest-growing SS-LNG sub-region.

Why APAC ex-China is not one market: Japan is mature + declining. Australia = mining niche. India = massive potential but CNG dominates today. SE Asia = fastest growth via LNG-to-power for islands + industrial zones. South Korea = KOGAS monopoly, declining LNG imports. Each sub-market has completely different drivers, players, and competitive dynamics.

Sources: Mordor Intelligence · Data Bridge MR · GEM SE Asia Gas · APEC SS-LNG · IGU 2025
Country-Level SS-LNG Industrial Applications
COUNTRIES
⛏️
Australia: Mining
EDL Energy: Agnew gold mine hybrid microgrid (55–60% renewables). Pacific Energy: Pilbara Minerals Pilgangoora LNG conversion (90% diesel replaced, 15-yr contract). 100+ off-grid mines in WA, NT, SA, QLD. LNG 30% less carbon-intensive than diesel. Now evolving to LNG + solar + battery hybrids.
🏝️
Indonesia: Islands
17,000+ islands dependent on diesel gensets. Pertamina floating mini-regas for Bali, Lombok, eastern islands. Genting FLNG FID (1.2 Mtpa, 2024). LNG replaces diesel at $25+/MMBtu. PLN (state utility) expanding gas-fired island power. Cost savings: $1.6M+/yr per island generator switch.
🔌
Philippines: LNG-to-Power
Batangas LNG terminal (First Gen, 2024). Malampaya gas field depleting → LNG import needed. Luzon power shortage looming. First Gen building multiple gas-fired plants. Philippines = poster child for SE Asian LNG-to-power wave.
⚡
Japan: Satellite & Peak
200+ satellite receiving terminals (world's densest network). Gas for city heating, remote island power, industrial CHP. Demand declining: nuclear restarts, solar growth. JERA (Tokyo+Chubu) shifting to ammonia co-firing. LNG imports: 66.5 Mt (2024), down from 83 Mt peak (2014).
🇮🇳
India: CGD + Pilot LNG
4,500+ CNG stations (5th globally). 1.8M+ CNG vehicles. GAIL: 2 SS-LNG liquefaction skids (pilot, 2022). Shell Hazira terminal truck-loading. IOCL, BPCL expanding LNG import. CGD network 295+ authorized GAs. Industrial gas dominated by pipeline CNG, not LNG.
🏭
Thailand + Singapore
Thailand: Map Ta Phut LNG terminal feeds industrial estates + power. PDP targets 53% gas by 2037. Domestic gas declining (peak 87 bcm in 2010). Singapore: 95% gas-fired power (piped gas + LNG). No industrial SS-LNG role — fully piped. Focus on bunkering hub.
Sources: EDL Energy · Pacific Energy · Mine Australia · Pertamina · First Gen · GEM · GAIL · IGU
Australia Mining Energy Transition: LNG → Hybrid Microgrids
TRANSITION
⛽
Phase 1: Diesel
Remote gensets
$25+/MMBtu
→
🧊
Phase 2: LNG
Trucked containers
-30% emissions
→
☀️
Phase 3: Hybrid
LNG + Solar + BESS
55–60% renewable
→
🔋
Phase 4: Grid
Pilbara CUI corridors
2030+ target
EDL Energy (CK Infrastructure)
713 MW total portfolio (AU + US + EU). LNG, CNG, landfill gas, solar, wind hybrids. Agnew gold mine: largest hybrid renewable microgrid for mining (55–60% renewables). Remote energy across Pilbara, Kimberley, Kalgoorlie.
Pacific Energy
Pilbara Minerals Pilgangoora: diesel→LNG conversion, 15-yr BOO contract, 90% diesel displaced. Tropicana gold mine (AngloGold): 24 MW wind + 24 MW solar + battery added to existing 27.5 MW gas plant (completion mid-2025).
Pilbara Energy Transition Plan
WA Government: 4 Common Use Infrastructure (CUI) transmission corridors connecting Pilbara mines to renewables. Burrup Corridor connects LNG + fertilizer plants to grid. Goal: reduce Pilbara's 40%+ share of WA emissions. Renewables currently <2% of Pilbara energy.

Key insight: Australian mining is in Phase 2→3 transition. LNG remains critical for the next 5–10 years as the reliable base in hybrid microgrids, but its share is declining at each site as solar + battery costs fall. The Pilbara CUI corridors (2030+) could eventually displace off-grid LNG entirely for mines near transmission lines — but remote mines in Kimberley, NT, and outback QLD will need LNG for decades.

Sources: Mine Australia · EDL Energy · Pacific Energy/Pilbara Minerals · WA PET Plan · Norton Rose Fulbright
APAC (Ex-China) Industrial SS-LNG: Segment Outlook & Risk Matrix
OUTLOOK
MarketTrendConfidenceKey Insight
🇦🇺 AU MiningStable →HighLNG's role as hybrid microgrid base secure for 5–10 yrs. 100+ off-grid mines. Risk: Pilbara CUI corridors (2030+), falling solar+BESS costs reduce LNG share at each site.
🇮🇩 Indonesia IslandsGrowing ▲High17,000 islands. Diesel replacement = structural opportunity. Pertamina floating regas proven. Genting FLNG FID. Risk: solar+BESS for islands competing, but intermittency limits 24/7 baseload.
🇵🇭 Philippines PowerGrowing ▲HighMalampaya depleting. Batangas terminal online. First Gen building gas plants for Luzon. LNG-to-power is the primary use case. SS-LNG component = small (island/off-grid).
🇯🇵 Japan SatelliteDeclining ▼High200+ satellite terminals but demand falling. Nuclear restarts (12 online). Solar growing. JERA → ammonia. SS-LNG demand peaked in Japan. Structural decline.
🇮🇳 India IndustrialGrowing ▲ (from base ~0)MedGas target 15% (from 6%). GAIL 2 pilot skids. But CNG/CGD dominates. LNG for industry = pilot stage only. Could be transformational by 2030 if CGD + LNG infrastructure scales.
🇹🇭🇻🇳 SE Asia OtherGrowing ▲MedThailand 53% gas target. Vietnam LNG-to-power planned. Bangladesh needs LNG imports. Risk: renewables LCOE falling fast in SE Asia — solar may beat LNG for new power.

APAC (ex-China) in one sentence: A tale of two trajectories — Japan & Korea are declining (nuclear restarts, ammonia pivot, mature infrastructure), while SE Asia & India are growing (island power, LNG-to-power, CGD expansion). Australia is stable but transitioning (LNG → hybrid → grid). The highest-growth SS-LNG industrial opportunity in APAC ex-China is Indonesia's 17,000-island diesel replacement — structurally the most compelling case for SS-LNG anywhere in the region because solar+BESS cannot provide 24/7 baseload on remote tropical islands yet.

Sources: Mordor · Data Bridge · GEM · IGU 2025 · Mine Australia · APEC · EDL · GAIL · Pertamina
LNG Regas Capacity (Aug 2025)
144.4 MMm³/d
From 70.8 MMm³/d (2020). Doubled in 5 years. 9 FSRUs + Suape (0.7 MMm³/d) under construction for 2026.
Source: US EIA (Oct 2025)
Gas-to-Power Mandate
8 GW
Law 14,182/2021 required 8 GW new regionalized gas power contracting. Anchors FSRU investments.
Source: EIA / EPE
Hydro Share of Generation
56%
2024. Reservoirs fell to 29% in key regions. Drought triggers gas dispatch. Gas = hydro insurance.
Source: EIA / ONS
US Share of Brazil LNG
72%
2024. Primarily spot market. Shifting to long-term: Centrica-Petrobras 0.8 Mtpa × 15 years (from 2027).
Source: EIA
Brazil's Gas-to-Power Model: How FSRUs + Thermal Plants Create an SS-LNG Ecosystem
GAS-TO-POWER
🚢
LNG Import
72% from US (spot)
Shifting to long-term
→
🏗️
FSRU Terminal
9 operational
144.4 MMm³/d total
→
⚡ Thermal Plant (anchor)
🏭 Industrial (excess gas)
🚛 VP Distribution
→
💡
Grid Dispatch
Triggers on drought
Reservoirs <40%
The Brazilian Model
Unlike the rest of the world where SS-LNG serves niche markets, Brazil's SS-LNG is anchored by Gas-to-Power. A large thermal plant guarantees demand to make the $1B+ FSRU investment viable. Excess gas is then sold to industry — creating an industrial gas market as a byproduct. This "anchor + spillover" model is why previously gas-deprived regions (North, Northeast) are now getting access. The New Gas Law (14,134/2021) broke Petrobras's monopoly, enabling private developers (NFE, GNA, Eneva, Compass) to build this infrastructure.

Domestic production: 152.9 MMm³/d (2024), 85% offshore. But 54% of gas produced is reinjected for reservoir pressure → net available gas is much less than headline production. This structural reinjection gap is why Brazil needs LNG imports even though it's a significant gas producer.

Sources: US EIA (Oct 2025) · Click Petróleo e Gás · Rystad Energy
Brazil FSRU Network: 9 Terminals + 1 Under Construction
INFRASTRUCTURE
TerminalOperatorCapacity (MMm³/d)Linked Power PlantStatus
Guanabara Bay (RJ)Petrobras0.80Multiple (grid injection)Operational
Barcarena (PA)NFE0.75Novo Tempo 2.2 GW (CELBA 2)Operational
Porto do Açu (RJ)GNA0.74GNA I (1.3 GW) + GNA II (1.7 GW)Operational
Sergipe (SE)Eneva0.74Porto de Sergipe 1.6 GWOperational
Bahia (BA)Petrobras0.71Camaçari ComplexOperational
Terminal Gás Sul (SC)NFE0.50Southern Brazil industrialOp. (2024)
Compass/Cosan (SP)Compass0.50São Paulo industrial + gridOp. (2024)
Sepetiba Bay (RJ)Petrobras0.36Grid injection (flex)Operational
Suape (PE)TBD0.70Northeast industrial + power2026E

Total operational: 5.10 MMm³/d (9 FSRUs). With Suape: 5.80 MMm³/d. Previously Brazil had only 3 Petrobras terminals. The private wave (NFE, GNA, Eneva, Compass) added 6 new terminals in 4 years. Each FSRU is paired with thermal power as anchor demand — the "Gas-to-Power + industrial spillover" model.

Sources: US EIA · NS Energy (Barcarena) · Rystad (GNA II) · GEM Global Gas Tracker
Brazil SS-LNG Industrial Verticals (Beyond Power)
INDUSTRIAL
⚡
Gas-to-Power
Dominant use case (~80%). 8 GW mandated. GNA II (1.7 GW) = Brazil's largest thermal, consuming ~6.4 MMcm/d dry season. Dispatches when hydro reservoirs <40%. Drought 2024: key reservoirs at 29%. Gas = hydro insurance.
🏭
Industrial Off-Pipeline
GásLocal, Compass, GNLink: LNG by trailer to ceramics, glass, food, chemical plants in SE/S Brazil. Growing from near zero as FSRU excess gas becomes available. Camaçari (BA) industrial complex. Novo Mercado do Gás opens access.
🔧
Alumina / Heavy Industry
NFE Barcarena → Norsk Hydro Alunorte: world's largest alumina refinery. Long-term industrial LNG contract. Replaces HFO in Amazon basin. Proof of concept for large-scale industrial SS-LNG in Brazil's North region.
⛏️
Mining
Vale (Carajás PA), Norsk Hydro (Barcarena PA), AngloGold (MG). Mining demand: 0.12 bcm (2026E). Remote Amazon/Minas sites. Diesel replacement for gensets. Small but growing as Barcarena terminal opens gas access to North.
🌱
Domestic Liquefaction
Eneva: Azulão (AM) + Parnaíba (MA) — only utility producing LNG from domestic gas. GNLink: 3 plants (264K m³/d total, R$450M). Alpha LNG + GásLocal fleet. 5 domestic SS-LNG producers vs 9 FSRUs. Domestic supply growing fast.
💻
Data Centers
Nascent. SP + RJ = emerging DC hubs. Equinix, AWS, Azure present. Grid-connected today (no US-style congestion). No behind-the-meter LNG for DCs. Could emerge if SP grid stress increases from industrial + EV load growth.
Sources: EIA · NFE · Rystad · Eneva · GNLink · GásLocal · Compass · EPE
Key Players Reshaping Brazil's Gas Market
PLAYERS
NFE (New Fortress Energy)
Barcarena FSRU (21.2 MMm³/d) + TGS (14.2 MMm³/d). Novo Tempo 2.2 GW power plant. Norsk Hydro Alunorte contract. US-based, Brazil = largest international market. Vertically integrated: LNG sourcing → terminal → power + industry.
GNA (Açu Natural Gas)
Porto do Açu terminal (21.0 MMm³/d). GNA I (1.3 GW) + GNA II (1.7 GW) = 3.0 GW total = Brazil's largest thermal complex. GNA II operations started May 2025. Siemens turbines. 21 MMcm/d regas capacity.
Eneva
Sergipe FSRU (21.0 MMm³/d) + 1.6 GW power plant. Azulão (AM) + Parnaíba (MA) domestic liquefaction. Only company doing both LNG import and domestic SS-LNG production. Vertically integrated upstream → midstream → power.
Compass (Cosan) + GNLink
Compass: SP terminal (14.2 MMm³/d), Comgás distribution. GNLink: 3 plants (264K m³/d), R$450M. Copa Energia entered capital. Both are building the industrial SS-LNG segment — delivering LNG by trailer to off-pipeline factories.
Sources: NFE · GNA · Eneva RI · Compass/Cosan · GNLink · Click P&G
Distribuidoras de Gás Canalizado: R$10B+ Investment Wave & Mercado Livre Revolution
CDLs
📈
1,000% REDE GROWTH
4,000 km (1999) → 45,000+ km (2024). Expanding 3–5%/yr (above GDP). ABEGÁS: R$10B+ investment by CDLs over next 5 years.
🔓
MERCADO LIVRE: 15 MMcm/d
Edge (Cosan): first 6 SP industries migrated 2024. Free market at 15 MMcm/d. Comgás: 60% of non-thermal volume livre by 2026.
🌱
BIOMETANO INTEGRATION
Compagas: 15% biometano by 2026 (100K m³/d). Bahiagás: 2 chamadas. Sulgás: connecting biometano plant RS. CDLs = channel for biometano.
Comgás (Cosan) — SP
Largest in LatAm. 2.6M clients, 21K km, 160K new connections 2024. Necta expanding to S.J. do Rio Preto. Compass terminal Santos = new LNG supply. Cogeração industrial = key growth.
Gasmig — MG
R$314M invested 2025, lucro R$515M. Gasoduto Centro-Oeste: R$217M, 107 km. 115K+ clients, 56 municípios. Industrial+commercial +13.7%. Regulated by Arsae-MG since Jan 2026.
Compagas — PR
R$500M to 2029. Expanding to Londrina + Maringá. Chamada: 250K m³/d gás + biometano (2026). Corredores Sustentáveis: GNV highways to Paranaguá. 15% biometano target.
Bahiagás — BA
V. da Conquista: supplied by GNLink. Gás Sudoeste: Jequié + 9 municipalities. GNC/GNL truck for remote Bahia. 2 chamadas biometano (sugarcane). Cerbras ceramics = first livre client (Mar 2026).
Edge (Cosan) — Mercado Livre
Launched Jan 2024. First 6 SP industrial migrations. Free market: 15 MMcm/d. First Argentine gas import via Bolivia (2025, Tecpetrol). "Second wave" approaching as more states open.
Other CDLs Expanding
ESGás (ES): new 5-yr tariff. Copergás (PE): polo gesseiro Araripina. Potigás (RN): Gás Sal → Areia Branca salinas. Naturgy: Corredores Sustentáveis RJ-SP. Sulgás (RS): biometano. SCGás (SC): industrial.

Why this matters for SS-LNG: CDLs are the last-mile channel for industrial gas. Where CDL networks reach → pipeline wins. Where they DON'T → SS-LNG virtual pipeline fills the gap. The 45,000 km network still doesn't cover vast industrial areas of Bahia interior, MG sertão, Pará, Maranhão. GNLink's model (supplying Bahiagás V. da Conquista + own liquefaction 264K m³/d) = SS-LNG bridge until pipes arrive. CDL interiorização creates the market; SS-LNG serves it first. Same China city gas VP dynamic, at much earlier stage.

Sources: ABEGÁS R$10B CDLs · Gasmig 2025 · Compagas · Bahiagás · Edge · Eixos
Brazil Industrial SS-LNG: Segment Outlook & Risk Matrix
OUTLOOK
SegmentTrendConfidenceKey Insight
⚡ Gas-to-PowerGrowing ▲▲High8 GW mandated by law. 9 FSRUs operational + Suape 2026. Hydro dependency = structural gas demand. GNA II just started. Risk: wet years reduce dispatch. Long-term: wind+solar erode gas share.
🏭 Industrial Off-PipelineGrowing ▲HighFSRU excess gas creates supply. Novo Mercado do Gás opens access. GásLocal, Compass, GNLink expanding. Brazil's industrial SS-LNG barely exists today — almost all growth is ahead.
🔧 Heavy Industry (Alumina)Growing ▲HighNFE-Alunorte = proof of concept. Barcarena opens Amazon to gas. Risk: NFE financial stress, global alumina price cycles.
⛏️ MiningSmall ▲Med0.12 bcm (2026E). Carajás, MG. Diesel replacement. Tiny but growing as Barcarena gas access reaches northern mines.
🌱 Domestic LiquefactionGrowing ▲HighEneva Azulão/Parnaíba + GNLink 3 plants. Domestic gas → LNG for VP distribution. Growing fast, but still dwarfed by FSRU import capacity.
💻 Data CentersMinimal →MedGrid works in Brazil today. No US-style queue crisis. Could emerge if SP grid stress increases from industrial + EV growth. Not a factor before 2030.

Brazil's SS-LNG story in one sentence: Gas-to-Power is the anchor that builds the infrastructure; industrial off-pipeline is the growth segment that emerges from the "spillover" gas. Brazil went from 3 Petrobras terminals to 9+ FSRUs in 4 years — a silent revolution. The New Gas Law + 8 GW mandate + private capital (NFE, GNA, Eneva, Compass) transformed Brazil from a gas-poor country to one with 144.4 MMm³/d of LNG regas capacity. The industrial SS-LNG market is just beginning — almost all growth is ahead of us. CAGR ~28% makes Brazil the fastest-growing SS-LNG market in the world.

Sources: US EIA · Rystad · NFE · GNA · Eneva · Compass · GNLink · Click P&G · GEM · EPE

Virtual Pipeline & Off-Grid Power

A virtual pipeline replaces physical pipeline infrastructure by transporting LNG or CNG via truck, rail, barge, or ISO container to off-grid locations. Serving remote mines, islands, data centers, construction sites, and pipeline-constrained regions — it is one of the fastest-growing segments of the SS-LNG value chain, valued at $2.9B globally (2024).

Global LNG Virtual Pipeline Market
$2.9B
2024 · Projected $3.6B by 2030 (CAGR 3.9%)
Source: Research & Markets
North America VP Market
$0.99B
2023 · 40% of global share · CAGR 5.29% to $1.37B by 2029
Source: ResearchAndMarkets
Diesel Savings
30–50%
Fuel cost reduction vs diesel for off-grid power · CO₂ reduction ~25–30%
Source: Aggreko
Delivered Power Cost
$0.09–0.14/kWh
On-site LNG/CNG generation · Often equal to or less than utility grid rates
Source: NGI (Dec 2025)
Virtual Pipeline Delivery Modes at a Glance
MODES
🚛
LNG Truck
DOMINANT MODE
~826 Dth (~11K gal). Range: up to 300 mi. Deploy in days. $250-450K/trailer. 30-50% cheaper vs diesel. Best for: mines, power, data centers, industry.
🔵
CNG Truck
SHORT HAUL
450-550 Dth. Range: up to 60 mi. Deploy in days. $150-300K/trailer. 40-60% cheaper vs diesel. Best for: urban fringe, peak shaving, fleet fueling.
📦
LNG ISO Container
INTERMODAL
~36,000 kg (45K liters). Global intermodal. Deploy in weeks. $80-150K/unit. 20-40% cheaper vs diesel. Best for: islands, export markets, temporary sites.
🚢
LNG Barge / Small Carrier
COASTAL
1,000-30,000 m³. Coastal/island delivery. Deploy in months. $5-50M/vessel. 30-50% cheaper vs diesel. Best for: island power, coastal industrial.
Sources: NGI (Dec 2025) · Aggreko · Wärtsilä / Engblom

HOW IT WORKS
ParameterLNG TruckCNG TruckLNG ISO ContainerLNG Barge / Small Carrier
Payload~826 Dth (~11K gal)450–550 Dth~36,000 kg (45K liters)1,000–30,000 m³
Optimal RangeUp to 300 milesUp to 60 milesIntermodal (global)Coastal / island
Deployment SpeedDaysDaysWeeksMonths
Equipment Cost$250–450K per trailer$150–300K per trailer$80–150K per container$5–50M per vessel
Best ForMines, industrial, power, data centersUrban fringe, peak shaving, fleet fuelingIslands, export markets, temporary sitesIsland power, coastal industrial
Delivered Gas Cost2.5–3× pipeline gas1.5–2× pipeline gas3–4× pipeline gas2–3× pipeline gas
vs Diesel30–50% cheaper40–60% cheaper20–40% cheaper30–50% cheaper
Sources: NGI (Dec 2025) · Aggreko · Wärtsilä / Engblom
Stabilis Data Center Contract
$200M
Multi-year take-or-pay. "Five nines" (99.999%) reliability. On-site LNG bridges grid shortfalls 30-35 days/yr.
Source: Stabilis IR
Eneva Azulão (Brazil)
490 tpd
33 Galileo Cryoboxes. Largest Galileo facility globally. 1,100 km LNG delivery to Boa Vista (Roraima). Replaced diesel in isolated grid.
Source: Galileo
US Peak Shaving Facilities
~80
50% NE, 25% Midwest. 1.3B gal/yr capacity. Jan 2025 polar vortex: record 3,591 Mm³/d consumption.
Source: NGI
SpaceX LNG Supply
~40%
Stabilis supplies ~40% of US high-purity LNG to space launch. SpaceX = top-3 customer (>10% of FY2025 revenue). +65% demand over 5 yrs.
Source: Stabilis IR
Virtual Pipeline Deployment Landscape
DEPLOYMENTS
⛏️
Remote Mining
PROVEN
Aggreko BOOM model. $0 upfront. 6-10 MW. 30-50% vs diesel. 3-7 yr contracts. 100+ countries. Australia, LatAm, Africa.
🖥️
Data Centers
FASTEST GROWING
Stabilis $200M take-or-pay. "Five nines" reliability. $0.09-0.14/kWh. Grid wait 3-7 yrs → LNG in months. CNG for backup.
🌿
Stranded Gas → Power
LANDMARK
Eneva Azulão: 33 Cryoboxes, 490 tpd, 1,100 km. Replaced diesel in Roraima isolated grid. Galileo Mendoza: 120 tpd → 40 MW.
🚀
Aerospace + Military
NICHE HIGH-VALUE
SpaceX: Stabilis top-3 customer. 40% of US high-purity LNG. USAF: $560M savings over 22 yrs. +65% demand over 5 yrs.
🚢
Marine Bunkering
SCALING
Carnival = Stabilis top-3. Two 10-yr SPAs. Galveston: 480K gpd, $350-400M. Jones Act vessel. DOE 1.47 bcm/yr export auth.
🏝️
Island + Peak Shaving
ESTABLISHED
Islands: diesel $20+/MMBtu. NFE Jamaica/PR. 17K+ islands (Indonesia). ~80 US peak-shaving facilities. Jan 2025 polar vortex record.
Key Use Cases & Real-World Deployments
USE CASES
Use CaseRegionOperator / ExampleKey Details
Remote MiningGlobal (Australia, LatAm, Africa)Aggreko (BOOM model)3–7 year mine-life contracts. $0 upfront for customer. 6–10 MW typical loads. Fuel savings 30–50% vs diesel. CO₂ reduction ~25%. Aggreko designs, installs, and operates off-grid virtual power plants across 100+ countries. CNG/LNG delivery by road, rail, sea. Also supplies construction camps and drilling pads.
Data Center BackupUS (nationwide)Stabilis / Sapphire GasStabilis multi-year take-or-pay data center contract (~$200M revenue, 2027–29). "Five nines" (99.999%) reliability. LNG stored on-site, bridges grid shortfalls 30–35 days/yr. On-site gen = $0.09–0.14/kWh. CNG: 180–200 cylinders per 100 MW (~1 acre). LNG better for >60 mi radius. At 1 GW scale → ~300 trucks/day, needs pipeline.
Stranded Gas → Power (Brazil)Brazil (Amazon)Eneva + Galileo TechnologiesLandmark project: Eneva liquefies gas at Azulão field (Amazonia) using 33 interconnected Galileo Cryoboxes (490 tpd — largest Galileo facility globally). LNG transported 1,100 km in cryo isotanks to Jaguatirica II power plant in Boa Vista (Roraima), replacing diesel in isolated grid. Avoided pipeline through indigenous territories.
Off-Grid Power (Argentina)Argentina (Mendoza)Galileo Technologies9 Cryobox units at La Mora (TGN pipeline) produce 120 tpd LNG. Supplies Methax 40 MW thermal plant in Luján de Cuyo via Virtual Pipeline™ continuously. Also: Buquebus ferry expanded to 9 Cryoboxes for LNG-powered "Francisco" vessel (Argentina→Uruguay daily).
Commercial AerospaceUS (launch sites)Stabilis SolutionsSpaceX = top-3 Stabilis customer (>10% of FY2025 revenue). Supplies ~40% of US high-purity LNG to space launch providers. Domestic demand for high-purity LNG expected +65% over 5 yrs to ~25M gal/yr. Marine + aerospace grew from 11% to ~50% of Stabilis revenue (Q3 2023→Q4 2024).
Marine Bunkering (Last-Mile)US Gulf CoastStabilis SolutionsCarnival Corporation = top-3 customer (>10% of FY2025 revenue). Two 10-yr bunkering agreements with cruise operators (from 2027). Galveston project: 480K gpd, $350–400M capex, 56% committed. Jones Act bunkering vessel. DOE: 1.47 bcm/yr export authorization for 28 years.
Island PowerCaribbean, Pacific, SE AsiaNFE, Eagle LNG, AggrekoDiesel $20+/MMBtu on islands. NFE targets Jamaica, Puerto Rico (Galileo Cryoboxes for RNG from waste). Eagle LNG Jacksonville targeting Caribbean. Philippines/Indonesia: 17,000+ islands with FSRU + tank truck. Galileo: Colombia stranded gas-to-power with Canacol.
Military / GovernmentUSUS Air ForceLNG across bases: forecast savings $560M over 22 years + cut 14,619 tCO₂/yr. Replaces diesel generators at remote installations. Clean Energy Fuels Boron hub (270K gal/day) supports fleet + base supply.
Pipeline SupplementationUS NortheastUtilities / LDCs~80 peak-shaving facilities (50% NE, 25% Midwest). 1.3B gal/yr capacity. Jan 2025 polar vortex record consumption (3,591 Mm³/d). Virtual pipeline supplements constrained pipelines. Satellite LNG plants refilled by truck.
Flare Capture / Stranded GasUS Permian, ColombiaEDGE LNG + GalileoEDGE: 290 tpd mobile fleet, 10+ Cryoboxes at Permian well sites. Captures flare gas at source → LNG → truck. No gathering systems needed. Galileo Cryobox runs on NG engines (no grid required). Colombia: Canacol stranded gas-to-power project.
Peru / LatAm DomesticLatin AmericaPeru LNG / GalileoPeru LNG: 2nd truck-loading bay opened Jun 2024, doubling distribution to off-grid customers. Galileo: 30+ years, patented Virtual Pipeline™ (1999), 70 countries. Cryobox™ cuts CAPEX up to 50% vs conventional mini-LNG. Target: 300+ Cryoboxes deployed over 5 yrs.
Sources: Aggreko · NGI · Mordor Intelligence · Stabilis IR · EIA
Brazil Off-Grid Municipalities
~90%
~90% of Brazilian municipalities have zero piped gas. 200+ cities >100K pop without access. LDC virtual pipeline = the expansion strategy.
Source: ABEGÁS
Colombia Gas Penetration
53%
Residential gas penetration (vs Brazil 24%). Promigas used VP to reach inland Andes cities. 10M+ connected households. VP was the key enabler.
Source: Brasil Energia
China Urban Gas Penetration
~95%
Achieved via combined pipeline + VP strategy. 300+ SS-LNG plants feed truck distribution to tier 3-4 cities. PipeChina builds "last-mile" networks.
Source: Mordor Intelligence
GNLink Pipeline (Brazil)
15 Projects
Mapped for 4 MMm³/d over 10 years. First plant: 88K m³/d at Barra Bonita (Paraná). Also: LNG cabotage with Norsul. NE onshore gas + imported LNG.
Source: epbr
LDC Virtual Pipeline: The Hub-and-Spoke Model
MODEL
🏭
1. Hub
FSRU, import terminal, or SS-LNG liquefaction plant. Truck-loading bay.
→
🚛
2. Truck Delivery
LNG/CNG cryo trailer. 500-1,000+ km range. Daily/weekly cycles.
→
🔄
3. Satellite Regas
Small regas station at city edge. LNG → gas. Storage + vaporization.
→
🏗️
4. Local Pipe Network
LDC builds small distribution network. Industry + residential + CNG stations.
→
📈
5. Scale → Trunk Pipe
If >100K m³/d demand, justifies permanent pipeline. VP = pathfinder.
🇧🇷
Brazil
GLOBAL LAB
90% off-grid. 200+ cities >100K pop. Copergás Petrolina, Compagas Londrina, Compass TRSP, GNLink 15 projects. Lei do Gás enabled.
🇨🇴
Colombia
SUCCESS STORY
53% gas penetration (vs Brazil 24%). Promigas used VP to reach inland Andes. 10M+ households. Proof the model works at scale.
🇨🇳
China
~95% PENETRATION
300+ SS-LNG plants → truck → tier 3-4 cities → last-mile pipe. PipeChina subsidiaries. Combined pipeline + VP achieved near-universal urban gas.
🇮🇳
India
CGD EXPANDING
GAIL → 300+ areas. 4,500 new CNG stations. Largest CNG fleet globally. CNG trucked to satellite stations. 46% YoY vehicle sales growth.
🇵🇪
Peru
ANDEAN MODEL
Peru LNG doubled truck-loading (Jun 2024). Andes terrain → pipeline too costly. VP = only viable path to interior gas. Quavii/Contugas in Ica, Arequipa.
🇵🇹
Portugal (Madeira)
ISLAND MODEL
Gáslink: Sines → ISO containers → ship → Madeira → truck → satellite regas → Vitória power + local distribution. Replicable Atlantic/Pacific.
LDC Virtual Pipeline: Expanding Gas Distribution Without Laying Pipe
LDC STRATEGY

In markets with low pipeline penetration, local distribution companies (LDCs) use virtual pipelines as a strategic tool to expand their concession footprint. The model: an LNG/CNG supplier delivers gas by truck to a satellite regasification station at the edge of the LDC's network. From there, the LDC builds a small local distribution pipeline to serve residential, commercial, industrial, and CNG vehicle customers. The virtual pipeline acts as the "last-mile bridge" — proving demand before committing to expensive trunk pipeline investment. Brazil is the global laboratory for this model.

ConceptHow It Works
Proof of DemandVirtual pipeline delivers gas to a city with no pipeline access. The LDC builds a small distribution network and connects customers. If demand reaches sufficient scale (typically >100K m³/d), a permanent trunk pipeline becomes economically viable. The virtual pipeline de-risks the investment by demonstrating real consumption before major CAPEX.
Concession ExtensionLDCs hold exclusive distribution concessions for their state/region. Virtual pipelines allow them to serve cities within their concession area that are hundreds of kilometers from their existing network — without waiting years for pipeline construction. This is critical for meeting regulatory expansion targets in concession contracts.
Competing with LPG/DieselIn unserved cities, industries burn LPG, fuel oil, or diesel at 2–5× the cost of piped gas. Virtual pipeline LNG/CNG is typically 30–50% cheaper than these incumbent fuels, creating immediate customer demand. The LDC captures market share from LPG distributors who previously had a monopoly in remote areas.
Hub-and-Spoke ModelAn LNG import terminal, FSRU, or liquefaction plant serves as the "hub." Truck-loaded LNG is distributed to multiple satellite regas stations (the "spokes") in different cities within the LDC's concession. Each spoke serves a local pipeline mini-network. This model can cover 500–1,000+ km from the hub.
Transition PathVirtual → Physical: once a city's gas consumption reaches critical mass, the LDC justifies building a conventional pipeline connection to the trunk network. The satellite regas station is then decommissioned or repurposed. The virtual pipeline served as a "pathfinder" that proved the commercial case for permanent infrastructure.
Sources: Brasil Energia · ABEGÁS · Galileo Insights
Brazil: The Global Laboratory for LDC Virtual Pipelines
BRAZIL

90% of Brazilian municipalities have no piped natural gas (ABEGÁS). Over 200 cities with >100,000 inhabitants lack gas access. These cities rely on expensive LPG, fuel oil, and diesel. Brazil's Lei do Gás (2021) opened the market, enabling new suppliers to compete with Petrobras and empowering LDCs to source gas from multiple providers — including SS-LNG distributors operating virtual pipelines.

LDC / OperatorStateVP ProjectKey Details
Copergás
(Compass/Cosan)		PernambucoPetrolina Structuring ProjectFirst structuring network in NE Brazil. Petrolina is 750 km from Recife — unreachable by pipeline. Golar Power (now NFE/Macaw) supplies LNG via truck from Suape terminal. 40 km local pipeline network built. Serves industry, commerce, CNG stations, residential. $2M initial Golar investment. 40K m³/d initial volume.
Compagas
(Compass/Cosan)		ParanáLondrina & Maringá Expansion30-year concession (2024) with R$2.5B investment plan. Londrina + Maringá operations starting 2025 — initially with biometano (100% renewable). GNLink building 88K m³/d liquefaction plant at Barra Bonita field to supply Compagas. Also: 52 km Araucária→Lapa pipeline for biodiesel plant.
Compass / TRSP
(Cosan Group)		São PauloSantos SS-LNG Distribution HubTerminal de Regaseificação de São Paulo (TRSP): 14M m³/d capacity. Höegh Giant FSRU. Plans for SS-LNG distribution center at Port of Santos — trucks loaded with LNG directly from terminal. Targets industrial B2B customers + structuring projects of neighboring LDC concessions.
GásLocal
(White Martins / Linde)		MultiplePioneer SS-LNG distributorBrazil's only SS-LNG truck distributor until early 2020s. White Martins subsidiary (Linde Group). Delivers LNG by trailer to industrial customers and LDC satellite stations. Now facing competition from Compass, GNLink, NFE, and others.
GNLink
(Lorinvest Group)		Paraná / NE15 projects mapped (4M m³/d over 10 years)SS-LNG distributor. 88K m³/d first plant at Barra Bonita (Tradener onshore gas, Paraná). 8 priority projects. Contracted with Compagas for Londrina. Partnership with Migratio (biometano). Plans for LNG coastal cabotage with Norsul (2025). Also targets NE onshore gas and imported LNG.
Eneva / Virtu GNLNorth / NEAzulão → Jaguatirica II + distributionEneva operates the landmark Azulão→Jaguatirica II virtual pipeline (33 Galileo Cryoboxes, 490 tpd, 1,100 km). Virtu GNL (Eneva subsidiary) plans to expand SS-LNG distribution in the North region from Azulão field production.
Origem Energia / Alpha LNGAlagoas30K m³/d distribution centerAlpha LNG has ANP authorization to build SS-LNG distribution center in Alagoas. Targets industrial and commercial customers in the NE interior without pipeline access.
bp / Porto do AçuRio de JaneiroGNL hub at Porto do Açubp developing SS-LNG distribution from Porto do Açu industrial complex. Targets NE and interior markets via truck-delivered LNG. Deepwater port with LNG import capability.
NE LDCs
(Algás, Cegás, Potigás, Sergas)		AL, CE, RN, SEJoint procurement + interiorizaçãoFive NE LDCs launched joint chamada pública to source gas from new suppliers. Shell, Compass, Golar, PetroReconcavo bid. Being sold to Infra Gás e Energia. Goal: extend distribution to interior cities via virtual pipeline.
Sources: ABEGÁS · Gas Week / Eixos · epbr · Suape / Copergás
LDC Virtual Pipeline Model: Global Examples Beyond Brazil
GLOBAL
CountryLDC / OperatorModelKey Details
ColombiaPromigas, VantiCNG/LNG truck → city gate → local networkPromigas (largest Colombian gas distributor) uses VP to reach inland Andes cities. Colombia: 53% residential gas penetration (vs Brazil 24%) — VP was key to achieving this. 10M+ connected households. Model replicable across LatAm.
IndiaGAIL, IGL, Mahanagar Gas, Adani GasCity Gas Distribution (CGD) + CNG truckedGAIL expanding to 300+ geographic areas. CNG trucked to satellite stations. 4,500 new CNG stations planned. 46% YoY CNG vehicle sales growth (H1 2024). India = largest CNG vehicle fleet globally.
ChinaPipeChina, Kunlun Energy, ENNSS-LNG plant → truck → city gate → local pipe300+ SS-LNG plants feed truck distribution to inland tier 3–4 cities. PipeChina subsidiaries build "last-mile" local networks. China achieved ~95% urban gas penetration via combined pipeline + VP strategy.
PeruPeru LNG, Quavii, ContugasLNG truck-loading → regas → city distributionPeru LNG doubled truck-loading (Jun 2024). Quavii/Contugas distribute in Ica, Arequipa. Andean geography makes pipeline extremely costly. VP = only viable path to interior gas access.
Portugal (Madeira)GáslinkISO container by ship → island regas → powerLNG from Sines terminal → ISO containers → ship to Madeira → truck to satellite → regas → Vitória power plant + local distribution. Classic island VP model replicable across Atlantic/Pacific.
Malaysia (Sabah)Gas Malaysia VP Sdn. Bhd.CNG truck → remote distributionDedicated virtual pipeline subsidiary of Gas Malaysia. CNG trucked to industrial/commercial customers in East Malaysia (Sabah, Sarawak) where no pipeline exists. Government-backed.
ArgentinaGalileo + gas distributorsCryobox → truck → city gate regasGalileo patented Virtual Pipeline™ (1999). Mendoza Methax: 9 Cryoboxes → 120 tpd. Targets 15–30 units in South America by 2025. Vaca Muerta shale gas surplus = growing feedstock for domestic VP distribution.
Sources: Brasil Energia · Gáslink · Galileo Insights

PLAYERS
CompanyRoleKey Data

Stabilis Solutions
NASDAQ: SLNG		SS-LNG production + last-mile580M+ gallons LNG delivered over 22-year history. ~20 customers. Top 3 (>10% each, FY2025): Carnival Corp, Aggreko, SpaceX. 130K gpd liquefaction (TX/LA). Galveston expansion: 480K gpd, $350–400M capex, 56% committed, 2 × 10-yr cruise bunkering SPAs (2027). Data center take-or-pay ~$200M (2027–29). DOE: 1.47 bcm/yr export authorization (28 yrs). 85 employees. FY2024: $73.3M revenue, $11.8M adj. EBITDA. FY2025: marine bunkering (32% of rev) + remote power (19% of rev).
Sapphire Gas SolutionsCNG/LNG mobile deliveryFleet of high-pressure CNG trailers + LNG transport. Serves data centers, industrials, utilities. CNG within 60-mile radius; LNG for longer haul. Carbon-fiber cylinder technology. Partnered with data center operators for "five nines" backup.

NASDAQ: NFE		Integrated LNG-to-powerBarcarena FSRU (Amazon). Jamaica/Puerto Rico island power. Sold Miami SS-LNG facility (Jul 2024) to focus on downstream. Fast LNG modular liquefaction technology. End-to-end: liquefaction → shipping → regas → power.
Chart Industries
NYSE: GTLS		Equipment OEMCryogenic storage tanks, LNG trailers, micro-liquefaction, mobile regas units. ISO containers. Being acquired by Baker Hughes ($13.6B, Jul 2025). Supplies equipment to Stabilis, Aggreko, utilities globally. Woodside LNG cold boxes contract.
EDGE LNGMobile SS-LNG fleet290 tpd mobile LNG fleet in Texas. Serves Permian Basin oilfield services. Truck-delivered LNG for well pads, drilling, frac operations. Rapid deployment model.
Hydria Gas TechnologiesCNG storage systemsCarbon fiber-wrapped CNG cylinders. 180–200 cylinders per 100 MW data center site (30–35 pods, ~1 acre). "Battery of gas" concept for peak shaving and backup.
Clean Energy Fuels
NASDAQ: CLNE		RNG/LNG fueling networkBoron LNG hub: 270K gal/day. Largest RNG/LNG fueling network in North America. 570+ stations. Also supplies virtual pipeline for fleet and industrial customers.
Galileo Technologies
HQ: Buenos Aires + NJ		Virtual Pipeline™ pioneerFounded 1987. Patented Virtual Pipeline™ concept (1999). Inventor of Cryobox® modular liquefaction (40-ft container, 10–20 tpd per unit, scalable in parallel). Largest facility: Eneva/Azulão Brazil (33 Cryoboxes, 490 tpd). Also: Argentina Methax 40 MW (9 units, 120 tpd), Buquebus ferry (9 units), Colombia Canacol, Puerto Rico RNG. EDGE LNG uses Galileo Cryoboxes in Permian. Operations in 70 countries. Cuts CAPEX up to 50% vs conventional. Target: 300+ Cryoboxes deployed over 5 years. Also makes CNG compressors, biogas upgrading, H₂ systems.
Sources: Aggreko · Stabilis IR · NGI · Mordor Intelligence
BOOM Model (Customer Upfront)
$0
Build-Own-Operate-Maintain. Provider (Aggreko/NFE) bears all CAPEX. Customer pays only per MMBtu consumed (energy-as-a-service). 3-7 yr contracts. Deploy in 3 months. 6-10 MW loads.
Source: Aggreko
On-Site Generation Cost
$0.09–0.14/kWh
Behind-the-meter gas gen from LNG/CNG. Equal to or less than grid. At grid wait of 3-7 yrs for data centers, LNG is the fastest path to power.
Source: NGI (Dec 2025)
US Military LNG Savings
$560M
Forecast savings over 22 years. US Air Force LNG across bases. Cuts 14,619 tCO₂/yr. Replaces diesel generators at remote installations.
Source: Mordor Intelligence

~16%
FY2024: $11.8M adj. EBITDA on $73.3M revenue. Expanding with data center + cruise contracts. Galveston FID = step-change in scale.

Substitute Products & Competing Fuels

LNG competes with a range of fuels across its end-use verticals — diesel and HFO in power/marine, LPG in heating/cooking, coal in industrial, methanol and ammonia in shipping's decarbonization race, and grid electricity everywhere.

LNG Energy Density
50 MJ/kg
vs Methanol 20 MJ/kg · Ammonia 18.6 MJ/kg · Diesel 45.6 MJ/kg
Source: Ship & Bunker
LNG vs HFO Cost (Marine)
1.8–2.2×
LNG costs 1.8–2.2× HFO. But methanol = 2.3–2.8× and ammonia = 3.5–4.5×
Source: Frontiers (2025)
LNG-Fueled Vessel Orders (2024)
58%
Of alt-fuel newbuilds by DWT · Methanol = 30% · Conventional = ~0%
Source: Clarksons / Frontiers
LNG vs Diesel (Off-Grid Power)
30–50% cheaper
Virtual pipeline delivered. CO₂ -25%. SOx near zero.
Source: Aggreko
Energy Density Comparison (MJ/kg)
ENERGY
Marine Fuel Cost Index vs HFO (×)
MARINE COST
Comprehensive Substitute Fuel Comparison
COMPARISON
FuelEnergy DensityCost vs LNGCO₂ vs LNGPrimary ApplicationsLNG's Competitive Position
Diesel / Gasoil45.6 MJ/kg1.5–3× more (off-grid)+25–30%Transport, off-grid power, marine (MGO), militaryLNG wins on cost (30–50% savings) and emissions in off-grid/power.
HFO40.5 MJ/kgLNG = 1.8–2.2× HFO-20–25%Marine bunker, power plantsMixed — HFO cheaper but phased out by IMO sulphur cap.
LPG46.4 MJ/kg10–15% more expensive+10–15%Heating/cooking, small industrialLNG wins at scale — LPG wins decentralized.
Coal24 MJ/kgCheaper raw fuel-40–50%Power gen, industrial heat, steelLNG wins on emissions — regulatory pressure driving switch.
Methanol20 MJ/kg2.3–2.8× HFOSimilar (grey)Marine (30% of newbuilds), chemicalsKey competitor — easier handling but lower energy density.
Ammonia18.6 MJ/kg3.5–4.5× HFOZero (green)Future marine, power co-firingLNG wins today — ammonia not viable before ~2035.
Hydrogen120 MJ/kg3–5× more expensiveZero (green)Fuel cells, blue H₂ from NG+CCUSLNG wins today — worst volumetric density.
Electricity3,412 BTU/kWh$0.05–0.30/kWhGrid-dependentMotors, heat pumps, EVsMixed — grid queue 5 yrs for DCs. LNG wins off-grid.
Firewood/Biomass16 MJ/kgCheapest raw+80–100%Rural heating/cooking (2.3B people)LNG wins on health — 3.2M deaths/yr from indoor pollution.
Bio-LNG/RNG50 MJ/kg1.8–5× fossil LNG-80–100%Marine (FuelEU), HD truckingComplement — drop-in, extends LNG relevance.
Solar+BESSN/A$0.15/kWh off-gridZeroOff-grid daytime, microgridsMixed — gas = 24/7 baseload. Hybrid optimal.
LNG Competitive Positioning by End-Use Vertical
POSITIONING
VerticalLNG DisplacesLNG Threatened ByNet Position
Marine BunkeringHFO, VLSFO, MGOMethanol (30%), ammonia (post-2035)Strong through 2035
HD TruckingDieselBEV (urban), H₂ fuel cell, RNGStrong in long-haul
Off-Grid PowerDiesel generatorsSolar+BESS, grid, SMRs (2030+)Dominant
Data CentersGrid (unavailable), dieselGrid expansion, nuclear, BESSGrowing fast
Industrial HeatCoal, HFO, LPGElectrification, H₂ (2030s)Strong >200°C
Residential HeatingFuel oil, coal, firewoodHeat pumps, district heatingDeclining (developed)
Island PowerDiesel ($20+/MMBtu)Solar+BESS, micro-hydroStrong
Peak ShavingOil peakersBESS (4-hr), demand responseStable but flat

Diesel & Fuel Oil: Deep Competitive Analysis vs LNG
ANALYSIS
DimensionDiesel / Gasoil / MGOHFO / HSFOVLSFO (0.5% S)LNG's Position
Energy Density45.6 MJ/kg40.5 MJ/kg~42 MJ/kgLNG = 50 MJ/kg (gravimetric). But worse volumetric density due to cryogenic storage. Diesel wins on tankage volume. LNG needs ~1.8× tank volume for same energy.
Marine Price~$700–900/mt~$450–550/mt~$650–800/mtLNG = $650/mt (Singapore, H1 2025). Now cheaper than VLSFO at major ports. But requires cryogenic tanks ($2–5M retrofit). Scrubber+HSFO = cheapest short-term but adds water pollution.
Off-Grid Power$0.20–0.35/kWh$0.15–0.25/kWhN/ALNG virtual pipeline: $0.09–0.14/kWh. 30–50% cheaper than diesel. China: LNG displaced 612K b/d of gasoil in 2024. Brazil: Eneva Azulão replaced diesel in Roraima isolated grid.
Emissions (CO₂)73 kg CO₂/MMBtu78 kg CO₂/MMBtu~75 kg CO₂/MMBtuLNG = 53 kg CO₂/MMBtu. 20–30% less CO₂. Near-zero SOx. -85% NOx. But methane slip (2–4% in legacy LPDF engines) partially offsets. NextDF reduces slip to 1.1%.
InfrastructureUbiquitous. Every port, every gas station.Every major port. Declining with IMO 2020.Growing. 72M+ mt in circulation globally.LNG: 222 bunkering ports, 62 bunkering vessels, 500+ EU truck stations, 1,500+ China stations, 600+ US stations. Growing but still fraction of diesel infra.
Regulatory TrendTightening (Euro VII, EPA MY2027, CARB ACT)Phasing out (IMO 2020 sulphur cap, Arctic ban)Growing as IMO 2020 compliance fuelLNG benefits from every tightening of diesel/HFO regulations. FuelEU Maritime, EU ETS, CARB LCFS all favor LNG over diesel. But IMO 2040 targets (-70–80%) may challenge fossil LNG post-2035.
Where LNG WinsOff-grid power (30–50% savings). Long-haul trucking (China 1M fleet). Mining (Aggreko 100+ countries). Data centers ($0.09–0.14/kWh). Island power (diesel $20+/MMBtu).Strong displacement in every off-grid and emissions-sensitive application. Diesel retains advantage only where LNG infrastructure doesn't exist yet.
Where LNG LosesUrban delivery (diesel trucks + EVs beat LNG). Short-sea routes (diesel simpler). Backup generators (diesel refuel = minutes, LNG = hours). Construction sites (diesel portability).Diesel's ubiquitous infrastructure and simpler handling keep it dominant in short-duration, small-scale applications. LNG needs volume + route density to justify cryo investment.
Sources: Mordor Intelligence · Fortune Business Insights · Allied Market Research · Global Market Statistics · Aggreko · S&P Global
Global Diesel & Gasoil Market: Scale & Sector Breakdown
MARKET
SectorShare of DemandKey MetricsLNG Displacement Potential
Freight Transport (Road)~45%280M HDVs globally. US: 122B gallons/yr (68% of national diesel). 72% of commercial fleets still diesel-powered. China: gasoil demand fell 2.5% in 2024 as LNG + BEV trucks displaced diesel.High — China 1M LNG trucks. EU 600+ LNG stations. Cummins X15N (US). But BEV trucks are the bigger threat to diesel (and to LNG).
Industrial & Manufacturing~27%Off-pipeline factories, process heating, kilns, furnaces. 68% of construction equipment runs on diesel. Mining = 35% of Africa's diesel.High — LNG virtual pipeline displaces diesel at $0.09–0.14/kWh vs diesel $0.20–0.35/kWh. Brazil ceramics, India glass, China industrial park satellite stations.
Power Generation~12%61% of backup generators run on diesel. Islands, remote mines, off-grid communities. Brazil: Roraima isolated grid was 100% diesel before Eneva Azulão LNG. Developing world: diesel gensets are the default.High — LNG-to-power 30–50% cheaper than diesel gensets. Barcarena 2.2 GW, GNA II 1.7 GW (Brazil). But solar+BESS increasingly competitive for daytime/steady load.
Agriculture & Off-Road~10%75M agricultural machines. Tractors, irrigation pumps, harvesters. 58% of US agricultural equipment is diesel. India: diesel pumps dominate smallholder irrigation.Low — Agriculture = diesel's most defensible segment. LNG infeasible for small, mobile farm equipment. Electrification (e-tractors) is the long-term threat, not LNG.
Marine~6%MGO/MDO for coastal, short-sea, ferries. Distinct from HFO/VLSFO (deep-sea). MGO: ~$750–800/mt (2025). Cleaner but expensive vs VLSFO.Med — LNG wins on long routes (CMA CGM, MSC, Carnival). MGO retains coastal/short-sea where LNG tanks are impractical. Methanol emerging as competitor to both.

Global diesel market: ~$1.1–1.2T (2025). Transportation = 44.7% of revenue. Asia-Pacific = 45.4% of consumption. CAGR 2.7–4.4% (varies by source). 38M tonnes of renewable diesel produced in 2024. 23+ nations with biodiesel blending mandates.

Sources: Grand View Research · Industry Research · Fortune BI · IEA
Marine Bunker Fuel Landscape: VLSFO vs HSFO vs LNG (2024–2025)
MARINE
FuelMarket Share (2024)Avg Price ($/mt)Key Dynamics
VLSFO (0.5% S)54.3%~$540–585Post-IMO 2020 compliance fuel. No engine modification needed. Dominates because of simplicity. But VLSFO share falling at Rotterdam (from 60% to 36% in H1 2025) as scrubber-equipped ships switch back to cheaper HSFO. EU ETS adds $170–210/mt for intra-EU voyages. FuelEU Maritime adds -2% GHG intensity target from 2025.
HSFO (3.5% S)29.5%~$450–480Requires scrubber installation ($2–5M). Scrubber payback accelerating: VLSFO-HSFO spread was $145/mt in Q1 2024, compressed to $73/mt by Q4 2024, stabilized at $83–91/mt in 2025. HSFO demand overtook VLSFO at Rotterdam in Q1 2025 — first time since IMO 2020. Refinery upgrades shrinking residual fuel output = long-term HSFO supply squeeze.
MGO / LSMGO14.2%~$750–800Marine Gas Oil. Cleanest conventional fuel. Used in ECAs (Baltic, North Sea, US coast). Singapore LSMGO sales rising (416K mt in Feb 2025). Premium over VLSFO = $200–260/mt. Used as backup/blending fuel for many vessels.
LNG~1.5%~$650/mt eq638 LNG-powered vessels in 2024, orderbook pointing to 1,200 by 2028 (Mordor). 31.6% forecast CAGR. Singapore LNG bunkering: 30K mt in Feb 2025, +11.5% YoY. Rotterdam + Singapore = anchor hubs. 62+ bunkering vessels globally. CMA CGM 77 DF ships. Carnival 10+ LNG cruise ships. Bio-LNG share jumped to 71% of total LNG usage at some operators in 2025 (up from 31% in 2024).
Biofuels (B24/B100)~0.8%~$900–1,200Singapore: VLSFO bioblend surged to 85K mt in Feb 2025 (from 15.6K mt a year earlier). B100 = 3.8K mt (record). FuelEU Maritime creates premium demand. Neste expanding bio-marine supply in NE Europe. Expensive but counts toward carbon compliance.
Methanol<0.1%~$400–500Pilot stage. Singapore targets 1M mt/yr methanol bunkering by 2030. ISO 6583:2024 standard issued. Maersk ordering 19 methanol-capable vessels. Rotterdam emerging as methanol hub. Fewer than 10 ports globally. Green methanol = $800–1,200/mt. Main LNG competitor for newbuilds post-2030.

Singapore: 31.9M mt total marine fuel sold (Jan–Jul 2025). Rotterdam: 9.2M mt throughput. Fujairah: 6.3M mt. Together = ~60% of global bunkering. Container/tanker vessels = 43.2% of total bunker demand.

Sources: Mordor Intelligence · Ship & Bunker · MABUX · Precedence Research
Renewable Diesel & HVO: The Emerging Disruption to Fossil Diesel
RENEWABLE
DimensionDetail
Production (2024)38M tonnes globally (primarily US, Brazil, Indonesia). IEA: ~2.1M bpd of renewable diesel produced in 2023. +40% refinery capacity added in US/EU between 2023–2025 (9B liters/yr). 50+ new HVO refining projects announced.
What is HVO/Renewable Diesel?Hydrotreated Vegetable Oil. Made from waste fats, oils, greases (UCO), animal fats, or vegetable oils via hydroprocessing. Chemically identical to fossil diesel — drop-in replacement, no engine modification. Different from biodiesel (FAME/B100) which has blending limits. HVO = unlimited blending.
Key PlayersNeste (Finland): world's largest HVO producer, 3.3 Mt/yr capacity. TotalEnergies: La Mède + Grandpuits refineries converted to HVO. Marathon (US): Dickinson ND refinery converted. Phillips 66: Rodeo Renewed (CA), 120K bpd. Eni (Italy): Gela + Venice bio-refineries. Repsol. Petrobras: HVO at REPAR refinery (Paraná).
Blending MandatesIndonesia: B35 (35% biodiesel blend, moving to B40). Brazil: B14 (14%, rising to B15 in 2025). EU RED III: 29% renewables in transport by 2030. US: RFS + LCFS (California). 23+ nations with mandates. 47% of new refinery investments (2023–25) focused on low-sulfur diesel + biofuel integration.
Renewable Diesel vs LNGDifferent competitive dynamic: renewable diesel extends diesel infrastructure's life, LNG replaces it. For a fleet operator choosing between LNG trucks and renewable diesel trucks, HVO means the diesel engine stays — no cryo investment needed. Renewable diesel is LNG's most dangerous competitor in trucking because it removes diesel's emissions disadvantage without requiring new infrastructure. In marine: biofuel blends achieve FuelEU compliance without LNG tanks.
LimitationsFeedstock constraint: global UCO/waste fat supply = ~30–40 Mt/yr. If all HVO producers scale to capacity, feedstock becomes binding. Price premium: HVO $1.20–1.60/liter vs fossil diesel $1.00–1.20/liter (before credits). Without credits (LCFS, RED, RFS), economics don't work for most buyers. Palm oil concern: EU deforestation regulation limits palm-based HVO.
Sources: IEA · Neste · Industry Research · USDA · EU RED III
Diesel Displacement by LNG: Country-Level Case Studies
DISPLACEMENT
CountrySectorScale of DisplacementKey Details
🇨🇳 ChinaHD Trucking612K bpd gasoil displaced (2024)1M LNG trucks → largest diesel displacement globally. Gasoil demand fell 2.5% in 2024. But BEV trucks now displacing both LNG and diesel: 20% of HDV sales in Q1 2025. LNG = transitional fuel between diesel and electric in China.
🇧🇷 BrazilPower Generation~490 tpd LNG replaces dieselEneva Azulão → Jaguatirica II: replaced Roraima's 100% diesel-dependent isolated grid. NFE Barcarena: 2.2 GW replacing diesel/HFO generation in Pará. GNA II Porto do Açu: 1.7 GW. Every new LNG-to-power project in Brazil directly displaces diesel gensets.
🇪🇺 EuropeMarine Bunkering~1.4 bcm/yr LNG (2024)CMA CGM: 77 dual-fuel vessels. Viking Line: Baltic ferries. Baleària: Mediterranean ferries. MSC: ordering DF container ships. LNG displaces both VLSFO and MGO. FuelEU Maritime + EU ETS driving further switch. Rotterdam: TotalEnergies launched VLSFO+LNG bunker barge (Feb 2024).
🇺🇸 United StatesPeak Shaving / Mining~1.0 bcm/yrNE US: LNG peak shaving replaces oil-fired generation in winter. Aggreko: LNG gensets for mining + oil field. Stabilis: Permian Basin LNG for frac fleets replacing diesel. Clean Energy Fuels: RNG/LNG trucking displacing diesel on CA corridors.
🇮🇳 IndiaIndustrial Off-Grid~0.7 bcm/yr (2024)Shell Hazira truck-loading → LNG for industrial off-pipeline. GAIL CGD expansion bringing piped gas to replace diesel gensets in 300+ geographic areas. But CNG (not LNG) is the primary diesel displacement fuel in India — 4,500 new CNG stations planned.
🌍 Sub-Saharan AfricaMining Power<0.1 bcm/yrAggreko: LNG gensets for gold, copper, bauxite mines in Ghana, Mozambique, Tanzania. Total displacement tiny. Diesel gensets still dominate because LNG logistics in inland Africa are near-impossible. Solar+BESS increasingly competitive for mine-site steady-load applications.
🇦🇺 AustraliaRemote Mining~0.4 bcm/yrRio Tinto, BHP, Fortescue: LNG for Pilbara/Kimberley mine-site power. EDL Energy: 24 remote LNG power stations. QGC: LNG from coal seam gas. Australia has domestic gas supply → LNG competitive vs trucked diesel in remote areas.
Sources: IEEFA · EIA · IEA · Eneva · NFE · Aggreko · CMA CGM · Company filings
