Singapore: World's #1 Bunkering Port & Multi-Fuel Pioneer
SINGAPORE
⛽
CONVENTIONAL
53.6 Mt (2024). HSFO 36.8%, LSFO 55.3%. Still 97% of volume. Scrubber fleet growth boosting HSFO share.
🌿
BIOFUELS
1.38 Mt (2025, +56%). B50 blends commercial. B100 trials ongoing. Largest alt fuel category by volume.
🧊
LNG
571K t (2025, +24%). 3 vessels: FueLNG Bellina (7.5K m³), Venosa (18K m³), Brassavola (12K m³). New licenses opening 2026. Bio/e-methane planned.
🧪
METHANOL
3K t (2025, +88%). 3 suppliers licensed Jan 2026. First SIMOPS (May 2024). Maersk bunkering (Nov 2025). TR129 standard operational.
⚗️
AMMONIA
9.7 t trial (2024 — world's first). Fortescue Green Pioneer. Itochu: first purpose-built ammonia bunker vessel (3,500 m³, delivery 2026). Standards by 2025–26.

Singapore's multi-fuel strategy: Unlike Rotterdam (LNG + bio-LNG focused) or China (fossil LNG focused), Singapore is building infrastructure for ALL future fuels simultaneously: LNG + biofuels + methanol + ammonia. Digital bunkering: 100% adoption rate since Aug 2025 (e-BDN mandatory). Green Corridors: partnerships with Rotterdam, Australia, China (Shandong + Tianjin), India, Korea, LA/Long Beach. MPA opening new LNG licenses (Jan 2026) including bio/e-methane to meet rising demand.

Sources: Riviera Maritime · S&P Global · LNG Prime · Splash247
APAC (Ex-China) LNG Bunkering: Country Profiles
COUNTRIES
🇸🇬
Singapore
World's #1 bunkering port. 56.8 Mt total (2025 record). LNG: 571K t (+24%). 3 LNG vessels + 3 methanol suppliers. Multi-fuel pioneer: LNG + biofuel + methanol + ammonia. Green corridors with 6+ partners. The global bunkering benchmark.
🇰🇷
South Korea
4 bunkering vessels at Busan/Ulsan. Green Ship Initiative. Paradox: Korea builds 40%+ of global LNG DF vessels (HD HHI, Samsung HI, Hanwha Ocean) but has modest domestic bunkering. Busan expanding as NE Asia hub. KOGAS LNG supply.
🇯🇵
Japan
3 bunkering vessels. Yokohama, Kobe, Nagoya. JERA + MOL + NYK operate fleet. But: focus pivoting to ammonia co-firing (JERA Hekinan power plant). LNG imports declining (nuclear restarts). Japan may leapfrog from LNG to ammonia for marine.
🇲🇾
Malaysia
Petronas Bintulu = LNG export hub. Port of Tanjung Pelepas (PTP): +30% owned by APM Terminals (Maersk). Emerging alternative to Singapore. Competing for Gemini Cooperation traffic (Maersk + Hapag-Lloyd).
🇦🇺
Australia
World's #2 LNG exporter but near-zero domestic bunkering infrastructure. Green corridor with Singapore (2024). Woodside Scarborough LNG (first cargo Q4 2026). Potential for bunkering at NW Shelf export terminals. Mining = primary LNG use, not marine.
🌏
SE Asia Emerging
Thailand (Map Ta Phut), Vietnam (planned), Indonesia (massive potential — 17,000 islands + Strait of Malacca transit). Near-zero LNG bunkering infra today. Asian port volumes growing (Malaysia, Indonesia, Vietnam +20–30% YoY). Opportunity for 2028+.
Sources: Riviera/MPA · IGU 2025 · Bunker Market Top 10 · Seatrade
The 3 Global Bunkering Mega-Hubs: Singapore vs Rotterdam vs Shanghai
COMPETITION
Dimension🇸🇬 Singapore🇳🇱 Rotterdam🇨🇳 Shanghai
Total Bunker Volume56.8 Mt (2025)9.8 Mt (2024)~5 Mt est.
LNG Bunkered571K t (2025)941K m³ (2024)462K m³ (2024)
LNG Growth+24% YoY+7% YoY (Q1 2025)+61% (Jan-May 2025)
Bunker Vessels (LNG)3Multiple (EU fleet)Growing (20K m³ on order)
Multi-FuelLeader (LNG + bio + MeOH + NH₃)Strong (LNG + bio-LNG + MeOH)Emerging (LNG + green MeOH pilot)
Regulatory DriverMPA standards + Green CorridorsFuelEU + EU ETSGovernment subsidies + NOC policy
Strategic AdvantageLocation (Strait of Malacca) + multi-fuelRegulatory certainty + bio-LNGSpeed to scale + shipbuilding
PostureDefend #1 + innovateCompliance + innovation leaderFastest growth challenger

Singapore's dominance is built on geography (Strait of Malacca = 25% of global trade), infrastructure maturity (100% digital bunkering), and multi-fuel readiness (only port offering LNG + biofuel + methanol + ammonia). Rotterdam is the compliance engine (FuelEU/EU ETS drive adoption). Shanghai is the growth rocket (+61% YoY) leveraging China's port system + shipbuilding ecosystem. These 3 hubs will collectively define global LNG bunkering standards.

Sources: Seatrade/MPA · Bunker Market Top 10 2024 · Lloyd's List · IGU
APAC (Ex-China) Maritime LNG Bunkering: Segment Outlook & Risk Matrix
OUTLOOK
MarketTrendConfidenceKey Insight
🇸🇬 Singapore LNGGrowing ▲▲High571K t (2025). New licenses 2026. Sea-based LNG reloading EOI. Bio/e-methane planned. Strait of Malacca = 25% of global trade. Unassailable #1 position. Growth will slow from 4× to ~20% YoY.
🇸🇬 Singapore Multi-FuelGrowing ▲▲▲HighMethanol: 3 suppliers licensed. Ammonia: first bunker vessel 2026. Biofuel: 1.38 Mt. Singapore is building the multi-fuel hub of 2030 TODAY. Benchmark for all other ports.
🇰🇷 South KoreaStable →Med4 vessels. Busan growing. But Korea's advantage is shipbuilding (40%+ global DF orders), not bunkering. Paradox: builds the ships but doesn't fuel them.
🇯🇵 JapanPivoting ⟷Med3 vessels. LNG imports declining. JERA → ammonia co-firing strategy. MOL + Itochu ordering ammonia bunker vessel. Japan may leapfrog from LNG to ammonia for marine.
🇲🇾 MalaysiaGrowing ▲MedPTP competing with Singapore for alliance traffic (Gemini/Maersk). Petronas LNG supply. Could capture spillover from Singapore congestion.
🌏 SE Asia OtherNascent →LowIndonesia, Thailand, Vietnam = massive port traffic but near-zero LNG bunkering infra. Opportunity for 2028+ but no investment signals yet.

APAC (ex-China) maritime bunkering in one sentence: Singapore is the undisputed global champion — not just in LNG but as the world's first true multi-fuel bunkering hub (LNG + biofuel + methanol + ammonia all commercially available). Korea builds the ships, Japan is pivoting to ammonia, and SE Asia represents the last large underserved market. The key strategic insight: Singapore's advantage is not just geography (Strait of Malacca) — it's the speed of regulatory + infrastructure buildout that makes it the standard-setter the rest of the world follows.

Sources: Riviera/MPA · Seatrade · Bunker Market · IGU · Splash247
LNG Bunkering Infrastructure
Zero
No LNG bunkering at any Brazilian port. Santos, RJ, Paranaguá, Rio Grande — none offer LNG as marine fuel.
Source: ANTAQ / Industry analysis
Petrobras Bunker Sales (2024)
7,000+
Vessels bunkered. VLSFO 296K t/month + MGO 52K t/month. Santos = largest BR bunkering port. 100% conventional fuel.
Source: Argus / Petrobras
Programa Mar Aberto
US$6B
Petrobras/Transpetro (2026–2030). 20 cabotage ships + 18 barges + 18 pushers. Bunkering verticalization. Conventional fuels.
Source: Agência Petrobras (Jan 2026)
B24 Biofuel Bunker
-20%
GHG reduction. 24% FAME + 76% VLSFO. Launched Rio Grande (2025). Santos planned 2026. EU RED compliant. Petrobras innovation.
Source: Argus Media (Jun 2025)
Brazil's Maritime LNG Gap: Why the World's 7th Largest Coastline Has Zero LNG Bunkering
THE GAP
🚫
NO LNG BUNKERING
Zero LNG bunker vessels. Zero shore-to-ship LNG. Zero STS. Brazil's ports offer only VLSFO + MGO via Petrobras/Transpetro barge-to-ship operations.
🚢
CONVENTIONAL ONLY
Petrobras: 7,000+ vessels bunkered (2024). VLSFO ~296K t/month + MGO ~52K t/month. SE ports (Santos, RJ) = 226K t/month VLSFO. South (Paranaguá, Rio Grande) = 46K t/month.
📏
7,491 KM COASTLINE
World's 7th longest. 37 organized ports. Santos = largest in LatAm. But Brazil is NOT on major deep-sea bunkering transit routes (unlike Singapore/Gibraltar/Suez).
⚖️
NO REGULATION
No FuelEU equivalent. ANTAQ hasn't issued LNG bunkering rules. No domestic maritime LNG mandate. IMO 2020 compliance via VLSFO. Brazil's merchant fleet is small + old.

Why zero? Three structural reasons: (1) Brazil is not a transit bunkering hub — ships don't stop to refuel in Brazil like they do in Singapore/Rotterdam/Gibraltar; (2) Petrobras dominates conventional bunkering with competitive VLSFO pricing → no economic incentive to switch; (3) no regulatory driver — Brazil has no FuelEU/EU ETS equivalent, and ANTAQ has not issued LNG bunkering framework rules. LNG bunkering will only develop if international LNG-fuelled vessels calling at Santos/Paranaguá demand it.

Sources: Argus / Petrobras Maritime Week Americas · Petrobras Bunker · ANTAQ
What Does Exist: Petrobras Bunkering Monopoly + B24 Biofuel Innovation
CONVENTIONAL
Petrobras Bunker Operations
7,000+ vessels/yr. VLSFO + MGO produced in Petrobras refineries. Bunkering via barge-to-ship or pipeline at berth. Top 4 ports: Santos (largest), Rio de Janeiro, Paranaguá, Rio Grande. Petrobras also purchases 133K t/yr of marine fuel for its own tanker fleet (47% from Singapore, 16% other Asia, 15% Americas).
B24 Biofuel Bunker (Innovation)
24% FAME (biodiesel) + 76% VLSFO. ~20% GHG reduction. EU RED compliant. Launched at Rio Grande (2025). Santos planned for 2026. Cruise + container ships = target customers. 5-day delivery lead time. Brazil leveraging its biodiesel leadership (world's #2 producer) for marine decarbonization. This is Brazil's unique contribution — biofuel bunkering, not LNG.
Programa Mar Aberto (Petrobras/Transpetro)
US$6B (2026–2030). 20 new cabotage ships + 18 barges + 18 pushers. Bunkering verticalization at Belém (PA), RJ, Santos (SP), Paranaguá (PR), Rio Grande (RS). 2 shipyards: Bertolini (Manaus) + INC (Navegantes/SC). All conventional fuel — no LNG vessels in the program. Focuses on reducing charter dependency for Transpetro fleet.
Sources: Argus (B24) · Petrobras Bunker · Agência Petrobras (Mar Aberto) · Portos e Navios
SS-LNG Maritime Opportunities: Cabotage, Rivers & Future Scenarios
OPPORTUNITIES
🚢
LNG Cabotage
GNLink + Norsul partnership for SS-LNG coastal shipping between Brazilian ports. Pilot stage. Could replace truck VP on coastal routes: Santos→RJ→Vitória→Salvador→Recife. 7,491 km coastline = natural highway. Cheaper than 2,000+ km road transport for bulk LNG.
🏞️
Amazon River SS-LNG
EPE studies: SS-LNG barges for Amazon/Madeira/Trombetas rivers. Barcarena FSRU (NFE) as origin hub. Destinations: mining (Trombetas/MRN), isolated communities (Manaus upstream), Porto Velho. ISO-tank operations already piloted by Amazônica Energy.
⛽
International Vessels Calling
CMA CGM, MSC, Maersk operate LNG DF vessels on Asia→Europe→LatAm routes. Some call at Santos/Paranaguá. If these ships NEED LNG bunkering in Brazil, demand will force infrastructure. But currently they bunker elsewhere (Singapore, Rotterdam) before reaching Brazil.
🏭
FSRU Truck-Loading Synergy
9 FSRUs (144 MMm³/d) along the coast. TRSP Santos (Compass), Porto do Açu (bp/GNA), Barcarena (NFE) all have or plan truck-loading. If any FSRU adds marine loading capability, it becomes a de facto bunkering terminal at near-zero marginal cost.
Sources: EPE Terminais GNL · GNLink · EIA · Industry analysis
Brazil Maritime LNG Bunkering: Segment Outlook & Risk Matrix
OUTLOOK
SegmentTrendConfidenceKey Insight
⛽ Conventional BunkeringDominant →HighPetrobras VLSFO/MGO: 7,000+ vessels/yr. Mar Aberto US$6B fleet renewal. Will remain >95% of Brazilian bunkering for at least a decade. LNG is not competing here.
🌿 B24 Biofuel BunkerGrowing ▲HighRio Grande (2025), Santos (2026). -20% GHG. EU RED compliant. Brazil's unique marine decarbonization play — biofuel bunkering, not LNG. Leverages world's #2 biodiesel production.
🚢 LNG Cabotage (SS-LNG)Nascent ▲MedGNLink + Norsul pilot. Could replace truck VP on long coastal routes. 7,491 km coastline. Not bunkering — this is SS-LNG distribution by sea. Different use case.
🏞️ Amazon River SS-LNGNascent ▲MedBarcarena FSRU as origin. Mining + isolated communities downstream. EPE studies. ISO-tank pilots. Unique Brazilian opportunity — river VP for Amazon basin.
🧊 International LNG BunkeringNot yet ✖LowNo LNG bunker infrastructure. No regulation. No demand signal from international fleet. Will only develop if CMA CGM/MSC/Maersk demand it at Santos. Not before 2030.
⚗️ Methanol / AmmoniaNot yet ✖LowNo infrastructure, no suppliers, no regulation. Brazil has green methanol potential (sugarcane + green H₂) but nothing in maritime yet. Distant future (2035+).

Brazil's maritime SS-LNG story in one sentence: Brazil has zero LNG bunkering and will likely remain that way for most of this decade — but it's building two unique marine SS-LNG niches that no other country has: coastal LNG cabotage (GNLink/Norsul, leveraging 7,491 km coastline to distribute SS-LNG by sea instead of truck) and Amazon River SS-LNG barges (Barcarena FSRU as hub, distributing LNG downriver to mining + isolated communities). Meanwhile, Petrobras is innovating with B24 biofuel bunkering (-20% GHG, EU RED compliant) — Brazil's unique contribution to maritime decarbonization is biodiesel, not LNG. The maritime LNG bunkering story in Brazil is about distribution (cabotage + rivers), not about fueling international ships.

Sources: Argus (B24) · Petrobras Mar Aberto · EPE · GNLink · EIA

Bio-LNG & Regulatory Framework

Bio-LNG is emerging as a critical compliance tool under FuelEU Maritime and EU ETS, offering up to 92% GHG reduction as a drop-in fuel using existing LNG infrastructure.

Bio-LNG Production: Feedstocks, Technology & Economics
PRODUCTION
FeedstockMarket ShareGHG Savings vs DieselTypical ScaleKey Details
Organic Household Waste~41% (2023)70–85%5K–50K t/yrLargest feedstock segment. Continuous availability. Lower collection costs. High biodegradability. Municipal separate collection systems in EU = reliable supply. Nordsol (NL) plants process 50K+ t/yr organic waste.
Agricultural Residues~25%80–90%5K–20K t/yrManure, crop residues, energy crops. Circular economy: digestate = organic fertilizer (replaces 17% of EU nitrogen fertilizers today, could reach 65% by 2040). Seasonal variability = challenge.
Manure / Livestock Waste~15%Up to -190%2K–10K t/yrNegative lifecycle emissions because methane from manure would otherwise escape to atmosphere. Highest-value LCFS/RFS credits. US dairy farms = major RNG source (Clean Energy Fuels, Montauk Renewables).
Industrial Organic Waste~12%70–80%10K–200K t/yrFood processing waste, brewery/distillery byproducts, paper mill sludge. Fastest-growing segment. EnviTec Biogas Güstrow (Germany): 9,000 t/yr bio-LNG. Consistent year-round supply from industrial processes.
Landfill Gas~5%60–75%1K–15K t/yrDeclining share as EU moves from landfill to separate collection. Still significant in US (hundreds of landfill gas-to-RNG projects). Montauk Renewables: 12 operating RNG facilities across US.
Sewage Sludge~2%65–80%1K–5K t/yrWastewater treatment plants produce biogas from sludge. Growing in Nordics and UK. Small scale but highly reliable feedstock (urban wastewater never stops).
Sources: Grand View Research · EBA 2024 · Market Growth Reports · Veyt
Bio-LNG Key Players & Recent Developments
PLAYERS
CompanyRoleKey Details
Gasum
(Finland)		Producer + distributorNordics' largest biogas producer. 17 biogas plants. 50+ bio-LNG/bio-CNG stations. EU's largest biogas station (Kallhall, Sweden). Partnered with Hapag-Lloyd (2024) for maritime bio-LNG under Zero Emission Maritime Buyers Alliance. Wärtsilä supplying new biogas upgrading + liquefaction plant (Nov 2024).
Shell
(Global)		Producer + bunkererOpened first Bio-LNG plant in Germany (Apr 2024) — converts organic waste → bio-LNG for trucks via Shell fueling network. Supplied 100t bio-LNG to Evonik (Aug 2022). Bio-LNG bunkering via Shell Marine fleet. Global LNG infrastructure positions Shell as dominant bio-LNG distributor.
TotalEnergies
(France)		Producer + JVTotalEnergies + Clean Energy Fuels: Friona TX biomethane plant (Nov 2021). Bio-LNG bunkering via Gas Agility, Gas Vitality fleet. CMA CGM JV Rotterdam = bio-LNG ready. 10-yr Brent-indexed SPA with CMA CGM includes bio-LNG volumes.
Nordsol
(Netherlands)		Technology + productionBio-LNG production from organic waste. FirstBio2Shipping project with Attero + Titan (2,400 t/yr bio-LNG). New plant in Portugal with Prodeval (May 2024). Modular liquefaction approach. Amsterdam plant operational since 2021.
Titan Clean Fuels
(Netherlands, Vopak)		Distribution + bunkeringAcquired by Vopak (2024). First bio-LNG STS bunkering at Zeebrugge with MOL (Mar 2025, Celeste Ace). ISCC-EU certified waste-based bio-LNG. FlexFueler barge fleet for ARA. Largest STS bio-LNG delivery ever (Apr 2024).
EnviTec Biogas
(Germany)		Technology + productionGüstrow plant: 9,000 t/yr bio-LNG (launched Apr 2023). First compact bio-LNG plant in Germany (Darchau + Ruhe Biogas + Ecospray, Aug 2022). IPO'd biogas technology company. 30+ years biogas expertise.
Clean Energy Fuels
NASDAQ: CLNE		US RNG leaderLargest RNG/LNG fueling network in North America (570+ stations). Boron LNG hub 270K gal/day. Dairy RNG partnerships. LCFS credit monetization. Signed RNG SPAs with DHL (100K gal/yr), Food Express (3M gal/10yr), LA Metro (14M gal).
WM (Waste Management)
NYSE: WM		Captive RNG consumer~14,000 NG trucks (70% of fleet). 225 fueling stations. 110M gal diesel displaced (2024). Plans 100% RNG by 2026. Operates own landfill gas-to-RNG projects. Closed-loop: collect waste → produce biogas → fuel collection trucks.
Air Liquide
(France)		Technology + production2 biomethane units in Italy (Truccazzano + Fontanella, 2020). Agricultural/livestock waste → biomethane + bio-LNG/bio-CNG filling station. Global industrial gas infrastructure positions for bio-LNG liquefaction at scale.
Sources: Fortune Business Insights · Precedence Research · Veyt · OMR Global
Bio-LNG in the Regulatory & Compliance Stack
COMPLIANCE
MechanismHow Bio-LNG BenefitsEconomic Impact
FuelEU Maritime (EU)Bio-LNG = drop-in for LNG-fueled vessels. Blending 20% bio-LNG can achieve 2025–2030 GHG targets. Pooling allows over-compliant LNG ships to sell credits to conventional-fuel ships.Pooling revenue can offset 100%+ of bio-LNG premium for fleet operators. CMA CGM, MSC, Hapag-Lloyd actively building bio-LNG supply chains. Bio-LNG demand from FuelEU alone could reach 12 bcm by 2050 (Wood Mackenzie).
EU ETS for ShippingBio-LNG = zero ETS cost (biogenic CO₂ exempt). Fossil LNG = ~20–25% less CO₂ than HFO but still requires allowances. 100% ETS surrender from 2026.At €60–80/tCO₂: fossil LNG faces ~€30–40/mt ETS cost vs zero for bio-LNG. As ETS price rises, bio-LNG's effective cost advantage grows. EU ETS carbon price expected to reach €100+ by 2030.
RED III (EU)Renewable Energy Directive III: 14.5% renewable transport energy by 2030. 5.5% advanced biofuels sub-target. Bio-LNG from waste = advanced biofuel (double-counting eligible).Guarantees of Origin (GOs) = tradeable certificates. Bio-LNG GOs fell 40–50% in 2024 from 2023 highs (post-energy crisis). Still significant premium over fossil gas. ISCC/REDcert certification required.
California LCFSRNG from manure = most negative CI fuel available (-30 to -400 gCO₂e/MJ). Bio-LNG/RNG generates highest LCFS credit value of any fuel. Quarterly credit auctions.LCFS credits can make RNG trucks lowest-TCO option in California. Clean Energy Fuels monetizes via integrated dairy RNG → truck fueling chain. Oregon Clean Fuels Program similar.
US RFS (EPA)Renewable Fuel Standard: biogas-derived RNG qualifies for D3 cellulosic RINs (highest-value tier). Landfill gas, dairy, and food waste RNG all qualify.D3 RIN values = $1.50–3.00/gallon-equivalent. Stacks with LCFS in California. Creates double incentive for RNG production. Montauk Renewables, BP, Chevron all investing in US RNG.
EU CO₂ Standards for HDVsBio-LNG counts toward fleet CO₂ reduction targets for truck OEMs (-45% by 2030, -90% by 2040). LNG trucks + bio-LNG blending = compliance pathway.IVECO, Scania, Volvo all marketing bio-LNG compatibility. Gasum + Molgas/Titan supply networks enable fleet-wide bio-LNG adoption.
Sources: Gasum · ACT Group · Veyt · Wood Mackenzie
E-Methane (Synthetic LNG): The Next Frontier
E-FUELS
AspectDetail
What is e-methane?Synthetic methane produced from green hydrogen (electrolysis) + captured CO₂ (biogenic or DAC) via Sabatier reaction. Chemical formula identical to natural gas (CH₄). Drop-in for all LNG infrastructure, engines, and vessels.
GHG PotentialNear-zero to zero lifecycle emissions when green H₂ + biogenic CO₂ are used. Can achieve 100% GHG reduction under IMO Net-Zero Framework and FuelEU. Extends LNG infrastructure's relevance through 2050 net-zero targets.
Current StatusPre-commercial. 55 e-methane facilities mapped by EBA (Sep 2024), 34 source CO₂ from biogas upgrading. Denmark: Energinet issued first e-methane Guarantees of Origin (Apr 2024). Japan: Osaka Gas incorporating e-methane into US LNG supply chain.
CostCurrently 3–5× fossil LNG. Dependent on green hydrogen cost ($3–6/kg today, target $1–2/kg by 2030). At scale with cheap renewables, could converge toward bio-LNG pricing.
Strategic ImportancePositions LNG-fueled vessels as future-proof: same engines, same tanks, same bunkering infra — just swap the molecule from fossil to bio to e-methane. This is the strongest argument for LNG as a "transition fuel that isn't stranded" — the infrastructure persists, only the feedstock changes.
Sources: Veyt (2025) · Global Maritime Forum
US RNG Market Value (2024)
$2.4B
From $458M (2018). ~300 registered pathways. Landfill gas = 2/3 of supply. Dairy/swine growing fast.
Source: cCarbon RNG Outlook 2035
Clean Energy RNG Sold (2024)
237M GGE
+5% YoY. RNG = 80% of Clean Energy volume. bp JV: 6 dairy projects ($455M). Maas JV: 3 new facilities (SD, GA, FL, NM).
Source: Clean Energy SEC Filing
45Z Credit (Extended)
→2029
OBBBA extended from 2027. Up to $1.00/gal. Dairy manure: NEGATIVE CI → credit >$1.00/gal. ~$1B transacted in 2025.
Source: Crux Climate (Feb 2026)
Manure AD Systems (US)
191
Producing RNG (June 2024) + 69 under construction. California alone: 50+ dairy projects planned/under construction.
Source: EPA AgSTAR
US RNG Credit Stacking: How Policy Makes Dairy Manure Worth More Than Natural Gas
POLICY
🏛️
45Z Credit (Federal)
Up to $1.00/gal
Dairy: >$1.00 (neg CI)
Extended → 2029
+
⛽
D3 RIN (Federal RFS)
$2.50–3.50/RIN
Cellulosic biofuel
pathway
+
🌴
LCFS (California)
$50–70/tCO₂
Dairy manure CI: -31
= 3–4× value vs landfill
+
🌲
State CFS Programs
Oregon CFP
Washington CFS
More states coming
→
💰
Dairy RNG: ~$14/MMBtu
Credit value alone
vs fossil gas $4/MMBtu
= 3.5× economics
OBBBA Game-Changer (Jul 2025)
The One Big Beautiful Bill Act extended 45Z from 2027 → 2029, removed Indirect Land Use Change (ILUC) penalties, and confirmed dairy/swine/poultry manure can have negative emissions rates → credits exceeding $1.00/gal. Dairy manure CI score: -31 kg CO₂e/MMBtu. This makes animal manure RNG the most valuable clean fuel pathway in the US. Proposed 45Z regulations published Feb 2026. ~$1B in 45Z credits transacted in 2025; $2–3B annual potential.

Why this matters: Credit stacking means RNG economics work even when the fossil gas-diesel spread is thin. 86% of all on-road NG fuel in the US is RNG (LCFS data). The US doesn't need a carbon tax or FuelEU-style mandate — the credit stack IS the economic driver. Dairy manure RNG gets 3–4× more value from CA LCFS credits than landfill RNG, which is why dairy projects are growing fastest.

Sources: Crux Climate (45Z) · Capstone DC · Baker Botts · BDO
Key US RNG / Bio-LNG Players
PLAYERS
⛽
Clean Energy Fuels
Largest US RNG retailer. 237M GGE sold (2024). Boron hub (CA): dairy → bio-LNG. bp JV: 6 dairy projects ($455M). Maas JV: 3 new facilities. Cummins X15N demo trucks promoting adoption. Bio-LNG pioneer.
🛢️
Montauk Renewables
Landfill gas + dairy RNG. 15 operating projects. Major beneficiary of D3 RIN + 45Z. Publicly traded (MNTK). Positioned to gain from finalized RFS 2026–27 volumes.
💧
OPAL Fuels
Vertically integrated: RNG production + dispensing. Landfill gas-to-RNG. 10+ operating projects. Station network. Focused on heavy-duty trucking fleet fueling.
🔴
Archaea Energy (bp)
Acquired by bp (2022, $4.1B). Largest US RNG producer by portfolio. 50+ development projects. Landfill gas dominant. bp provides capital + offtake for scaling.
♻️
Republic / WM
Republic Services + Waste Management: largest US waste haulers. Converting landfill gas → RNG. WM: 100% RNG fleet target by 2026. Run CNG/RNG trucks on their own gas. Closed-loop model.
🐄
Dairy RNG Developers
Maas Energy Works (Clean Energy JV). Brightmark. Vanguard Renewables (BlackRock). Ag-Grid Energy. 191 manure AD systems + 69 under construction. CA: 50+ dairy projects. Negative CI = highest-value RNG.
Sources: Clean Energy SEC · EPA AgSTAR · cCarbon · Montauk · OPAL · Archaea/bp
Bio-CNG vs Bio-LNG: Why US RNG is Mostly Compressed, Not Liquefied
CNG vs LNG
🔵 BIO-CNG (Dominant)
RNG injected into pipeline → withdrawn at CNG station → compressed → dispensed. No liquefaction needed. 1,385 CNG stations nationwide. Book-and-claim accounting: gas injected anywhere, credited at any station. Lower capex. ~90% of US transport RNG.
🧊 BIO-LNG (Growing Niche)
RNG liquefied on-site or at hub → trucked to LNG stations. Clean Energy Boron (CA) = largest bio-LNG facility. 81 LNG stations. Higher energy density for long-haul. Growing as Cummins X15N drives LNG truck adoption. ~10% of US transport RNG but growing fastest.

US potential: ICF estimates US technical potential at 34.4 billion GGE/yr by 2040, including 20.6 billion GGE from agricultural digesters alone. Current production: ~1.5 bcm/yr. Massive untapped headroom. The constraint is not feedstock — it's capital deployment + permitting + interconnection to gas pipelines. The 45Z extension to 2029 + negative CI for manure provides the longest policy runway the RNG industry has ever had. Marine bio-LNG (RNG for shipping) is nascent in the US but has massive potential given the established RNG market — if book-and-claim works for marine bunkering under IMO rules, US RNG could serve the global bio-LNG bunkering market.

Sources: Clean Energy ARS · cCarbon · EPA AgSTAR · ICF Consulting
US Bio-LNG / RNG: Segment Outlook & Risk Matrix
OUTLOOK
SegmentTrendConfidenceKey Insight
🐄 Dairy Manure RNGGrowing ▲▲▲HighNegative CI = credit >$1.00/gal. 191 AD systems + 69 under construction. OBBBA confirms negative emissions. CA: 50+ projects. Highest-value RNG pathway. The growth engine.
🗑️ Landfill Gas RNGStable →High2/3 of current supply. Mature technology. Republic + WM fleet conversion. Lower credit value than dairy but still profitable with D3 RIN stacking. Foundation of US RNG market.
🚛 Bio-LNG for TruckingGrowing ▲▲HighClean Energy Boron hub pioneer. Cummins X15N driving LNG truck adoption. 81 LNG stations. Long-haul = bio-LNG niche. Growing as LNG trucking grows.
🚢 Bio-LNG for MarineNascent ▲MedUS has massive RNG supply base. If book-and-claim works for IMO/FuelEU, US RNG could serve global bio-LNG bunkering. Huge potential but regulatory clarity needed.
📋 45Z Credit MarketGrowing ▲▲High~$1B transacted in 2025. $2–3B annual potential. Proposed regs Feb 2026. Pricing: $0.88–0.93 per dollar. Crux Climate: "significant volume remains unsold."
⚠️ Policy RiskModerate ⚠️MedOBBBA extension to 2029 = strongest runway yet. But: LCFS credit price volatility. D3 RIN uncertainty with SREs. Final 45Z regs still pending. Policy is the tailwind AND the risk.

US RNG/bio-LNG story in one sentence: The US has the world's most sophisticated RNG credit-stacking system (45Z + D3 RIN + LCFS + state CFS), which makes dairy manure RNG worth ~$14/MMBtu in credits alone — 3.5× the value of fossil gas. This policy architecture, not technology, is what makes the US the world's largest and fastest-growing RNG market ($2.4B, 2024). The extension of 45Z to 2029 with negative CI for animal manure is the biggest policy gift the RNG industry has ever received. Bio-LNG (liquefied) is the emerging sub-segment as Cummins X15N drives long-haul LNG trucking adoption and marine bio-LNG bunkering begins.

Sources: Crux Climate · Capstone DC · cCarbon · EPA AgSTAR · Baker Botts
EU Biomethane Production (2024)
4.1 bcm
EU-27. From 1 bcm (2015). Installed capacity: 7 bcm/yr (Q1 2025, +9%). REPowerEU target: 35 bcm by 2030. Gap: ~20 bcm.
Source: EBA Statistical Report 2025
Biomethane Plants (Europe)
1,678
2025 (+111 vs 2024). 21,100+ AD plants total. 101 bio-LNG plants. 86% grid-connected. 25 countries producing.
Source: EBA Biomethane Map 2025
Private Investment Committed
€28B
Ahead of 2030. +€1B vs previous year. Expected to deliver 7.3 bcm capacity by 2030. Growth slowing despite strong investor appetite.
Source: EBA Investment Outlook 2025
Long-Term Potential (2050)
150 bcm
Biogases total by 2050. + 177 Mt organic fertilizers + 120 Mt biogenic CO₂. Circular economy backbone.
Source: EU Commission / EBA
The Ambition vs Reality Gap: 4.1 bcm Today → 35 bcm Target by 2030
THE GAP
📊
WHERE WE ARE
EU-27: 4.1 bcm produced (2024). 7 bcm/yr installed capacity. 1,678 plants. 22 bcm total biogas + biomethane. = 6% of EU gas consumption (332 bcm).
🎯
WHERE WE NEED TO BE
REPowerEU target: 35 bcm by 2030. National plans (NECPs): only ~12–15 bcm committed. Gap: 20–23 bcm. No binding EU-wide target. Growth slowing at the worst possible moment.
⚠️
WHAT'S MISSING
No binding EU biomethane target. Clean Industrial Deal overlooks biomethane. Funding disparity vs solar/wind/H₂. Fragmented regulation across 27 member states. Permitting bottlenecks. Infrastructure gaps.

EU-27 gas consumption: 332 bcm. Imports: 273 bcm (82%). EU domestic gas production: ~40 bcm (-12%/yr). Biomethane at 4.1 bcm = only 1.5% of imports. Even at 35 bcm = only 13% of imports. But: biomethane is the only renewable gas that can use existing pipeline infrastructure directly. 5 countries (DE, FR, DK, IT, NL) account for ~90% of EU biomethane. Poland injected first biomethane into grid in 2025.

Sources: EBA Statistical Report 2025 · Rabobank · Oxford Institute for Energy Studies
EU Regulatory Framework Driving Bio-LNG Demand
REGULATION
🚢
FuelEU Maritime
In force Jan 2025. -2% GHG (2025), -6% (2030), -80% (2050). Bio-LNG = drop-in compliance fuel. Negative CI bio-LNG commands premium prices in 2025. THE demand driver for bio-LNG.
💨
EU ETS (Shipping)
100% from 2026. Bio-LNG = zero CO₂ under ETS. €2,400 penalty/t CO₂e avoidance value. EU ETS 2 (buildings + transport from 2027) will further boost biomethane demand across all sectors.
🌿
RED III
14.5% renewable energy in transport by 2030. Bio-LNG qualifies. Advanced biofuels (waste-derived) get multiplier bonus. Mass balance accounting enables blending at supply chain level.
🏠
REPowerEU + Gas Package
35 bcm biomethane target (2030). Biomethane Industrial Partnership (BIP) launched Sep 2022. Gas Package: grid access rights for biomethane. But: no binding target = weak enforcement.
🇳🇱
Dutch Blending Mandate
Netherlands: mandatory biomethane blending obligation. Strongest national-level demand signal in EU. Denmark: feed-in tariffs driving largest avg plant size (1,468 Nm³/h). France: purchase obligations (tarif d'achat).
📜
Guarantees of Origin (GoO)
EU-wide biomethane GoO system enables cross-border trade of green gas certificates. Allows physical production in one country, consumption credited in another. Key enabler for EU single biomethane market.
Sources: EU Commission Biomethane · Rabobank · CEE Energy News · OIES
Top EU Biomethane Producers & Bio-LNG for Maritime
COUNTRIES
🇩🇪 Germany
Largest biogas producer. ~9,500 AD plants. Avg biomethane plant: 605 Nm³/h. 26 manufacturing sites for heat pumps AND biogas equipment. Biomethane share growing as biogas→biomethane upgrades accelerate.
🇫🇷 France
Most biomethane plants by count. Avg: 251 Nm³/h (smaller scale). Purchase obligations (tarif d'achat). Agricultural feedstock dominant. Plenitude + Methagora: 50 GWh/yr 15-year deal. Strong pipeline of projects.
🇩🇰 Denmark
Highest avg plant capacity: 1,468 Nm³/h. Leader in large-scale biomethane. Feed-in tariff system. Biomethane = significant share of Danish gas grid already. Model for industrial-scale deployment.
🇮🇹 Italy
Avg: 727 Nm³/h (large-scale). Strong in agricultural residues. Industrial heat pump sales growing alongside biomethane. Bio-CNG for transport well established. Southern Italy = untapped agricultural potential.
🇳🇱 Netherlands
Blending mandate = strongest demand signal. Gate terminal truck-loading for SS-LNG/bio-LNG. Nordsol (Shell + Renewi JV). Rotterdam bio-LNG bunkering pioneer. Platts daily bio-LNG price assessment (Nov 2024).
🇸🇪 Nordics (Gasum)
Gasum: 5 bio-LNG plants in Sweden. Nordic bio-LNG bunkering leader. Chart Industries: largest bio-LNG station in continental Europe (Kallhäll, Sweden). Finland: 225 plants. Early SECA compliance drove LNG→bio-LNG adoption.
Bio-LNG for Maritime: Europe's Strategic Differentiator
101 bio-LNG plants operational in Europe (2024, EBA). ~50 with physical liquefaction. Bio-LNG bunkering active in 8 countries (BE, FR, FI, NL, NO, ES, SE, UK). 7+ major suppliers (Shell, Gasum, Titan, Molgas, Bunker One). FuelEU Maritime makes bio-LNG the lowest-cost compliance pathway for LNG-fuelled ships. Mass balance accounting: physical production anywhere in EU, credited at any bunkering port. This is why Europe — not the US or Asia — is where the bio-LNG bunkering business model is being built.
Sources: EBA 2025 · EBA Biomethane Map · OIES · Bioenergy Insight
EU Bio-LNG & Biomethane: Segment Outlook & Risk Matrix
OUTLOOK
SegmentTrendConfidenceKey Insight
🚢 Bio-LNG (Maritime)Growing ▲▲▲HighFuelEU Maritime = strongest demand driver globally. Active in 8 countries. Negative CI commands premium. 101 bio-LNG plants. Drop-in fuel, no engine change. Europe's unique competitive advantage vs US/Asia.
🏭 Industrial BiomethaneGrowing ▲▲HighEU ETS 2 (2027) will boost demand in heavy industry. Grid-injected biomethane = direct gas replacement. 86% of plants grid-connected. GoO enables cross-border trade. Scale depends on binding targets.
🏠 Buildings (Heating)Growing ▲MedDutch blending mandate as model. Biomethane in existing gas grid = decarbonization without heat pump retrofit. But: 17 countries banning gas boilers = gas grid shrinking. Competing with electrification.
🚛 Bio-LNG (Road Transport)Stable →Med800+ LNG stations in EU. Bio-LNG blending growing. Gasum Nordic leader. But: EU HDV CO₂ mandates favor zero-emission (BEV), not low-emission (bio-LNG). Structural headwind post-2030.
📈 REPowerEU 35 bcm TargetAt Risk ⚠️MedCurrent: 4.1 bcm. NECPs: ~12–15 bcm. Gap: 20–23 bcm. No binding target. Growth slowing. €28B invested but not enough. Target will very likely be missed. Revised realistic range: 10–15 bcm by 2030.
🔬 E-Methane (Synthetic)Nascent ▲Low35 e-methane plants in Europe (2023, EBA). 12 under construction. Green H₂ + captured CO₂ → synthetic methane. Very early stage. 2030+ technology. Not material before 2035.

EU bio-LNG / biomethane story in one sentence: Europe is the world's largest biomethane producer (~50% of global output) with a massive regulatory stack (FuelEU + EU ETS + RED III + REPowerEU) driving demand — but the 35 bcm target will almost certainly be missed because growth is slowing (4.1 bcm in 2024, +9% capacity in 2025) while the gap to target is 20+ bcm. The real success story is bio-LNG for maritime bunkering: FuelEU Maritime creates a premium market for bio-LNG that no other region can match, and Europe's 101 bio-LNG plants + mass balance accounting + 8-country bunkering operations are building the business model the rest of the world will eventually follow. Europe's biomethane advantage is not scale — it's regulatory certainty + infrastructure maturity + maritime compliance demand.

Sources: EBA 2025 · Rabobank · OIES · EU Commission · CEE Energy News
China Raw Biogas Output
~19 bcm
World's largest biogas producer. 40M household digesters + 118K small/medium + 8,720 large-scale plants. But almost none upgraded to biomethane.
Source: Springer / NBS China
Manure Methane Potential
130 bcm
Theoretical annual. 1.8B tonnes livestock manure/yr. 700M pigs slaughtered (2024). >70% from large-scale farms. China = 20% of global manure methane.
Source: Wilson Center / CAS
Biomethane Target (2030)
20 bcm
Government target. Current production: near zero upgraded biomethane. 65 BNG demo projects approved (2015–2017). 197 planned by 2020 (13th 5-Year Plan).
Source: MDPI Sustainability
Bio-LNG for Transport
≈ Zero
No bio-LNG production at scale. China's 34 bcm LNG trucking runs 100% fossil LNG. No D3 RIN or LCFS equivalent. No FuelEU equivalent for maritime.
Source: Industry analysis
China's Biogas Paradox: World's Largest Producer, Smallest Biomethane Upgrader
PARADOX
🐷
MASSIVE FEEDSTOCK
700M pigs slaughtered/yr (2024). 1.8B tonnes manure. 130 bcm methane potential. 580M+ tonnes agricultural waste. China = world's #1 methane from manure (20% global). The feedstock is there.
💨
RAW BIOGAS EXISTS
~19 bcm/yr raw biogas. 40M household digesters. 118K small/medium plants. 8,720 large-scale. But: 16 bcm from household (cooking/lighting). Only 3 bcm from large plants. Almost none upgraded.
🚫
BIOMETHANE GAP
65 BNG demo projects (2015–17). 197 planned. Target: 20 bcm/yr by 2030. Reality: near-zero upgraded biomethane. Most biogas used raw for rural cooking/lighting. Upgrading economics don't work without credits.
⚡
COMPETING PRIORITIES
Government focus: solar + wind + BEV + hydrogen. Coal lock-in still massive. Biogas = rural waste treatment, not energy strategy. No carbon credit mechanism for bio-LNG. Policy gap = the binding constraint.

Why the paradox? In the US, dairy manure RNG is worth ~$14/MMBtu in credits (45Z + D3 RIN + LCFS). In the EU, bio-LNG for maritime commands FuelEU + EU ETS premium. In China, biogas has no equivalent credit-stacking economics. Government subsidies (~1,500 yuan/m³) focus on plant construction, not biomethane upgrading. The non-energy value of anaerobic digestion (waste treatment + organic fertilizer) far exceeds the energy recovery value in China's current policy framework. Until China creates a bio-LNG incentive structure comparable to US D3 RIN or EU FuelEU, biomethane upgrading at scale won't happen.

Sources: Springer · MDPI Sustainability · ScienceDirect · Wilson Center
China Biogas Policy & Infrastructure: What Exists vs What's Missing
POLICY
What Exists ✅
Government subsidies: ~1,500 yuan/m³ for large-scale biogas (2015). Manure utilization mandate: 80% by 2025, 90% by 2035. 65 BNG demonstration projects. 14th Five-Year Plan includes biogas development. "Pig-biogas-fruit" circular model in Jiangxi. Nanchang Ecological Park: 300K tonnes manure → 5M m³ biogas/yr. Zhejiang: all farms >50 pigs must have digesters.
What's Missing ❌
No carbon credit mechanism for biomethane (no D3 RIN equivalent). No FuelEU-style maritime mandate. No LCFS-style transport fuel standard. No grid injection mandate or GoO system. No bio-LNG blending obligation. Gas pricing mechanism monopolized by government — biomethane can't compete. National Energy Administration prioritizes solar, wind, hydrogen over biogas. Coal-to-gas dominates industrial fuel switching.
What Could Change 🔄
National carbon market expansion (currently covers power only — could expand to transport/industry). Methane reduction commitments under Global Methane Pledge. Livestock manure methane = low-hanging fruit for emissions targets. Large-scale farming consolidation (>70% of pigs now in large farms) = easier AD deployment. 63 bcm/yr biomethane potential from manure + straw. If China created any credit mechanism, scale could happen fast given existing digester base.
Sources: Frontiers (2026) · Wilson Center / iGDP · ScienceDirect · MDPI
China Bio-LNG / Biomethane: Segment Outlook & Risk Matrix
OUTLOOK
SegmentTrendConfidenceKey Insight
🐷 Manure Biogas (Rural)Stable →High40M household digesters. 118K small/medium plants. Focus: waste treatment + cooking/lighting + organic fertilizer. Not energy recovery. Foundation exists but not optimized for biomethane.
🏭 Large-Scale ADGrowing ▲Med8,720 large plants. >70% of pigs now in large-scale farms = concentrated feedstock. Government subsidies. Jiangxi N2N model. Best candidates for biomethane upgrading if economics improve.
🧊 Bio-LNG (Transport)Not yet ✖LowZero bio-LNG at scale. 34 bcm LNG trucking = 100% fossil. No credit mechanism. No economic incentive to liquefy biogas. Massive 63 bcm potential but zero policy runway.
🚢 Bio-LNG (Maritime)Not yet ✖LowNo FuelEU equivalent. No maritime biomethane mandate. China's bunkering boom is 100% fossil LNG. Unlike EU, no regulatory driver for bio-LNG bunkering.
📊 National Carbon MarketWatch ⚠️MedCurrently covers power only. If expanded to transport/industry, could create biomethane economics. Global Methane Pledge = external pressure. THE potential game-changer. But no timeline.
🌾 Biomethane Grid InjectionNascent ▲Med20 bcm/yr target (2030). 65 BNG demo projects. But: gas pricing monopoly, no grid access mandate, high upgrading costs. Requires fundamental policy reform to work at scale.

China's bio-LNG / biomethane story in one sentence: China has the world's largest biogas production base (~19 bcm/yr) and the world's largest manure methane potential (130 bcm theoretical from 700M pigs) — but near-zero biomethane upgrading and zero bio-LNG because there is no credit mechanism equivalent to the US D3 RIN/LCFS stack or the EU FuelEU/ETS framework. The constraint is not feedstock, not technology, not capital — it's policy. China's energy policy prioritizes solar/wind/BEV/hydrogen over biogas, and the national carbon market doesn't yet cover transport or agriculture. If China ever creates a bio-LNG incentive structure, the existing 40M+ digesters and 8,720 large-scale plants could scale biomethane production faster than any other country. The potential is staggering — 63 bcm/yr from manure + straw alone — but there is no sign of the required policy shift before 2030.

Sources: ScienceDirect · MDPI · Springer · Wilson Center · Frontiers
India CBG Plants Operational
~115
Dec 2024. SATAT target: 5,000 by 2024-25. Reality: 2% of target. 1,094 active LoIs. 147 operational on GOBARdhan portal (Jul 2025).
Source: Global Methane Initiative (Jan 2025)
India CBG Potential
62 MMT
Cattle dung 25 MMT + agri residue 20 MMT + sewage 10 MMT + MSW 5 MMT + press mud 2 MMT. 500M+ cattle. World's largest livestock population.
Source: ScienceDirect (2025)
India CBG Blending Obligation
1→5%
Mandatory from FY 2025-26. Phase-in: 1% (2025-26), 3% (2026-27), 4% (2027-28), 5% (2028-29+). THE demand creation mechanism.
Source: SATAT / IOCL
India Biogas Market (2024)
$1.6B
→ $3.5–4.9B by 2032. Could save $29B in LNG imports (FY25–30) if 20% gas replaced by biogas. LNG import dependency: 50%.
Source: Ramboll · IEEFA
India: The World's Most Ambitious CBG Program (SATAT + GOBARdhan + CBO)
INDIA
🐄
SATAT (2018)
Target: 5,000 CBG plants, 15 MMT/yr. OMCs provide guaranteed offtake. CBG price: 85% of CNG retail (₹1,478/MMBtu from Jun 2025). Reality: ~115 plants operational (Dec 2024). 1,094 active LoIs. Massive gap.
♻️
GOBARdhan
Umbrella initiative under Swachh Bharat Mission. 1,083 projects registered (Jul 2025). 147 operational, 236 under construction, 690 not yet started. Covers rural + urban organic waste → biogas/CBG.
📊
CBO (Blending)
Mandatory from FY 2025-26. Phased: 1% → 3% → 4% → 5% by 2028-29. CBG blended into CNG (transport) + PNG (domestic). GAIL operationalizes CBG-CGD sync. THE demand creation tool.
🏗️
Infrastructure
Pipeline infra scheme: ₹994.5 crore for CBG injection into CGD grid (100 projects). BAM scheme: ₹564.75 crore for feedstock aggregation machinery. CBG available at 350 retail outlets, 64 CGD areas.

India's CBG story: The most comprehensive government CBG framework outside Europe — but execution massively lags ambition. 5,000 plant target vs ~115 operational. Key bottlenecks: feedstock supply chain, slow permitting, CBG pricing (85% of CNG = discount, not premium), limited technical capacity, multi-ministry coordination gaps. The CBO (mandatory blending from 2025-26) is the game-changer: creates guaranteed demand. But all of India's biogas is CBG (compressed) — there is zero bio-LNG (liquefied) production at any scale.

Sources: Global Methane Initiative · REGlobal (Jul 2025) · SATAT/IOCL · IEEFA
APAC (Ex-China, Ex-India) Bio-LNG / Biomethane: Country Profiles
COUNTRIES
🇲🇾
Malaysia
Palm oil mill effluent (POME) = proven biogas feedstock. 450+ palm oil mills with AD potential. Biogas-to-electricity growing. Some POME-to-biomethane pilots. But zero bio-LNG. Government focus: palm biodiesel (B20/B30), not biogas.
🇮🇩
Indonesia
World's largest palm oil producer. Massive POME potential. Biogas for rural electrification in remote areas. Some palm-to-biogas pilots funded by ADB/World Bank. Zero bio-LNG. Focus: palm biodiesel (B35/B40).
🇹🇭
Thailand
Rice straw/husk + cassava waste + pig manure. 300+ biogas plants (mostly electricity). Tapioca starch factory biogas well developed. Zero bio-LNG but growing bio-CNG interest for industrial use.
🇯🇵
Japan
Biogas from sewage sludge + food waste. Small scale. ~100 AD plants. Focus is hydrogen + ammonia for decarbonization, not biogas. Zero bio-LNG. Some food waste → biogas projects in Hokkaido (dairy).
🇰🇷
South Korea
Sewage sludge + food waste AD. Small biogas sector. Government renewable energy focus: solar, wind, hydrogen. Zero bio-LNG. Some pilot biomethane-to-grid projects under K-Taxonomy green finance.
🇦🇺
Australia
Landfill gas + piggeries + abattoirs. Clean Energy Regulator tracks biogas. Some ARENA-funded biomethane pilots. CIP planning 150 GWh bio-LNG plant (manure-based). Australia's first bio-LNG could be APAC's first at scale.
Sources: WBA India Briefing · IEA Bioenergy Task 37 · Industry analysis
APAC (Ex-China) Bio-LNG / Biomethane: Segment Outlook & Risk Matrix
OUTLOOK
MarketTrendConfidenceKey Insight
🇮🇳 India CBGGrowing ▲▲Med~115 plants. CBO mandatory from 2025-26 (1→5%). 62 MMT potential. $1.6B market. Best policy framework outside EU/US. But execution 2% of target. Bio-LNG = zero.
🇲🇾🇮🇩 Palm Oil BiogasStable →MedPOME = massive feedstock (Malaysia + Indonesia). Biogas-to-electricity growing. Biomethane upgrading pilots. Governments prioritize palm biodiesel (B35/B40) over biogas. Bio-LNG = not on radar.
🇯🇵🇰🇷 Japan/KoreaMinimal →HighSmall AD sectors. Both prioritize H₂ + ammonia for decarbonization. Biogas = waste treatment niche, not energy strategy. Japan/Korea will leapfrog to H₂/NH₃, not bio-LNG.
🇦🇺 AustraliaGrowing ▲MedLandfill + piggery biogas. CIP 150 GWh bio-LNG plant planned. ARENA funding. Green corridor with Singapore. Could become APAC's first bio-LNG producer at scale. Watch closely.
🌏 SE Asia OtherNascent →LowThailand: rice/cassava biogas. Vietnam: pig manure AD growing. All biogas-to-electricity. Zero biomethane upgrading. Zero bio-LNG. 2030+ opportunity.
🧊 Bio-LNG (All APAC)Near Zero ✖LowNo bio-LNG production at scale anywhere in APAC (ex-China). India = CBG only. SE Asia = electricity only. Japan/Korea = H₂ pivot. Australia CIP plant could be first. But APAC is 5–10 years behind EU on bio-LNG.

APAC (ex-China) bio-LNG story in one sentence: India has the world's most ambitious government CBG program (SATAT: 5,000 plant target + CBO mandatory blending + 62 MMT feedstock potential) but is executing at only ~2% of target and producing zero bio-LNG — all compressed, not liquefied. SE Asia has massive POME/rice/manure feedstock but governments prioritize palm biodiesel over biogas. Japan/Korea are leapfrogging to hydrogen/ammonia. Australia may become APAC's first bio-LNG producer at scale (CIP 150 GWh plant). The region is 5–10 years behind Europe on bio-LNG and has no equivalent of FuelEU Maritime to create maritime bio-LNG demand. The key insight: APAC biogas = waste treatment + rural energy, not transport fuel or maritime compliance. That's the fundamental difference from Europe.

Sources: Global Methane Initiative · REGlobal · IEEFA · Ramboll · ScienceDirect
Biomethane Plants (Brazil)
17
Operational. +42 under construction. 23 agribusiness units (end 2025). Capacity: 224M m³/yr (0.61 MMcmd). Production: ~0.25 MMcmd.
Source: ScienceDirect (Oct 2025) · ANP
Biogas Potential (Brazil)
84.6 bcm
Raw biogas/yr theoretical. Sugarcane = ~50% of potential (vinasse + filter cake). 600–650M tonnes sugarcane/yr. Cattle, poultry, soybeans, MSW.
Source: MDPI (Jan 2026)
Lei Combustível do Futuro
1→10%
Mandatory biomethane decarbonization target for gas producers/importers. From 1% (2026) up to 10%. CGOB certificates. Fines R$100K–R$50M.
Source: WBA (Oct 2024)
20× Scale-Up Required
5.1 MMcmd
Needed for 10% target. Current: 0.25 MMcmd. = 20× increase. At least 4 MMcmd new investment required. Petrobras: procurement from 2026.
Source: Rystad Energy (Mar 2025)
Lei do Combustível do Futuro (Oct 2024): Brazil's Biomethane Game-Changer
LAW
📜
Lei 14.993/2024
Fuel of the Future Law
Signed Oct 2024
Decree Sep 2025
→
📊
Mandates (from 2026)
Gas producers/importers
must decarbonize 1→10%
CNPE sets annual targets
→
📜
CGOB Certificates
Guarantee of Origin
for biomethane
Like CBIOs for ethanol
→
🔄
RenovaBio Integration
CBIOs + CGOBs
on same platform
ANP supervision
→
⚡
Grid Injection Enabled
Biomethane into pipelines
Fines R$100K–R$50M
for non-compliance
Why This Is Transformational for Brazil
Before Oct 2024, Brazil had no dedicated biomethane policy — only RenovaBio (focused on ethanol/biodiesel) and voluntary biogas projects. The Lei do Combustível do Futuro creates: (1) mandatory demand via gas supplier decarbonization targets, (2) a certificate mechanism (CGOB) enabling monetization, (3) pipeline injection rights for biomethane, (4) alignment with RenovaBio's CBIO platform. Petrobras launched biomethane procurement (Jan 2025) with contracts up to 11 years. Biomethane in Brazil just went from voluntary niche to mandatory compliance fuel.
Sources: WBA · Mayer Brown (Sep 2025) · Rystad Energy
Brazil's Feedstock Advantage & Key Players
SUPPLY
🍬
Sugarcane Vinasse
~50% of Brazil's biomethane potential. 600–650M tonnes sugarcane/yr. Vinasse + filter cake = byproducts of ethanol/sugar mills. Raízen: vinasse → biogas pioneer. Cocal Plant (Narandiba): first city biomethane network (Presidente Prudente, SP).
🐄
Livestock Manure
World's 2nd largest cattle herd (~220M heads). Massive poultry industry (1.5B+ birds). Pig farming in South. GNLink/Migratio JV: manure-based biomethane in Paraná. Negative CI scores possible.
🗑️
Landfill / MSW
Compass/Orizon: Paulínia landfill (SP), 180–300K m³/d from 2025. SABESP (SP water utility): sewage → biomethane. Urban waste = most accessible feedstock. 4 plants RenovaBio-certified.
🛢️
Petrobras
Launched biomethane procurement (Jan 2025). Contracts up to 11 years, deliveries from 2026. Delivery at refineries, thermoelectric, grid. THE anchor buyer for Brazil's biomethane market.
🌱
Raízen / Cosan
World's largest sugarcane company. Shell JV. Vinasse → biogas at mills. Cellulosic ethanol from bagasse. Biometano at integrated biorefineries. CBIOs under RenovaBio. Brazil's bio-energy champion.
🔗
GNLink + CDLs
GNLink: biometano distribution via SS-LNG trucking. Migratio JV (Paraná): agri biomethane. Compagas targeting 15% biometano by 2026. Edge Gas: mercado livre distribution. Emerging midstream.
Sources: MDPI · Click P&G · Rystad · ScienceDirect
Brazil Bio-LNG / Biomethane: Segment Outlook & Risk Matrix
OUTLOOK
SegmentTrendConfidenceKey Insight
🍬 Sugarcane BiomethaneGrowing ▲▲▲High50% of potential. Raízen/Cocal pioneer. Integrated biorefineries. CBIOs under RenovaBio. Brazil's unique feedstock advantage — no other country has 650M tonnes sugarcane/yr.
🐄 Livestock BiomethaneGrowing ▲▲Med220M cattle, 1.5B+ poultry. GNLink/Migratio JV (Paraná). Negative CI possible. Scale depends on logistics — Brazilian farms are enormous + dispersed.
🗑️ Landfill/MSWGrowing ▲▲HighCompass/Orizon Paulínia. SABESP sewage. Most accessible. 4 plants RenovaBio certified. Fastest to deploy — feedstock is concentrated in cities.
📜 Lei Combustível do FuturoTransformational ▲▲▲High1→10% mandatory targets. CGOBs from 2026. Petrobras as anchor buyer. Fines for non-compliance. THE catalyst. Biomethane went from voluntary to mandatory.
🧊 Bio-LNG (Liquefied)Nascent ▲MedMost biomethane will be pipeline-injected or bio-CNG, not bio-LNG. GNLink positioning for bio-LNG distribution. Bio-LNG for trucking = 2028+ opportunity. For export: requires int'l certification.
⚠️ Execution RiskHigh Risk ⚠️MedCurrent: 0.25 MMcmd. Target (10%): 5.1 MMcmd. = 20× scale-up. Only 17 plants. Pricing: R$2.20–3.95/m³. India's SATAT achieved 2% of target. Can Brazil do better? Law is stronger but execution is everything.

Brazil's biomethane story in one sentence: The Lei do Combustível do Futuro (Oct 2024) transformed Brazil's biomethane market overnight — from a voluntary niche (17 plants, 0.25 MMcmd) to a mandatory compliance fuel with 1→10% decarbonization targets, CGOB certificates, and Petrobras as anchor buyer. Brazil's unique advantage is sugarcane vinasse (~50% of potential, 650M tonnes/yr) — no other country has this feedstock at this scale. But the execution challenge is staggering: 20× scale-up from 0.25 to 5.1 MMcmd. The pricing question (R$2.20–3.95/m³ vs fossil gas) and logistics (dispersed farms, limited pipeline access) remain the binding constraints. If Brazil executes, biomethane could replace 100%+ of its gas imports and reduce emissions by up to 89% vs natural gas. The law exists. The feedstock exists. The question is: can Brazil build 40+ new plants fast enough?

Sources: Rystad Energy · WBA · MDPI · Mayer Brown · ANP/Click P&G

LNG as Fuel for Road Transport

LNG is a proven alternative to diesel for long-haul heavy-duty trucks, offering lower fuel costs, reduced emissions, and increasing integration with bio-LNG/RNG for near-zero carbon logistics.

500+
Quadrupled since 2018 · Est. 2,000+ by 2030
Source: LNG Prime / NGVA Europe
US CNG/LNG Fueling Stations
1,200+
Across North America (CNG + LNG combined)

$1.50–2
Per diesel gallon equivalent · Avg. diesel $3.76/gal (2024)
Source: Cummins / US DOE

500 HP
1,850 lb-ft torque · Diesel parity · Full prod. 2024
