Key LNG Truck OEMs, Engines & Fleet Operators
ECOSYSTEM
CategoryCompanyProduct / DetailKey Data
Engine OEMCummins (US)X15N (15L) — heavy-duty NG engine500 HP / 1,850 lb-ft. Full production late 2024. Meets EPA & CARB MY2024+2027. 90% lower NOx vs diesel. 40,000+ global installs on platform, 2B miles. 1,200-mi range with Hexagon Agility fuel systems.
CumminsL9N (9L) — medium-duty NG engineFor regional haul, refuse, vocational. Maintenance-free Three-Way Catalyst. No DEF/DPF/SCR required.
Truck OEMs (LNG models)IVECO (Italy)S-Way NP (CNG/LNG)460 HP. LNG range ~1,600 km. Leading EU LNG truck seller. 13% EU HDV market share.
Scania (Sweden)LNG-powered long-haul tractors12.5% EU HDV market share. KP Logistik ordered 100 LNG trucks from Scania in Germany.
Volvo Trucks (Sweden)FH LNG / FM LNG models14% EU HDV share. Also hydrogen ICE R&D (Westport JV). LNG range ~1,000 km. Bio-LNG compatible.
Kenworth / Peterbilt (US)T680 / 579 with Cummins X15NClass 8 long-haul. Production started Aug 2024. Freightliner expected 2025. Up to 1,200-mi range with behind-cab fuel systems.
Major Fleet OperatorsWM (Waste Management) (US)Largest NA natural gas truck fleet~14,000 NG vehicles (~70% of fleet). 225 fueling stations. Displaced 110M gal of diesel in 2024. Plans 100% RNG by 2026.
UPS (US)Long-haul X15N deploymentDeploying X15N tractors on most demanding long-haul routes. Previously limited to ISX12N for regional. CNG/LNG fleet growing.
KP Logistik (Germany)100 LNG-truck fleetLargest LNG project in Germany at launch. Partnered with Scania & Liquind 24/7 fueling infrastructure.
Meyer Logistik (Germany)20 IVECO LNG HGVsSaves ~50,000 kg CO₂ per truck over 5 years vs diesel. 1,500 km range. 10% lower fuel cost.
Fueling InfrastructureMolgas / Titan (Spain/NL)70+ road-fueling stations + 200 partner pointsPan-European LNG/bio-LNG network. 7 bunkering vessels. InfraVia-backed. Full Titan acquisition Oct 2025.
Gasum (Finland)Nordic LNG/bio-LNG stations12 new stations in 2024. EU's largest biogas station (Kallhall, Sweden). 17 biogas plants supply bio-LNG.
Sources: Cummins · FreightWaves · Fleet Equipment (Run on Less) · Transport Topics · ICCT · Molgas · Valves Community
Global LNG HD Truck Fleet
200,000+
China ~1M (incl. CNG/LNG) · North America ~50K · Europe ~20K · 2,500+ stations globally
Source: Market Reports World
CNG+LNG Commercial Vehicle Market
$38.5B
2025 · → $86.2B by 2032 · CAGR 12.2% · HD trucks = 71% of market
Source: Persistence Market Research
China LNG HDV Market Share (2024)
~30%
Of HDV sales. All-time high. 109K units sold H1 2024 (+127% YoY). Displacing 612K b/d of gasoil
Source: IEEFA / CAAM
Freightliner Cascadia X15N
Summer 2025
Daimler Truck NA production start · 500 HP / 1,850 lb-ft · Class 8 long-haul · 1,200 mi range
Source: Mordor Intelligence
China: The World's Largest LNG Trucking Market
CHINA
MetricValueDetail
LNG Truck Fleet (2025)~1 millionShell LNG Outlook 2025 estimate. Fleet tripled since 2019 (IEEFA). Expected to reach 1.2M+ by 2030. World's largest LNG trucking fleet by far.
HDV Sales Share (2024)~30%LNG captured ~30% of HDV purchases in 2024 (all-time high). H1 2024: 109K units sold (+127% YoY). H1 2023 was only 40,640 units. LNG share in tractor trucks specifically even higher.
LNG Consumed by Trucks (2024)22 MtDomestic liquefaction plants produced ~25 Mt (doubled in 5 years, +41% capacity in 2024 alone). Trucking demand met almost entirely by domestic production, not LNG imports.
Diesel Displacement30M mt / 612K b/dPetroChina CPPEI estimate for 2024 (+50% vs 2023). LNG trucks already displaced ~8% of China's gasoil consumption. China may have already reached peak gasoil demand (2023).
Key ProvincesHebei, Shanxi, Shandong, HenanTop 4 provinces = 55%+ of LNG truck sales (Q1 2024). Driven by logistics density and coal-to-gas industrial corridors. Shaanxi + Inner Mongolia = traditional strongholds.
Price DriverLNG/Diesel ratio <0.6LNG-to-diesel price ratio below 0.6 since May 2023 (same heat value basis). At this ratio, higher LNG truck purchase price (¥268–532K vs ¥250–290K diesel) recovers in ~1 year.
Top OEMsFAW Jiefang, Sinotruk, Shaanxi Auto, Dongfeng, FotonTop 5 = 85%+ of HDV market. FAW J7 600HP gas tractor (flagship). Sinotruk HOWO T7H LNG. Shaanxi Delong X6000 700HP LNG. Dongfeng Tianlong KX 580HP. All offer 15–16L engines with AMT.
Fueling Infrastructure1,500+ LNG stationsSinopec + PetroChina expanding aggressively. Sinopec shifting existing gas stations to add LNG pumps. National coverage on major expressways (G1–G30 corridors).
EV CompetitionRising fastElectric HDV sales +140% in 2024, reaching 13.6% share (vs LNG ~30%). Q1 2025: electric = 20%, LNG = 31%. Battery-swap models gaining traction. XCMG, SANY leading electric. LNG share declining in 2025 tractor segment (-12 pp).
Sources: IEEFA (2025) · S&P Global · Shell LNG Outlook 2025 · ChinaTrucks.org
Regional LNG Trucking Market Comparison
REGIONS
RegionFleet SizeStationsKey OEMsMarket Dynamics
China~1M (2025)1,500+FAW Jiefang, Sinotruk, Shaanxi Auto, Dongfeng, FotonWorld's largest market. 30% of HDV sales (2024). Blue Sky policy + subsidies for diesel replacement. Domestic LNG production covers trucking demand (25 Mt). But electric HDVs growing fast — may overtake LNG by 2027–28.
North America~50K~600Kenworth T680, Peterbilt 579, Freightliner Cascadia (all with Cummins X15N)Cummins X15N launch (2024) = game-changer (500 HP, 1,200 mi range). WM = 14K NG trucks, 225 stations. Clean Energy Fuels Boron hub 270K gal/day. RNG mandates (CA LCFS, OR CFP) drive demand. Freightliner Cascadia production summer 2025.
Europe~20K~500IVECO S-Way NP (leader), Scania, Volvo FH/FM LNGIVECO leads EU LNG sales. 500+ stations (Spain, Italy, NL, Germany dense). Bio-LNG blending critical for FuelEU compliance. Gasum + Molgas/Titan = largest fueling networks. EU CO₂ standards -45% by 2030 for HDVs. Strong in Spain, Italy, Nordics.
IndiaEmergingGrowingVolvo FM 420 LNG (HPDI), Tata, Ashok LeylandCNG vehicle sales +46% YoY (Jan–Aug 2024). 4,500 new CNG stations planned. GAIL city gas distribution expanding. Volvo supplied 20 LNG tractors to Delivery (Jun 2024). LNG = nascent but CNG = fast-growing.
Rest of APACSmallLimitedChinese OEM exports (Sinotruk, Dongfeng)Indonesia, Vietnam exploring NG vehicles. Australia: LNG for mining fleet (Aggreko model). Japan: limited (focus on H₂ fuel cell for trucks). Korean yards build LNG equipment but limited domestic truck adoption.
Sources: Persistence MR · Mordor Intelligence · Market Reports World · GMI
Regulatory Drivers for LNG Road Transport
REGULATION
Regulation / PolicyRegionKey RequirementsImpact on LNG Trucks
EU CO₂ Standards for HDVsEU-45% CO₂ by 2030, -65% by 2035, -90% by 2040 (vs 2019 baseline). Applies to trucks, buses, trailers.LNG trucks achieve -15–20% CO₂ vs diesel. Bio-LNG blending critical to hit targets. Drives IVECO/Scania/Volvo to offer LNG + bio-LNG compatible models.
Euro VII EmissionsEUExpected 2027+ entry. Tighter NOx, PM, PN limits. Cold-start and real-driving emissions testing.LNG engines already 90% lower NOx. No DPF/SCR needed for NG engines (Cummins). Positions gas trucks as compliant without aftertreatment complexity.
US EPA / CARB MY2027USEPA 2027+ rule: 80–90% NOx reduction vs 2010 levels. CARB Advanced Clean Trucks (ACT): increasing ZEV sales mandate.Cummins X15N meets EPA MY2024 + CARB MY2027. 90% lower NOx vs diesel. ACT focuses on ZEV but allows NG trucks for non-ZEV portion. RNG = carbon-negative under LCFS.
California LCFS / OR CFPUS (CA, OR)Low Carbon Fuel Standard: credits for fuels with lower lifecycle CI. RNG can achieve negative CI scores (-30 to -400 gCO₂e/MJ from dairy/landfill).RNG generates highest-value LCFS credits of any fuel. WM, Republic Services, Waste Connections use captive RNG. Makes NG trucks lowest TCO option in CA/OR.
China Blue Sky PolicyChinaThree-Year Action Plan for Blue Skies. Accelerated retirement of China III/IV diesel trucks. Subsidies for LNG replacement. Provincial air quality mandates.Primary driver of China's LNG truck boom. Subsidies for scrapping old diesel trucks + buying LNG replacements. Provincial mandates in Hebei, Shanxi, Henan = highest adoption.
China VI Emission StandardChinaEquivalent to Euro VI. Mandatory for all new HDVs since Jul 2021 (6a) and Jul 2023 (6b).Forced fleet renewal cycle. LNG trucks = compliant path with lower NOx than diesel. Combined with subsidies, triggered mass adoption 2023–2025.
India BS-VIIndiaBharat Stage VI (equivalent to Euro VI). Mandatory since Apr 2020 for all vehicles.CNG trucks = primary compliance pathway for Indian OEMs. LNG nascent but GAIL + Volvo building supply chain. 4,500 CNG stations planned.
Sources: Persistence MR · ICCT · IEEFA · S&P Global
Cummins X15N Engine
500 HP
1,850 lb-ft torque. 750+ mi range. Diesel-parity. Near-zero NOx (90% below EPA). Full production late 2024. "Fabulous" first year — repeat orders.
Source: Cummins (Dec 2025)
Clean Energy RNG Delivered (2024)
236M gal
Largest US RNG provider. 600+ stations (200+ Class 8 friendly). 128M gal in California alone (50%+ of state NG vehicle market). 2 LNG plants (CA + TX).
Source: Clean Energy Fuels
US NG Fueling Stations
~1,560
~1,500 CNG (public + private) + ~60 LNG. Clean Energy dominant. RNG = ~70% of commercial NG vehicle fuel. Infrastructure concentrated on key corridors.
Source: Cummins / DOE AFDC
RNG Fuel Savings vs Diesel
$1.50–2.00
Per DGE cheaper. X15N payback: 2.5–3 years. RNG with dairy: up to -300% CO₂ vs diesel (negative CI). The more miles, the faster payback.
Source: Transport Topics (Nov 2025)
Cummins X15N: The Engine That Rebooted US Natural Gas Trucking
ENGINE
⚙️
DIESEL PARITY
500 HP, 1,850 lb-ft torque. 750+ mile range. Same platform as diesel X15 — shared components below head gasket. No DEF required. 3-way catalyst (maintenance-free). Designed for 80,000 lb Class 8 loads.
🌿
EMISSIONS
CO₂ and NOx: 90% below current EPA standards. With RNG: up to 97% CO₂ reduction. With dairy RNG: negative CI = -300% vs diesel. Already meets 2027 EPA/CARB rules. Near-zero is today, not future.
🚛
AVAILABLE NOW
Kenworth T680 + T880. Peterbilt 579, 567, 520. Freightliner Cascadia (5th gen). Full production since late 2024. Demo program: J.B. Hunt (first), UPS, Wegmans. Repeat orders within year 1.
💰
ECONOMICS
RNG = $1.50–2.00/DGE cheaper than diesel. Payback: 2.5–3 years. Higher upfront cost offset by lower fuel + maintenance. No DEF = savings. High-mileage fleets benefit fastest.

Why the X15N matters: Before 2024, natural gas engines topped out at 400 HP (ISX12N) — insufficient for long-haul, heavy loads. Fleets that tried NG trucks in 2013–16 abandoned them due to power gaps. The X15N eliminates that barrier: 500 HP / 1,850 lb-ft = true diesel replacement. Cummins' fuel-agnostic X15 platform strategy (CNG, diesel, hydrogen future) means this engine investment is futureproof. NACFE's 2025 Messy Middle event tested X15N with UPS, Wegmans, and Kleysen across demanding terrain — all praised its heavy-haul performance.

Sources: Cummins X15N · Cummins Year 1 Review · Transport Topics · Clean Energy
Fleet Adoption & Fueling Infrastructure
FLEETS
📦
UPS
X15N in Kenworth T680. Salt Lake City: 92% of package fleet on RNG. Long-haul routes previously diesel-only. NACFE Messy Middle 2025 participant. Longtime alt-fuel leader.
🚚
J.B. Hunt
First X15N demo partner (Sep 2024). 180+ RNG trucks. 15+ years studying NG viability. One of North America's largest fleet operators. Logistics + intermodal.
♻️
WM (Waste Mgmt)
Largest waste hauler. 100% RNG fleet target. Closed-loop: landfill gas → RNG → own CNG trucks. X15N adoption. Runs thousands of CNG refuse trucks.
🥛
United Dairymen AZ
Milk cooperative. 200K gal RNG/yr for 5 fleets. X15N trucks hauling dairy products. Leader in sustainable ag trucking. Clean Energy fuel deal (Oct 2025).
📄
Paper Transport
~50 RNG trucks, 9 states. 250K gal/yr. 10+ year Clean Energy partner. Sustainability-focused brands as customers. Long-haul dedicated fleets.
⛽
Clean Energy Infra
600+ stations. 200+ Class 8 friendly. 2 LNG plants (CA + TX). Corridors: Southwest, LA–Atlanta, Texas Triangle, Midwest, Northeast. Also serving space companies (Astrobotic, Stoke Space) with bulk LNG.
Sources: Cummins · Clean Energy / J.B. Hunt · Clean Energy Fuel Deals
US LNG Road Transport: Segment Outlook & Risk Matrix
OUTLOOK
SegmentTrendConfidenceKey Insight
⚙️ X15N AdoptionGrowing ▲▲▲HighFull production since late 2024. Repeat orders year 1. UPS, J.B. Hunt, WM, Wegmans, Kleysen. NACFE validation. THE catalyst for US NG trucking renaissance.
🌿 RNG for TruckingGrowing ▲▲High70% of US commercial NG vehicles on RNG. 236M gal (2024). Dairy RNG: negative CI. Credit stacking: 45Z + D3 RIN + LCFS. RNG economics make the business case, not regulation.
🧊 LNG StationsRecovering ▲Med~60 LNG stations. Down from 140 peak (2016). Slight recovery since X15N. Clean Energy expanding corridors. LNG infra trails CNG. X15N demand may drive new LNG stations.
📦 Shipper DemandGrowing ▲▲HighScope 3 pressure from sustainability-minded shippers showing up in RFPs. RNG = measurable emissions reduction. Shipper demand is pulling fleets toward RNG, not just pushing them.
🔋 BEV CompetitionThreat ⚠️MedTesla Semi (500-mi range). CARB ACT mandates (ZEV % requirements). Nikola (bankrupt). BEV will dominate urban/short-haul. NG/RNG wins long-haul + heavy-load niche. Coexistence, not replacement.
🏛️ CARB ACT MandatesHeadwind ⚠️MedCalifornia ACT requires increasing % of new truck sales to be ZEV. NG trucks = near-zero, not zero. CARB favors BEV/FCEV over NG. Structural regulatory headwind in CA. But rest of US = no ACT.

US LNG road transport story in one sentence: The Cummins X15N (500 HP, 2024) rebooted US natural gas trucking after a decade of false starts — for the first time, NG engines match diesel on power, torque, and range, while RNG delivers negative CI and $1.50–2.00/DGE savings. The business case is real: UPS, J.B. Hunt, WM, and dozens of fleets are placing repeat orders within year one. The US model is unique: it's economics-driven (RNG credit stacking), not regulation-driven (no FuelEU equivalent). The X15N fills the "messy middle" — heavy-haul, long-distance routes where BEV can't yet compete. The risk is CARB ACT mandates in California pushing fleets toward ZEV, but the other 49 states have no such constraint. LNG trucking in the US will be a growing niche (~5-10% of long-haul), not the dominant fuel — but a profitable, proven, carbon-negative one.

Sources: Cummins · Clean Energy · Transport Topics · Clean Energy X15N
EU LNG Stations
800+
DE 195, IT 173, ES 105, FR 91, SE 40. Doubled in ~3 years. AFIR mandates LNG every 400 km on TEN-T core network.
Source: CNG Europe / NGVA
ZE Heavy Truck Sales (EU, 2025)
~5,000
1.9% share (up from 1.2% in 2024). Q4 2025: 2.7%. Mercedes eActros surged. 23,700 total ZE-HDVs (trucks + buses + vans). BEV = 90% of ZE.
Source: ICCT Race to Zero (Mar 2026)
EU HDV CO₂ Mandate
-90%
By 2040. Milestones: -15% (2025, in effect), -45% (2030), -65% (2035). Zero-emission focused. LNG trucks = low-emission, NOT zero.
Source: ICCT / EU Commission
ZE HDV Fleet by 2030
410–600K
EU Commission estimate. ~90% will be BEV. 100+ BEV models in series production (end 2024). All use cases, all segments covered.
Source: EU Commission (Jun 2025)
EU LNG Trucking: Growing Infrastructure, Shrinking Regulatory Runway
HEADWIND
⛽
INFRA: GROWING ✅
800+ LNG stations (doubled in 3 yrs). AFIR mandates: LNG every 400 km on TEN-T. DE 195, IT 173, ES 105, FR 91. Most mature LNG trucking network globally (ex-China). ~10K+ LNG trucks on road.
📉
REGULATION: HOSTILE ❌
EU HDV CO₂: -15% (2025), -45% (2030), -90% (2040). Targets are ZERO-emission, not low-emission. LNG trucks cannot comply post-2030. OEMs pivoting investment to BEV + hydrogen. City buses: 90% ZE mandate by 2030.
🔋
BEV: SURGING ⚡
100+ BEV models in series production. ZE heavy trucks: 1.9% (2025, 5,000 units). Mercedes eActros 600 breakout year. Volvo FH Electric. 410–600K ZE-HDVs by 2030. BEV = 90% of ZE fleet.
🌱
BIO-LNG: LIFELINE 🔄
Bio-LNG extends relevance via CO₂ accounting benefits. 101 bio-LNG plants in EU. Gasum Nordic pioneer. Drop-in fuel for existing LNG trucks. Bio-LNG is what keeps EU LNG trucking alive past 2030.

The paradox: Europe built the world's best LNG trucking infrastructure (800+ stations, AFIR mandates) just as EU regulation began killing the market for fossil LNG trucks. The -45% by 2030 / -90% by 2040 HDV CO₂ mandate means OEMs must sell zero-emission trucks, not low-emission LNG trucks. IVECO (biggest LNG truck OEM) sales down 20% in 2025; Tata Motors acquiring it (€3.8B). All major OEMs (Daimler, Volvo, MAN, Scania) pivoting R&D to BEV + hydrogen ICE. Bio-LNG is the only pathway that keeps existing LNG trucks relevant — but new LNG truck sales will decline as -45% by 2030 forces OEMs to sell BEV instead.

Sources: ICCT Race to Zero 2025 · ICCT CO₂ Targets · EU Commission HDV Readiness
EU Truck OEMs: LNG Legacy → BEV/H₂ Pivot
OEMs
🚛
IVECO
S-Way LNG: biggest EU LNG truck seller. But: sales -20% in 2025. Tata Motors acquiring (€3.8B, Jul 2025). Struggling with -15% CO₂ target. 12% market share. LNG truck champion becoming acquisition target.
🔵
Scania (TRATON)
LNG trucks + already met -15% CO₂ target 2 years early (2023). 14% share. H₂ ICE prototype with Westport (51.5% efficiency). Credits pool with MAN. Transitioning to H₂ ICE + BEV.
🟢
Volvo Trucks
FH LNG (1,000 km range with bio-LNG). Met -15% target early. Leading EU ZE-HDV seller (54% of ZE heavy truck sales in Q1 2024). FH Electric. 15% share. BEV + LNG dual strategy.
⭐
Mercedes/Daimler
No LNG truck. 19% share (largest). eActros 600: breakout in Q3-Q4 2025 (ZE share jumped to 5%). H₂ Unimog ICE tested. All-in on BEV. LNG was never Mercedes' play.
🟡
MAN (TRATON)
LNG trucks offered. 14% share. Pooling CO₂ credits with Scania. hTGX H₂ ICE prototype (16.8L, 200 units planned 2025). H₂ ICE = MAN's post-LNG strategy.
🔴
DAF / Renault
DAF: 11% share, LNG offered. Renault: 8%, BEV leader (#2 after Volvo in ZE sales). Both met -15% via early credits. Renault pivoting faster to BEV. DAF slower.
Sources: ICCT 2025 · ICCT CO₂ Compliance · ICCT H1 2024
EU LNG Road Transport: Segment Outlook & Risk Matrix
OUTLOOK
SegmentTrendConfidenceKey Insight
⛽ LNG InfrastructureStill Growing ▲High800+ stations. AFIR mandates. But: built for a market that regulation is now shrinking. Stranded asset risk for LNG stations post-2035 unless repurposed for bio-LNG or H₂.
🚛 Fossil LNG TrucksDeclining ▼▼High-45% CO₂ by 2030 forces OEM pivot to ZE. IVECO -20%, acquired by Tata. New LNG truck sales will fall as BEV share rises. Structural decline. No recovery. Regulatory death sentence.
🌱 Bio-LNG TrucksNiche Growth ▲MedBio-LNG extends existing LNG truck relevance. Gasum + bio-LNG plants (101). CO₂ accounting benefits. Bio-LNG is the lifeline for the 10K+ LNG trucks already on EU roads. Not for new sales.
🔋 BEV TrucksSurging ▲▲▲High5,000 ZE heavy trucks (2025, 1.9%). eActros 600 breakout. 100+ models. 410–600K ZE-HDVs by 2030. BEV is winning. Regulatory + OEM + infrastructure alignment. LNG cannot compete.
⚗️ Hydrogen ICE TrucksEmerging ▲MedMAN hTGX (200 units 2025). Scania/Westport (51.5% efficiency). Daimler Unimog. H₂ ICE counts as zero-emission. Could replace LNG trucks on long-haul post-2030. Uses similar engine platform.
🇨🇳 China ComparisonChina ahead ▲▲▲HighChina ZE-HDV: 25% share (2025, 450K+ units) vs EU 4% (23,700). China massively ahead on ZE truck adoption. EU's regulatory ambition hasn't yet translated to China-scale deployment.

EU LNG road transport story in one sentence: Europe built the world's most mature LNG trucking infrastructure (800+ stations, AFIR mandates, IVECO/Scania/Volvo LNG fleets) — then passed the world's most aggressive HDV CO₂ mandates (-45% by 2030, -90% by 2040) that structurally kill new fossil LNG truck sales. The 10K+ LNG trucks on EU roads today will run for another decade on bio-LNG, but no significant new LNG truck orders will come as OEMs pivot to BEV (eActros 600, Volvo FH Electric) and hydrogen ICE (MAN hTGX, Scania/Westport). The EU story is a cautionary tale: infrastructure built for the wrong era. The future of EU heavy trucking is BEV for short/medium haul and H₂ ICE for long-haul — not LNG. Bio-LNG's role shifts from truck fuel to maritime bunkering (FuelEU Maritime), where it has permanent regulatory support.

Sources: ICCT 2025 Full Year · ICCT CO₂ Targets · EU Commission · ICCT H1 2024
China LNG Truck Fleet (2025)
1M+
Tripled since 2019. 1.2M+ expected by 2030 (Shell). ~30% of HDV sales in 2024 (record). 4,500+ LNG stations — world's largest network.
Source: IIFIIR / Shell LNG Outlook
BEV Share of HDV Sales (H1 2025)
22%
Up from 8.6% (H1 2024). Aug 2025: 28%. NEVs outsold LNG trucks for 2 consecutive months. BMI projects 46% full year 2025, 60% in 2026.
Source: IEEFA (Aug 2025)
LNG HDV Share (H1 2025)
26%
Down from ~30% (2024). Diesel-LNG price spread fell by 2/3 since peak. LNG sales declining as BEV surges + LNG cost advantage narrows.
Source: IEEFA
Domestic LNG Supply
25 Mt
Produced domestically (2024) > 22 Mt consumed by trucks. Capacity +41% in 2024. Self-sufficient. NOT pulling global cargoes (IEEFA).
Source: IEEFA (May 2025)
China's HDV Market: The Great Shift from Diesel → LNG → BEV
TRANSITION
⛽
DIESEL: COLLAPSING
Share fell to ~57% (2024), near 50% by early 2025. Diesel use dropped ~11% YoY in 2024. Diesel trucks peaked 2021 (8.76M fleet). Structural decline — never coming back.
🧊
LNG: PEAKING
~30% of sales (2024 record). 1M+ fleet. But: H1 2025 → 26%. Diesel-LNG price spread fell 2/3. LNG sales declining as BEV surges. Bridge fuel — not permanent.
🔋
BEV: SURGING
22% of HDV sales (H1 2025). Aug: 28%. NEVs outsold LNG trucks 2 months running. TCO 10–26% below diesel. €58–85K for 400–600 kWh. BEV = the winner.
🔄
BATTERY SWAP
~1/3 of BEV HDV sales. CATL "75#" standardized pack: 30 truck models, 12 OEMs. Swap = 5 min. Cost advantage $8,320/100K km vs LNG. Solves range/charging for long-haul.
LNG Trucking = Bridging Solution, Not Permanent
LNG truck sales are highly sensitive to the diesel-LNG price ratio — operators need <80% ratio to justify the ¥80,000 higher purchase price. When the spread peaked in 2024, LNG sales hit records. When it fell by 2/3 in H1 2025, sales declined immediately. Meanwhile, BEV truck prices (€58–85K) are falling fast and TCO is already 10–26% below diesel. Leading manufacturers expect BEV to capture 50–80% of HDV sales by 2028. China sold 900K+ heavy trucks in 2024 — making this the world's largest and fastest-moving heavy truck electrification market. IEEFA: LNG trucking is unlikely to materially increase China's LNG imports because domestic liquefaction fully covers trucking demand (25 Mt produced vs 22 Mt consumed).
Sources: IEEFA (Aug 2025) · IEEFA (May 2025) · CleanTechnica · McKinsey
China LNG Trucking Ecosystem: Infrastructure + Supply + Competition
ECOSYSTEM
LNG Infrastructure
4,500+ LNG stations (55× US, 5.6× EU). Sinopec entering LNG retail at existing fuel stations. Extensive pipeline network enables low-cost domestic gas supply to every province. Government promoted LNG trucking as priority gas utilization. Regional concentrations: Hebei 20%, Shanxi 17%, Shandong 9%, Henan 9% of sales.
Domestic LNG Supply
25 Mt produced domestically (2024, +41% capacity). 22 Mt consumed by trucks. Self-sufficient — trucking boom does NOT pull global LNG cargoes (IEEFA). Feedstock: domestic gas production + pipeline imports (Central Asia, Russia). Liquefaction at regional hubs. Pricing: LNG-diesel spread is THE demand driver — volatile.
BEV Competition
BYD, SANY, XCMG, Foton, Dongfeng = major BEV HDV OEMs. 400–600 kWh trucks at €58–85K. CATL battery swap: "75#" standardized pack, 30 models, 12 OEMs. NEV subsidies for replacing old diesel HDVs. China sold 450K+ ZE-HDVs in 2025 (25% share) — 19× the EU's 23,700. World's largest BEV truck market by far.
Sources: IEEFA · McKinsey · China-Global South Project · CleanTechnica
China LNG Road Transport: Segment Outlook & Risk Matrix
OUTLOOK
SegmentTrendConfidenceKey Insight
🧊 LNG HDV SalesPeaking/Declining ▼High~30% in 2024 (record) → 26% H1 2025. Price spread fell 2/3. BEV outselling LNG 2 months running. LNG has peaked. Sales will decline as BEV captures 50–80% by 2028.
🔋 BEV HDV SalesSurging ▲▲▲High22% H1 2025 (from 8.6%). Aug: 28%. 450K+ units in 2025. TCO 10–26% below diesel. €58–85K. China = world's largest BEV truck market. 19× EU.
🔄 Battery SwapGrowing ▲▲▲High~1/3 of BEV HDV sales. CATL "75#" standard. 5-min swap. Solves range/charging for long-haul. $8,320/100K km advantage vs LNG. THE innovation that makes BEV competitive for long-haul.
⛽ LNG InfrastructureMature →High4,500+ stations. Fully built out. Will serve existing 1M+ fleet for years. But new station buildout will slow as BEV takes over new sales.
🌍 Global LNG ImpactMinimal →HighDomestic production (25 Mt) > trucking demand (22 Mt). Self-sufficient. China LNG trucking boom does NOT pull cargoes off global market. No import dependency.
📉 Diesel HDVCollapsing ▼▼High~57% in 2024, near 50% early 2025. Diesel use -11% YoY. Peaked 2021. Both LNG and BEV are eating diesel. Diesel will be <30% of new sales by 2028.

China's LNG road transport story in one sentence: China built the world's largest LNG trucking ecosystem (1M+ trucks, 4,500+ stations, 25 Mt domestic LNG production) in just 5 years — then its own BEV industry overtook it. LNG captured ~30% of HDV sales in 2024 (record) but is already declining to 26% in H1 2025 as BEV surges to 22% (from 8.6% a year earlier). CATL's standardized battery swap system solves the long-haul range problem, and BEV trucks at €58–85K with 10–26% lower TCO are irresistible to fleet operators. LNG trucking in China was the bridge from diesel to electric — and the bridge is now shorter than anyone expected. The existing 1M+ LNG fleet will run for a decade, but new LNG truck sales will be overtaken by BEV in 2025–26 and will be a minority by 2028.

Sources: IEEFA (Aug 2025) · IEEFA (May 2025) · McKinsey · China-Global South · CleanTechnica
India LNG Trucks on Road (2025)
~1,300
+75% from 747 (2024). But far below 3× growth expected. vs China: 178K+ LNG HDV sold in 2024 alone. India = very early stage.
Source: Business Standard (Jan 2026)
India LNG Station Target
800–1,000
By 2030. Current: ~25–30. UGEL: ₹900 Cr for 100 outlets. GAIL: ₹6.5B for 29 stations. THINK Gas: 18 LCNG. PSUs pausing, private sector leading.
Source: Indian Infrastructure
India Policy Target
1/3 LNG
Of long-haul trucks within 5–7 years (Oil Ministry draft, Sep 2024). Gas share in energy mix: 6% → 15% by 2030. NITI Aayog roadmap.
Source: Tata Motors / MoPNG
India CNG Stations
7,000+
Dominant alt-fuel for passenger + light vehicles. Target: 10,000 by 2030. CNG is India's success story. LNG for HDV is trying to replicate this model.
Source: PNGRB / GAIL
India: Nascent LNG Trucking — Ambitious Policy, Slow Execution
INDIA
🔵
Blue Energy Motors
Essar group. 500 LNG trucks deployed by Mar 2024. Planning 10,000 (LNG + electric). Circular model: Essar builds trucks → Greenline operates → UGEL fuels. JK Lakshmi Cement = first customer.
🚛
Tata Motors
Prima 5530.S LNG (Cummins 6.7L, 280 HP, 1,000+ km range). MoU with THINK Gas (Oct 2025) for corridors. Deployed with Tata Steel. -30% CO₂ vs diesel. Also acquiring Iveco (€3.8B).
⛽
UGEL (Essar)
₹900 Cr for 100 LNG outlets. Corridor-first: Golden Quadrilateral, Delhi-Mumbai, NH44/48. 25 outlets by Mar 2026. Capacity for 15,000 trucks. Private sector leading as PSUs pause.
🔗
THINK Gas
Backed by I-Squared Capital, Osaka Gas, Sumitomo. 18 LCNG stations. MoU with Tata Motors. Connecting industrial hubs, agri regions, logistics clusters. Private LNG infra pioneer.
🏛️
GAIL + PSUs
GAIL: ₹6.5B for 29 LNG stations. IGL, MGL, MNGL contributing. But: PSU oil companies (BPCL, IOCL, HPCL) pausing LNG retail expansion in 2026 due to low utilization. PSU pullback = risk signal.
🚚
Other OEMs
Ashok Leyland AVTR 1922 LNG (₹28.30 lakh). Volvo India. Eicher. 9+ LNG truck models available. BS-VI compliant. Price range: ₹28–42 lakh. Multiple body types (truck, trailer, tipper).

India's LNG trucking reality: ~1,300 LNG trucks (end 2025) vs China's 1M+ fleet. India's Oil Ministry wants 1/3 of long-haul on LNG within 5-7 years — but at current pace (75% growth on tiny base), that target requires ~100× more trucks. The challenge: high upfront cost (¥80K premium over diesel equivalent), LNG price volatility, sparse station network (~25-30 operational), and PSU oil companies pausing expansion. Private sector (UGEL/Essar, THINK Gas) is leading because of circular business model (build trucks + operate fleets + fuel them). India has 7,000+ CNG stations but only ~25-30 LNG stations — the CNG→LNG infrastructure leap hasn't happened yet.

Sources: Business Standard · Motor India · Tata Motors · Indian Infrastructure
APAC (Ex-China) LNG Road Transport: Outlook & Risk Matrix
OUTLOOK
MarketTrendConfidenceKey Insight
🇮🇳 India LNG TruckingNascent ▲Med~1,300 trucks. +75% YoY but tiny base. Oil Ministry: 1/3 long-haul on LNG. UGEL 100 stations. Tata + THINK Gas corridors. Ambitious but execution 1% of China. PSU pullback = risk.
🇮🇳 India CNG (Benchmark)Mature ▲▲High7,000+ stations. Dominant alt-fuel. 1.8 lakh CNG vehicles sold (2023, +53% YoY). CNG is India's success story. LNG for HDV trying to replicate this model for heavy trucks.
🇯🇵 JapanMinimal →High~30 LNG stations. Small, no growth. Japan trucking = diesel + emerging BEV. Isuzu/Hino BEV trucks. Japan is skipping LNG trucking → BEV + H₂ FCEV for HDV.
🇰🇷 South KoreaMinimal →High~20 LNG stations. LPG (Autogas) dominates (3M+ vehicles). Hyundai XCIENT FCEV for HDV. Korea → hydrogen fuel cell trucks, not LNG. H₂ is the Korean strategy.
🇦🇺 AustraliaNiche →Med~10 LNG stations (east coast). Mining trucks = diesel. Viva Energy, BOC supply. Remote corridors (Perth-Melbourne, Brisbane-Sydney). Niche for remote long-haul. BEV won't work for outback.
🌏 SE AsiaNot Yet ✖LowZero LNG trucking in Thailand, Indonesia, Vietnam. All diesel. TRATON e-Delivery (BEV) being produced in Thailand for export. SE Asia may skip LNG trucking entirely → diesel → BEV.

APAC (ex-China) LNG road transport story in one sentence: India is the only APAC country (ex-China) with a real LNG trucking program — but with ~1,300 trucks vs China's 1M+, it's running 5–7 years behind and faces the same BEV competition that is now eroding China's LNG truck market. The Essar/Blue Energy vertical integration model (build + operate + fuel) is smart but hasn't scaled. Japan and Korea are skipping LNG trucking entirely (→ BEV/FCEV). Australia is a niche for remote corridors. SE Asia has zero LNG trucking. India's real question is whether LNG trucking will have enough runway before cheap Chinese BEV trucks arrive — because India's trucking market (4M trucks, 40% of pollution) cannot afford to build LNG infrastructure that becomes stranded by BEV within a decade, exactly as happened in Europe.

Sources: Business Standard · IEEFA · Indian Infrastructure · Tata Motors
Gas Trucks Sold in Brazil
~1,000
GNV + GNL, cumulative through 2024. From zero 4 years ago. Scania: 400 units (2024, +14%). Iveco: gas line at Sete Lagoas (MG). VirtuGNL: 200 vehicles.
Source: ABEGÁS / Scania
GNV Stations (Brazil)
1,900+
CNG for light vehicles. RJ ~650, SP ~350, MG ~180. Corredores azuis (heavy truck) being built: Fernão Dias, Londrina-Paranaguá, NW São Paulo. But zero dedicated LNG stations.
Source: Gasmig / ABEGÁS
BEV Trucks Sold (2024)
480
~90% light/medium. MAN e-Delivery (Resende/RJ): 49% share. JAC, BYD entering. Volvo FM Electric testing (PR/SC). Import tax returning to 35% by 2026.
Source: ICCT Brazil (Jun 2025)
Diesel Share of Heavy Trucks
99.8%
2023. Biodiesel B14 (2024) → B15 (2025) → B20 (2030). Volvo 24%, MAN 22%, Scania 20%, Mercedes 18% market share.
Source: ICCT Brazil
Brazil's Nascent Gas Trucking: From Zero to ~1,000 in 4 Years
NASCENT
🚛
Scania (Pioneer)
First GNV truck in Brazil (2019). First GNL trucks (2022, Morada Logística). R$2B investment. 400 gas units/yr (2024, +14%). São Bernardo do Campo = global gas truck export hub. 20% HDV share. GNV/GNL flex with biometano.
🔴
Iveco
R$1.1B investment at Sete Lagoas (MG), gas truck production line. 6.8% HDV share. Completing by end 2025. Tata Motors acquiring globally (€3.8B). Brazil = key LatAm production hub.
🟢
VirtuGNL
R$5.7B investment until 2030. 200 vehicles (end 2024), quadrupling in 2025. Grain transport in Matopiba (MA/TO/PI/BA). LNG stations at Balsas + Santo Antônio dos Lopes (Maranhão). First LNG truck shown at Ministry of Transport (Aug 2024).
⛽
Corredores Azuis
GNV/GNL freight corridors being built: Compagas (Londrina-Paranaguá), Gasmig (Fernão Dias BR-381), Necta (NW São Paulo, 12→20 stations by 2026), Sulgás (RS, 14 heavy-truck stations). Connecting agri regions to ports.
🌱
Biometano Flex
All gas trucks accept biometano in any proportion — true flex fuel. Presidente Prudente (SP): first city with urban biomethane network (Cocal Plant). Biometano can replace GNL at corridors near production hubs.
🔋
BEV Trucks
MAN e-Delivery (Resende/RJ, since 2021): first BEV truck made in LatAm. 49% of BEV sales. Volvo FM Electric testing. Scania vision: BEV urban (<250 km), GNV/biometano medium (200-600 km), GNL long-haul (>600 km).

Scania's multi-fuel vision for Brazil: BEV for urban/regional routes (up to 250 km), GNV/biometano for medium distances (200–600 km), diesel/GNL/biodiesel for long-haul (>600 km). This segmented approach recognizes Brazil's continental dimensions (5,500 km coast-to-coast) — BEV alone cannot serve the entire market. The key constraint is fueling infrastructure: Brazil has 1,900+ CNG stations but zero public LNG stations. VirtuGNL and the corredores azuis concept aim to solve this with targeted corridor buildout connecting agri regions (Matopiba, Mato Grosso) to export ports (Santos, Paranaguá, Itaqui).

Sources: ABEGÁS · Agência Gov (Aug 2024) · Brasil Energia · ICCT Brazil
Brazil LNG Road Transport: Segment Outlook & Risk Matrix
OUTLOOK
SegmentTrendConfidenceKey Insight
🚛 GNV Heavy TrucksNascent ▲▲Med~1,000 units in 4 years. Scania + Iveco investing billions. Corredores azuis forming. Biometano flex. Real momentum but from near-zero base. Infra is THE bottleneck.
🧊 GNL Long-HaulPilot ▲MedVirtuGNL: first LNG truck (Aug 2024). 1,200 km range. R$5.7B plan. Matopiba grain corridors. Zero public LNG stations. GNL for >600 km is the niche — if stations are built.
⛽ GNV InfrastructureGrowing ▲High1,900+ CNG stations. Corredores azuis: Compagas, Gasmig, Necta, Sulgás building heavy-truck corridors. BRDE credit lines for truck conversion + station buildout. CNG infra is growing. LNG infra is zero.
🌱 Biometano TruckingGrowing ▲▲HighGas trucks = flex (accept biometano in any proportion). Lei Combustível do Futuro mandates. CBIOs for transport use. Biometano could be Brazil's unique trucking fuel — agri residue → truck fuel → decarbonized logistics.
🔋 BEV TrucksGrowing ▲Med480 sold (2024). MAN e-Delivery leads. Volvo FM Electric testing. But: import tax returning to 35%. Brazil's distances limit BEV to urban/regional. Not a threat to GNL long-haul.
⛽ Diesel + BiodieselDominant →High99.8% of heavy trucks. Biodiesel B14 → B15 → B20 by 2030. Ethanol flex for light vehicles. Diesel will remain >90% of heavy trucks for at least a decade. But B20 biodiesel = significant blending.

Brazil's LNG road transport story in one sentence: Brazil went from zero gas trucks to ~1,000 in four years (Scania pioneer since 2019, Iveco investing R$1.1B, VirtuGNL R$5.7B) — but with diesel at 99.8% of heavy trucks and zero public LNG stations, the market is embryonic. The unique Brazilian opportunity is the biometano flex angle: gas trucks accept biometano in any proportion, and the Lei Combustível do Futuro (Oct 2024) creates mandatory demand. Scania's segmented vision — BEV for urban, GNV/biometano for medium, GNL for long-haul — is the most realistic framework for Brazil's continental dimensions. The corredores azuis (blue corridors) connecting agri heartlands (Matopiba, Mato Grosso) to export ports (Santos, Paranaguá, Itaqui) are where GNL trucking will first emerge. Brazil's LNG trucking story is not about replacing diesel fleet-wide — it's about creating decarbonized agri-logistics corridors where biometano + GNL power the grain trucks that feed the world.

Sources: ABEGÁS · ICCT Brazil · Agência Gov · Brasil Energia · Protea
